section.p: PART ONE

p.emphasis: Talks from Nashik: March to November 2017

p.strong: 1.THE DIAGNOSIS

section.p: Who wants happiness and peace?
Why is there fear?
Why is life so painful? I am depressed and worried
Why can’t I find happiness and peace?
Why is my peace disturbed?
Why are these bad thoughts coming?
Why do we have so many questions?
What do we really want?
What do you mean by “real happiness”?
How did everything come about?
What is the meaning of life?
So, my mistake was to identify with the body-form?
Why is there pressure to go searching here and there for Truth?
How can we leave all our problems behind?

p.strong: 2.THE PRINCIPLE OF SPIRITUALITY

section.p: Why do I need spirituality?
What is the principle behind spirituality?
Is spirituality useful?
What is the secret of spirituality?
What do we mean by spirituality?
What is that Secret Asset?
What is Reality?
What is illusion, and what is Reality?
Is duality an illusion?
What do you mean by identification?
What is Conviction?
How will Spontaneous Conviction appear?
What is this body?
How is this world illusory?
If the Identifier is invisible, how do you identify the Identifier?
If the Seer disappears from the body, what happens to the world?
You say, that nothing has happened, and nothing is going to happen?
What is Brahma Dnyan?
Why are there disciples and Masters?
Why do we bow to the Master?
Who, or what, is holding this food-body?
Who is acting within the body?
Do we need God?
Who wants Realization?
Who is the Master addressing?
“I” am doing this or that. Where is that “I”?
Who says “I am Brahman”?
I don’t know why I focus on the darkness, and not the light?
How can I identify myself?
What do you mean by refreshing our memory?
What is the mind, ego and intellect?
What is illusion?
What is sin?
What exactly do we need to know?
What is spiritual awareness?
After reading all these spiritual books, where do you stand?
I do have knowledge, but, it is not practical knowledge
What do you mean by “I don’t know”?
Does spirituality mean dissolving all our concepts?
What do you mean by knowledge?
What is spiritual knowledge?
Who wants spiritual knowledge?
Why are there still some questions?
Why do these concepts appear?
How can I discard all these concepts?
Why do I need meditation, a Master, and all this spiritual knowledge?
Why spiritual books and Masters?
Why is a Master needed?
What exactly is this body-form?
Why is Conviction necessary?
How do I identify myself?
What is the best way to go beyond, or prior to, all this?
How do we absorb this Truth, totally and completely?
So, we need to put in effort, but not struggle?
How can I Realize?
Is it possible to identify what I am positively? It seems we can only know what we are not...?
What do you mean by total involvement?
What does Selfless Self mean?
You often mention “glimpses of I”?
You never talk about death?
What kind of changes will take place?
How do I concentrate on the Concentrator that is formless?
“Be strong! Have courage!” What does that mean?
Even though I am convinced intellectually, I still have material needs
Can we know anything?
If I am not the body, what am I?
What value does this body have?
What is our lifestyle based on?
I know I’m not the body, but still I want a long, and happy life?
Is it okay to follow my family tradition, of devotion to Rama and Krishna?

p.strong: 3.THE MEDICINE: MANTRA, MEDITATION, DEVOTION

section.p: How can I digest the knowledge?
How can I control this crazy mind?
What is meditation?
What is the principle of meditation?
Is meditation absolutely necessary? I am averse to it
Why do you insist on continuous meditation?
Why the Naam Mantra?
Why do we have Naam Mantra meditation?
Will the Naam Mantra help my spirituality?
Why is the Naam Mantra required?
In the beginning, I must put in some effort?
So, will the Naam Mantra help me to identify myself?
I cannot come to India to receive the Naam Mantra...
A casual approach to meditation is futile
How do I practise the Mantra, and how does it work?
So, we recite the Mantra “I am Brahman, Brahman I am”?
Do I recite the Mantra aloud, or silently in my mind?
When I recite the Mantra, do I have to pay attention to the meaning of the Mantra?
Does meditation mean repeating the Naam Mantra, or sitting in one place and meditating?
I have been reciting the Mantra for a few months. Should I continue?
Nisargadatta Maharaj said: “the purpose of the Mantra is to surrender the seen to Brahman” How is this done?
What is the minimum time for meditation?
Will meditation make me spiritually strong?
Does meditation mean that I have to sit for four hours?
Some days I cannot even recite the Mantra for half an hour. What then?
Do I just close my eyes for a few minutes?
When I recite the Mantra, should I concentrate on the words, the sound, the breath, the Master, or just Selfless Self?
While we are going through this meditation process, can we read books?
Can you say something about devotion?
Should we keep the sadhana going till our last breath, or only until Spontaneous Conviction is reached?
Would you say, that meditation is letting go of our attachment to thoughts?
What do you think of other kinds of meditation, like Buddhist meditation?
What does concentrate on the Invisible Concentrator mean?
When I recite the Naam Mantra, do I need to fight the illusory world?

p.strong: 4.THE PROGNOSIS

section.p: What are the results of meditation?
What are the changes that will take place after Conviction?
After tasting the nectar, how is it reflected?
How do I know if Brahman is absorbed in me?
You say, that I am the architect of my life?
If we compare a realized person to a non-realized person…?
What do you mean when you say “the body touch dissolves”?
If this is a dream, can we do anything we want?
Why is devotion needed after Realization?
Nisargadatta Maharaj used to pray after Realization. Why?

p.strong: 5.EXHORTATIONS AND WARNINGS

section.p: Be brave, and live like a lion!
You must have the courage to accept the Reality
Spirituality gives you courage
You say we need courage, but I don’t feel that I am a very courageous person…
“Bless me! Bless me! I need a blessing!” Why are you begging?
Why do we search for a Master?
Why do we need to listen to a Master?
How can anyone transfer power to you?
Why go here and there?
Why are we running after a mirage of spirituality?
What does spirituality mean? Touching the Master’s feet, clapping, garlands?
We are not doing any miracles
Why does Nisargadatta say this, and Ramana Maharshi say that?
“Oh, I am a spiritual man, how can I spend time with my wife, my father, my parents?”
What is the use of this knowledge?
Some people want to become sannyasins
Is your Master realized, or not?
Reading books and dry discussion, will not serve your purpose
What is the point of counting beads?
Nisargadatta Maharaj said, “Don’t mix with such elements which will distract you from Reality”. What did he mean?

p.strong: 6.TROUBLESHOOTING

section.p: I don’t understand the second part of the Mantra. Are the syllables of the second part, the reverse of the first part?
When I recite the Mantra, my mind is always trying to hold onto something
I already have a Mantra. If I take your Mantra, does this mean that, I am starting all over again from the beginning?
In the first two or three months, after receiving the Mantra, I couldn’t concentrate on the meditation, there
was a lot ofconfusion and depression
I took the Mantra, and it has no effect
That Conviction is not happening to me
I face so many difficulties, how can I concentrate, and just be the witness?
Every time I feel closer to Selfless Self, it is, as if something comes in the way
I have the feeling that I am coming closer and closer to my Selfless Self, but then I fall…
I am far too attracted to women…
We feel very good when we hear these talks. Why does this not last?
If some illusion or dream arises, should I try to stop it with the Mantra?
When I meditate, a lot of thoughts are coming up. How do I make the mind still?
When there are challenges, can I still stay with the sense that my real identity is Selfless Self?
I feel trapped in the body, in maya
What are the things that are blocking us, Maharaj?
When I came two years ago, this knowledge was being absorbed very easily, but not anymore
Why is Realization so hard?
I just have this desire for closure of this whole spiritual search, rather than continue to meditate, do Self-enquiry and listen to teachers
I have problems with my family. Why is my son not studying?

p.strong: 7.MISCELLANEOUS

section.p: If there is no Realization, what happens?
As long as I recite the Mantra, I will be able to accept whatever comes my way? Is that right?
You say, there is nothing, but I think there is something else, there is a sense of beingness…
How can it be said that the waking life is no different from a dream?
If I do this sadhana, will I make progress?
Should I eat vegetarian food, or not?
What comes first, the Absolute, or food?
What is the significance of Hari Narayana?
Every question is answered in your books, so I can’t make up any questions
Can we say that Ultimate Truth uses the body to know Itself?
It is said in spiritual science, that your last desire takes you to another dream
You are talking about the new world. What about the old world?
Is that also another thought…that which wants peace?
Is meditation and Self-enquiry the same thing?
How important is Self-enquiry? And, is there an end to it?
What is enquiry?
Where does the body and the Spirit come from?
What is Self-knowledge? Is it a direct sense of the primary “I”?
Spontaneous waves of light occasionally appear. Do these experiences enable Self-enquiry, or are they just mind-body-knowledge to be discounted?
Once, when I was on holiday in Tokyo, I went to a Buddhist temple. It was very peaceful. Is that okay, Maharaj?
Maharaj, the body feels like it is vibrating. Is this good?
When do I get liberation, mukti?
What will happen after my death?
After that continuous hammering, what happens to the
personality, does it dissolve with the body?
Is there a God?
Sometimes in meditation, I am aware of the colour blue. I wonder if that is why Shiva, Rama and Krishna are depicted in blue?
I wonder if, when I die, when I leave this body, I will realize that Selfless Self?

title.p: PREFACE

section.p: “Everyone should be enlightened!” is the clarion call of our Beloved and Great Sadguru, Sri Ramakant Maharaj.

Ultimate Truth will transform the reader. It is not just another book of spiritual knowledge. Using words as a medium, the Master places the reader’s Identity before him. Maharaj’s boundless compassion, and relentless drive, to bring the suffering of all beings to an end, radiate from each page.

Here, the reader will find the rarest, clearest, and most powerful teaching, that is not only comprehensive, but practical. They emanate from the “Direct Spontaneous Knowledge and Experience of a rare Self-Realized Jnani”.

Maharaj focuses on the needs of the seekers of our time: transform the easily available intellectual knowledge into actual, living knowledge. He is a lawyer that gives the courage to fight one’s case for freedom. He is a doctor that helps dispel the disease and recover the original health. He is, as he once said, the garbage man, calling for your trash!
It is the greatest fortune that you have encountered this unparalleled book, that is imbued with infinite wisdom, and the strongest exhortation for liberation.
But as Maharaj says again, and again, “Only You Can Make it Happen, Have Courage, Nothing is Impossible, the Ball is in Your Court – Smash it!”

Ultimate Truth is a crown jewel, in the casket of treasures, that Maharaj has given to us during His lifetime. It will lead you to uncover the True Treasure of your Selfless Self: at last you know yourself in a real sense! There is the Conviction that you are unborn, and therefore, immortal. Your life is filled with causeless, permanent happiness and peace. You are ever free of worldly suffering!

In 2016, Maharaj repeatedly expressed the wish to have a new book with his talks published, so that all aspects of his teaching are covered, that no question remains unanswered, that there is no place for doubts for devotees in the future. He would often conclude his talks with, “What you recorded, you type it – it will be useful for other devotees”.
The Master saw a beta version of this book just before He took Mahasamadhi on the 31st August 2018. Though the body has gone, He is with us, closer than ever before.
Per His wish, the book is presented for the first time on the day of the commemoration of the Mahasamadhi of his beloved Master, Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj, at Ranjit Ashram, 18th of September 2018.
The first print of the book was sent to the press on Guruvar (Thursday, Guru’s day in Indian Calendar), 6 September 2018

title.p: ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS

section.p: We wish to express our gratitude to Sadguru Sri Ramakant Maharaj, and to Ranjit Ashram. Special thanks are due to the following:

Gopal (Luc Bonameau), Joseph Tonelli, Dimitri Belos, Bertlee Garced, Chhaya Loomes, Saraswati (Anne van Nie), Rami Kowaluru, Rahul Pandav, David Bowen, Jim Nowak, and Muru Chauhan for recording and transcribing the Master’s Talks, organising the material, gathering the questions from seekers.
John Richards, Michael Reginato, and Amit Sawant (Maharaj’s son) in the U.S.A. for recording, transcribing, editing and arranging the Talks of Part 2.
Deepak Dhangar for co-ordinating and organizing the project, as well as liaising with the publishers, Zen Publications.
Thanks to Anvita R. Sawant (Maharaj’s wife) for the proofreading, to Yogesh Sharma and Zen Publications for the quick print of a beta book for Maharaj to see before his Mahasamadhi.

And last but not the least, all of us, including the readers, owe a special thanks to Ann Shaw (editor of Selfless Self) for the final editing. Despite personal illness, which involved prolonged hospitalization, Ann worked tirelessly for over two months to ensure that the final editing and formatting of the book was completed and delivered to the publishers so that they could meet the deadline of September 18th.
Sincere apologies for any omissions and errors.
Jai Sadguru!

title.p: INTRODUCTION

section.p: Ultimate Truth consists of 3 parts: Talks from Ranjit Ashram in Nashik, India, between March and November 2017, and Talks from the Master’s U.S.A. visit in 2016. (Bold text is used to hammer home the teachings.) The final section, “How the Master Changed my Life” contains personal sharings, from seekers, whose lives have been transformed forever, by Maharaj’s teachings.

There are around 250 questions. Those ultimate, and fundamental, questions of our existence, as well as the more commonplace, everyday ones, are answered with uncompromising power, clarity and directness, that leave nothing to the imagination. There is no hiding place from Truth!

These profound teachings, that take the reader beyond knowledge, are conveyed in a simple, light and effective way. Ultimate Truth leaves no stone unturned, so that all the questions that are posed by seekers, are finally exhausted. How to achieve Self-Realization is presented simply, and logically, flavoured with the Master’s own unique brand of humour!

Our most humble and compassionate Master, tirelessly shared these teachings, again and again, expecting nothing in return. He had one wish only: to put an end to our needless suffering, once and for all! Even in His final talk, when he was visibly frail, and his voice was very feeble, his focus remained steadfast: liberation for his devotees. With great poignancy, he pleaded: “Please accept the Reality”.
We humbly bow, with deep and overflowing gratitude, at the feet of our Great Master, Sri Ramakant Maharaj. Ultimate Truth is a timely gift to the world!
– The contributors

title.p: PART ONE Talks from Nashik March to November 2017

p.strong: 1 THE DIAGNOSIS

p.emphasis: “You are posing as a somebody, a something, and you are trying to live within the circle of illusory concepts. And because of this, life is painful. It is intolerable.”

p.strong: 1. Who wants happiness and peace?

section.p: The body does not want happiness and peace. Spirit does not want happiness and peace. So, who wants happiness and peace? When the Spirit touched with the body, the concepts began to appear. They started creeping in, and growing, one by one. Prior to beingness, there were no concepts, no language, and no words, such as peace, happiness Spirit or Presence. There was nothing. Who is going to be looking for happiness and peace, after leaving the body? Everything comes from nothing, and everything goes back to nothing. This Reality must be established within you. Many seekers can talk very knowledgeably about Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God and the Masters, but they do not have any Self-knowledge! This kind of intellectual, or theoretical entertainment, is completely meaningless.

Who wants happiness? Does the body-form want happiness? Does the Spirit or Presence? Who wants happiness? Find out! Who is enjoying happiness? Who is expecting peacefulness? Who is under the pressure of fear? You must find out, because unless you undergo Self-enquiry or Self-investigation, and receive answers to these questions, you will not be able to identify yourself. And, sadly, you will continue with your tragic and painful, illusory life.

Roaming here and there, from Kanyakumari, to the Himalayas, and approaching endless Masters, without using any discrimination whatsoever, is totally meaningless, and it is such a waste of your time and energy. Reading countless books is also meaningless. Why? Because the Source of this world lies within you. “You Are the Source of This World.” But you do not know, that you are the Source. You are unaware of your spiritual existence. You are unaware of your immensity.

Whatever knowledge you may have, and whatever you may be doing to find happiness, is coming from your body-base. It is coming from your “I am the body-form” stance. You consider yourself to be the body-form, and therefore, your search is driven by the ego, which has assumed the pose of a separate individual. Accumulating knowledge only serves to enlarge the ego. Sometimes, you will experience temporary relief from the stresses of life, but this relief is only temporary because your base, your viewpoint, your perspective is body-based, and therefore, illusion. It is a fact, that the body is not your identity at all.

What is the purpose of spirituality? Prior to beingness, there was no spirituality. After the body expires, there will be no spirituality either. Find out how your spirituality is helping you – if, it is, in fact, helping you! What is your conclusion, after reading and studying all these books? There must be a conclusion. Don’t just keep reading and reading. “You Have Read All the Books, But, You Have Not Read the Reader.”

It is pointless to keep reading and reading, without identifying the Reader. Identify the Reader! Identify the Searcher! What are you looking for? Who is looking? What are you getting out of spirituality? There must be a conclusion. Is your knowledge practical knowledge? Will it stop you shaking and trembling on your deathbed? How deep does it go? Find out!

I am not telling you to stop reading books, or to stop visiting Masters. I am asking you to try and identify yourself. Ask yourself, “Who wants all these things?” “That Unidentified Identifier is Already Within You.” But we have forgotten our true identity. When the Spirit touched with the body, all your discomfort and dis-ease began. And so, your expectations for happiness suddenly arose. In India, we give honey to the young children. We give them something sweet, to make them feel better when they are upset or crying. Similarly, we try to make ourselves feel better using different means: comfort food, comforting relationships, stimulating company, family support, etc. We find whatever we can, to ease our discomfort, and ameliorate our suffering.
We carry with us some illusory hope, that we can obtain happiness from this illusory world. That is an impossibility!
Unless you try to identify your Selfless Self, you will not be able to find real happiness, true happiness.

Where were such words as “happiness” and “peacefulness” prior to beingness? The Spirit, or Presence, that is acting within the body, does not need these. Spirit does not need anything. We have so many needs and wants, simply because we consider ourselves to be separate body-forms with separate identities: “I am the body. I am somebody. I am Chinese. I am Indian. I am Japanese”, or whatever. This is not your identity at all. To identify yourself in a real sense, a process of reciting the Mantra and meditation is essential. With this practice, you will regain your Original Identity, called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master, That you are! And, after Spontaneous Conviction, you will be able to face any challenge head-on. Nothing will be able to touch you because you will know, truly know, that you have nothing to do with this illusory world. You will be solid, immovable, and established in your own status, your true status of Ultimate Truth.

How were you prior to beingness? And, how will you be when you leave the body? You were formless, stateless, thoughtless. That Stateless State is your real Identity, your pure Identity. The outcome of the meditation practice, and consistent hammering is a return to your original state. This state has always been there. It has never altered. It cannot change because it is changeless. You have been unaware of your true nature because of the body-form, which covered over your Presence with layers of illusion.

When all the concepts of fearlessness, happiness, unhappiness, depression, tension, etc, etc, disappear spontaneously, you will be in that Original Stateless State – the way you were prior to beingness. The true purpose of spirituality is the removal of ignorance. Spiritual knowledge dissolves and demolishes all the body-based concepts. At present, you are not able to identify yourself. Instead, you are identifying yourself in the body-form, as the body-form. This is not your identity at all. The Naam Mantra is required to dissolve all the concepts. It will establish your true identity within you.

Reciting the Mantra will bring about miraculous changes. For example, currently, if someone criticizes you, you may feel upset and hurt. However, after Spontaneous Conviction arises, whether someone praises you, or abuses you, will be of no consequence whatsoever to you. Any castigations that are made in your direction, will just wash over you. You will remain untouched, and non-reactive, at all times, because you will know, not even know, but you will be convinced, that, “I am not the body. I was not the body. I am not going to remain the body”.

Adopting this new stance, and altering your whole perspective, means that you will have no cause to worry about anything ever again. There will be no more suffering. How will this be possible? Because you will know, that you have nothing to do with this whole illusory world. When you are convinced that you are separate from the world, you will be able to live like that. This knowledge is not theoretical knowledge, it is practical knowledge, that is to be applied to your daily life.

Having said that, even though you have nothing to do with the body-form, this does not mean that you are to neglect your body. Not at all! Take good care of your body and attend to its needs. Your parents and relatives, your work, and your ability to earn, are all part of your bodily existence, your earthly life. Live a simple and normal life. Work is necessary to earn money, otherwise you will not be able to survive. Don’t be a beggar, and expect, or demand money from anyone. Stand on your own two feet.

While you are attending to your routine, daily life, and to your physical health, it is most important that you attend to your spiritual health too. Be passionate! Have that burning desire to find out, “Who am I?” That is the question. That is the main question: “If I am not the body, if I was not the body, if I am not going to remain the body, then, who am I?”

“The Questioner, is Itself, the Ultimate Truth, the Final Truth. The Invisible Questioner is the Answer.” The Invisible Questioner has been called Brahman, Atman Paramatman, or God. Who, or what, is that Brahman, Atman, Paramatman? It cannot be defined. It is nameless. It can be compared to the space or the sky. Your Presence is just like the space or sky, but you are subtler, even more subtle than that. You are formless, omnipresent.

After Spontaneous Conviction: “I am not the body. I was not the body. I am not going to remain the body”, your Original Identity will be exposed. And then, in spite of living in this illusory world, you will remain unconcerned with the world. This is the purpose of spirituality.

p.strong: 2. Why is there fear?

section.p: It is the nature of the Spirit, because the Spirit only knows itself through the body-form. The Spirit does not know that, “I am Spirit”. Presence does not know that, “I am Presence”. It knows itself only through the body-form. It is attached to the body, and therefore wishes to continue with the known, ie, life in the body, for as long as possible. For example, say, you poured water over a few ants, they would immediately start running away, looking for an escape. Why? Because even the smallest, and the subtlest Presence, wants its body to survive. It is the nature of Spirit or Presence because the body-form is all that it knows, either as a man or a woman, animal, insect or some other being.

You fear losing the body because you have identified with it, and accepted it, as your Reality. The body is not permanent. It is not your Reality. You do not know yourself. You may know many things, but you do not know yourself. Spirituality teaches you how to identify, and know yourself in a real sense. Spirituality gives you the knowledge, so that you can emerge from ignorance.
We know ourselves in the body-form, and that is illusion. But, at the same time, don’t neglect your body, or your duties.

Carry out your responsibilities. If you are a businessman or an employee, or, if you have a family, attend to the tasks at hand. Attend to your responsibilities. However, at the same time, during the course of this life, try to identify yourself. Try to find the answer to the question, “Who am I?” Know yourself in a real sense.

Some day or other, willingly or unwillingly, you will leave the body, and the body will embark, on its last journey to the cemetery! But, the good news is that you are not the body. The Conviction that you are not the body will arise spontaneously, and then you will be free from any fears associated with the concept of death.

After Conviction, you will know that the body-form was never your identity, and will never be your identity. There is nothing to fear. What have you to fear? Who is afraid? Your fearful thoughts will pass. Don’t struggle with them! Let them go!

Meditation will bring your struggles to an end, so that all your fears will disappear for good. Spirituality gives you the strength and courage, the power and the energy, to live life free of struggle, to live life spontaneously. Live life bravely. Live with your light. Live by that light. Live as that light. Don’t be a coward! You will leave the body one day. No one can escape from this fate. So, prepare for that day! This is the idea behind spirituality, and the meditation practice because through meditation, all the illusory concepts, such as death, and the fear of death, will simply dissolve.

What is meditation? It means non-stop recitation of the Naam Mantra. This priceless Mantra came from Dattatreya. Theoretically, we know that, “I am Brahman”, but theory is not sufficient. Literal knowledge or bookish knowledge is not enough because theoretical knowledge can easily be forgotten. Therefore, material knowledge, literal knowledge, or dry knowledge, will not help you.

Be practical! Practicality is most important. Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj carried on working in his shop, till he was 70 or 75 years old. He did not leave his job, or abandon his duties, saying, “Oh I am a spiritual man. I am a Master. I will not continue with my business”. No! I was also working in a bank.

Stand on your own feet, and don’t depend on anyone else. Don’t depend on body-based knowledge, or, on your routine life. Don’t depend on spirituality outside of you either, because spirituality is inbuilt in you. You are already a spiritual man, or a spiritual woman. You are not the body-form. Why, therefore, do you wish to go here, and there, in search of spirituality?

So, “Be With You” all the time. You must find the courage to stay away from “bad company”, or those elements that can have a negative influence on you, and as a result, distract you from your Reality.

There are so many teachers and Masters in the world today, who charge vast amounts of money for their spiritual talks. I am not criticizing them, but, it is happening. Here, we are not running a business, or trying to profiteer from seekers. There is absolutely no commercialization of spirituality. Our Lineage places great emphasis on sharing this knowledge, free of charge. We are not withholding anything. We are sharing all the knowledge. How can you charge for knowledge that is yours! It is your innate knowledge.

We are pointing out that except your Selfless Self, there is no God. This is direct knowledge. It is very, very simple knowledge. All these Lineage Masters have shared the same, very basic, and simple knowledge, that there is nothing except your Selfless Self. So, why are you still going elsewhere to find your identity? Where is Peter? Where is John? [laughter] You are here, and nowhere else.

That which you have been searching for, is Itself the Ultimate Truth. Ultimate Truth is not separate from you. Be strong! Be spiritually strong – not egotistically strong. When you are spiritually invincible, (and physically strong), no one, absolutely no one, will be able to impress you with his own ideas or concepts.

When you know yourself in a real sense, it does not matter where you live. You can be anywhere in the world, living as the Ultimate Truth that you are. You need not go searching again. If you have sufficient time, you can visit this ashram. You don’t need to stay for lengthy periods. Just remember, that you don’t have to travel to Ranjit Ashram, [often referred to as Nashik Ashram] because “Wherever You Are, There is Your Ashram”. You may be living in a large townhouse, or you may be living in a small cottage, somewhere out in the country. Where you are is of no consequence. Your ashram is with you at all times, wherever you go.

You do not need a variety of exercises to identify yourself. All you need for Conviction is strict meditation. This practice is most important. It is the foundation. Through it, you will truly know that you are not the body-form. With this Conviction, all your fears will melt away, because you will no longer be attached to the body-form. You will know that deep down, at the very core of your being, you are eternal. You are unborn. There is no death for you. There is only death for the five element body.

p.strong: 3. Why is life so painful? I am depressed and worried

section.p: You find life miserable and intolerable because you have become a slave of your mind. Was there any depression, worry or pain, prior to beingness, prior to the body-form? Was there any anger? No! There was nothing. And, when the body expires, what will be left? We keep trying to extract happiness, from many different external sources because we find our body-based-knowledge unbearable.

The gist of spirituality is: “Except your Selfless Self there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman”. You are Atman. You are Paramatman. You are God! This means that everything is to be found within you. Happiness is not to be found in this illusory world. It is to be found in you because you are the Source of happiness. You do not know that You are Almighty God.

Directly, or indirectly, you are considering yourself to be the body-form. The body-form is not your identity at all. You are basically formless. It is an open fact. Prior to beingness, or, say, prior to a hundred years, you had no form. You had no knowledge about yourself. You did not know yourself at all. But now, because of the body-form, you know, that you are.

When the body eventually expires, dissolves or disappears, the known will dissolve back into the unknown. There will not be any trace of your identity left. Where will your pain, or depression be then? Who wants peace? Who wants a tension-free life? Who wants a fearless life? All these requirements exist because life in the body is, in fact, unstable and unbearable.

Why is a Master needed? Because you have forgotten your Identity. In order to have the Conviction that you are not the body, meditation is needed. Reading books, and listening to various Masters is not enough. It is only dry spirituality, dead spirituality. It is not practical spirituality. Here, we are teaching practical spirituality. Don’t abandon your work and family for spirituality. Your routine life and spirituality can co-exist perfectly. Be normal! Be humble! Live as a good human being. And, at the same time, try to identify yourself. This human body is such a golden opportunity to identify yourself in a real sense. Be careful not to miss out, and lose this valuable time, this wonderful opening, to find out who you really are.

Be firm! Be courageous and accept your Reality. I am placing Reality before you because your body-identity is temporary. You must be brave! Have courage! Yes, of course, there may be some depression or worry, and other calamities that may come your way, but even with these difficulties, know that they are only passing clouds. They are temporary bodily vibrations.

Don’t become a victim of your own self-created psychology, worrying all the time: “Oh! What will happen? What if this happens? What if that happens?” Nothing is happening. Nothing has ever happened, and nothing is going to happen because there is no doer, and there is no deed. You are not the doer. You have not done anything. You are unborn! Fact! Accept it!

Your Presence is like the sky or space. The sky does not know that it is the sky. Does it announce itself in this way, “I am sky?” The sky does not have any family members. Does the sky have a wife, a mother, or a father? Similarly, you are everywhere, like the sky. In fact, you are subtler than the sky because you can identify it. As you are listening to what I am saying just now, you may not believe it to be true, but in time, you will.

Don’t be anxious or depressed. You are unaware of your value, your true worth. You have great importance. “Your Presence is Invaluable Presence.” Your greatness lies within you, but you are ignoring it, neglecting it. You are underestimating yourself, always worrying, “Oh, dear! What is going to happen to me?” You are worried about the past, the future, or the present. And it is all a waste of your energy because there is no past, no present, and no future! These are the concepts. Nothing has ever happened. These are just the concepts, that we have blindly accepted. You can erase them now!

Don’t depend on anyone, or anything, outside of yourself. There is no need to say, “Oh God bless me”, because you are already whole and complete. You are completely independent. A Master is needed because you have forgotten your Identity. Your Spontaneous Presence is itself your Master. Your Spontaneous Invisible Presence within this body is the Master. I am placing this open fact before you. It is your Truth. Therefore, don’t torture yourself with pain, guilt or worry. Be brave! Have some guts! Everything comes and goes. It may be good or bad. Forget it all! Do not give so much importance to everything. Instead, find out how to come out of all these illusory circumstances.

To make this human life bearable, you have to identify yourself. You need not go anywhere to find yourself. Try to please your own Inner Master. The Master is within you. “The Finder Itself is the Master.” Read Selfless Self, I Am That, and other spiritual books, like Master of Self-Realization. They will help you. They are really good books for spiritual information and spiritual identification. They will help your Conviction.

A lack of confidence is causing your spiritual malaise. Be brave, and try to find stability. You are lacking in confidence and willpower. Don’t underestimate yourself. Be confident: “Yes! I can do it! Nothing is going to stop me”. Confidence is most important in life. Discard everything: “I am not the body. I was not the body. I am not going to remain the body”. And in time, whenever problems arise, they will not sting you, because you will be convinced that they are related to the body only – and you are not that! Whatever appears to be going on, is going on with the body, and therefore, cannot touch you. When you know that everything is illusion, you will find yourself forgiving and forgetting, anything and everything, that happens in your life. You will notice, an increase in tolerance and patience, as well as many other changes taking place.

We are worried and depressed, because we are measuring ourselves in the body-form. We have mistakenly accepted the body-form as our identity. This should be erased, dissolved. Everything will melt with meditation.

Meditation is needed at the initial stage. The Spirit is very sensitive, so when it is hammered with, “I am Brahman. Brahman I am”, over and over, again and again, a purification process takes place. You are hammering the ego, and saying, “You are a liar! You are not male or female, you are Brahman!”. This is how the Naam Mantra works. With repetition, the illusory layers that have covered your Reality over a lifetime, are gradually, and effectively removed. After continuous hammering, eventually, you will come to the realization: “Yes! I Am That! Why worry? Why be depressed? Everything is illusion. It is all a dream, a long dream. And you will laugh at yourself, pondering and reflecting on your life, asking, “Who was in pain? Who was depressed? Who was worried?

In the morning, we chant: “Shivoham Shivoham” which reminds us that everything is illusion. There is no Master, no disciple, no father, no mother. You have nothing to do with this illusory world. Shankaracharya says, “To say ‘I’ is illusion, to say ‘you’ is illusion, to say ‘Brahman’ is also illusion”. The entire world is illusion, therefore, considering yourself as the body-form is also illusion.

You may have accepted that you are not the body-form, but maybe only intellectually. This knowledge must be absorbed, and then there will be Spontaneous Conviction. There will be no more pain, depression or worries.

p.strong: 4. Why can’t I find happiness and peace?

section.p: There is always tension and fear around us because we are not identifying ourselves in a real sense. Since childhood till today, we have been identifying ourselves, as men and women. We have been trying to know ourselves, using the body-form. This mistaken identity, this illusion that you are the body-form, must dissolve. You may have some knowledge about Reality, that you are Brahman, but your knowledge is only literal knowledge. Knowledge of your Reality must be absorbed, and the only way this will happen, is through meditation. Meditation is essential. It is the base, the foundation.

What is meditation? It does not mean sitting cross-legged, with your eyes closed, nor does it mean reciting the Naam Mantra in a mechanical fashion. What is needed is your total involvement. Self-involvement will bring you closer and closer to Selfless Self.

You are to accept what the Master says, so that, slowly, silently and permanently, all the bodily effects that have obscured your Reality over a lifetime, will dissolve. Then, all your unhappiness, dispeace and irritation will simply melt away. Your troubling, illusory concepts will dissolve with meditation.

At present, you are posing yourself as a human form, when, basically, you are formless. In the beginning, you will need to put some effort into the meditation, until it happens spontaneously. The time will come when it will continue by itself, without your knowledge.

At the Ultimate Stage, you will forget about your whole illusory identity. And, in spite of holding the body, and attending to your work, and many other tasks, you will not be concerned about anything, even little dramas or big crises that come your way. And not only that, you will find that you experience exceptional happiness, and exceptional peace. You will no longer need to search for happiness from material sources, as you did previously.

Who am I? This is the most important question. Be serious, not casual! This human body offers a golden opportunity, to identify yourself in a real sense. But if your approach is a casual, half-hearted one, then, once more, you may see a similar type of “dream”. [dream refers to the concept of “rebirth”. There is no rebirth]

This is to be your last dream! Make it your last! We have become victims of our own concepts. These body-based concepts are to be erased, and permanently dissolved. The purpose of spirituality is to know yourself in a real sense. Spirituality invites the attention of the Listener within you. It indicates your Reality, that: “except for your Selfless Self there is no God, No Brahman, no Atman”.

Be strong! Be serious! Some visitors come here, and take the Naam Mantra. They listen to the Talks, but then... nothing! Because they are lacking faith and confidence, they are soon back on the road again, travelling, here and there, Guru-hopping. They are looking for more, looking for something else. There is nothing more. There is nothing else. “You Are Final Truth.”

There has to be a full stop! You have reached the destination. Everything is within you. I am repeatedly saying: “Except Your Selfless Self, There is no God, no Brahman”. “Why Keep Travelling, When You Are the Destination?”
“Everything is Within You. You Are The Central Point of this Universe.” Happiness and peace, are to be found within you alone, because you are the Source of happiness and peace.

p.strong: 5. Why is my peace disturbed?

section.p: Because directly or indirectly, you are posing as a somebody. You think that you are an individual, a separate person, a separate entity, an individual. The ego says, “Oh, she is disturbing me”, or, “he is verbally attacking me”. You are living as if you are a separate individual, and reacting to others, as if they are individuals too. “There is Peace. You Are Disturbing the Peace.”

You are Presence! You are not the body. That Presence is within the body. There is not “my Presence”, and “your Presence”. There is only One Presence. It is the same Presence that is everywhere. If you want to compare yourself to something, compare yourself to the sky. You are everywhere just like the sky. There is no difference between the speaker in this body, and the listener in that body over there. Accept Reality! It is your Reality. It takes courage to accept Reality because it demands a letting go, of everything that you have previously taken for real.

There is no past life, no future life, no hell, no heaven, because you are not a doer. Without a doer, there can be no deed. Some people say that because of their previous bad deeds, they have taken birth as a man or a woman, etc. This is nothing but imagination, concepts, illusion. There is nothing. There is no birth. There is no death. You are unborn. We are talking about the fate of the unborn child.

Take up the challenge, and accept Reality. Don’t be a coward, worrying, “Oh what is going to happen? Oh, what if he hurts me, or what if I lose my job?” You are not helpless. Be bold and fearless: “Yes! I can do it!”. Age is not a barrier to Self-Realization. It is not an excuse: “Oh, I am an old man.
What is going to happen to me?”, or, “I cannot change”. Spirit has no age. Is the sky one, or two hundred years old? Your Presence is ageless. Our age is only connected to the body.

To know yourself in a real sense requires total and complete involvement. Devote some time for yourself, so that you can meditate, for at least, one, two or three hours per day. All these saints devoted about ten hours a day to meditation. But nowadays, this is not possible. If you don’t have much time, at least manage one hour. In the beginning, practice is essential. Afterwards, it will not be necessary. When you first begin the practice, your mind will rebel against you, and not give you any peace. Just ignore these disturbances, and keep going.

The mind, the ego, and the intellect are there because of this body-form. Prior to beingness, they did not exist. Yet, we always give them so much importance, proudly stating: “My great mind, or my superior intellect”. But consider what is going to happen to the mind, the ego, and the intellect when the body expires? Everything will disappear. You are not the mind, the ego or the intellect. Everything came out of nothing, and everything will dissolve within nothing. You need spiritual courage to accept this knowledge, to surrender everything that you have known. “I can do it”, says my Master. “My Master says that I am Brahman, therefore, I am Brahman”.

To illustrate the strength of the ego and its identification with concepts, let’s say that someone called you a donkey [ass]. You would immediately feel like reacting aggressively. Correct? Why? Because you are insulted. The ego has taken a bruising. You have accepted the words and their meanings. Yet, when the Master says, “you are Brahman”, you will not accept that. Accept what the Master says. Nothing is impossible. Impossible is a word in the dictionary of fools! You are not a fool. Nothing is impossible!

You are not a doer. You cannot do any deed. But still, you continue to carry the burden of your human life. Change your view, change your perspective, and then, everything will be peaceful. There will be no tension! When you stop viewing yourself as a human being, there will be peace. Identify yourself: “Who Am I?” You are neither a man, nor a woman. You are Brahman. You are Ultimate Truth, which is not separate from you. Be realized, and make others realized. Come out of the darkness of ignorance!

p.strong: 6. Why are these bad thoughts coming?

section.p: Thoughts are flowing all the time. This is the nature of the body-form. The mind is a factory that manufactures thoughts. But because we are struggling with them, we get irritated. Don’t struggle with the thoughts. Accept the thoughts which are useful for your routine life. Otherwise, throw them out. It is very simple. Dissolve all these illusory concepts, and then your entire lifestyle will change.

This is plain knowledge, plain Truth. The language I am using here is very simple language, very basic language. Even if you think about it logically and intellectually, you can see Reality. You know, the Reality that is there. This body is not your identity at all. Some day or other, willingly or unwillingly, we will all have to leave our bodies. Try to identify yourself, and then you will feel secure, knowing that there will not be any more dreams like this one.

You are to discard all the concepts, including, “I am Brahman”. “Brahman” is also a concept because prior to beingness, you did not know, “I am Brahman”, or “I am Atman”. These particular words only appeared with the body-form, after the Spirit touched with the body. There was no language prior to beingness. We created language. It is illusion. We have created so many spiritual words such as Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master. Prior to beingness, you did not know one single word. You did not know anything because you did not know yourself. It is a fact that, “You are Totally Formless”. As of today, you are formless. Listen to me! This form is not your identity at all.

p.strong: 7. Why do we have so many questions?

section.p: In spite of listening to the Masters and the teachers, and, in spite of reading many, many books, there is still no Conviction. And because we lack Conviction and confidence, we are left with many unanswered questions. Where were those questions prior to beingness? Where will those questions be, after you leave the body? Maya, Brahman, Paramatman, God, Master, all these spiritual terms are merely concepts, which came along with the body. This is not to say that they are bad concepts. But while you are using these concepts, I advise you to also identify the Invisible Listener, the Invisible Witness within you.

You can see different worlds, you can see everything in your dreams. When you are dreaming, and in deep sleep, you assume many forms: human beings, animals, the sun and moon, gods and goddesses, etc. You can manifest in various forms simultaneously. This means that the whole world is projected in a dream, along with you. You are watching the dream, witnessing the dream. When you wake up, you will usually say, “I had a very good dream”, or, “I had a very bad dream”. Who is videoing that dream?

Who, or what, appears in the dream world? It is your Selfless Self. The same thing happens in this life. You take the form of a human being, and through this form, you are able to see the entire world. Your Invisible Identity which is unknown to you, appears as your father, mother, sister, Master, God, etc, etc.

Your identification with the body-form, has created all this confusion which, in turn, has given rise to endless questions. All your questions are body-related. You are not the body. You were not the body. You are not going to remain the body. You are totally formless. I am telling you, again and again: “You Are Totally Formless”. But in order to have that Spontaneous Conviction, you need to follow a disciplined practice.

Continuous self-hammering is needed: “I am Brahman. Brahman I am”. Brahman is a name that has been ascribed to Ultimate Truth, the Final Truth That You Are. You are Brahman. You are Ultimate Truth. Brahman or Ultimate Truth is not separate from you. So, stop counting yourself as the body-form. Stop considering yourself as some kind of small, insignificant, powerless, illusory body-form. You are seriously underestimating yourself. You do not know your own greatness. The fact is that you are vast. You have tremendous power, strength and energy.

This knowledge is rare. It is priceless, but sadly, because it is offered free of charge, it is not really valued. If I were to charge something, then the response would be different: “Oh yes! This Master has given us great knowledge”. Such is the world we live in today!

I am presenting you with your Reality. I am placing the facts of your existence before you. I am not giving you any theories of Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, or Master. These are nothing but words and names.

You are Ultimate Truth. You are Almighty God. It is a fact! Therefore, bow down to yourself! Bow to Selfless Self. The body is not your identity. Bow to That through which you know yourself. There is no difference between the Master and the disciple. Be with you! Uncover your strength!

We all have so many questions, thousands of questions. Why are these questions overcrowding your mind? Because the mind is rebellious. The crooked mind is rebelling against you, pinching and pricking you, stinging you all the time. The mind is a crazy mind. It is very crazy, and causes a lot of havoc and confusion, yet, it has no independent identity of its own. You are supplying power to the mind. The mind cannot function without your energy.

The mind, the ego and the intellect, are concepts that appeared upon your Presence. They have set up home in the body-form. Prior to body-based-knowledge, there was no mind, no ego, and no intellect. When the body expires, they will not be there either. Nothing will dissolve back into nothing. There is no need for you to make any special effort using the mind, in order to know yourself. Identify yourself! Just identify your Unidentified Identity. For the purpose of identification, and, to attain perfection, absorb the knowledge that the Master is conveying, and meditate. Meditate until you have Conviction. In what way is Brahman knowledge, Atman knowledge, helpful to us? How is it useful? It is useful because it reminds us of our forgotten identity.

As long as you consider yourself to be the body-form, you will continue to be swamped by concepts. As long as you are measuring yourself in the body-form, these concepts will keep on attacking you. Be as you were, prior to beingness. There was nothing there. When you leave the body, there will be nothing there. Prior to beingness, you did not know yourself. Everything that you are experiencing during this span of physical knowledge, body-based-knowledge, is illusion. You do not know who you are.

You have many questions because you consider yourself to be somebody, an individual, a separate entity. You should not have any questions at all. It is only because we mistakenly consider ourselves to be body-forms, that these concepts and questions arise. The questions keep flowing. Endless questions! When you know that you are not the body-form, the questions will finally dry up.

Everything is illusion, including you, and I. To establish the Reality that you are Ultimate Truth, a process of meditation, spiritual knowledge and Naam Mantra is recommended. Meditation, Naam Mantra, spirituality, spiritual knowledge, a Master, these are all needed to bring about Spontaneous Conviction.

The Invisible Listener, the Invisible Presence within you is Ultimate Truth, Final Truth. It has been referred to as Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master, etc. The name is irrelevant because that which you are is nameless. Respect yourself! Your Presence is invaluable. You are not respecting your invaluable Presence. Instead, you are giving respect to the illusory concepts, holding onto them, and protecting them. In fact, you have become a victim of all your illusory concepts.

What is the remedy? It is simple: just have the courage to accept Reality. Reality is not separate from you. Ultimate Truth is not separate from you. Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, or the Master, are not separate from you. But, in order for this knowledge to be absorbed within you, in order for you to have the Conviction that, “I am formless”, then, meditation is essential. The fact is that you are formless. The logical development of this, is that there is no birth or death. There is nothing to worry about. We are talking about the unborn child. You are unborn, formless, therefore, why worry about the fate of the unborn child? Spontaneous peacefulness will be yours after Conviction!

This is straight knowledge, direct knowledge. I am showing you the whole picture. Here, we are not playing any power games of hide and seek. The Master is not interested in gathering disciples. The Master is only interested in re-igniting the flame of your Inner Master. All you need, is to muster up the courage to accept your Reality, and to know sincerely and wholeheartedly, that: “I Am That!”

The human body offers you a golden opportunity, through which you can listen to your true story, and be reminded of your forgotten Identity. Listen carefully, engrave Reality within, and enjoy life! All your questions arise from the body-form. You are formless. There were no questions prior to beingness. There will be no questions after beingness. When you know Reality, the questions will evaporate.

p.strong: 8. What do we really want?

section.p: Everyone wants peacefulness. Every single being on this earth is looking for calm and tranquillity. We want to be at peace, and happy, free of the tension and crippling fear.

We all carry a fear of death. The concept of death frightens us because we consider ourselves to be the body-form. We are terrified of being extinguished, annihilated, and of losing everything, all that we believe ourselves to be. We cling to life, because we wish to hold onto everything that we think we are, have, and own.

The Spirit has accepted the body-form as its identity. That Spirit wants to continue life in the body because it knows itself as “I am”, through the body. It will do anything to survive, to perpetuate its bodily existence. When something goes physically wrong with the body, we will do all in our power to find a cure. We will search for medical specialists, who may be able to prolong our lives. Even if we are suffering from an incurable disease, the majority of us will choose, to survive for as long as possible. It is the nature of Spirit.

All our fears are rooted in the great fear of death. Death is a concept. There is no death. The body will expire, that is inevitable, but You will not expire. You are formless. You are immortal. When you identify yourself, there will be abundant peace and happiness. And, most importantly, the ghost of death that has haunted you throughout your life, will disappear, once, and for all. When you know that you are not the body, when you know that your identity is eternal, that you are immortal, you will no longer have any difficulty handling any problems that come your way.

What does this human body need? What do we want? Why are we visiting all these Masters and teachers? Why do we read all these books on philosophy, spirituality, religion? What exactly is it that we are looking for?

We are all looking for happiness and peace. We are looking for ways to overcome our difficulties. We are looking for answers. We do not know who we are. We are living our lives in a state of anxiety, depression, fear and dispeace. We are searching for meaning. We are asking “Why this? “Why that?” “Who am I?” We do not know. We keep searching and looking, but we are always looking in the wrong places. We are expecting to find the answers from outside of ourselves. We are expecting to find the answers from the illusory world.
You will not find the answer to your predicament, unless you turn within. “Look at You. Be With You, and Not With the World. All That You See is Illusion. That Through Which You See is Reality.”

Life in the body is intolerable because of its endless, disruptive demands and desires, that disturb the peace. “There is Peace. It is You Who Are Disturbing the Peace.” Concept after concept keep arising, telling you to do this, or do that, go here or there. And like a puppet, you blindly follow these dictates. Prior to beingness, we had no desires, no wants and no needs. Life in the body is insufferable, unbearable, because you are controlled by all the bodily demands that are in a constant state of flux. You know that you are not the body-form, but you are allowing the body-form to use up all your energy, and give you the runaround according to its whims.

If you are not the body, then, who are you? What are you? Find out! Ask yourself “Who am I?” Through Self-enquiry, one by one, you will eliminate all the illusory concepts, and know what you are not. You have made a huge mistake about your identity. You have forgotten your true identity, that you are Spirit or Presence, and not the body.

We have endless desires that we try to fulfil, to help us endure this illusory existence. We fulfil our desires to alleviate our pain and suffering. We seek out attractions and entertainment because we do not know who we are. We want sex, money and fame because it will give us momentary comfort and happiness. But we are unable to find any lasting happiness, permanent happiness, because we are sourcing our happiness from the illusory world.

At present, you do not know yourself in a real sense, therefore, all these desires are controlling you, overloading you. But, when you identify yourself perfectly, and realize: “Yes! I am nothing to do with this body-based knowledge. This body is only my external cover, and not my identity at all”, then you will find permanent peace and happiness.

“You Are Not the body. You Are the Holder of the Body.” This means that you are the Power, and the Source of Energy that sustains the body. Without Presence, your body is nothing but a dead body! The Holder of this body has no birth or death. Birth and death are mere concepts, that will dissolve with meditation. When everything has dissolved, when all the illusory layers covering your Presence have been removed, then you will live, an exceptionally peaceful, and happy life.

You will live life as before, ie, carrying out your responsibilities – but, with one major difference – you will live life knowing, that you are simply acting out different roles. You will no longer identify with the body-form, and, as a consequence, you will not be concerned with any problems that may come your way. You will be established in your Ultimate Truth. Permanent peace and happiness, without any stress or fear, will be yours! You will be living “in the world”, but, at the same time, knowing that you are, “not of the world”.

Meditate and absorb the knowledge that “I am not the body”. Meditation will reignite your Inner Master, who, in turn, will give you the guidance and instruction that you need. You will know how to act, and behave at all times, in a humble, and normal manner. Beware of hurting anyone’s feelings. If this happens, it is a sign that the ego is still in operation. Avoid causing anyone hurt and pain, in the name of spirituality. Beware of the subtle ego! Be calm and quiet! Reality will be absorbed within you, and then, you will know! You will no longer have any wants and needs because you will know yourself in a real sense. “What do You Want? Nothing! What do You Need? Nothing! Because Now You Know That Everything is Within You.”

p.strong: 9. What do you mean by “real happiness”?

section.p: Whatever happiness you have now is material happiness, external happiness, that provides you with material comforts. You find comfort from money, sex, publicity or fame. A variety of consoling, calming, soothing things, are there for the taking, out there, in the illusory world. They can offer you temporary happiness.

However, if you want to find real happiness, permanent happiness, you must identify yourself. You are Ultimate Reality. Reality is already within you, but you have accepted the body-form as your identity and your reality. A mistake has occurred. This mistake was not your fault. You were conditioned. Your Unidentified Identity is Ultimate Truth. You are Ultimate Truth.

Under these illusory circumstances that you find yourself in, try to know, “Who Am I?” This is the base, the foundation. You are the Unidentified Identifier which has been referred to by many different names: Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God. Reality is already within you, but, you are unaware of that. The body-form is not your identity at all.

You are depending on others to give you happiness. I have seen so many young boys leaving their family life, leaving behind their wives, their parents and children. They are going here and there, searching for someone who knows the secrets, someone who can tell them the truth, and give them happiness. They are suffering from the delusion that the truth lies outside of themselves, in the world, in the mind of someone who is superior to them. They are also deluded into thinking that someone can give them happiness. They feel that they are handicapped, less than whole, and therefore dependent on others. So, there is the mistaken conviction that, “if I approach this Master, I will find truth and happiness”. This is an illusory concept. There is no Master who can give you happiness. The Master does not grant any special favours.
The Master’s role, is one of inviting the attention of the Invisible Listener within you, that you are Ultimate Truth. The Master shows you that you are the Source of happiness.

Whatever is within this body, [Maharaj points to his body] is the same, as that within your body. But, you are identifying yourself in the body-form. This illusion has to dissolve. It is very simple. Why go here and there? You are amusing yourself, playing with spiritual words. This is a meaningless pastime: Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master, maya, chakras”, this chakra, and that chakra – an utter waste of your time! Don’t fall victim to spiritual words, or become their slave.

It is an “Open Fact” that the body is not going to remain a constant, so you must identify yourself. Who is acting in this body? Who is experiencing in this body? The body cannot act without Presence. It is the combination of Presence and the body, that enables the functioning of the body.

Presence does not know that, “I am Presence”. The body does not know that, “I am a dead body”. Just like with an electric fan, if there is no electricity, the fan cannot work. But, with the combination of the fan and the power – the fan is able to function. Similarly, the combination of the body and Spirit, enables the body to function … and then, the talking starts [Maharaj chuckles] The body is like a magic box.

Try to identify yourself, “Who Am I”? It is pointless to just continue travelling here and there, like a spiritual tourist, and devouring books on the way. Meditation will make it easy for you to identify yourself. “You Are the Source of All Happiness, Real Happiness, Lasting Happiness.” Meditation means sitting down quietly, and then trying to identify what is there.

Self-enquire! “What is in this body? Who is experiencing the thoughts?” Thoughts are flowing, but who is experiencing thoughts? Who is watching the dreams? Who is the watcher that is watching the dreams, and watching this world? Find out! Think on it! No one is thinking any more. They are accepting everything blindly.

After meditating for some time, the thinker will eventually disappear. Nothing will remain. It will be like the way you were prior to beingness, That Reality will open up. When everything dissolves, nothing will remain. Nothing! No “I”, no “he”, no “she”, no “it”, no “Master”, no “disciple”, no “God”.

This Conviction must appear. You need not go anywhere to find real happiness. “Everything Starts From You. Everything Ends Within You. Everything Comes Out of Nothing. Everything Dissolves Within Nothing.” Why are you in search of finding nothing? “Know That There is Nothing to Know.” You will find happiness without any material cause through meditation.

You are unborn. The concepts of birth and death are only connected to the body. They are not connected to You. You are like a child who was never born, but who is always thinking, and worrying about his future!

“Your Invisible Presence is Just Like the Sky or Space.” It cannot identify Itself. We are trying to identify ourselves with the ego that says, “I am somebody”, and then, we are trying to identify our identity in the light of that ego. What is the ego? What is the mind? What is the intellect? These came along with the body. They are the subtle, functioning organs of the body-form. They are not Ultimate Truth. “Ultimate Truth is Lying Within You.” Real happiness is to be found only in you. It is nowhere else.

“Who Am I”? The conclusion is: “You are Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God. The Master is not separate from you. That Masterly essence is within you. But you cannot truly know this without meditation. Meditation is essential. We are constantly being bombarded by concepts. They are pressurizing us. Meditation will bring us out of this vicious, illusory circle of concepts, which has entrapped us since childhood.

p.strong: 10. How did everything come about?

section.p: When the Spirit clicked with the body, you started to know: “I am”. And then, instantly, the world came into focus. This means that: “The Entire World is Projected Out of Your Invisible, Anonymous Presence”. If Presence were not in the body, who is going to be able to talk about the world? This means that you are the Principle behind these words. You cannot see the world without Presence. If there is no Presence within this body, the body is literally a dead body. A dead body cannot talk about the world, or Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, Master. Your Invisible Presence is called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, Master, through which you are talking, through which you are able to see everything around you.

Try to “Concentrate on the Invisible Concentrator Within You.” You are not doing that. What are we all trying to do? We are all trying to know what Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master, is through various external sources. Who is trying to find Brahman? You cannot find what you already are. Brahman is not separate from you. Atman is not separate from you. God is not separate from you. “The Master is Not Separate From You.”

You have forgotten your formless Identity, therefore, you are roaming here and there, trying to discover the meaning of Brahman. You are Ultimate Truth. You, yourself are Ultimate Truth. This is the principle of spirituality.

When you are meditating, you forget about your body-identity. You see, the mind, the ego and the intellect came afterwards. They are subtle veils that appeared upon your Presence. If there is no Presence, where is the ego? Where is the intellect? Where is the mind? The mind, the ego, and the intellect appeared upon your Presence. They are illusory layers. You are supplying power to them. You have become a victim, a slave of that mind, ego and intellect. You are measuring yourself as “I am somebody”, and that is the illusion. Use discrimination, and try to find out: What is illusion, and what is Reality? “Everything you see is illusion. That through which you see, is Reality.” “I am Inviting the Attention of the Invisible Listener Within You”, which is called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master. That is your Reality. Stop ignoring it! Stay with the Seer, instead of the seen. Right knowledge will dissolve all the illusory notions you have about yourself, and will reveal your true Identity.

p.strong: 11. What is the meaning of life?

section.p: There is no meaning of life because you are basically unborn, and hence formless. What was your identity prior to the body-form? And what will it be when you leave the body? When the Spirit clicked with the body, the words came into existence. Words like “meaning”, “life”, “death”, etc.

Nothing has happened in this illusory world. As long as there is body-based existence, these body-based concepts such as “meaning of life” will continue to appear upon your Spontaneous Presence. The meaning of life for whom? Self-enquire! This question, like all the other questions, is body-based. You are unborn. You are not, were not, are not going to remain the body.

Your Presence existed prior to beingness, prior to the mind, the ego and the intellect, prior to everything. You are trying to apply reason and logic to your illusory life, because you have identified with the body-form. Your Reality has nothing to do with reason and logic. Find out who is living? Find out who is dying? Nobody!

p.strong: 12. So, my mistake was to identify with the body-form?

section.p: It was not your mistake. It was not your fault. You did not know any better. All our needs and requirements came along with the body-form. Prior to beingness, there were no desires and no expectations, for happiness or anything else. Who wants happiness? Who wants knowledge? Who wants anything? You are an illusion, therefore, knowledge for whom? You have mistaken your identity. In order to identify yourself correctly, we need guidance from an authentic, enlightened Master. The Master reminds you of your forgotten Identity.

A Master is only needed at the beginning. After Realization, the outer Master is no longer necessary. You will be guided by your Inner Master. The Master re-introduces you to your Ultimate Truth. Considering yourself as the body-form is what has caused all your problems and confusion, your unhappiness and dispeace.

You are not the body-form. “You Are the Destiny, the Terminal. The Final Destiny, the Final Terminal.” “There is Nothing Except Formless You. There is Nothing Except for Selfless Self.” Nothing Is There. That Conviction will appear spontaneously. Conviction is not intellectual Conviction, it is Spontaneous Conviction. This is the purpose of our life. This Conviction will banish all the disturbing and painful atmospheres surrounding you. The concepts such as pain, and peacefulness are just that, concepts. They have come with the body-form. There is nothing there.

The human body is an external cover, just like the clothes we are wearing. It has a time limit, and an age factor. There is no age factor for Ultimate Truth. Does the sky have any age? Can we say, “Now the Indian sky is twenty years old, thirty years old, or a hundred years old”? Of course not! The sky does not have any age factor. Similarly, you, the Projector of the sky, and of this world, have no limitations at all. We are unlimited. We have limitations only because of the body. You are not the body. Find this out for yourself, and accept it. This is a fact. One day or other, willingly or unwillingly, we will leave the body. The body will be burned, or buried. This is not our concern. Your first lesson is Conviction, the Spontaneous Conviction, that “I am not the body at all. I was not the body at all”. You must have this Conviction, while at the same time, not neglecting your daily, routine life.

p.strong: 13. Why is there pressure, to go searching here and there for Truth?

section.p: Because you are posing yourself as the body-form. It is a fact, even if you think about it intellectually, that the body is not your identity. When you leave the body, what is going to happen to it?

Your Presence is called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master. The Conviction will appear in you spontaneously, as a result of meditation and reciting the Naam Mantra. You have been devoting all your time to the illusory world, now devote some time for yourself! Dry, spiritual discussion is meaningless. Be practical, do your job, do your duties. I am repeating the same thing, over and over again: Don’t neglect your spirituality. Spirituality is not separate from you. Everything is within you. There is no need to go anywhere to search for Truth. “You are Ultimate Truth.”

p.strong: 14. How can we leave all our problems behind?

section.p: That is the question for us all. You meet a Master. Then the Master tells you what the cause of your problem is. He gives you a diagnosis: You are measuring yourself in the body-form. “Basically, You Are Formless. There Is No Form.” Why feel guilty? You haven’t done anything. “There is No Deed, There is No Doer.” There is no experience, and no experiencer. “Your Presence is Just Like the Space, or the Sky.” “The Entire World Appears Upon Your Presence.” “You Are Completely Unconcerned With This World.” “That Invisible Presence Within You, is the Ultimate Truth That You Are!” To effectively ignore, dissolve and overrule all these illusory concepts, serious, concentrated meditation is needed, in the beginning.

The Lineage Masters have given us some secret words, through which to hammer yourself. You are reminding yourself that: “I am Brahman, Brahman I am, I am Brahman, Brahman I am”. Why do we need these words? Because you have forgotten your Identity. As long as you are considering yourself as the body-form, you will not be able to identify yourself in a real sense. All your illusory problems will continue as before, along with your illusory suffering.

For us to gain the Reality, and know Ultimate Truth, we need to hammer ourselves. The principle of this hammering, is that the Invisible Presence within us is very sensitive. Whatever is impressed on Spirit, is instantly reflected. Considering this fact, the Master gives the Naam Mantra, the meaning of which is: “I am Brahman, Brahman I am”. Therefore, while you are continuously reciting the Mantra, and sitting for meditation, you are regaining your old memories, slowly, silently and permanently. And then, you will have strong Conviction: “I used to consider myself as the body-form, but now, I know it is not my identity at all”. “I Am Ultimate Truth, Which is Called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master. It is Not Separate From Me.”

Previously, you were living like some sad, pathetic, little human being, holding the food-body. But the food-body has a time-limit. It belongs to the five elements. It is not your identity at all. When you undergo the process of meditation, some vibrations are created within you, that will effectively dissolve all your wrong concepts, your guilty concepts, your illusory concepts. You will emerge from the darkness, and live in the light.

When seekers start reciting the Mantra, some of them experience mood changes like depression, nervousness, irritation, etc. Contrary to what you may feel is going on, this is actually a good sign. It means that the Naam Mantra is working. It is having the desired effect. All the illusory concepts and impurities are being removed, one by one.

Keep at it! Continue with the Naam Mantra, and the meditation. And thereafter, you will have strong Conviction. Your painful life will dissolve, and in its place, there will be immense pleasure. You will feel it strongly: happiness, joy, elation, bliss. There will be no more pain because the illusory you is no longer operating.

There will still be good or bad moments in your life, little problems, or big problems, but you will emerge victorious, in spite of these difficulties. No matter what challenges come your way, your peacefulness will not be disturbed.

You will be happy, and you will stay happy. Nisargadatta Maharaj, Ranjit Maharaj, Siddharameshwar Maharaj, all suffered from ill-health, at some point in their lives. Even though their bodies were not co-operating, and in spite of their incurable diseases, they were always content, tension-free and happy.

Oftentimes, if we fall sick, suffering from flu, a fever, or something more serious, we will experience tension, accompanied by negative thoughts. But these great, saintly people stayed strong. They were untouched by anything. They remained firm, and solid, in the Stateless State because the Conviction that they were not the body was established. They viewed their illness in this way: “I am not the body at all. The body contracted this disease, not me!” “My Presence is Just Like the Space or the Sky.” The sky does not suffer from any kind of disease. They knew for certain that cancer, or tuberculosis, or any other disease, had nothing to do with their Ultimate Reality. Can the sky suffer from cancer or tuberculosis? It cannot! And, neither can you, because “You Are Subtler Than the Sky. Your Unidentified Invisible Identity is Subtler Than the Sky.”

You are posing as a man, or a woman. Think on it! Is it true? No one is thinking anymore. No one is discriminating. You are blindly accepting everything. Come out of all these self-imposed constraints! Prior to beingness, you were not a man or a woman. There was nothing there. You were totally Unknown, Invisible, Anonymous. And, when you leave the body, it will be the same. There will be nothing there. The logical deduction therefore, is that all your needs, requirements, and all your expectations, came along with the body. And they will inevitably dissolve, along with the body. This is easy to understand, but, it is a little difficult to maintain.
