In the beginning, you need to concentrate, and meditate, otherwise, the mind, ego, and intellect, and the body-based knowledge will creep up on you unaware. You will be distracted from Reality.

You are not a sinner. You have nothing to feel guilty about. You have not done anything. How can you? There is no doer. Forget about the food-body. Concentrate on the Invisible Concentrator within you. Devote your maximum time available for meditation. If you are not able to sit for any concentrated period of time, you can recite the Mantra all the time, while
doing other tasks. Remember my words also! You are listening just now. When you go home, picture yourself sitting in front of me, listening, like you are now. Remember my words!

That Masterly Essence is already inbuilt in you. There is no difference between the speaker and the listener. Nobody is telling you this truth, because many teachers wish to exert power over vulnerable seekers. The Master is already within you, but you are missing Yourself. Therefore, I am hammering you all the time, with the same words. Nisargadatta Maharaj said: “Except for your Selfless Self, there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman”. Accept the Reality, and watch the show. Enjoy the movie! See the funny side of it all. There is no need to feel bad, or to consider yourself a sinner. Be free from all bondage.

Be spiritually strong. You are physically strong, but be spiritually strong. Nothing is impossible. Don’t become a victim of your mind. The mind is not so important. The mind is the flow of thoughts. It is always manufacturing more and more illusion, and giving you new problems with which to contend.

You have tremendous strength, unbelievable strength, but, you are not using that spiritual strength. Okay, that is enough! I am repeating the same thing, again and again, and again. Now, meditate on this talk. Absorb it! And then, put the knowledge into practice.

p.strong: 48. What exactly do we need to know?

section.p: The basic thing that we need to know, is that the body we hold, is not our identity at all. Because prior to beingness, prior to consciousness, our Existence was there but it was an unknown Existence, without an experiencer, or any experiences. Then the question arises: How was I prior to beingness, prior to consciousness? Prior to beingness or consciousness, your Presence was there. It was like the space or the sky. There was no awareness. Your Presence is everywhere. Everything is within you. You are Ultimate Truth. There is no power except you, Formless You. The Truth is that except for Selfless Self, there is no God.

p.strong: 49. What is spiritual awareness?

section.p: Spiritual awareness will come from spirituality. “I was not the body prior to beingness. I did not have any form”. When Presence touched with the body, consciousness came instantly. And then, the mind and the intellect took birth. This is the process. Amidst all of this, there is your Presence which is completely separate from everything else. It is Pure Presence, pure consciousness, which remains untouched by the body-form. There is nothing there.

Spirituality means that you ought to identify yourself in the midst of all the illusion. “Where do I stand? I am not the body. I was not the body”. The mind, ego and intellect are also illusory, subtle organs. Through a process of “neti neti”, and eliminating and discarding the illusions, one by one, we will arrive at Reality. When all the illusory layers have been dissolved, when everything has been removed, what is left is Reality. There you are – the “Naked Truth”.

So why do we need spirituality? Why visit the Masters? There is no difference between the Master and the disciple. That Presence, that pure consciousness, that is within the Master, and within the disciple, is exactly the same. There is no difference, and there is no separation.

Using language, we are trying to convince ourselves, “I am separate from this illusory world!” Why? Because there is the pressure of death, and the fear of death, which keeps gnawing, and eating away, at our peace and happiness. There is always stress and tension. To emerge from all of this, you need to identify yourself: “I am not the body-identity. I am not the body. I was not the body. I am not going to remain the body”. It is a fact that all these practices will result in your Spontaneous Happiness.

Prior to consciousness, how were you? At that time you had exceptional peacefulness, where there was no experiencer, and no experiences. Now, because of this body-form, we have a lot of experiences. There is so much to see around you, that you feel, “I am having this experience, that thrilling experience, this amazing spiritual experience”, etc. Something appears to be happening. It is a happening, and a non-happening, appearing upon your consciousness. If there is no consciousness, there is no happening, no non-happening, no knowledge. Knowledge and no-knowledge are in that consciousness. If the Spirit had not touched with the body, these concepts would not be there. When all your body-based concepts disappear, there will be spontaneous happiness and peace. Guaranteed!

p.strong: 50. After reading all these spiritual books, where do you stand?

section.p: There must be a conclusion. What conclusion have you reached, after reading so many books? You have read endless books, but have you read the Reader, the Invisible Reader that is within you? You are not to be found in books because everything is within you.

The principle of spirituality states that God is not separate from you. God does not have an independent identity. If your Presence were not there, who can identify God? To say God, your Presence is needed. This means that you are the father of God, and the mother of God. You may be surprised at what you are hearing just now, but these are the facts! If there is no Seer, if there is no Presence in the body, who is going to identify God? God is the supernatural power that is already within you, but you are counting yourself as the body-form, and that is an illusion.

What is the message given by the various Masters through their spiritual statements? And where do you stand, with regard to their message? It is most important that you know. Question yourself, and find the answers from within. Through meditation, you will prompt Selfless Self to dialogue with you, and answer all your questions. In the beginning, the practice must be a deliberate act. Keep it up until there is Spontaneous Conviction. Then, everything will be easy, and unfold naturally, and spontaneously.

“Where do you stand in the light of all your knowledge? This is the question. Who am I? After finding out, “Who Am I?”, that “I am” will also disappear. This is the purpose of spirituality. Jnaneshwar says, “I am going down, prior to, prior to, where nothing was, through which speech, language, and words are appearing”.

Four stages are involved in the process of speaking: Para, Pashyanti, Madhyama and Vaikhari. Para means the thoughts, the subtle thoughts. Pashyanti means the experience of the thoughts. Madhyama means coming from the mouth, instantly. First of all, the thoughts are appearing without your knowledge. Then, at the second stage, you experience the thoughts. Then it moves to the neck, and then it comes out. This is the spiritual process. There is no need to give it much attention! This means that: “That” through which you are talking, through which you are listening, that Invisible Presence is called Brahman.

When you are trying to identify Brahman, the Identifier will eventually disappear. There will not be anything remaining. That You Are! “Where Everything Ends, There You Are.” That Conviction will appear with meditation. Meditation means full involvement. Complete devotion is required. There must be commitment. You must be driven, burning to know, “Who am I?”

You can find out where you stand through meditation. You cannot know yourself through books. Force your Selfless Self to talk! You have knowledge, but if you just keep reading and reading, you will never reach a conclusion.

When you return home, carry on with the practice, with complete devotion: “Yes, my Master says I am Brahman, therefore I am Brahman” – end of story! Game over! The Conviction will come. There are no rules to follow, no expenses, no restrictions, or exercises. Just try to identify yourself, and then you will stay firm in the Perfection of your Identity. That is the purpose of spirituality. Identify yourself, convince yourself, and know where you stand!

To carry on talking for hours, will make it all meaningless. The principle is very simple. The entire world is projected out of your Invisible Presence. That You Are! The body is not your identity at all. “Be With You” all the time. Recite the Mantra given to you by your Master. Meditation will lead you to your Inner Master. It will bring you to your Inner Listener.

p.strong: 51. I do have knowledge, but, it is not practical knowledge

section.p: Everyone knows that the body is not going to remain. But, as long as you are attached to the body, the mind, the ego and the intellect, this knowledge will not be absorbed. You are under pressure from the mind, the ego, and the intellect. You are supplying energy to these. Your energy is most important. Your Presence is most important. You do not know your value. You are still considering yourself as a male or female. Your attachment to the body is a hindrance.

Listen carefully: the human body presents you with a golden opportunity to identify yourself. Though it is not your identity, the human form is the medium through which you can identify yourself. Use it for its real purpose!

p.strong: 52. What do you mean by “I don’t know”?

section.p: How were you prior to beingness? You cannot guess, or imagine, how you were prior to beingness. Everything is illusion, including you, and I. Everything we see, think, know, and understand is illusory. Our essence is beyond everything that we can possibly know. It is beyond the mind. We cannot know the unlimited, from our limited body-form stance.

All that we can know, is “I don’t know”. In that negative answer, lies a positive meaning. This apparently negative answer comes from a positive. There is something there, something that knows, in order to say, “I don’t know” “I don’t know” means: “I am Not in any Form. My Presence is there, but it is Invisible Presence, Unknown Presence, Anonymous Presence, Unidentified Presence, which is called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master”.

When Presence clicked with the body, the world became visible. Prior to that, was there any body there? You say, “I don’t know”. And, after leaving the body, will there be any world there? You say, “I don’t know”. It is only because of the Spirit clicking with the body, that you are able to see the world.

Let me give you a simple example. If you strike a matchbox with a matchstick, you will see a flame. The combination of the matchbox, and the matchstick, produces friction which brings about fire. But that fire is already there. It exists everywhere, but, it is unknown, and invisible. This means that the unmanifest fire, at a particular moment manifests, due to the combination, i.e. the match striking the matchbox. The same thing happens when the world manifests. The combination of the Spirit and the body – the “click” of Spirit with the body, brings about the appearance of the world. Your Presence was already there. It was everywhere. But that Presence was unknown to itself.

When the Spirit clicked with the body, you became aware of your existence, and started saying, “I”. Prior to that “I”, that Invisible “I” existed. Your Presence is totally Anonymous, Unidentified, Invisible Presence. This means that your Presence is everywhere, just like the fire that is everywhere, in the example I have just given. Convince yourself in this way.

Teaching others is very easy, but teaching the teacher is very difficult. Hammer your inner teacher: “I am Brahman. Brahman I am. I am Ultimate Truth!” Hammer, and keep hammering, and then you will see the results. Accept your Reality! “Yes! I Am That!” Then all of your concepts will melt, including, “I am Brahman”, “Presence”, “Spirit”, etc. Language has served its purpose.

This is a fascinating process that will eventually lead to, “Yes! I Am That!” Don’t worry! It will happen with meditation. I guarantee it! An interesting example of this process is documented in the Dasbodh:

There is a large stone statue that is awaiting the skill of the Master Sculptor. There are many parts in this stone statue that need to be removed. They are not wanted. They do not belong there. Complete faith is needed in the sculptor, in that person that is chiselling and hammering, and removing all the parts that have blemished this statue. The original statue has been damaged by add-ons. The statue has always been there inside the stone! But, some skilful hammering is needed to remove the unwanted parts. Once these additions have been removed, the statue will be restored to its original glory. When the statue is humbly returned to its pristine state, nothing can ever stick to it again, or be added to it, or obscure it again.

Here, the Master is the sculptor who removes all the add-ons, that have covered over your Presence. He hammers and chisels, penetrating the numerous, unwanted, illusory layers. At the same time, you are also remoulding yourself with the Naam Mantra, clearing away everything that is not part of your Ultimate Reality.

Thoughts, concepts, habits, ingrained illusions that you have been living with all your life, may rear their head once more. That is to be expected. However, to prevent this from happening, you are to take some precautions. My Master, Nisargadatta Maharaj, says: “I am Not Making you a Disciple, I am Making you a Master”. You are to maintain that Reality with meditation. As I told you, meditation is also an illusion, but, in order to remove one illusion, you need some help from another illusion. To remove a thorn from your foot, you need to use another thorn. After removing the first thorn, then throw it out, along with the second thorn!

If, and when, concepts reappear, dissolve them quickly! It is very simple. This is rare knowledge, the rarest of knowledge. “It is Your Story, the Listener’s Story.” We are not talking about Brahman, Atman, Paramatman. But, you must have some courage to accept this: “I Can Do It! Nothing is Impossible.” Trust and faith, is most important. Trust and faith is lacking because we have a dual mind that pulls us in different directions.
Stay with “I don’t know”. This means, “I am formless”. It means that “I know that I have always been, and, I will always be.

p.strong: 53. Does spirituality mean dissolving all our concepts?

section.p: Yes! You have forgotten your Identity, and that is why you need spirituality. Prior to this body-based-knowledge, there was no spirituality, and no knowledge. There were no concepts of gods and goddesses, and no concepts of disciples and devotees. All these concepts of gods, goddesses, Masters, disciples, and spiritual knowledge, came along with the body. We need to dissolve each and every concept!

You could say, that the Spirit touching with the body, was accidental. When this occurred, you started saying “I”. Because you started experiencing this “I”, and feeling it, a whole new history came into being. This illusory history is filled with illusory concepts such as “I”, man, woman, birth, death, last birth, future birth etc. etc.

To come out from all this illusion, we need spirituality. And, once it has done its job, once it has served its purpose, it can be thrown out. Spirituality, like everything else, is an illusion. After Conviction, nothing is required.
Your Invisible Reality is speaking, listening, watching, acting. That Invisible Reality is currently not known to you. You need a Master, and spiritual knowledge, to make it known.

Let us scrutinize the body! The body belongs to the five elements. After death, it dissolves back into the elements. The entire body, including the mind, the ego and the intellect (the subtle body), is a creation of these five elements. “Nothing Remains Except Your Selfless Self. Selfless Self is Not Visible. It is Invisible.” It cannot be recognized or identified. That is why I always say that it is Unidentified, where there is neither an experiencer, nor experience.

What is Reality? You will find out! You will receive answers from within. Remember that, “The Master is Not Separate From You. Ultimate Truth is Not Separate From You”. As long as you are measuring yourself in the body-form, you will not be able to identify yourself – That You Are Ultimate Truth.

There is no search. “Where All Search Ends, There You Are.” You do not need to search for Truth because “You Are Truth.” Why go elsewhere to find That which is only in you? “Everything is Within You”, but you are not aware, and that is why you are roaming, and wandering, here and there. This wandering should be brought to a halt!

Show everyone respect. Take care of your body, and take care of your family members. At the same time, however, there should be the Conviction within you, that, “I Am Nothing to do with all this Body-Based-Knowledge. I Am Nothing to do with Body-Based Relations”. Shankaracharya says, “All these relations are fictitious, artificial, imaginary relations. My father, my sister, my wife, my son, my Master, my God. They are all a fiction”.
All these relations have absolutely no grounding in Reality because prior to beingness, there were no relations: no

father, mother, sister, brother, wife, Master, God, etc. There was absolutely nothing. When you leave the body, where will all your relatives and relations be? There is no “you”, and there is no you, that has any relations. That is Ultimate Truth.

When all these imaginary concepts of relations and relationships have dissolved, along with all your desires, what is left is nothingness. And, “Within that Nothing, There is Something That is Unknown”. “The Unknown Came Into Existence in The Known.” When the unknown became known, you announced your existence, as “I am”.

Spirituality has nothing to do with holiness or piety. Spirituality has one purpose, and one purpose alone, which is to erase illusion, and establish your original Identity, that you are Ultimate Truth. Talking incessantly is meaningless. Identify yourself! Identify yourself through meditation. Through it, a complete cleansing process will take place. So, please try to identify yourself. Meditation is a powerful tool, and it will effectively dissolve all your concepts, “Slowly, Silently, Permanently”. And then, Reality will open up within you.

You will begin to see with your spiritual eyes, and not with these eyes. You will be wearing God’s spectacles! And, you will come to know, that what you thought to be true, is, in fact, untrue. That Conviction will appear, and that is sufficient. You can read books, you can listen to anyone if you wish, but be careful not to become their victims. Nine times out of ten, these so-called teachers, are imposing their own illusory concepts on you, and thus creating more layers on your Presence.

Don’t accept any more concepts such as past birth, future birth, past sin, future sin, gods and goddesses, kundalini and so on. You are to come out of all these concepts because everything is a concept, and concepts appeared along with the body. Prior to the body? No concepts! That reality is supposed to be absorbed, engraved within you. That is enough.

Discard all body-based concepts. There are many different religions. All of them cause great confusion. It is said in the Bhagavad Gita “Discard all religions”. All religions are fictitious, imaginary religions. Religion is not bad. Religion exists to offer society a peaceful atmosphere because human beings are always in conflict, fighting and struggling with each other. Therefore, we have religion to establish some peace and happiness.

However, behind every religion, there is dispeace, conflict and struggle. Those who belong to one religion, declare it to be better than the rest, or the best: “My religion is greater than yours! This sentiment should not be there. All religions have their imperfections. Though they are good in principle, the way they have been implemented is very, very bad indeed. There is a great deal of hypocrisy. Behind the curtain of religion, there are power struggles, with everyone fighting each other. This should not happen!

Here, we are trying our best to incorporate and deliver all the necessary spiritual knowledge to seekers, through these discourses, as well as through the books such as Selfless Self, I Am That, Master of Self-Realization, and this book, Ultimate Truth. They are very good books, because they are pragmatic. They are trying to convince the “Invisible Listener Within You That Except Your Selfless Self There is Nothing”. “You Are Ultimate Truth.”

After Conviction, you can live anywhere in the world, you can be doing anything, eating anything without any problem because you will know, that you are not the body, and you are not the doer. Life becomes very, very simple indeed.

You have all the knowledge. Just convince yourself that nothing is true. Convince yourself, and you will have practical knowledge. Just now, you have theoretical knowledge – make it practical! Apply the knowledge! You have all the information! I have told you everything. Nothing has been kept from you. I am not hiding anything from you. Nisargadatta Maharaj, Ranjit Maharaj, Siddharameshwar Maharaj, and myself, have not kept one grain of knowledge from you.
Everything is open. I have placed all the cards on the table. Now you know everything. It is now over to you to implement, enjoy and absorb this knowledge. Go ahead! Do not delay!

The visible world will vanish along with you. Firstly, you are a small baby, then, you become a young man, then, an old man, and then, some day or other, willingly or unwillingly, you will leave the body. It is not your identity. When you know Reality, there should not be any fear around at all. If spirituality has done its job, you will be completely free from fear. You will be totally fearless, when your time comes to leave the body: “I am not dying. Death and birth do not apply to me because death and birth are body-related, and not connected at all to my Presence”. The Spirit/Presence within the body does not die. This will be your Conviction.

If an earthquake occurs, some buildings will start collapsing. And the sky? What happens to the sky? Nothing! The sky cannot collapse. An earthquake has no effect on the sky. Similarly, if anything goes seriously wrong with the body, it may die. But, You cannot die because there is no death for the Spirit. “You Are Immortal.” Through meditation, you will discard all the concepts: the concepts of God, Brahman, Atman, etc, everything. Conviction will arise spontaneously. This is plain talk, direct talk, with no in-betweens.

p.strong: 54. What do you mean by knowledge?

section.p: You have theoretical knowledge. You are playing with all these spiritual words. You are to be serious about spirituality, not casual. It is not entertainment. You are trying to know yourself, your perfection. Be perfect! There are so many spiritual words in use, but in Reality, there is nothing there. You are talking about the child that was not born – the “Unborn Child”.

Everything came out of nothing, and everything dissolves within nothing. Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master, Maya, these are only words. “There is Nothing to Know, That You Are to Know.” You must know that there is nothing to know! There is nothing that needs to be known! Whatever knowledge you may have, came after the Spirit clicked with this body. And, as you know full well, your body-based knowledge is illusion.

If you have the inclination, you may write books, but only after Spontaneous Conviction. Writing books on spirituality, as a theoretical exercise, or for spiritual pleasure, is meaningless. It is nothing but mental chatter, egoistic chatter, intellectual chatter for those who only wish a spiritual massage.

You are to impress Reality within you because you are the cause of this world. If there were no Presence within the body, how can anyone write books? “Your Presence is Everywhere.” I am not discouraging you from writing books, but your spiritual life should be a practical life. First, have Spontaneous Conviction: Perfection, Perfect Identification, then, you may write books.

Writing books is very easy to do, when you have read hundreds of books. You can write any book you wish, but don’t write books filled with concepts. The spiritual market is already flooded with these. They do not help, but, instead, hinder the seeker, because they add confusion, and further illusory layers of concepts. The only books worth writing, are those that have the power to demolish all your beloved concepts!

Grow in spiritual maturity! “The Advanced Stage is a Thoughtless Stage.” There will be no thoughts, because everything that is illusion has dissolved. “Where Everything Ends There You Are.” Practical knowledge, therefore, means that “Your State is Stateless and Thoughtless.” Practical knowledge means that there is stability. You are solid as a rock, unwavering, so that, whatever comes your way, will not unnerve you. Nothing can touch you. Practical knowledge means that you live like the sky. You know that you are not the body, the mind, the ego, or the intellect. You don’t argue with others because you know, that there are no others.

This is the last destiny, the Terminal. There should not be any instability. The mind is a crazy mind! Don’t let it divert you from Reality. Be a Master of the mind, and take control of it. Be firm! Don’t let it continue with its mischievous ways. Be stable! After knowing the Reality, be stable, knowing that you have reached the destination. Be secure in the knowledge that you finally know the secret. “You Know That There is Nothing to Know!” “Nothing is There! Nothing Has Happened, and Nothing is Going to Happen.” There is no deed, and there is no doer. “Your Presence is Just Like the Sky, or Space.”

The sky does not know that it is sky. The sky does not have any spirituality. Bring all your spiritual travelling to an end. Travelling is not needed. Who is travelling? Where was the ego, intellect and mind, prior to beingness? And, where will they be when you leave the body? This trio came with the body, and will leave with the body. Forget about all these illusory layers on your Presence. Stay with Reality! To say “I”, is illusion. To say “you”, is illusion. To say “Brahman”, is also illusion, because this is an illusory world. And, in an illusory world everything is illusion, including yourself and myself.

p.strong: 55. What is spiritual knowledge?

section.p: Spiritual knowledge offers information. It is information about the searcher, the identification of the searcher, Listener or Reader. It is nothing more than that. Was there any spiritual knowledge prior to beingness? And, will there be any spiritual knowledge after you leave the body? No! Because spiritual knowledge is illusion.

Will that so-called, spiritual knowledge help you at the time of leaving the body? No! It is a fact. Spiritual knowledge just means identifying yourself. These are just the words. You have a wavering mind which causes confusion. Meditation is the cure because it cleans everything, clears out everything. I call it the Antivirus Software. Meditation is the antivirus software which will dissolve all your viruses, bugs and worms that have infiltrated your “big computer”!

After you insert this Anti-Virus Software, your entire make-up will be changed completely. It will be thoroughly cleaned, and completely washed out. All your desires and concepts will be rinsed out, emptied out. And then, Spontaneous Happiness will arise. Peacefulness will be there, without any material cause. Meditation is needed to identify yourself in a real sense. Correct? Start today!

To live comfortably with the body-form, you must exercise forgiveness and forgetfulness. Suppose a dog starts barking, do you give it any attention? Or, if a drunk person starts abusing you, do you give him any attention? No! Don’t give attention to the continuous flow of thoughts. Register the useful thoughts, and throw out the rest! Be ruthless! Don’t entertain every bark that comes to your attention. You are in charge because you are a Master, and so, it is your call. Start making the decisions and stop following the mind.

p.strong: 56. Who wants spiritual knowledge?

section.p: You are Knowledge! The Listener is Knowledge, the Reader is Knowledge. Don’t count yourself as the body-form. Who wants the knowledge of Brahman? Is Brahman there? Is Atman there? Is Paramatman there? Nobody knows about all of that. These are only specific names that have been assigned as pointers to your Ultimate Truth. They are indicators. Prior to beingness, did you have any knowledge that you were Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, or Master? No! You did not know anything.

Beingness started growing along with the consciousness. The stages are: Beingness, Consciousness, and then Awareness. Beingness is totally pure. There is nothing there. Then Beingness is converted in the consciousness, resulting in, “I am somebody”, and that is consciousness. And then, instantly, it takes form, manifesting as “I am somebody. I am the human body”. After this human body-consciousness, awareness follows. There is the conviction, “I am somebody”. This is just the theory, so, don’t give it too much importance.

However, one thing is for sure: the human body is not a permanent body. It has its own limits, and therefore, cannot be your identity because you are unlimited! There is no kind of body-form whatsoever, that is anyone’s identity because you are basically formless. What you see in this world is the Seer’s reflection. The entire world is projected out of your Invisible Presence. If there is no Presence in the body, who will talk about the world? Who will talk about spiritual knowledge?

Spiritual knowledge has one purpose only, and that is to help you identify yourself. Through meditation, through reciting the Mantra, you are hammering yourself, because you have forgotten your Identity. You are hammering yourself with, “I am Brahman, Brahman I am”. And this continuous hammering, will open the door to Reality within you. And then, you will forget all about your bodily identity. That is the purpose behind meditation. All these concepts of depression, nervousness, loneliness, sadness, anxiety, etc, all these disturbing clouds, that have been intermittently obscuring the light from the sun, will automatically disappear for good. You are that sun that is always shining!

And not only will you be free from all these disturbances, you will have exceptional Spontaneous Conviction, Spontaneous Spiritual Intoxication. And you will remain in that field of Reality. Though some unpleasant atmospheres may be around, you will still be spiritually intoxicated. You will be oblivious to everything, just like a drunkard who is immersed in himself. If anything happens around him, he is unaffected, because he is self-engrossed in his own world.

Similarly, when you have this nectar of spiritual knowledge, you will be self-absorbed in Reality. If something undesirable happens, you will not give it your attention. This is a sign of Realization.

Listening to direct, spiritual knowledge is effective because you have collected a lot of information from various material sources. Everything has to be removed because it is not part of you. It does not belong to your Essence. These “add-ons” have corrupted your “big computer”. There is a need to de-program all your data. It is like when a computer has a lot of wrong files, corrupt files, you have to remove them with an anti-virus software. Meditation is the anti-virus software, through which you will remove all the illusory concepts.

Any more questions? There won’t be any questions because all questions are rooted in, and through, the human body. The human body contains the mind, the ego, and the intellect, the subtle, functioning organs. They come with the body, and they will leave with the body.
Now you know that the human body is not your identity at all, because you are basically formless. There is no form there! Accept and absorb these teachings!

Prior to beingness, you did not know yourself. After leaving the body, you will not know yourself. Prior to beingness, there was no knowledge. You did not know anything. When Presence came along with the human body, you were able to see the consciousness, “I am somebody. I am a human body”. This has to dissolve. It is very simple!

p.strong: 57. Why are there still some questions?

section.p: All questions are related to the body-form. Basically, they are rooted through the mind. And as you know, the mind, ego and intellect are themselves illusion. They are functioning organs. Your entire body functions through the mind, ego and intellect. Who is supplying power to them? You are! You are supplying the electricity, you are supplying the power. The mind, ego and intellect exist in this form only because of your Presence.
How were you prior to beingness? Unknown. After leaving the body? Unknown. This means that the unknown dissolves in the unknown. What you feel as the “known” is due to this body alone.

Basically, you do not know yourself. After leaving the body, you will not know yourself. Therefore, the unknown came into existence in the body-form, and after leaving the body, the unknown will dissolve back into the unknown. In between, you are posing yourself as: “I am somebody”. You are not somebody, you are everybody. You are everywhere, just like the space or the sky. So be with you, it is not impossible. Don’t waste your time, travelling here and there.

p.strong: 58. Why do these concepts appear?

section.p: The body is not your identity at all. Unless you accept the Reality that “the body is not your identity”, the experience of a body-form will continue to appear upon your Presence. These concepts create so much confusion, and then, the mind raises all sorts of questions. So many questions keep arising, and they are all body-based questions! I receive so many questions from seekers, and they are always body-based concepts. Melt, dissolve, demolish all your body-based concepts. This is the primary task at hand.

Your problems are all body-related, arising from this human life that is only a dream: depression, worry, sadness, loneliness, feeling unloved, fearfulness, stress, dispeace, unhappiness, misery, etc, etc. We have become fearful of everything. We behave like sheep, instead of lions! We have a lot of tension. There is no peacefulness, or blissfulness. And, we are always fearful of death. All these illusory concepts are to be demolished. Demolish all your concepts! This is the main goal of spirituality.

p.strong: 59. How can I discard all these concepts?

section.p: Spiritual Science teaches that you are just like the sky or space. The sky is everywhere. It is not limited to India. It is everywhere. The sky does not have any special identity. It does not have any individuality. It is vast. Your Presence is vast, formless, expansive. Your Presence is in every being. The bodies may all appear different, but Presence is One. How do you identify yourself? Meditation and reciting the Naam Mantra will hammer all your concepts, and then you will be able to identify yourself.

When the Spirit clicked with the body, the entire world was projected. It is just like when you are dreaming. Who created this dream world? You see the entire world. You see the sun, the moon, the gods, the goddesses, friends, your father and mother, all kinds of things, everything, in fact. Not even that, you also see yourself playing different roles.

At the time, you feel that the dream is true. You are enjoying it, or not enjoying it. It may be a good experience, or a bad one. Who is experiencing that dream? Who is watching that dream? “That is your Identity.” Because you are sleeping, there is a subtle, subtle Identity, which is called Brahman, Atman, Ultimate Truth. I am talking about that subtle, Unidentified Identity which is called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master, That You Are!

Though you are holding the body, when all your concepts have been discarded, you will no longer be concerned with the body. Absorb the Reality, your Reality! You have literal knowledge and literal conviction. Your Conviction is to be Spontaneous Conviction. Once you have that Spontaneous Conviction, though the thoughts – both good and bad – continue to flow, you will not give them any importance. They will not be an issue for you. You will treat everything, as you treat your dreams on waking up: “Oh! I had a good dream!” or “I had a bad dream!”, (knowing that there is no good or bad: “good” and “bad” are mere concepts!)

King Janaka, once had a very, vivid dream in which he was begging. He proceeded to question his stalwart, spiritual counsellors, “Is that dream true, or is this life true?”, “Was that a dream, or is this a dream?”. This story appears in the Ashtavakra Gita. Ashtavakra was a great spiritualist in those days. He was only a fourteen or fifteen year old boy then, but he had exceptional Realization.

Janaka asked him: “I had a dream where I was a beggar. And now, I am a king. The only question I have is whether this, (the world), is true, or that, (the dream), is true?” Ashtavakra replied, immediately: “This is false, and that, is also false”.

Everything you see in a dream is illusion. And everything you see with this body-form, is also illusion. But, the Presence in a dream, and the Presence in this body-form, are one and the same. Without Presence, you cannot watch a dream, and without Presence, you cannot watch this body-based knowledge. Therefore, your Presence is here, and it is also there. [there is no “here” or “there”] You are counting yourself as the body-form. That concept must dissolve.

p.strong: 60. Why do I need meditation, a Master, and all this spiritual knowledge?

section.p: Why? To know your Selfless Self! We know ourselves in the body-form, and that is illusion. You are Formless. All that you see is illusion. Concentrate on the Invisible Concentrator, on the Invisible Seer, within you. If there were no Seer, how would you be able to see the world?

To say, “this world”, to say, “God”, to say, “Brahman”, to say anything, your Presence is needed. Presence is a prerequisite. That means that, “The Entire World is Projected Out of Your Invisible Presence”. You do not know yourself. To know who you are, you need to meditate. Meditation cannot be avoided. It will give you a solid foundation. Strong willpower is needed, too. Be determined and strong-willed, so that you will be able to discard all of your dearly-held concepts.

You will not be able to identify yourself, unless you dissolve all the concepts. There is always the temptation to visit different Masters. You won’t find anything. There is nothing there. Visit your own Master!

Reading endless books is meaningless. What is the purpose behind the books? What do all these spiritual books indicate? The gist, the summary of all these spiritual books is: “Except For Your Selfless Self, There is No God, No Brahman, No Atman, No Paramatman, No Master”. This is a statement of fact, but, you are reluctant to accept it. You have some doubts: “Why? How? Really? How Come? Are you sure?”, etc. This happens because the mind, ego and intellect keep stinging us, or pricking us all the time, trying to disturb and distract us from Reality. That is why the practice of meditation is essential, so that you can know, and accept Reality, and dissolve illusion. If you do not follow the Master’s recommendations and instructions, you will continue to be distracted by illusion.
This knowledge is not the knowledge of Brahman, Atman, Paramatman. It is the knowledge of the Invisible Listener within you, that is Formless, and, not subject to birth and death.

We are not accepting Reality, therefore, we feel all this sadness and depression. You are trying to find the Reality, roaming here and there, from South India, to North India, looking for this Master, and that Master. And all the time, you are neglecting your own Master within you. You are like a spiritual tourist. Why do you still have the urge to go elsewhere?

The search is supposed to come to an end. When are you going to end your search? There must be a full stop! Why? It is simple: “Because The Searcher Itself is Ultimate Truth”. But you are ignoring “That”. You are ignoring “That” within you, and trying to search for a Master that is out there.

There is no Master! Who is the Master? You are the Master. Your Invisible Listener Is The Master. You have been neglecting yourself, underestimating yourself. Dive deep within yourself. There you will find the treasure. Know that everything is within you. Be calm and quiet. Your search is over!

Why am I sharing this spiritual knowledge? To identify your Selfless Self. This knowledge “Invites the Attention of The Invisible Listener Within You, That There is No Difference Between The Invisible Listener Within You, And The Invisible Speaker Within This Body”. Ramakant Maharaj, and everyone else, are one and the same. There is no difference, except for the appearance of the body-forms.

When, say, someone called Peter, leaves the body. We don’t say, “Oh, this is Peter’s body, Peter’s Spirit. Or, if Ramakant Maharaj left the body, we don’t say, “This is Ramakant Maharaj’s Spirit”. It is not like that! It is all one and the same. We are all one and the same. It is only for teaching purposes that I take on the role of Master, and you take on the role of disciple. But, there is no difference at all, except for the bodies. That Identification, that Conviction is supposed to arise spontaneously.

p.strong: 61. Why spiritual books and Masters?

section.p: We need to use different sources to identify ourselves. You have not found any peacefulness or happiness because you have forgotten your Identity. Since we are holding the body, there is instability, tension, fear of death, etc. We need spirituality to come out of the circle of illusory concepts.

Spirituality is a concept. “I am Brahman”, is also a concept. But, it is a concept based on sattva guna. It is a good and helpful concept, through which you can identify yourself. After Identification, after Conviction, you will not need any kind of practice, not even meditation, bhajans, or the Master’s help. You need the Master until you are able to stand on your own feet. You are under the mistaken impression that you are not whole. You think you are lacking, or handicapped in some way. You don’t consider yourself to be strong and independent, and therefore, you feel dependent upon the blessings of the Master, or God’s blessings.

The Master is not separate from you, but you lack this Conviction. For you to have strong faith that you are a Master, a process is needed. Meditation is required as a form of hammering. Breathing and concentrating on the tip of the nose, all these aids are there. If, however, you have full confidence that you are Brahman, then nothing is required. But this very rarely happens, as the mind does not easily accept Reality. And that is why a process is necessary.

Reading books, listening to the Master’s words, and meditation, are all needed to regain your original memory: “I am Ultimate Truth”. This Reality needs to be absorbed in you. The gist of all this is: Except for Selfless Self, there is nothing else. The Master and the disciple are the same. God and the devotee are one and the same. It is just the bodies that differ. This body is nothing but a dream body. The entire world is projected out of your Presence. Listen to me! Once you accept this principle, you will have no need of anything else. Even meditation and bhajans will no longer be necessary, as, after all, everything came out of nothing.

There is confusion around because of the mind, the ego and the intellect. You cannot find peace or happiness. You feel insecure and fearful. To come out of this bad patch, you have to undergo meditation. It is unavoidable. Meditation will help you. It will enable the attainment of direct self-knowledge. Read books that contain direct information, direct, spontaneous knowledge. Don’t read theoretical books! They will only confuse you further. Nisargadatta Maharaj, Siddharameshwar Maharaj, Ranjit Maharaj, all these saints have placed before seekers, the Ultimate Truth. They were not simply talking about Ultimate Truth, they were living the Ultimate Truth.

Ultimate Truth is not separate from you. God is not separate from you. The Master is not separate from you. This whole process, and all this theory, is there solely to convince you that you are Brahman, Atman, Paramatman. You are God. You are the Master. This is short and sweet, spiritual knowledge.

p.strong: 62. Why is a Master Needed?

section.p: The Master shows you Ultimate Truth, just like in the famous spiritual story, of the lion who grew up with the sheep. A lion cub is brought up amongst a flock of sheep, and so, inevitably, it also starts to consider itself to be a sheep. When a lion in the forest sees the small cub in the company of sheep, he asks: “What are you doing with the sheep? You are a lion, the king of the forest, the king of all animals!” The cub replies, “No, no, how can I be a lion king? I am a sheep!” So, the lion shows him the different parts of his body. When the cub is finally convinced, with self-recognition and realization, he says, “Aha! So, I am a lion!” In a similar way, the Master shows you that you are Ultimate Truth. You are Brahman.

Be serious! Recite the Mantra, and sit for meditation. After Conviction, there will not be any further need of this. That Identification, that Conviction is only possible with meditation. Dry knowledge and dry talk regarding spirituality, and dry listening, are all totally meaningless. You must start with you, because you are the starting point. You are the central point. “Everything Starts From You, and Everything Ends Within You.”

It is just like this body has been called a “man”, so you are acting as a man. In a similar way, the Holder of the body is called Brahman. So, you are acting as Brahman, and are unconcerned about everything else. There is no doer, and there is no deed. There is no experiencer and no experiences. There is no witness either.

Does the sky experience anything: “I am the sky”? No! You are the projector of the sky, the projector of the world. That Identification, that Conviction will arise, if you undergo the meditation. Dry knowledge, dry spiritual talk, and dry listening are all pointless. You have to start with yourself, start from you!

The Master is needed, so that we can not only know Reality, but live Reality, live like Brahman! We are always under pressure from the body, the mind, the ego, and the intellect. Thoughts are racing all the time, trying to leave their impressions behind. Follow Your Inner Master! Listen to your Inner Master. Through the meditation, you are giving encouragement, strength and power to your Inner Master, to open the door to Truth. Thereafter, your external identity will dissolve. There will not be any remnants left of your former, illusory identity. You merge with Selfless Self. “You Become One With Your Selfless Self.”

p.strong: 63. What exactly is this body-form?

section.p: Forget about spirituality for the time-being. Apply simple logic, and you will see that the body-form is a temporary carrier. Sit calmly and quietly. What is in the body? Nothing but glimpses of “I”, just glimpses of “I”. The body is not eternal, that’s for sure. It is limited by time, age, etc. Don’t give anything too much attention. Whatever appears upon your Presence, whether it is agreeable or disagreeable. The body belongs to the five elements. When there is an imbalance of the five elements, you will feel it. Sometimes you feel depressed, or there’s an unpleasant atmosphere. This happens because you are holding the body. If you are suffering from a cold, there will be some weakness. Understand that whatever bodily feelings you may have are fleeting. They will come, they will go, changing all the time, just like the seasons.

Stay loyal to your Master. If you still have the urge to visit other Masters, Self-enquire, and find out what it is that you are still looking for. This knowledge that I have shared with you is the Final Truth. It is direct knowledge. There is nothing beyond. If you visit other Masters, you will soon be back in the ditch of illusory, conceptual knowledge. The clouds come, and the clouds go, but you are not going anywhere. You are there, and there only.

You are to convince yourself. How? By hammering yourself all the time, and reciting the Naam Mantra non-stop. You should not be attracted to visit another Master, for some other kind of knowledge, a different kind of knowledge. This will only create confusion, and, as a result, it will damage your faith. Don’t be tempted! This is a red alert. Don’t be attached to anyone’s thoughts, as it will create problems for you, and undo all your efforts. What are you going for? You already know Reality. You know that you are Ultimate Truth, so, what more do you want?

It is the restlessness in the body-form, that is prompting you to wander again. If you meet another Master, he will command you to “do this, and do that”, and soon, you will accept the grand illusion once more, that you are a doer. This Master will talk to you about your past life, and your future life. There is no life at all! He will reintroduce you to the concepts of sin and guilt, karma etc, and before you know it, you will be caught up in the vicious circle of illusion, forsaken, and, once more stuck in the illusory ditch!

Here, we are guiding you to remove all your illusory concepts. Elsewhere, you will be asked to do various things, like carry out acts of self-denial, or even, to hand over all your material possessions to the Master, in order to prove your faith in him!

After listening to this knowledge, you are to make yourself spiritually very strong. Try not to hurt anyone’s feelings. Comparing yourself to others, will only create struggle. Don’t hurt anyone’s heart. Remove the ego that feels, “Oh, I have so much knowledge. I am a spiritual Master”. It is not like that.

Don’t count yourself as an individual. Everybody is a Master. Accept the Reality of your Formless Self. If you don’t, there will be confusion again, and illusion. Be strong! This human body is such an opportunity for you to know yourself. Don’t waste any more time. Attend to your work and your duties, and take care of your health. If you take this knowledge lightly, and casually, then you will become a victim of your own desires. Make this your Last Terminal. No birth! No death! There is absolutely nothing. So, be strong, be spiritually strong. Okay? It is simple! Accept the Reality of your Formless Self.

p.strong: 64. Why is Conviction necessary?

section.p: We are under the pressure of various anxieties, fears and tension. Happiness and peace always seem to be out of our range, because we are identifying ourselves as the body-form. When you identify yourself in a true sense, all your tensions and fears will simply evaporate. There will be peace and happiness in abundance. Find out who it is that wants happiness? Who is it that wants peace? Who wants a tension-free life? Who wants a fearless life? The Spirit does not want it.

We are always worried about our bodies, because our consciousness is mixed in with the body, and this is what makes our existence in the body-form intolerable. We know that this body is a temporary state, but, even so, we are still very much attached to it. We fret about the slightest ailment, to the point of neurosis. And, no matter who we are, or how spiritual we are, deep down, we all wish for a long life. By all means, look after the body, but, at the same time, you must identify yourself. Identify “Who am I? If I am not the body, then who am I?” Identify your real nature, your true essence.

The Questioner That asks “Who am I?” is Itself Ultimate Truth, Final Truth. You can visit as many holy sites and temples as you wish, but if you do not visit the Visitor, then all your pilgrimages, will have been in vain.

During your search, you may have discovered various deities, but you are still searching for more. You are still searching for something, that you feel is missing deep down. You are looking for something to fill that hole, that gap that makes you feel incomplete! How long are you going to keep searching for? And how many more gods are you still hoping to discover? Stop the search, and realize that you, the Searcher Itself are God. You have simply forgotten! In order to regain your original memory that you yourself are God, that you are that Masterly essence, that you are, in fact, the missing link, you must undergo strict meditation. Look at you! You are God! You are Almighty! There is nothing out there. Everything is within you.

Don’t waste your time. If you don’t identify yourself during this human life, there will be another dream, and you will continue to remain in some type of body-form. Be very serious about this knowledge. Think about it very seriously and carefully.

Read books like I Am That as well as Siddharameshwar’s Master of Self-Realization. And, also read Selfless Self, thoroughly. Pay attention to what these books are trying to convey. Identify their message behind the words. You are not here to analyze the words, but to accept the truth that is placed before you. These Masters give one message only, and that is: “Except for your Selfless Self, there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman”. This knowledge is priceless, and free. There is no charge because it is your innate knowledge.

The Master tells you: “You are a Master. You are God!” But, you don’t believe the Master. You doubt what he is saying, and question this preposterous notion, saying, “how can I be God”? Yet, you never question: “How can I be a man?” All the body-based-knowledge impressions over a lifetime, are preventing you from accepting the Reality that the Master is placing before you. We know Reality, but not Practical Reality. We only have the theory. The Master tells you that you are Brahman, God, beyond God, but you are not accepting the Truth. Instead, you want to go exploring again, and see new vistas, new experiences, fresh entertainment. Why visit so many places? Visit your own place within. Visit your inner temple, your inner sanctuary.

This is your unique opportunity to find yourself. You were never lost, only forgotten. The Finder is Ultimate Truth. Identify yourself! Any questions? Be free! If you have any questions regarding spirituality, just ask. And read all these books very carefully, very thoroughly. When you read them, again and again, each time, you will receive a different message because it is the Reader’s information that is unfolding.

What is arising, is information that is coming directly from the Invisible Reader within you. Nisargadatta Maharaj, Ranjit Maharaj, Siddharameshwar Maharaj – they were all such saintly people. Read their teachings. The knowledge is priceless. And there is no need to spend one single penny for it either. Money contaminates the knowledge. Our lineage has firm rules, that categorically forbids, charging any money for spreading the teachings. All these saintly people taught for free. Sadly now, in our day, whatever is offered for free is considered valueless. If a teacher said, “give me one lakh of rupees”, he will be esteemed a great saint!

Welcome this glorious opportunity to find yourself. The Finder itself is Ultimate Truth, Final Truth. Therefore, “Be With You All the Time”. Work, and take care of your loved ones, but also, identify yourself. This is your time, the right time. You don’t want to endure another dream which has been called “rebirth”, [“rebirth” is merely a concept] There is no rebirth, no past, or future birth. There is no past karma, future karma, or any kind of karma. How can there be karma, when there is no doer and no deed?

Countless concepts were impressed upon us throughout our lifetime. You have become used to living with them. Now, you are being asked to throw them all out. Why? Because all your concepts are borrowed. Why become a victim of someone else’s concepts? For example, you accept your birth as fact, and yet, no-one has any memory, of his or her birth. So, forget about birth, past birth, future birth! This knowledge has all come out of the realms of the imagination.

Do you have any memories prior to your childhood? No! None at all! Therefore, stop accepting everything that you hear! Apply some intellect, and use it to discriminate. Don’t become a victim of your mind. You are beyond that! Prepare now! Identify yourself now, before your life-span in the body-form terminates.

Just listen! Spontaneous Conviction is not only necessary, but essential, if you are to dis-identify with the body-form identity. The emergence of Ultimate Reality, will only happen when all the body-based knowledge, with which you have constructed your identity dissolves. Be courageous, and accept the Reality. Why do you need courage? Because you are letting go of everything that you know, and everything that you are attached to. And, even though it is all illusion, it provides you with an illusory sense of safety and security.

Don’t underestimate yourself. That Masterly Essence is in you. It is not separate from you. You were not the body. You are not the body. You are not going to remain the body. This is the message behind spirituality.

p.strong: 65. How do I identify myself?

section.p: You know that the body is not your identity at all. Think about this for a moment! Who is speaking from this body? Who is asking questions? Is the body asking questions? Is the mind asking questions? There is some essence within the body, an Invisible Presence that is the Source of all Power. Who is watching your dreams, projecting your dreams, and that whole dream world? Have you given this any consideration? Who or what is projecting this dream-world? Self-enquire! Who is the projector of this world? Identify yourself. It is the Invisible Listener, the Invisible Experiencer – That you are!

You are formless. This five elemental body is like our clothes, that will only last for a certain length of time. The sooner you wake up to this fact, the sooner you will be at peace, as you will no longer have to worry about “dying”. There is no death for you. The body will expire one day, but you will never expire because you are immortal.

You were once a small child, who grew up into a young man. And now, you have become an old man. Soon, this bag of bones will crumble, and the time will come for you to leave the body. Why not prepare for a peaceful death, so that you can leave the body willingly, without any fear. Because when you know what is actually taking place, that you are simply discarding a layer, and not dying, this transition can be a beautiful event.

Identify yourself in a real sense, by knowing that you are not the body, and the rest of your life will be filled with happiness and peace. It is up to you. Accept the facts, the Reality, your Reality, or, perpetuate the illusion, suffering and misery!

p.strong: 66. What is the best way to go beyond, or prior to, all this?

section.p: It is not a question of going beyond, or prior. You are not going anywhere. Reality has no “coming”, and no “going”. Does the sky in India want to go to Germany? Or, does the sky in Germany want to go to America? The sky is everywhere. Likewise, your Invisible Presence is everywhere. You are everywhere. There may be many, many different body-forms, but these body-forms are impermanent. Your Presence, however, is permanent. When the body expires, it dissolves into that Presence, and the vastness of that Presence.

Just now, we can see a variety of bodies around us. They come in all sorts of shapes and sizes. There are so many different body-forms, but after a hundred years, how are these body-forms going to appear? They are not! They will not be around, guaranteed.

It is your Presence that keeps the body alive. When the body expires, Presence becomes omnipresence, dissolving in its vastness. Just like if you pour a bucket of water into the sea, it will merge and become the sea. There is no duality. You experience illusion because you are considering yourself to be the body-form. Within that illusion, within that duality, lies Reality, an exceptional Presence that cannot be defined. There is neither an experiencer, nor any experience.

Presence does not know that, “I am Presence”. This stage, or Ultimate Status is to be absorbed. The only way this Stateless State can unfold, is with, and through, meditation. It is the only way. It is the base, the foundation. Without meditation, you cannot know yourself directly. At best, you can only know yourself through words.

p.strong: 67. How do we absorb this Truth, totally and completely?

section.p: This will happen spontaneously and automatically. Faithfully follow the meditation, for a period of time, without thinking about the consequences. The process is not about your transformation into Reality because you are already Reality. But, because the ego is in the way, and the mind keeps interfering, all this stops you from absorbing the teachings, and knowing your Ultimate Truth.

We have had so many discussions together, and throughout all of these, everything has been recorded within you. Just contemplate, over and over, all that the Master has conveyed to you. Remember what was said, and you will see the dramatic changes occurring. Through the process of meditation, illusion will dissolve, and Reality will come to the fore.

You will regain your “original memory”. This memory has nothing to do with the body. Your Presence is Ultimate Truth, Final Truth. Reading books, and entertaining yourself with spiritual words, is futile. Ask yourself, what purpose they will serve, after you leave the body?

What we see is the Seer’s reflection. Don’t take these words literally, but here, the Seer is most important. The Seer is the Invisible Seer, the Invisible Listener within you. It is that which you consider to be the body-form, the human-form. The human form is not the Reality you take yourself to be. But, in order to have this Conviction – that you are formless, and that your Presence is everywhere like the sky – spirituality is most important, as you must be convinced that you are not the body-form.

Meditation is central to bringing about this Spontaneous Conviction. It is the groundwork, the foundation, the hammering that is necessary in the beginning. Meditation has a temporary function, that of shattering illusion. Prior to beingness, we did not meditate. And, after leaving the body, there will not be any meditation going on. Meditation means hammering yourself with the knowledge of your true nature. It means reminding yourself of who you are, again and again, like brainwashing. Its repetitive nature clears out all the debris of your illusory life, and establishes Ultimate Reality. After listening to the Master, and continuously hammering yourself, Reality will be absorbed, and the Truth opened up, and flowing within you.

p.strong: 68. So, we need to put in effort, but not struggle?

section.p: Why struggle? We are always struggling with our thoughts. It is the nature of the body to manufacture thoughts 24/7. The thoughts are always flowing. You give them too much attention, and by doing this, you let them control you. This will change with meditation. Why be a victim of your thoughts? Just let them flow by you. When you ignore the thoughts, your confusion will lessen because you are no longer entangled in them. Keep ignoring the thoughts. This will lead to thoughtlessness.

In our lineage, we practise systematic devotion: singing bhajans, saying prayers to keep you alert, as well as meditation, and listening to knowledge. All these activities are designed, to prevent you from falling victim, to somebody else’s thoughts and concepts, or your own concepts. If you are dedicated, no one will be able to play tricks on you again.

You must be dedicated at all times, so that you remain alert. You don’t have to go to an ashram, or visit a temple. You can be anywhere, reciting the mantra, and sitting for meditation. “Wherever You Are There is Your Ashram.” Remember that the uplifting atmosphere that is created through singing the bhajans, serves to keep the mind in check. The mind cannot get up to any mischief, while you are singing.

The mind is always, always, trying to distract you. Everyone says, “Yes, I know Brahman. I know Brahman. I know Brahman.” It is like the story of the blind men and the elephant. Whatever part of the elephant they touched, they concluded it was such and such. But when the whole elephant is seen, there is no discussion, or argument. When you know the Reality, why struggle? What are you struggling with? There is nothing.

If you have any free time, then come and be with the Master. But stand on your own two feet, both spiritually, as well as, physically. The Master is always there. The Master is always with you. Even if the Master is not with you physically, he is still there. So why go looking for other living Masters? Many disciples say, “Oh, I have to find a living Master”. What do you mean by a “living Master”? You are a Living Master!”

p.strong: 69. How can I Realize?

section.p: Who says that you are not already realized? How can you not be realized? The Spirit within the body is already realized.

Spiritual knowledge will help you to identify yourself. And then you will, “Bow Down to That Through Which You Know Your Self. “Bow to Your Selfless Self.” “Bow Down Only to Selfless Self.” Bow down with your heart, and not just mechanically. Accept the Reality! Don’t just take what I am trying to say literally!

Convince yourself that “You Are The Architect of Your Own Life”, your spiritual life. “Everything Starts From You And Everything Ends Within You.” So, be humble, be simple, be normal. “And, Tomorrow You Will Be a Master.” There are no restrictions. My Master says, “If You Are Realized, Make Others Realized”. “I Am Not Making You Disciples, I Am Making You Masters.” It is not difficult because you are already a Master. Live Like A Master! The Master Is Your Ultimate Truth. You Are Ultimate Truth. Why are you spoiling yourself, wasting yourself, ruining yourself?

p.strong: 70. Is it possible to identify what I am positively? It seems we can only know what we are not…?

section.p: There is no positive, and no negative. Even to try and find out, “Who am I?” is an illusion. In order to know “I am”, you have to take ego: “I am something”. There is nothing. There is no “I”, there is no “you”, no “he”, “she”, “it”. These are the concepts, the words we need to use for communication, just for understanding purpose. Therefore, I am always insisting that you don’t play with the literal, and spiritual words. That “I” is to dissolve totally. Okay, we can use these pronouns for daily conversations, while knowing that they are concepts. You say that Brahman symbolizes God. Which God? Do you know anything about God? Have you seen God? Have you seen Brahman? How can you see Brahman or God? You cannot – because You Are That. You cannot see yourself without using a mirror. Because of your book-reading, you have become victims of literal knowledge. Knowledge is not separate from you. Brahman is not separate from you. “You Are The Final Destination.” “Where Everything Ends, There You Are.” Prior to beingness you knew nothing at all. Absolutely nothing was known to you. Find out what you are not!

p.strong: 71. What do you mean by total involvement?

section.p: Total involvement means that you are always with Selfless Self. You are always reciting the Mantra. You are earnest, and on fire. You must know the answer to, “Who am I?” You have to know. This question should become so vital to you, that pursuing it, becomes almost a matter of life or death. It is so important to you, that you are always thinking about Reality, contemplating your true nature, and reciting the Mantra.
Your Unidentified Identity is nameless, therefore, using basic language, and simple meanings, we are trying to impose Reality upon the Invisible Listener.

Be convinced that without Presence, your body is nothing but a dead body. Can a dead body see anything? Can it hear anything? If there is no power, no energy, no spiritual strength, then there can be no activity, or functioning whatsoever. Who sees? Who listens? Who tastes the food? It is that supernatural power in you, that supreme Essence, that Invisible Presence called Brahman.

This human body has a very important purpose. It is the vehicle for Self-Realization. Through this body, you can identify yourself, know yourself, and realize that you are not the body. You were not the body. And, you are not going to remain the body.

Listen to these discourses, and after a few days, I will teach you how to meditate. If you are serious, I will give you the Mantra. But, until then, listen with complete attention and involvement. And read Selfless Self thoroughly. Then you can make up your mind, about whether you wish to take the Mantra or not. If you feel it is right for you, I will guide you. It is up to you. Taking the Mantra is not obligatory. And I always tell seekers, not to take the Mantra if they have any doubts. Take it only if you have full confidence and faith.

When you listen to these discourses, some impressions will be engraved within you. We are hammering Reality into you. In brief: “You are not the body at all. You were not the body. You are not going to remain the body”. The concepts of death and birth are only connected to the body, not to you. Your Presence is wholly separate from the body. The body is a food-body. You are not that. The body is made up of the five elements. You are beyond that.

Listen carefully! Absorb the knowledge! You are not the body at all. You are invisible. You are like the space or the wind, acting invisibly within the body. Just like the wind does not know that it is the wind, the Spirit does not know that it is Spirit.

I am happy to see that at this young age, you have embraced spirituality, and are taking it seriously. Seriousness, and deep self-involvement in spirituality are essential. It is also easier to adapt when one is still young, and not set in one’s ways, views or beliefs. With the onset of old age, it is not so easy to reject a lifetime of ingrained concepts. It is almost impossible, in fact.
Don’t use spirituality as an escape from life’s responsibilities. You need not quit your job. If you are studying, complete your education, don’t abandon it, saying:

“Oh, I am going to give everything up, renounce the world and live a pure, spiritual life. I will travel to the Himalayas and live in a cave”. This kind of talk is nonsense talk! If you do this, nothing is going to happen, except that you will move your body from A to B.

Thousands of people leave home on a spiritual whim, discarding everything, and going for spirituality full-pelt! This kind of decision and action is entirely irrational! You cannot run from the battlefield. Renouncing the world is pure escapism. It is running from the battlefield. Face the battlefield! “Yes! I can remain a part of society and, at the same time, still live a spiritual life!” The two are not mutually exclusive. “I can find out who I am, while still going about my work and business, and attending to my family”.

The same Spirit that is in all the saints, resides in you. But, alas, you do not know yourself. You are unaware of your true nature, your divine essence. It is a tragedy to go through life, year after year, never knowing who you are. It is a tragedy to go through life asleep, unaware that this is nothing but a long dream. So many tears! So much suffering! It is all illusion, and such a waste of the opportunity you have now, to free yourself from all illusory bondage.

Here we are sharing practical knowledge. How will this knowledge bring about change? You will carry on as before, except with one difference, that you will no longer take it all for real. When there is Spontaneous Conviction, you will know that you are playing a role because nothing is true. “The World is Projected Out of Your Spontaneous Presence.” You will stay with the Seer, and not get drawn into the seen.

When Reality is established, the implications are far-reaching, and intensely liberating: All your fears will be gone, because you will know that there is no death, no birth, no past or future births, no heaven or hell. These fears that accompanied you through life, will simply collapse like a pack of cards. You will no longer have any cause for anxiety or worry. When you recognize all the concepts, that have had a stranglehold over you, they will simply dissolve.

When you know who you are, you will tap into the Source of your Power. You are Almighty, Ultimate Reality. You are the Source of this world. You are the central point of the universe. When all your concepts have been erased, you will be free from all bondage. You have always been free, but you were not aware of this, because of mistakenly accepting your identity as the body-form.

Dissolve each and every concept! Be ruthless! Get rid of all your concepts. This is the principle of spirituality. Remember that Ultimate Reality will not emerge until all illusion has dissolved. You are formless. You are unborn, vast, expansive. Live like That! Don’t be casual about your spiritual practice. It is only with deep and full self-involvement, that you will progress, and inch closer and closer to Selfless Self.

Start reading Selfless Self and I Am That, and then you will understand the principle of spirituality, and the importance of meditation, and the Naam Mantra. Then knowledge will begin to flow. This is your innate knowledge. Everything will be revealed to you spontaneously. Your Inner Master will start communicating with you. All your questions will be answered because you have arrived at the Source. There will be happiness and peace, and unimaginable silence. These qualities are the fragrance of Selfless Self. Keep this Truth, near to your heart at all times: “Except for your Selfless Self, there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman”. You are Atman. You are Brahman. You are Paramatman. All the spiritual power is in you. All the supernatural power is in you. “Everything Starts From You, and Everything Ends Within You.”

If any miracles occur, don’t be impressed by them, and don’t be fooled into thinking that someone made them happen. There is no one who is greater than you. You must have this Conviction of your greatness within you. All miracles start from you, so do not be impressed by anyone performing miracles. No one is greater than you. And, at the same time, show respect to the Master who has shown you your greatness.

p.strong: 72. What does Selfless Self mean?

section.p: You are not the body. You are not the mind. You are not the Self. Self refers to identification with the body. You are Selfless Self. This means that there is nothing there. That which you are is beyond words. Selfless Self is beyond any words, and you are beyond Selfless Self. Everything that came along with the body is illusion, and body-based knowledge. When these illusory layers on your Presence have been removed, we call that which is left, “Selfless Self”.

Selfless Self is one’s Spontaneous, Invisible, Anonymous Presence which is beyond any identification whatsoever. This means that your identity is invisible, anonymous, unidentified identity. You are unborn, formless. It is a fact that a hundred years ago, you did not know anything about yourself. You did not know who you were. In a hundred years’ time, again, you will not know anything about anything. When the Spirit clicked with the body, and you became aware of your existence, you started to consider yourself as the body-form. This body-form existence, with its never-ending cycle of needs, made life intolerable. When you accepted the body-form as your identity, you experienced unhappiness, dispeace, tension and fear. But, as you know, the body-form is not your identity. You are formless, unidentified identity. There is nothing except Selfless Self. Except for Selfless Self, there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman, no Master.

p.strong: 73. You often mention “glimpses of I”?

section.p: It is just that feeling of “I”, just “I”. That feeling has no specific identity or form. In the early morning, when you first awaken from deep sleep, at that particular moment, even though you are holding the body, there is just a feeling of “I” there. It has been called glimpses of “I”. In this fleeting instant, there is no identity of maleness, femaleness. There is nothing, just “I”. This glimpse is an exceptionally, peaceful feeling. There is no feeling or sense of the world – nothing at all. That exceptional state was there while you were in deep sleep, and that feeling of blissful peace lingers, just for a brief moment, on awakening.

You can compare it to samadhi. In the samadhi state, there is no awareness. There is nothing but glimpses of “I”. Don’t take the words literally. We have no choice but to use language for understanding purposes. At that particular stage, there is no gender, no form, absolutely nothing. After this glimpse has been felt momentarily, then the body-form kicks in, and declares: “I am”, followed by a stream of illusory memories.

But prior to that, there is only that pure, untainted feeling of, just “I”. This peace is momentary. It is there before the world is projected, and before the illusory feelings arise. At the Ultimate Stage, there Is no Experiencer and no Experience. It is because we are holding the body, that we are feeling “I”, “you”, “he”, “she”, “it”. You know so many things, but as a matter of fact, your Presence or Unidentified Identity is unknown. Knowledge came afterwards. If there were no body, how can you identify knowledge?

Even if you try to imagine the state prior to beingness, using the intellect, you cannot. You were totally formless. It was only when Presence touched with the body, that you started saying, “I”. And that “I” took the form of either a man, or a woman, or of any animal, or being. That beingness is coloured with some form. It is not permanent. As long as you have a body, you will feel the beingness.

p.strong: 74. You never talk about death?

section.p: There is no death. You are unborn. Death for whom? You are formless. Nobody has ever experienced death. We say, “he, or she, died, and has now taken birth”. But that deceased person does not know whether he died, or whether he has taken birth. This is all imagination and hearsay. Prior to body-based knowledge, nothing was known. Identify that nothingness. Don’t take the words literally. Throw all these concepts, along with their associated anxieties, in the garbage can.

“Everything Came Out of Nothing, and Everything Dissolves Within Nothing.” Identify That Nothing.
