Using different words, “I Am Trying to Invite the Attention of the Invisible Listener Within You, That Except Your Selfless Self There is Nothing. Though the Bodies are Different, the Spirit, the Invisible Listener, is One and the Same.”

Spirit has no form. It is totally formless. But, we are always measuring ourselves in the body-form, and we accept the knowledge through the body-form. “The Body-Form is Not Your Identity at All.” Sit for meditation, and then that Conviction will arise spontaneously.

You are immortal, indestructible. If you were never born, how can you possibly die? You are formless, omnipresent. In the Bhagavad Gita it says, “Your Presence is everywhere. It is formless”. Your Presence is just like the electricity that powers the lights or fans, and other items. Your Presence is invisible, like electricity. It is an invisible power, an invisible energy. The speaker in this body, and the listener in that body, are one and the same. This Energy is the power that enables us to act. Without that Energy that is Presence, I cannot speak, and you, cannot listen. It is as simple as that.

That Energy is One Energy. That energy is the Source. That energy is eternal. All the body-forms will expire at some point, but not that Invisible Energy. Conviction is needed because of the illusory concepts, that have been impressed upon us, such as: “You are going to die”, and, “this birth will be followed by another birth”. Or, “In a past birth, you did something wrong”. Then there are the concepts of heaven and hell. The only way to erase them all, once and for all, is with meditation.

It is both very easy, and very difficult, at the same time, because “You Must Have Strong Faith Within You, and Strong Faith in Your Master”. When your Master tells you that you are Brahman, you are Brahman. Accept it! There are to be no, “Yes, buts!” And then, stay with your Master. Be loyal to Him! Be loyal to yourself. Don’t grow restless, and with itchy feet, impulsively go looking for change, or novelties.

It is a habit of the mind, to go looking for distractions and new entertainment. You are wasting your time, and allowing whims to distract you from Reality. I keep telling you that Reality is within you. Stay where you are. You keep wanting to go on your travels again. What is to be done? “Reality is Not Separate From You. Reality is Within You, and Nowhere Else.” But, in spite of knowing this, you are tempted to go, and see other Masters: “Oh, I have heard about this Master. I will go and check him out. And then, I might visit that other Master nearby, while I am at it”.

There is no such thing as a disciple, and there is no such thing as a Master. No one has ever visited a Master because there is only One Masterly Essence that is Formless. That Masterly Essence is in you. You are the Master. “The Master is Not Separate From You.” But the mind creates confusion, and is always trying to catch you unaware, ready to dump you back into the illusory ditch. Therefore, be alert, be cautious, and try to identify yourself because “There is Nothing, Except Your Selfless Self”. Be serious! Take on this challenge. Grab this valuable moment to know yourself in a real sense. If you neglect it, if you take it lightly, one cannot say what kind of illusory, body-form might be projected again. Once you accept Reality, there will never ever be a re-appearance of form.

“Stay as You Were Prior to Beingness!” You did not know yourself. You did not know anything. Stay with “I Don’t Know”. Forget all the knowledge and the concepts: “Oh, my last birth must have been a nightmare! What will my future birth be like? This birth, that birth, blah, blah, blah”. If you don’t get rid of all the concepts that you hold just now, they will continue to create more concepts, and then, multiple concepts will keep appearing and re-appearing.

Get rid of all the guilty feelings too. You have not done anything wrong. You cannot do anything because you are not a doer. This is a long dream in which nothing has happened. Nothing will happen, and nothing is happening. We are talking about the unborn child.

p.strong: 75. What kind of changes will take place?

section.p: Since your childhood days, you have accepted the body-form as your identity. After strict meditation, and reciting the Mantra, you will notice some changes beginning to take place. There will be less internal clutter, because some of the illusory concepts, the rubbish, or garbage, has been removed. Generally, you will feel more peaceful. You will not be affected by outer circumstances, as you once were.

Overall, you will feel more content and happy, extremely happy. There will be a kind of inner joy. This joy or bliss is not a response to something, it is just a natural, flowing state, your natural state. Slowly, and silently, there will be peacefulness.

I have received messages from many foreign devotees, who tell me, that after reading Selfless Self, and meditating, they are experiencing exceptional pleasure. Even when they are facing unpleasant circumstances, this feeling of exceptional happiness and peacefulness remains.

When you have the Conviction that you are not the body, you will no longer be so concerned about what is happening around you. This can happen to you, too! While holding the food-body, at the same time, there should be the Conviction that “I am not the body at all. I was not the body. I am totally formless”. There is no death or birth. Pleasure and pain are connected to the body, and not to You. They are body-related, and have nothing to do with your Reality. Where was pleasure and pain, prior to beingness? There was nothing there.

You are already free. You have always been free, but you feel that you are in bondage. When, through meditation, these changes take place, you will live as free as a bird, permanently happy and peaceful.

p.strong: 76. How do I concentrate on the Concentrator that is formless?

section.p: Just follow what I have told you: except for your Selfless Self, there is no Brahman. So, at the initial stage, you have to act deliberately. Concentrate on that Invisible Concentrator within you, which has no form. The Invisible Concentrator within you is witnessing everything. You feel “I” in the body. Without Presence, you can’t feel “I”. In the beginning, you are to make an effort to find this “I”. Then, after finding out, that Finder will disappear. There is no finder!

With constant hammering, the Spirit within you will selflessly self-enlighten. That Realization will be Spontaneous Realization, Conviction, “I Am That”, without ego, without words. You are thinking, “I have to concentrate”. That invisible “I” indicates “I Am Ultimate Truth”. These are only words! Through this vehicle of words, we are trying to invite the attention of the Invisible Concentrator. Words are not Ultimate Truth. It is only language and meaning. Through various words, we are trying to invite the attention of the Invisible Listener within the body, that except your Selfless Self, there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman.

After concentrating on the Concentrator, the Concentrator will disappear. Both the concentrator and the concentration will disappear. There will be nothing left because everything came out of nothing, and everything dissolves within nothing. There is no deed, there is no doer. Unless you stop measuring yourself in the body-form, you will not be able to identify yourself in a real sense. It is an open fact: the body is an external cover.

p.strong: 77. “Be strong! Have courage!” What does that mean?

section.p: Strength and courage are needed to surrender all your concepts, beliefs, experiences, and your whole pseudo-identity as the body-form. “Try to Identify The Invisible Identifier Within You.” You must have the courage to accept the Reality, “That Except Yourself, There is Nothing”.

You have accepted so many concepts. Suppose that when you were a child, you used to collect, and save various currency notes. Then, some time later, after diligently collecting all this money, you found out that they were fake notes. When you discover this, you will need to find the courage to throw them all out! Similarly, after Realization, you will know, that whatever knowledge you have collected from various sources, is fake knowledge. All your knowledge of the gods and goddesses, your knowledge of spirituality, etc, etc, is, in fact, all fake knowledge because Ultimate Truth is not separate from you.

“No Knowledge Is Knowledge.” The concept of knowledge appeared upon your Presence. It is a layer. What is knowledge? Knowledge means just to identify oneself in a real sense. You identified yourself as the body-form. That is not knowledge, but ignorance, illusion.

p.strong: 78. Even though I am convinced intellectually, I still have material needs

section.p: This body-based knowledge still has a strong hold over you. Again, you are functioning within the illusory circle. I have repeatedly told you, that you are not the body at all. Where were those thoughts prior to beingness? And will you still have material needs after you leave the body? There are no thoughts after leaving the body. It is a thoughtless state. Thoughts come, and thoughts go. Don’t be a victim of your mind!

What is the truth? After you leave the body, will there be any thoughts there? Any actions? Not at all! Was there anything there prior to beingness? Did you know anything, whether you were a man or a woman? Were there any gods or goddesses there? Meditation is the way to disidentify with the body-form, and pierce through all your illusory concepts. Dry, spiritual knowledge will not help you to dissolve the body-based knowledge. Your original state is stateless, thoughtless, and action-less.

p.strong: 79. Can we know anything?

section.p: Be normal! Don’t think so much! Don’t struggle with your thoughts. Know, that there is nothing to know. You are trying to find something, so that you can grasp a hold of it. There is nothing to grasp hold of! You are forgetting that knowledge is only needed, to inform you that there is nothing to know. You cannot know what you are because you are Unidentified Identity. You are Ultimate Truth. “The Finder is Itself Ultimate Truth.” You are ignoring yourself, neglecting yourself, and searching for Brahman, somewhere outside of yourself.

Your Inner Voice is most important, your Inner Master is most important. So, listen very carefully! “The Master is Not Separate From You.” “Be With You, and Listen From the All.” Don’t play with all these spiritual words. You are not to be found in words. Be deeply involved in your Selfless Self, your Inner Master. Don’t underestimate, or ignore, your Inner Master!

p.strong: 80. If I am not the body, what am I?

section.p: The answer is: “You Are Everything”. You are not limited to the body. You are unlimited. “Your Presence is Everywhere, Just Like the Space or the Sky, for Which There is No Death and Birth.”

Because we have forgotten our identity, we need to regain that original memory of our Reality, our true Identity. So, we need hammering, non-stop hammering. The Master convinces you, saying, “You are Brahman. You are Brahman.” Then you must convince yourself, “I am Brahman. I am Brahman”. After sufficient “brainwashing”, the Spirit/Presence will accept the Reality: “Yes! I Am That!” And then Knowledge will flow from within you.

You will feel an inner transformation. All your questions will evaporate as, one by one, what appeared to be some very tough, even insurmountable questions, will be resolved automatically, with ease. Your Inner Master will answer all your questions. And, not even that, you will be blessed with the gift of foresight, and miraculous experiences as well. But even these are not Ultimate Truth. These are progressive stages. Miracles are not Ultimate Truth. Forget about them! How do these miracles happen? Where do they come from? The Master does not perform any miracles. Your Presence alone is the Source, the cause of all these miraculous experiences.

When you know who you are, as unlimited, formless, and eternal, there will not be any more fear. There is no one there to fear, and, there is nothing to fear. Fear is a concept that arose out of your mistaken identity, when you took yourself to be the body-form. Now, you can live a life free of pain and suffering, a fearless life, that is filled with peace and happiness, because you are not dependent on the body, or the world for your happiness. Basically, there is no doer, and there is no deed. “The Entire World is Projected Out of Your Invisible Anonymous Presence.”

p.strong: 81. What value does this body have?

section.p: Your body is a dead body. What value can a dead body have? By itself, it has no value. It is your Presence that gives it value. If Presence were to leave the body, just for one split second, the body’s value would drop to minus zero.

Shankaracharya says: “This body is the dirtiest of bodies, but within this dirtiest of bodies, lies the Purest Presence. Therefore, this body has great value, untold value, unspeakable value, because without it, you cannot, you will not be able to know yourself. The body is the medium, through which you can know your Identity. “The Body is Not Your Identity. The Holder of the Body is Your Identity.”

The Holder of the body has no shape. The body-holder or Sustainer is formless. Meditation is essential, to establish a strong foundation, so that you can identify yourself. You may find spiritual discussions stimulating and entertaining, but they will not help you to identify yourself, and bring about Conviction. Meditation is illusion, but, through it, you will be able to know yourself directly. Spiritual discussion alone, will not cut it. It will only enable you to know yourself via second-hand knowledge, through words, and therefore, indirectly.

p.strong: 82. What is our lifestyle based on?

section.p: Our lifestyle is based on all the concepts that were engraved, and continue to be engraved in us. We have been told so many different stories, which we have accepted at face value. We were told many things about the existence of God, Brahman, Atman, and the Masters. But you do not know your Reality because of the influences of the body-form. You are unaware of your true identity because you are holding the body. Spirit lies within the body. Along with the Spirit, consciousness appeared. Your Presence is beyond consciousness. Consciousness has taken on a colour that says, “I am a man”, or, “I am a woman”, or “I am something else”. Prior to consciousness, you did not know yourself.

As I have told you so many times, there are numerous concepts around, regarding death and birth, past birth, future birth, salvation, God, Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, Master, disciple devotee, liberation, etc, that we have accepted them blindly. They are, in fact, nothing but allegations, without any substance. They are all illusion! However, these mere concepts have bombarded us for so long, that they continue to carry immense power, to influence and confuse us.

p.strong: 83. I know I’m not the body, but I still want a long, and happy life?

section.p: Who wants a long life? The body is a dead body. Who is acting from that body? Who is listening from the body? Who is enjoying this worldly knowledge? That Invisible Presence called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master – That You Are. You are formless, without fate, rebirth, past birth, or future birth.

You are Almighty God. God means Ultimate Truth. God does not take birth. To know yourself in a real sense requires strong confidence, and strong faith in yourself and your Master. If you have a wavering mind, you will be here today, and gone tomorrow. You will quickly forget about your Master, and exchange him for someone else the next day. Changing Masters is like changing wives, Mmh! How can you find anything, if you go hopping from one Guru to another? There should not be this kind of polygamous mentality. You are to remain faithful to yourself, and to one Master. Don’t sell yourself! Don’t be a prostitute! You may feel that this is a slanderous word, but abusing your Spirit in this way is like that. Show yourself some loyalty. Be loyal to yourself, and be loyal to your Master, too.

Time is very short. Each and every moment of your life is very, very important. You are lacking spiritual confidence. Nothing has been hidden from you. All has been revealed to you. It is Open Truth! What more do you want? There is nothing more. Accept it: “Yes! I Am That! Yes! I Am That”.

Basically, there is only one Spirit, within all the billions of human bodies. Forget about human beings and human bodies, you are Spirit. Other beings are not able to identify themselves, but we can, with the help of our intellect. Now forget about that!

Here, the principle of spirituality is to identify yourself in a real sense. And, in spite of many dialogues, the concept that you hold of, “I am somebody”, is still not dissolving. This is a tragedy! The only remedy is meditation, and Mantra recitation. You are not meditating regularly. You will only be able to identify yourself through meditation. Concentrate on how you were prior to beingness? Contemplate! There is no form, no needs. You are totally unaware of “happiness” and “unhappiness”. All these concepts came with the body. Identify yourself, and then, you will not be so concerned about the body, and living a long life.

p.strong: 84. Is it okay to follow my family tradition, of devotion to Rama and Krishna?

section.p: Do what you like! Engage yourself in that particular spiritual atmosphere, if you wish. When you are worshipping and singing bhajans, it keeps you in a sattvic atmosphere, where you are always thinking about your Reality, your Master, and spirituality.
When we recite the Mantra, we are doing it to halt the flow of concepts. You will enjoy reciting the Mantra and singing the bhajans.

Rama and Krishna are the names that were given to the Ultimate Truth. Rama and Krishna do not have any independent identity. Who knew of Rama prior to beingness? And after leaving the body, will Rama still be there, or Krishna? You started to identify Rama and Krishna, only after your identification with the body-form. If the Spirit disappeared from the body, you will not be able to identify Rama, Krishna, or any Master or disciple?

Prior to beingness, you did not know anything. You did not know of Rama, Krishna, or any other Master. Nothing was known. So that Reality is to be absorbed. Rama, Krishna, etc, are only names for that Supernatural Power within you.

Be firm! I am specifically asking you to be firm. Do not allow any illusory concepts to enter. There is nothing happening. No one is going to give you a boon. And no one is going to curse you. You are All, and All is you. You are alone. “You Yourself Are Almighty God. Your Invisible Presence Is Almighty God.”

Everything is there within you: Sri Ram, Sri Krishna, everybody. Dattatreya as well! You are Dattatreya! It is a fact, but you refuse to accept it. The Listener within you is Almighty God. What more do you want? You are subtler than the sky or space. You are! You are everybody. You Are That! Accept what I am placing before you, because it is your Truth.

p.strong: 3 THE MEDICINE: MANTRA, MEDITATION, DEVOTION

p.emphasis: “Through meditation and spiritual knowledge, you are sweeping everything away. This cleaning process purifies the body. Shankaracharya says that this body is the dirtiest body. But within it lies the Purest Presence. It is this Presence which gives the body its high value. Without Presence, the body is worthless because it is a dead body. This is the principle.”

p.strong: 85. How can I digest the knowledge?

section.p: Meditation is needed to digest the knowledge. The Invisible Presence within the body is very sensitive. It is impressionable. Therefore, when a Mantra is given, such as “Aham Brahmasmi”, “I am Brahman” or “Soham”, it has a strong effect on the Spirit. Every lineage initiates disciples, using different words. These words create powerful vibrations, when you are reciting the Mantra, meditating with the Mantra, and concentrating with the Mantra.

Meditation is very important, as without it, you cannot digest the knowledge. Without meditation, the knowledge will remain at an intellectual level. Intellectual knowledge is insufficient. You need practical knowledge, so that it can be applied to your daily life.

In the beginning, you must be disciplined. Sit crossed-legged with your eyes half-closed. Concentrate on the tip of your nose. Why place your focus here? Because your mind is a crazy mind. To keep it busy, we ask you to concentrate on the tip of the nose. While the mind is concentrating on the tip of the nose, you are reciting the Mantra. The mind cannot distract you, when it is kept occupied, so you are able to recite the Mantra without interference.

Concentration keeps the mind busy, so that you are not allowing it to create any questions, or some other crazy activity. When you recite the Mantra, you are directly hammering your Selfless Self with: “I am Brahman. Brahman I am, I am Brahman, Brahman I am”. After a period of non-stop recitation, your Originality will open up: “I Am That!” Exactly when this occurs will vary widely. It depends on your approach, your involvement, and your spiritual maturity. If you are very serious, earnest and sincere about uncovering your Reality, and if you are fully involved in the pursuit of Truth, then, you will find it easy. However, if you are casual and half-hearted, then, you can forget about it! It is not going to happen! The ball is in your court! It is up to you!

Don’t take these words literally, but the Heart must long to know Itself. There must be some anxiety behind your quest, an urgency, a deep, burning feeling within you, that is driving you forward to know who you are. You are anxious to know. You are on an urgent mission. Self-Realization has become your one and only purpose. You will only find the treasure by diving deep within. Have faith in yourself, and faith in your Master.

When you accept what the Master says, Reality will open up within you in an instant. And, finally, you will be Home. You will forget about the body-form. You will forget about everything. And you will experience, exceptional peacefulness and happiness.

The waves of energy, or vibrations coming from the Mantra, create a spiritual atmosphere which helps to absorb the Reality. In the beginning, meditation is to be strict, and very disciplined. Literal knowledge or dry knowledge, will not help you. Be strong! Have the courage to accept the Reality! Hold the Conviction that “I am not the doer, and there is no deed”. It is a fact, that there is no doer and no deed. A form is needed for there to be a doer and deeds. You don’t have any form. You are basically formless. You know that this human form is not going to last, so, it is time to be serious about your practice. After Conviction, you will be detached from all the concepts, that used to cause you so much confusion and problems. You will practice discrimination, and be alert at all times, so that you don’t let illusion back in.

You will become your own teacher, as you learn, to first witness, and then accept, the useful thoughts, while rejecting those thoughts that are unhelpful. All your attachments will dissolve, when you know that they are illusory. Your desires will subside, and then, completely disappear. Previously, when we did not know any better, we were slaves of the mind, ego and intellect. The mind was controlling us, dictating to us: “do this, and then, do that”. But now we know, that the mind is totally dependent upon our energy. Without our Presence, how can the mind work? If there is no Presence, the mind cannot function.

Prior to beingness or consciousness, there was no mind, no ego, and no intellect. There were no body-based issues. The issue of thoughts never arose. The question of existence never arose. You are aware of your existence now because of the body-form. You feel it. But, who is acting? Who is listening? Who is carrying out the activities? It is not you, but the Invisible Presence called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, Ultimate Truth, That You Are. It is Final Truth.

This is your time. Apply the knowledge, recite the Mantra, and abundant happiness will be yours. Abundant peace will be yours. Where will this happiness and peace come from? From you, of course! From your Source! You are the Source of Happiness. You are the Source of Peace. “Your Nature is Happiness, Peace and Bliss.” Though you are living with this body, when the Spontaneous Conviction arises, and the knowledge has been digested with meditation, happiness will begin to flow. You will be overflowing with happiness.

You are in the world, but not of the world. You can live without desires or attachments. When the knowledge has been absorbed, you will no longer be a slave to the mind, ego and intellect. Before, when you lived in the darkness of ignorance, the mind was dictating the terms. Now that the light of knowledge, the Truth has been illuminated, you know that you are the only Source. You are the Source of Power, that enables the functioning of the body-mind.

When the knowledge of your Reality is digested and absorbed, there will be no more thoughts, and no more questions, about your bodily existence and experiences. You are that Invisible Presence. You are Final Truth. Through the medium of the body-form, that Conviction of your Reality, will explode spontaneously within you. You will not be able to contain your happiness, your ecstasy, which will be so explosive, that you may feel that you are going to burst! There will be so much fire within and around you, that you may feel that you are going to combust!

p.strong: 86. How can I control this crazy mind?

section.p: You need practical knowledge. Theoretical knowledge can be grasped intellectually, but by itself, it will not serve to establish Ultimate Truth in you. The Naam Mantra will give you that practical knowledge. If you don’t have the Naam Mantra, you can recite “Aham Brahmasmi”, or, “I am Brahman. Brahman I am”.

The human body is a structure that contains the mind, the ego and the intellect. This trio always functions together. The mind is very crazy, always creating problems, moods, depression. Sometimes, there are good thoughts flowing, and sometimes, there are bad thoughts. Bad waves of thoughts create confusion, depression, nervousness, disappointment, negativity. They disturb our spirituality.

Today I will talk about how to control the crazy mind. You know the theory: “I was not the body. I am not the body. I am not going to remain the body. The body is not my identity at all.” Therefore, the implications are that there is no birth, and no death, no past life, and no future life. But, that whole bunch of concepts, which you have accumulated since childhood until today, still have a very strong hold over you. To combat this, we advise regular meditation. This will place all your concepts in the melting pot. They must all be melted, dissolved, or burned away. This can only happen with the practice of meditation.

That crazy mind lifts our mood at one moment, and then brings it crashing down, at the next moment. This see-saw effect disturbs our spirituality. Meditation will make us Masters of the mind, so that the mind will no longer control you. “Don’t Follow the Mind. Let the Mind Follow You.”

What is meditation? Meditation means practical concentration. Your concentration should be very strong, so that if bad thought-waves arise, you can ignore them. To be able to ignore these negative waves of thought, takes self-control. And this is where meditation comes in.

Don’t struggle with the thoughts. Learn how to tactfully avoid them. Suppose that in the distance, you see an undesirable person approaching you. You may either avoid him, or, if that were not possible, you will just exchange pleasantries, “How are you? Good morning” etc. and quickly, be on your way. The aim here is to avoid this unwanted encounter, but in a tactful manner. In a similar way, when undesirable thoughts approach you, you are to skilfully avoid them, because these thoughts will create some unpleasantness and dispeace. Your blissfulness will be affected by the encounter. So, number one is meditation. Meditation will control the crazy mind.

p.strong: 87. What is Meditation?

section.p: You are Ultimate Truth. The practice of meditation invites the attention of the Meditator within you, that you are Ultimate Truth. But, you are so wrapped up in concepts, that you do not know who you are. You are not the body-form. Who is holding this body? Meditate, and you will find out! You have to go deep within your Selfless Self. Only then, will you discover that you are not separate from Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God or the Master.

The body has a time limit, an age limit. You are a small boy, who then gradually, becomes a young man, and then, an old man. Some day or other, willingly or unwillingly, you will leave this body. This means that the body is not your identity at all. Who is holding this body? You must go deep, and deep, into your Selfless Self. There, you will find out and realize: “Oh! I Am That”! The Conviction that you are Ultimate Truth, will appear spontaneously with meditation.

The outcome of the process of hammering yourself with sacred words, is that your true identity will be re-established. Here is an example, supposing that for some reason, you temporarily forgot who you were, like in the case of an amnesiac. In this situation, family members will show you familiar objects, photos, etc. They will try and remind you of events and associations, in the hope of triggering your memory, so that you can regain your identity.

In a similar way, the Master is trying to convince your Inner Invisible Presence of your eternal, but forgotten, Identity. What you see is illusion. That through which you see is Reality. That is the principle of spirituality. Dry discussion alone about “Brahman, Atman, Maya, Paramatman”, is meaningless because you are only grasping the words about Ultimate Truth, descriptions of Ultimate Truth, but not Ultimate Truth directly. Words are just that, words. Where were these words prior to beingness? There were no words, no language prior to beingness.

Meditation means concentration. Meditation means reminding ourselves of That forgotten Identity. We have lost our memory. We are mistakenly posing ourselves as men or women, male or female. That is our external cover. But within that, there is that Exceptional Invisible Presence. That Reality That You Are must be established.

The purpose of spirituality is not about adding more theoretical knowledge. It is about subtracting all the illusory knowledge you have gathered. It is about removing the ignorance, or, one could say, replacing the “wrong knowledge” with “right knowledge”. [not that there is any “right” or “wrong” – concepts only!] When this happens, you will realize that what you took yourself to be throughout your life, was not your identity at all. That through which you are able to know yourself, is your Identity.

Even though we know that we are not the body-form, we continue to ignore this fact because of the bundle of concepts, that are still wrapped around us. Melt all the concepts! Visiting different Masters, and reading endless books, is meaningless. Why? Because unless you approach your Selfless Self, you will not be able to identify yourself.

The basic principle is that in this human body, in this human life, we are not identifying ourselves. We are identifying everyone else, “How is he? How is she?” But we are not identifying how we are! The books say it all. They tell you everything about Reality. However, that Reality is not absorbed. It is not digested. To digest this Reality, you are to undergo strict meditation. Meditation is the only way. You may have any Master of your choosing, but you must have strong faith in that Master. You must have confidence and faith within your Selfless Self. This is a very important ingredient, essential, in fact.
“Last birth, future birth, last destiny, present, past”, these are all illusion. There is no last birth, no future birth. All these concepts have been sourced from our imagination.

Of what importance is spirituality? Did you know anything about spirituality, prior to beingness? No! Spirituality has one purpose alone, and that is, to remind you that everything is within you. You are the only Source. You are Ultimate Reality. You are Ultimate Truth.

We want to be Masters of Literature, Masters of Philosophy, but not Masters of ourselves – not Masters of Selfless Self! The Masterly Essence is within you alone. We are always forgetting our Own Master, the Reader Within, the Listener Within, That Divine Essence. Through spirituality, and more specifically, through meditation, you can identify yourself. Your Identity cannot be grasped, through the mind or the intellect. But with disciplined meditation, you will be able to identify yourself. You have not been able to identify yourself, because of the ego that still believes itself to be somebody.

Existence in the body-form is intolerable. Body-based knowledge is unbearable, and because of this, we are always looking for comfort, distractions, or relief from various material sources that will alleviate this discomfort, if only momentarily: food, alcohol, entertainment, power, sex, fame, gambling, etc. All our desires, requirements and needs exist to make our precarious situation a little more bearable.

Who wants happiness? Who wants satisfaction? Who wants peacefulness? It is neither the body nor the Spirit. Self-enquire! Who is acting? Who is listening to this dialogue? Who is reading this? What will I get from all my reading? You don’t know because you are always searching, and never reaching a conclusion. There must be a full stop.

We are always analyzing what is being said, analyzing words, instead of accepting, what the Master is trying to convey. This is a big stumbling block! Don’t try to define the words of the Masters. We created language, and gave words their meanings. Instead, try to identify yourself! In the light of all the spiritual knowledge, find out where you stand. What is your position? Travelling, searching, journeying here and there, is not going to lead you anywhere. “You Are the Destination!” Look within and meditate. Look at You, not at the world.

If I am not the body, then “Who Am I?” This is the question that demands an answer. After searching, and searching some more, the searcher will disappear. The whole experience of this illusory world will disappear. Exceptional peacefulness will be there, without any identity, or experience. This state is stateless. The thoughtless state will be there continuously. There will be no thoughts.

But, in order to reach this Ultimate Stage, knowledge is needed to remove the ignorance and illusion. And, meditation is needed to absorb the knowledge. Just reading, reading, and reading some more is meaningless, and only perpetuates the illusory, body-based knowledge.

You can read thousands of spiritual books, but if you still do not know who you are, if you still think that Ultimate Truth is separate from you, then you have wasted all your time and energy. “The Searcher is Itself The Master.” The Master is not separate from you. I am helping you to regain your memory of your Identity. Meditate on that Masterly Essence within you, and refresh your memory! Meditation will dissolve everything that you are not.

p.strong: 88. What is the principle of meditation?

section.p: The principle of meditation is that the Meditator within you, which is called Brahman, has forgotten its Identity. In order to re-establish your true identity, we use Mantra Meditation. There are various, sacred words, such as Shivoham, Aham Brahmasmi, etc, that are used as hammers, to remind you of your Ultimate Truth, the Ultimate Reality that you are. The Spirit is highly sensitive, and will absorb whatever is in its vicinity. Therefore, with constant repetition of “I am Brahman, I am Brahman”, that Reality will eventually be accepted. If you hear something often enough, it will become part of you.

The Spirit within you, or Energy, Atman, Paramatman, or whatever name you wish to call it by, is the Invisible Power, that enlivens, or animates, an otherwise, inanimate or dead body. The Invisible Spirit within the body through which you are acting, through which you are watching, through which you are listening, that Invisible Presence is Ultimate Truth. What happens is that when the body-consciousness appears, it forgets its Identity and assumes an independent pose: “I am somebody, an individual”.

Prior to beingness, how were you? You say, “I don’t know”. After leaving the body, how will you be? “I don’t know”. “I don’t know” means you are formless. The answer is correct. I often give this very, simple example of the children’s game: “Knock, knock, Who is There?” One child is at home. The second child knocks on the door, asking, “Is there anybody there?” The first child replies: “No! There’s nobody there”. The child who says, “there’s nobody there”, is there! In a similar way, when we say, “I don’t know”, this means that “I am not in any form. I am formless, but my Presence is there”. Your Presence is everywhere, just like the space or the sky. When the meditation has done its job, there will be Conviction. You will know: “I am Brahman. I am Paramatman. I am God. I am Master”. And you will live like That.

p.strong: 89. Is meditation absolutely necessary? I am averse to it

section.p: Meditation is most important because through it we are always hammering, and impressing Reality in you. Mantra meditation is essential, to encourage, and bring about the Spontaneous Conviction, that you are Brahman. With continuous hammering, the Spirit will accept its Reality. It is bound to happen, as long as you put in some effort, in the beginning. Afterwards, it will not be necessary.

Your Presence is Ultimate Truth. That is your Secret Asset, your Confidential Asset, your Invisible Asset, that is within you. But most of us are unaware of That, and so we go wandering here and there, in search of a Master, in whom we hope or believe, can give us what we are looking for.

That Spirit or Presence, that is acting through the body is not limited to the body. It is beyond the body. Knowledge means the identification of your Secret Asset, your Ultimate Truth. In theory, we may know that we are Brahman, but to make this knowledge practical knowledge, meditation is a must.

We need meditation because we are always under the pressure of illusory concepts. You rely on God to help you lighten the load, saying to yourself, “God will help me. God will bless me”, while at the same time you are fearful, that if you do something wrong, He will punish you. These are some of the concepts, that you need to look at, and dissolve.

There is no God out there who is going to help you, or bless you, or punish you. God is not separate from you. You are Ultimate Truth. You are Almighty God! Find this out for yourself! Ask yourself: “Where do I stand? Is this world true or not? What is the fate of this world?” To emerge from all this confusion, the secret of spirituality is revealed to you, along with the Mantra.

It is only your firm and deliberate meditation, that will shift the entrenched, illusory concepts, that have been part of your life since childhood. We have been living in the illusory field of the body-form, feeling trapped and unfree. You are actually free already, but you feel that you are tied up, bound, and wrapped up by a bundle of concepts.

Reality has remained hidden from you, in the dark, because of these concepts. But, when all the illusion is removed, your Originality will emerge, and you will exclaim: “Yes! I Am That!” This will be the happiest moment of your life!

You will see amazing results. You will be aware of all the changes within you, dramatic changes. Previously, you may have been intolerant, even violent. But, after Conviction, there will be exceptional tolerance, exceptional silence, and exceptional peace and happiness. All these qualities are already within you, but you are not aware of them. After removing all the illusory concepts, your Reality, your Originality will open up within you. This feeling will be spontaneous.

Meditation is essential. Without it, we cannot know our Identity. Our present conviction is, “I am the body”, or “I am John”, or some other name that has been given to the body. With meditation, you will uncover your true Identity. You are Ultimate Truth, Ultimate Reality.

p.strong: 90. Why do you insist on continuous meditation?

section.p: People often ask, “Why do I have to meditate, when I already know that I am Brahman?”. It is not enough for you to know that you are Brahman, because when you make this statement, the subtle ego has crept in, along with the mind and the intellect. “That Ultimate Truth That You Are, is to Be Established Without the Mind, Ego and Intellect.” It is just like this body is called a “male body”, or a “man”. I am living as a male body, but I am not thinking, “I am male”. This body was named “Ramakant Maharaj”. I don’t have to repeat, “I am Ramakant Maharaj”, over and over again. That name has been accepted, so that, if anyone calls out, “Ramakant Maharaj”, I will respond, and act as Ramakant Maharaj. The form is Ramakant Maharaj. But I am acting spontaneously. I am not thinking, “Oh, I am Ramakant Maharaj, and so, now I have to behave in a certain way”. Deliberate action is not needed because the name, “Ramakant Maharaj”, was accepted a long time ago. Likewise, You Are Ultimate Truth, Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master, That is the Established Truth, Final Truth. Deliberate action is not needed, such as, “Oh, I am Brahman, so I have to accept it. I have to prepare, or plan, etc.” No action is needed, no thinking is needed, and no analysis is needed. “Accept It as It Is, Without Thought.”

If you have a question, you will receive an instant answer from within, because you are the Source. Selfless Self is a fast computer. When you need some information, and you Google it, you will receive a response. When you want some information quickly from your very own, inbuilt computer, you will get it instantly. Selfless Self runs faster than Google!

Continuous meditation does not mean sitting still in one place, for hour after hour. Continuous meditation means that we are remembering our true nature all the time. We are staying with Selfless Self, and not being pulled into the world. Meditation is concentration.

Yesterday, there was a lecture in Marathi regarding the Dasbodh. It described how there are many layers of bodies: the food-body, the subtle-body, and this, and that other body. These are the concepts, or layers, upon your Ultimate Truth or Presence. “Through Meditation, You Are Hammering Selfless Self, With the Result That the Reality Will Open Up.” You could say, that we are giving psychological treatment, which will cause you to forget your food-body identity. Then the door to Reality will burst open wide.

“What is Reality? You Are Nothing to do With the Body-Based-Knowledge.” Body-knowledge is illusion, that is related to a temporary body-form. So, until that touch of the body-form dissolves, you need to keep the meditation going.

I am insisting on the Naam Mantra, and on meditation, so that when your time comes to leave the body, there will not be any concepts around like, “I am dying”. Death and birth do not apply to you. Every day, I am shouting out the same thing: “You are not the body. You are not going to remain the body. You are Ultimate Truth. You are Brahman. You are Atman. You are Paramatman. You are a Master”. Is it just going in one ear and out the other?

The entire world is the Seer’s reflection. If there is no Seer, nothing can be seen. You are enjoying the seen, but if there is no Seer, what importance does the seen have? You have become a victim of your own concepts: “I am somebody”. And with that concept, you are trying to identify yourself. It is an impossibility. Unless you throw away your copper coin, you will never be able to enjoy the golden coin! You will all be familiar with the psychology of a dog and its bone. If he catches a bone, he is not going to leave it alone for a moment, even if you place a delicious dish in front of him. [Maharaj chuckles]. Human beings are like that. You have accepted so many concepts, that you steadfastly refuse to leave them behind, even for this treasure!

Even when the Master says that, “You are Brahman”, you reply with “No, no! How can I be Brahman?” This is just an example I’m giving you. Don’t take what I am saying literally. What I am saying is that you have to change your psychology, and change your identity. Mould yourself according to this spiritual knowledge. Spiritual knowledge just means Perfect Identification, Spontaneous Conviction. It is a simple thing, but courage is needed for you to accept the Reality, that you are Brahman. You are no longer a disciple, or a devotee. It is a fact, and not a concept, because you are Ultimate Truth.

p.strong: 91. Why the Naam Mantra?

section.p: The origin of the Naam Mantra goes back to Dattatreya, the founder of our lineage. Then came Revanath Maharaj, and for the next thousand or so years, this Mantra has been given to devotees and disciples. The Naam Mantra has been passed down, from one illustrious Master to another, and more recently, from Bhausaheb Maharaj to Siddharameshwar Maharaj to Nisargadatta Maharaj, who passed it on to all his disciples, including myself. Don’t underestimate its power. The sacred words of the Naam Mantra, are used to hammer the ego, in the preliminary stage.

The Mantra is prescribed, so that you can come out from the vicious circle of illusion. After Spontaneous Conviction, comes the “Stateless State”, or the way you were prior to beingness. This will unfold naturally, without any deliberate act on your part, or without the use of the imagination. You will feel: “I am totally separate from the body, separate from all these bodies, separate from the five elements. “I Have Nothing to Do With Anything.”

p.strong: 92. Why do we have Naam Mantra meditation?

section.p: When you recite the Naam Mantra, you are impressing that invisible consciousness with Reality: “I am Brahman. Brahman I am”. This deliberate act will bring about spontaneous living. You will forget about yourself. You will forget about everything. This state is called samadhi trance. It is one in which your body-identity is completely forgotten. The consciousness is absent. Though your Presence is there, there is no consciousness in any form. In this state, the consciousness is unknown. It is invisible consciousness, Invisible Presence, where there is no fear of death, where there is no need of happiness, where there is no need of peacefulness, where nothing is needed. “That is Your Original Place of Original Things.”

The Naam Mantra clears away all the mind’s habits and confusion, and rids it of its illusory problems. Use the Naam Mantra with full faith and confidence. It is a very powerful tool. At the same time, accept and absorb what the Master is saying. Do not dissect the words, as this only brings about mental struggle and contradictions. The power behind the Master’s words will ignite your Inner Master. If you question, and doubt what the Master is saying, you are just wasting your time. There are no contradictions. The Master has only one message. It is the Final Truth: “Except for your Selfless Self there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman, no Master”.

Because of the consciousness, you consider yourself to be a man or woman. You view yourself as a human being. Consciousness is working in that human form. All the activities are performed through consciousness. The mistake was made when consciousness accepted the illusion, “I am the body”. This identification with the body-form created all the problems. The Naam Mantra is an effective tool that will reverse the process, and correct the mistake. Reality will replace illusion, and “I am Ultimate Reality” will supplant “I am the body”.

To return to your place of origin, all the layers that have covered over your Presence, need to melt, so that there are no add-ons left. The power of the Naam Mantra will demolish everything that is not permanent, and changeless.

p.strong: 93. Will the Naam Mantra help my spirituality?

section.p: I am asking you to recite the Mantra, at all times, with full faith. The power of the Mantra, the strength of the Mantra, the transformative power of the Mantra depends entirely on the value and importance that you place on it. If you value it highly, and with complete faith, and deep self-involvement, it will take you to the bottomless bottom of Reality. The Naam Mantra has immense value, but, if you do not take it seriously, and recite it in a mechanical fashion alone, it will simply not work. It will not empower you.

The Spirit or Presence, through which you are talking, through which you are listening, through which you are watching, through which you are understanding, through which you are doing all your activities, is highly sensitive. Whatever is impressed upon it is immediately reflected.

I often use this example of someone who abuses you verbally. Your response is immediate. You wish to retaliate with similar abusive language, or maybe even punch him on the nose! This interaction demonstrates how seriously we take words and their meanings. How easily we absorb everything! If someone uses abusive language, we see it as a “personal attack on the ego”. This creates an atmosphere. Someone calls you “an ass”. You are offended. So, you react, and call that person an ass as well, and so it goes on.

The sacred words of the Naam Mantra mean “I am Brahman. Brahman I am”. They are imbued with vibrations, energy that have a profound effect on Spirit. Do you follow? Therefore, taking into account, the sensitivity of Spirit, we give these sacred words. And after continuous reciting of the Mantra, the effects will be absorbed by the Invisible Listener within you. The Naam Mantra is working on your Invisible Presence, not on the body-form.

That Invisible Listener within you is hidden. I am addressing the Invisible Listener within you, not your body-form. It is not the body-form that is listening, but the Spirit or Presence. And, that Spirit or Presence is absorbing the powerful vibrations of the Naam Mantra. The Mantra confirms your real Identity, your true Identity. That is its purpose.

Of course, the Mantra is also illusion, but we need to use a secondary thorn to remove the primary, illusory thorn. Will the Naam Mantra help you spiritually? Of course, because it is a practical tool, a medium, that will make your spirituality pragmatic. The Naam Mantra will establish a strong foundation, by removing all the illusory concepts, with which you have constructed your dream life. It will make your spirituality real, and alive. You will live it!

How many so-called spiritual people, end their life trembling with fear on their death-bed? This means that their spiritual knowledge has not been absorbed. Recite the Naam Mantra at all times, and then, that pressure of death, that fear of death will dissolve, because you will be convinced that there is no death. There is only death for the food-body-form. After Conviction, there will be no more tension or fear, unhappiness or dispeace. The Naam Mantra is the Master Key that opens the door to your Ultimate Reality. Respect it, value it, and use it at all times!

p.strong: 94. Why is the Naam Mantra required?

section.p: Because you are under the false impression that you are somebody, and Brahman is something else, that is separate from you. Your Original Identity is Brahman. You, yourself are Brahman. Brahman is not separate from you. Ultimate Truth is not separate from you. It is like you have been under some kind of a spell looking for Brahman.

The concepts of “God” and “religion” were also created to offer comfort to the masses, as well as trust and faith. They are there to provide security, and to give us something to aspire to: “God is great! Oh God, bless me! God will help me!” This is all okay to give us some temporary relief, however, what is being dished out to the masses, is not the whole truth. There is some truth there, but it is not the whole elephant!

God is not separate from you. If there were no Presence, who is going to identify your God? For you to even utter the word “God”, your Presence is essential. Your Spontaneous Invisible Presence is most important. Reciting the Mantra will establish this Conviction. Non-stop concentration and hammering will lead to the realization of your Reality: what you always were, are, and always will be – “I am That”. That Spontaneous experience will appear, and then, there will be peacefulness, beyond your imagination.

p.strong: 95. In the beginning, I must put in some effort?

section.p: You have accepted the concepts, without any discrimination. You have accepted that you are a man or a woman, a father, son or daughter. You have accepted that you were born on a certain date, and you expect to die at a certain age. You have absorbed all these illusory concepts like a sponge. It is not your fault, because as children we had no control over, who or what, was influencing and conditioning us.

This indoctrination has made your ego very strong, and set in its ways. To loosen the stronghold that the ego has over you, the Master gives you the sacred Mantra. The Master tells you that you are Brahman. You are Almighty God. You are Ultimate Truth. But, tragically, you are not accepting the Truth. You are accepting your gender, your position in society, your adopted faith or religion, without question, but when the Master places your Truth before you, you laugh, and say, “How can I be Brahman?” And maybe you even think that the Master is making fun of you, by proposing such a preposterous idea!

What the Master says is true, but you are unable to accept it, because of your long association with the body. You have forgotten your identity, and therefore, you will need a lot of hammering, to convince and remind you of your original identity.

The Master says: “Except for your Selfless Self, there is no God, no Brahman, Atman, Paramatman”. However, you are not accepting this. It is such a waste of your life! But, if someone calls you an “ass”, you accept it instantly, and retaliate with full force.

When the Master tells you that you are Ultimate Truth, Ultimate Reality, you should be deeply touched, stirred and moved, moved because Spirit is hearing its own story. It is being reminded of its Identity. But, because your concepts are in the way, the Master’s words are not penetrating these illusory layers, that have covered over your Presence.

The Naam Mantra will reduce the force of the mind, ego and intellect. The process is a melting process. You are covered in ash. The Master removes the ash. And underneath the ash, the fire is burning.

Various words have been used as Mantras: Aham Brahmasmi, Shivoham, Soham, etc. Concentrate on the words given to you by your Master. It is only by concentrating on these words, that they will be absorbed. And then, eureka: “Aha! I Am That”.

p.strong: 96. So, will the Naam Mantra help me to identify myself?

section.p: Words are only words, however, through these specific, sacred words, you will be inspired from within. When you hammer yourself with “I am Brahman. Brahman I am”, you are removing concepts, that are obscuring your identity. For example, if you consider yourself to be female or male, then this identity is stopping you from accepting your true Identity – that you are Ultimate Truth. This illusory concept, and many others like it, are barriers to your ability to accept the Reality. These concepts narrow your vision, and keep you functioning as a mere, limited, powerless, body-form. Even though you know intellectually, that you are Ultimate Truth, or Brahman, that knowledge, is not known to you. It is still out of reach. You have not been able to absorb this knowledge because of the illusory, conceptual clutter that is in the way.

And even though you may know everything about spirituality, and about Brahman, you are still searching outside of yourself to find Brahman. You are one with Brahman. Ultimate Truth is not to be found anywhere, but within you.

p.emphasis: “Try to identify the Searcher within you. The Individual Searcher within you, is Ultimate Truth, Through Which the Entire World is Projected.”

section.p: Listen carefully! Wake up to this fact. I am repeating the same thing, again and again. Without Presence, your body is a dead body. Accept this Truth. If you accept it, you will stop taking the body-form for real. Absorb this knowledge! If Presence is not there, who will talk about the world? Who will talk about the gods and the goddesses? All the gods and goddesses came along with you, when Presence touched with the body. Will you still know of these gods and goddesses when you leave the body? Will you still know Rama and Krishna? Where was Rama and Krishna prior to beingness? And where will Rama and Krishna be, after your body dissolves?

So many stories came along with the body-form, fictional, fairy stories. I am not criticizing these stories, but, be clear, in order to identify Rama or Krishna, or any god for that matter, and in order to identify your Master, Presence is a prerequisite. Without your Presence, there can be no identification of anything, no identification whatsoever. Therefore, forget about the gods and goddesses. Forget about Rama and Krishna. Identify yourself! Identify that Unidentified Identity that is your True Identity. Your Presence is Invaluable Presence. Identify your Presence. I am talking about that Presence, that Invisible Presence within you.

This practice is free. There is no charge. The only cost is your devotion. I am asking for your devotion, devotion to your Selfless Self. At least, spend two or three hours out of twenty-four regularly. You are doing it for you. I am not asking you to meditate for me. This is for your benefit. If you don’t get rid of your concepts, and wake up to your Reality, there is the possibility of seeing another dream, like this one. Therefore, be serious! Casual spirituality will not cut it.
The Mantra is composed of ancient, sacred words. Through these words you are hammering yourself with this basic knowledge, that is universally known – “I am Brahman”.

But, even though it may be universally known, that knowledge needs to turn into Realization. The Mantra will assist in bringing this about spontaneously, but only, if your earnestness and sincere devotion is driving you forward.

I take on the role of Master for the purpose of teaching. But “Master”, is just a name that has been ascribed, like “disciple” or “devotee”. There is no difference between us. Our bodies differ, that is all. Identify this knowledge. Digest it! Convince yourself! Read the book Selfless Self. It addresses the questions, difficulties and problems that are concerning you.

These days, my health is not permitting me to talk with you. I have already told you everything, again and again. Now you have to absorb it. You have the basic knowledge, the complete knowledge. So, try and identify yourself. Apart from the daily, two hour concentrated meditation, recite the Mantra at all times. Whenever you remember to recite it, whether you are working, cleaning, bathing, cooking, just recite it. In time, you will no longer have to recite it, as this will just happen by itself, spontaneously.

p.strong: 97. I cannot come to India to receive the Naam Mantra...

section.p: If you cannot come to India, then use the Mantra Aham Brahmasmi, or, “I am Brahman. Brahman I am”. It is not compulsory for you to receive the Naam Mantra. Have strong faith in the Master’s words: “Except for Selfless Self, there is no Brahman, Atman, Paramatman”. If you have full faith in the Master who is teaching you, then a Mantra is not necessary. The Mantra is only a medium that creates a conducive atmosphere. It is not only about reciting the Mantra, there is to be a spontaneous awakening of: “I am Brahman, Brahman I am”. Only then, will your originality emerge, with the result that, when it is time for you to leave the body, there will not be any more dreams.

p.strong: 98. A casual approach to meditation is futile

section.p: If you do not have the Naam Mantra, recite Aham Brahmasmi, with full confidence and concentration. You may sit for half an hour, or one or two hours, or divide your time accordingly. But don’t waste your time. In the beginning, devote as much time as you can!
When the illusory concepts disappear, then, the reflection of Ultimate Truth appears upon your Presence. This, in turn, leads to Conviction.

What is needed is regular churning, regular reciting, and regular hammering. You are to act forcefully, and deliberately, in the beginning. This intellectual act will lead to a practical experience, whereby all these concepts dissolve slowly, silently and permanently. Then, you will feel spontaneous blissfulness inside because your Invisible Presence is the Ultimate Truth, the central point of this whole world.

The world does not have its own identity. It is reflected from your Presence. The entire world is projected out of your Presence. If there is no Presence within the body, there is no world. Simple fact! When the Spirit clicks with the body, the world is projected. When you leave the body, the world disappears. Your Invisible Anonymous Presence is Ultimate Truth, through which the entire world is projected. We know the basics intellectually, but to absorb this knowledge, to digest this knowledge, to digest this Reality, you must be courageous and patient. Meditate, contemplate, take it seriously.

Time after time, what happens is that seekers come here and take the Mantra, but afterwards, they do not do any meditation practice. They don’t want to put in any effort. Instead, they expect some changes to take place miraculously. And then, they complain: “Oh, Maharaj, I am not finding that happiness you spoke of, or that blissfulness. There’s too many thoughts going round and round. It is hopeless. I’m depressed”. This casual approach does not work. These spiritual travellers are just roaming here and there, looking for one spiritual massage, after another.

How many times do I have to tell you, that without your involvement, without your earnestness, devotion, sincerity and longing to find out who you are, you will remain in the illusory ditch, crying over a dream. This process is not a one-way process, where the Master does the work for you. No! It will not happen like that. The Master is giving you a hundred percent guarantee, but only if you fulfil your side of the bargain, and devote some time for Selfless Self.

If you are sincere with your meditation, ultimately, all the concepts will be cleared out, and in its place, there will be exceptional happiness and peace. But this will not happen without your effort. Meditation is a must. It is indispensable because there is no other way, of absorbing the knowledge, and removing illusion.

In the beginning, as you recite the Mantra, you may experience a degree of monotony, laziness, and reluctance. Ignore these feelings! Don’t neglect your meditation because this human body, has presented you with a golden opportunity, to wake up from this long dream, and to ensure that it is your last dream. The Master says, “this is your last dream”, therefore, it is your last dream!

While attending to your responsibilities, and caring for your family, you have a duty to find out the secrets of this world. The secret of this world is that it is an illusory world. The entire world is an illusory world. The entire “seen”, is reflected out of the Seer that is Ultimate Truth. This means you are Final Truth, for whom there is no birth and death, no past, no future, no deed, no doer, no experience, no experiencer, no witness, etc. The open secret is that, except for your Selfless Self, there is nothing. Everything is within you. Nothing is outside of you. But to absorb this knowledge, and to live like the Master that you are, you must undergo disciplined meditation, and continuous hammering.

In the beginning, when we give the Soham Sadhana, make an effort to recite the Mantra non-stop. This is commando training, with a no-nonsense strategy. It is a no-nonsense assault on your illusory body-based knowledge. Be serious! There is work to be done, to remove the layers of illusion that have accumulated over a lifetime.

Beware of any tricks played by the mind, say, any unpleasant thoughts and feelings. Don’t be dissuaded! Tell yourself, “I have to do this practice. I have to continue. If I don’t, I will never be free!” And then, after continuous hammering and churning, it will become easier.

You are like a big computer that has caught some kind of virus. That virus has to be removed, so that the computer can be restored to its pristine state. Meditation is the base, the foundation, the antivirus software that will solve this problem. When you install this programme, then all the files that have been corrupted by the virus will be erased. The virus is body-based knowledge. Layers of this knowledge are obscuring your Pure Presence, your Invaluable Presence.

This practical approach of reciting the Naam Mantra, will clear the battlefield of conflicting problems, that you think you have, with illusory others, and imaginary enemies. It will bring your struggles to an end. You will realize the fact that there are no others, and there are no enemies. And there is no battlefield! You are like the space or the sky. All your battles have been imaginary ones. You have not been fighting with anyone. You have been fighting with the air.

Again, I must emphasize, that meditation is the only effective medium for Conviction. Theoretical knowledge will not do, neither will visiting Masters. Why not? Because the Master is within you. You must know yourself directly, and not indirectly, through words. Don’t depend on the Master. The Master will guide you, but he will guide you, to stand on your own two feet. You are wholly independent.

How long are you going to remain a disciple for? Nisargadatta said: “I am not making you disciples, but Masters”. So, even if you visited a thousand Masters, it would still not help you because the Master is within you, and you are ignoring your Inner Master. You are still under some delusion that the Master is separate from you. There is no separation. This misunderstanding comes from your identification with the body-form.

You are formless. You are within every being. The bodies are different, but the Spirit is one. Meditation is the medium through which you can identify yourself in a real sense. It is not possible otherwise.

Your approach is casual because you have no loyalty. Your wavering mind is taking you on a joyride. “Stop Following the Mind. Let the Mind Follow You.” Be loyal to yourself, and be loyal to your Master. If you don’t have faith in any Master, then place your faith in a material idol, or in a symbol made of stone, or whatever. Somehow, and somewhere in you, there has to be loyalty – faith in yourself. You must have faith in something, a focus, trust in something, like in the famous story of Eklavya from the Mahabharata.

The Master Dronacharya, refused to teach Eklavya the art of archery. But Eklavya did not give up. With the help of his Inner Master, he learned, and perfected the art of archery. Dronacharya was astounded by his skill, and immediately, accepted Eklavya as his Master. This illustrates the strength of the Inner Master. Your Inner Master is the central focus, the central point of the universe. Discover your Inner Master, and let your Inner Master teach you.

The Outer Master is there to indicate your Identity. However, in practice, the Inner Master and the Outer Master. are just concepts. Don’t ignore your Inner Master! The Listener within your body is the Inner Master. That is Ultimate Truth. Therefore, have faith in yourself, and stay loyal to your own Master.

Here, I am sharing practical knowledge. You have a lot of theoretical knowledge, but it is only practical knowledge, that will lead to Conviction. Be determined, and self-disciplined! Every moment in your life is very precious. Don’t waste your time. Life is very short. Continuous hammering, continuous churning, continuous concentration are all needed.
You don’t need to spend a single penny for any of this. The only cost is your time, and your devotion, in the pursuit of your Reality.

You have it easy these days. I am asking for one, two or three hours maximum. In Bhausaheb Maharaj’s day, he used to sit in meditation for ten or twelve hours! Nowadays, this is no longer possible. Surely, you can still find two hours or so! And at other times, throughout the day, you can recite the Mantra, too. I have already given you a guarantee, but you need to do the practice. Then, everything in your life will change. Any negative feelings of depression and anxiety, will vanish after Spontaneous Conviction. So, don’t worry about anything, and continue the meditation with firm determination.

Be serious! Concentrate seriously on your true nature, your Ultimate Reality. Try to understand the secret of this world, and the secret of your Selfless Self. In short, be realized, and make others realized! Be enlightened, and make others enlightened. When you stop counting yourself as the body-form, enlightenment is not very far from you.

If there is sickness in the body, you will find the courage to cope with it. Spirituality teaches you, how not to be touched, by illness or disease. When you know that you are not the body, you are no longer so concerned about your physical illnesses, because you know that you have nothing to do with the body. Prepare yourself for the time when your body expires. Make it fearless and beautiful! When you know that there is no death for you, that you are immortal, and that knowledge has been absorbed in you, your last moments will be full of peace.

Suppose that, today your body was going to expire. Your attitude would be something like this, “Okay then, forget it! You will accept your fate because you know that the body-form is not your identity at all. The body may come, and the body may go. You are not bothered. You are not interested, because you know, that you are not going anywhere. Your Presence is just like the sky.

Always take maximum care of your body, and seek medical help when it is needed. This body is a five-elemental body. It is always out of balance. Anything can go wrong with it, at any time. Look after it! However, if the body is no longer co-operating, then, it is time to say bye bye!

Not so long ago, I met a lady. She was not a spiritual lady, but she wanted to tell me about a decision she had made. She said, “I wrote in my will, that if it so happened, that I needed to be kept alive with a ventilator, it was to be removed at once. I do not want to live with a ventilator”. She showed such courage!

Sometimes, it becomes evident, that the time has come to close the curtains. You have seen, what you were supposed to see, in the world. What more do you want to see? It is just more of the same thing, again and again. If the body is not co-operating, why choose to continue with life artificially, depending on machines to keep you alive? Forget it!

You will see this kind of courage manifesting. Understand that this is not suicide! If something happens to the body, that is beyond your control, “Fine!” That lady expressed her wishes to the doctor. It happens, and when it does, it demonstrates bravery and integrity. We are all going to leave the body at some point. Start preparing, now! Be strong, and have courage.

p.strong: 99. How do I practise the Mantra, and how does it work?

section.p: Reciting the Mantra is a proven, scientific method. The Mantra creates some vibrations. Breathe in as you recite the first part of the Mantra, then breathe out, as you recite the second part of it. With your eyes half-closed, focus on the tip of your nose. Concentrate on the Mantra, for around three to six months. Then, the reciting will continue by itself, spontaneously. It is like when you first learn the alphabet, and you are writing the letters, a, b, c. But then, after a time, this exercise is no longer needed, because you have mastered the language. But, in the beginning, you need to act accordingly, until the knowledge is established, until it is formed. The Mantra’s vibrations address your Selfless Self.

Everything you have ever heard, and listened to, has been recorded. It has left a trail of impressions. You are like a computer that has created some wrong files. When we entered this body-form, you accepted it. You blindly signed on the dotted line, “I am the body”, even though it was not your identity. And after that, you accepted all the other concepts that were engraved in you, by parents, teachers, church, employers, etc. We recite the Mantra in order to undo everything that we have accepted as our identity.

Basically, we are formless nobodies, but the body-form creates the illusion that we are somebodies. Your body-form may be very handsome now, as a young man, but when you reach old age, all your pretty looks will be gone. What is Reality? Whose Reality? Your Inner Spirit is neither young, nor old.

If you sit alone for five minutes, you will quickly start to feel bored. And so, you will read a newspaper, watch a movie, or turn on the TV, and choose from a long list of entertainment programmes on offer. Presence likes to remain engaged. It doesn’t enjoy boredom, and so, needs a lot of distractions. All our needs are for the body-form. We behave like children who quickly tire of doing the same thing again and again, and who are always complaining: “Oh! I am bored. I don’t want to do this anymore”, etc. You have to keep your child busy doing various things. In a similar way, Presence likes to engage through the body. If it remains idle, disturbing concepts may start to flow.

You will have spiritual courage after identifying yourself. If you fall ill, you will know that whatever illness you have is the body’s illness, and not yours. We have some devotees who are seriously suffering from cancer, of one form or another. But, they are happy because they don’t accept it, or buy into it.

They know that nothing happens to Presence. Nothing can touch Presence. Whatever happens, happens to the body. They are not that. When you know the Reality, you become the Master of the mind. This means that the wrong concepts will not enter your mind.

Suppose someone says that you are a very bad person. You will just let this comment wash over you, because you know that “bad” and “good” are concepts. Nisargadatta Maharaj said, if someone told me, “you are a very, good saint”, it would not give me any pleasure. If he said, “you are a very, bad saint”, it would not depress me. When Reality is established within you, whatever is thrown at you, will not touch you. The world cannot touch you. You will continue to live as before, but without any ego, and without a sense of “I am doing this, and that”.
After absorption, knowledge will start to flow. You will have the spontaneous feeling that you are everywhere in the world, and in every being.

You used to consider yourself in the world. After Realization, you will see that the world is within you. The body, and the entire world, are projected from you. You are not concerned with the body, and not concerned with the world.

p.strong: 100. So, we recite the Mantra “I am Brahman, Brahman I am”?

section.p: The meaning of the Mantra is “I am Brahman. Brahman I am”. It is the Final Truth that you are, which cannot be described, or seen, by the naked eye. We can be aware of it, and sense it.

You may feel a little depressed, or nervous when you start reciting the Mantra. Dismiss these waves! It is a good thing. It means that the cleaning process is in operation. Suppose your house was full of dust. When you start to wipe the dust away, you may smell an odour, but only for a brief moment. The smell goes after the dust has been removed.

During this cleansing process, the smell takes the form of disturbing feelings, or thoughts. Put up with it! It is a small price to pay for the removal of concepts. And then, life, will live life, perfectly! When you know that you are not the body-form, life will unfold beautifully with abundant happiness.

p.strong: 101. Do I recite the Mantra aloud, or silently in my mind?

section.p: Recite the Mantra in your mind only. Meditation is done through the mind only. Recite the Mantra, along with your breathing, to make it more effective in the beginning. The Mantra creates exceptional vibrations, which make you forget your body-identity. It will help you to identify yourself, and live a happy life, with the knowledge that you are Ultimate Truth. And then you will talk spontaneously, just like I am talking now. Your Self-knowledge will begin to open up.

You are young at the moment. But after some years, you will become old like me! Our bodies grow old, but our Spirit is changeless, ageless. You can be young or old, but the Spirit within that form is neither of these. Reality is there, you just have to grasp it. Be serious. I am talking to everybody. Don’t take it lightly, because this is an illusory world. Whatever you plan, is, in fact, meaningless. Unless you identify yourself in a real sense, you cannot live happily. Without proper identification, your life will be meaningless.

p.strong: 102. When I recite the Mantra, do I have to pay attention to the meaning of the Mantra?

section.p: It is not necessary. Just do it. You know the meaning, so you need not concentrate on it. The Mantra acts spontaneously.

When you take a drop of poison, do you start thinking about what is going to happen? No! The result is death. When you take this drop of nectar, the result is immortality. So, there is no need to concentrate on the meaning of the Mantra. The Mantra will act spontaneously within you. The vibrations will take effect. And, you will identify yourself in a real sense.

This is direct knowledge. “You are the Principle. You are the Central Point. Your Spontaneous Identity is the Central Point of this world.” All that you think, consider, and see, is the Seer’s reflection. As soon as consciousness arose, you immediately started thinking. That ego, intellect and mind started influencing you, and created endless thoughts, which made you accept this world as true. This world is not true, in the same way that the world of dreams is not true.

Prior to beingness, nothing was there. There was no dream world, and no illusory world. Your Presence is behind everything. You are the Central Point. If there is no Seer in this body, there is no one to think about any of these things?

Get some practical knowledge! Do some practice! It is most important, and desperately needed. So don’t waste your time! Try to identify yourself. My Master, Nisargadatta Maharaj, says: “Except for your Selfless Self there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman. What more do you want?
