It is only because you consider yourself to be the body-form, that many, many questions keep arising. There are no questions. All the questions are illusory! The Questioner does not know that, “I am the Questioner”. But you have imposed some form on the Questioner, such as a man or a woman. Suppose that just for a moment, there was no Presence, what will happen to your questions? Where is the dream? Is the dream there? Is this illusory world there?
If there were no Presence in your body-form just for a moment, you would be certified dead, and your body taken away to be burned or buried. You follow? Any questions?

p.strong: 103. Does meditation mean repeating the Naam Mantra, or sitting in one place and meditating?

section.p: I will instruct you. You are to repeat the Naam Mantra, and recite it all the time. When you are sitting for meditation, sit in a lotus position, with a straight back. If that is not comfortable for you, then sit on a chair. If you try to stay in an uncomfortable position that is putting a strain on your body, it will distract you from your meditation. Be relaxed!

In the beginning, you are to allocate a minimum of two hours for meditation. This period is to be given to complete concentration, with your full involvement. During this meditation practice, sit in one place, and do nothing but meditation. You can either meditate for two hours continuously, or for two one-hour slots, or even four half-hour slots. Find out what suits you best, and what fits into your daily routine.

Throughout the day, repeat the Naam Mantra as often as possible. Get into the habit of chanting it, remembering to make it part of your daily routine. You can recite it while you are carrying out any activity, working, shopping, cooking, walking, bathing. Recite the Mantra, at every available opportunity, and then it will become for you, as natural as breathing. Do it, and do it sincerely! In time, the Mantra will recite by itself spontaneously. It will be humming in the background 24/7. You will go to sleep with the Naam, and wake up with the Naam. But, to begin with, you must make a determined effort to recite it.

You are to meditate until you recover from your amnesia, and regain your memory. This means, until you have Conviction, until the knowledge that you are Ultimate Truth has been absorbed. Many seekers say, “Oh, I am realized, I have the knowledge!” Literal knowledge, and bookish knowledge, only offer you temporary relief, not permanent.
You may have different experiences during meditation. You may feel that you have lost your body-based-knowledge.

This is a good experience, but it, too, needs to be dissolved because whatever spiritual experiences occur, are also only layers on your Presence. It is an appearance upon your Presence. And, at the Ultimate stage, there is no experiencer or experience.

All experiences are body-related, and therefore, illusion, including those experiences of “I am Brahman”. There were no experiences prior to beingness. There will not be any experiences after you leave the body. Therefore, whatever experiences you may have, and however blissful they may be, keep going beyond them, keep going deeper and deeper, until both the experiencer and the experiences have dissolved. That is the Ultimate Stage.

p.strong: 104. I have been reciting the Mantra for a few months. Should I continue?

section.p: Of course, you must continue! You have been reciting your concepts for years! Continue reciting the Mantra. You need to be more patient. You have had a lifetime of living with concepts, so, it will take a little time to remove everything which you have accepted as reality. Also, it takes some time to absorb the knowledge. I have told you, that you need to put in a determined effort in the beginning. You say you have been reciting the Mantra for a few months now, and you already want to stop. Who is counting the months? Who is counting? You have this amazing opportunity to find out who you are, and it is like, you cannot even be bothered! I am only asking that you devote two hours per day for yourself.

You must be serious about the practice. Recite the Mantra for as long as it is needed. This will vary according to one’s involvement. Your casual approach is not helping you. So, don’t be half-hearted! The rewards that will come your way are unimaginable. Therefore, be strong, and with renewed vigour and determination, continue reciting the Mantra!

The original consciousness will create an atmosphere, after a period of reciting the Mantra. This atmosphere is stateless, thoughtless. It is your natural state, the way you were prior to beingness, without any qualities. When this happens, your bodily identity will be completely forgotten.

When the Spirit clicked with the body, you experienced “I”. The consciousness came across with the mind, ego, and intellect, and then the body started functioning. Understand this process! Prior to beingness, there was no experiencer and no experiences. There was nothing. When the beingness took form, and the consciousness touched with the mind, ego and intellect, everything started functioning. And, instantly, you accepted your bodily identity, and adopted the pose of a human being.

The mind manufactures thousands of thoughts per minute. And we accepted all these thoughts, because we did not know ourselves. But now, you are approaching the Ultimate Stage. The veils are being removed slowly, silently and permanently. So, carry on reciting the Mantra. Don’t be impatient. It will take some time to undo the years of conditioning. I am giving you 100% guarantee that if you follow my instructions, a peaceful, happy, fearless, tension-free life is already yours.

p.strong: 105. Nisargadatta Maharaj said: “the purpose of the Mantra is to surrender the seen to Brahman”. How is this done?

section.p: Yes! Because when the Spirit touched with the body, the concept of “I am somebody” emerged. The consciousness accepted “I am somebody”. And, so, Nisargadatta Maharaj said that the Mantra acts in a way that will remedy this mistaken notion.

The Mantra, “I am Brahman. Brahman I am”, is meant as the Final Truth. The Mantra is hammering you with your Reality spontaneously. You forgot about your Identity, therefore, the Invisible Meditator within you, reminds you: “I am Brahman”. Brahman is the Ultimate Truth, your Final Truth.

All the saints say exactly the same thing: “Try to identify yourself”. You are identifying yourself in the body-form, and that is illusion! Therefore, concentrate on the Concentrator within you, until the Concentrator disappears!

Surrender the seen! You are not the body! Surrender your body-based-knowledge! Identify your Selfless Self. How were you prior to consciousness? How will you be after leaving consciousness? Through this consciousness, you are observing this world, watching this world. Is existence true or false? Identify that! Identify!

p.strong: 106. What is the minimum time for meditation?

section.p: There is no hard and fast rule. It all depends on the extent to which you can devote your time. Sometimes people meditate for one, two, three, or four hours, and others, for only ten minutes. In the beginning, give the maximum time possible to meditation. Whatever time you can afford, make sure that you are able to give your full concentration to reciting the Mantra. Concentration is most important, so that you can hammer yourself relentlessly with, “I am Ultimate Truth. Ultimate Truth I am”. We need to recite the Mantra, for as long as we are under the illusion that we are the body-form.

Spiritual knowledge is needed to correct wrong knowledge. Spiritual knowledge is needed, so that we can identity ourselves. Spirituality is needed to dissolve this illusory identity. Once it has served its purpose, it has no further use.

You have forgotten your Original Identity. Your Identity is vast, like the space or the sky, and it is way, way beyond that. But we have reduced ourselves to the status of the human, body-form, to mere, limited mortals. Be serious! Try to find the answer to, “Who am I?” You should be deeply involved in your quest. It should be earnest, passionate, a matter of life or death. Why? Because it is such a waste of a life, to take the dream for reality, and to sleepwalk through life, never knowing the secret of your existence. Make it a priority to know yourself. Follow the Mantra at every available moment. We have to keep on hammering ourselves, to rid us forever of all that is false.

Knowledge basically means identifying yourself, or knowing yourself in a real sense. When you convince the meditator that you are Ultimate Truth, you are dissolving the strongly-held, fixed illusion, that you are separate from Ultimate Truth. Body-based knowledge disturbs and confuses us all, therefore it is to be completely discarded and ignored. Meditation, concentration, and reading books are all theoretical knowledge. This theoretical knowledge then becomes practical knowledge.

What is practical knowledge? Practical knowledge means living as Brahman. You live without concern for the body, because you know that you are formless. You no longer have any cares because you know that you are unborn, and that there was nothing prior to beingness, nor will there be, after beingness. Therefore, when the Spontaneous Conviction arises, your foundations will be rock solid. You will live spontaneously, and nothing will have any power whatsoever, to touch you, or unsettle you.

Before meeting the Master, and pre-initiation, we were under the enormous pressure of thousands of concepts. With all these concepts that were tightly wrapped around us, we were struggling to live peacefully and happily. It was an impossibility! Be enthusiastic and eager to remove the concepts that have crippled you! Don’t ask what is the minimum time! Give meditation your maximum time. You are showing a lack of interest and involvement.

p.strong: 107. Will meditation make me spiritually strong?

section.p: It depends on how serious you are about it, and how involved you are. Meditation is the best foundation, the only foundation. Spiritual discussion will not help you. We could go on talking for hours together, but this exercise would be a meaningless exercise!

I am trying to make you stand on your own feet. You are independent. There is no need for you to depend on anyone else. You can do it! All you need is the courage to accept Reality. I am encouraging you. Encourage yourself! Build some extraordinary confidence in yourself! It is already there. Tell yourself: “Nothing is impossible, I can do it!”

All these saintly people have the same Spirit that you have. The Spirit within this body, and the Spirit in all these other bodies, is not different. But you have doubts, and are always underestimating yourself, wondering, “How can it happen?” All that the Master says, is it really true?”

We depend upon others. We depend upon some spiritual power, some godly power. This is illusion. It is coming from your insecurities and fearfulness. The fact is that you are completely independent. You are free from all burdens. There are no burdens. You are worrying about what is going to happen. Nothing has happened. Nothing is happening, and nothing is going to happen. Be strong! Meditation shows you your inner strength, and then, you will start roaring like a lion!

When you wake up from a dream, it is very quickly forgotten. You can have many problems in dreams, all sorts of worrying problems that cause you stress. For example, say in a dream, your business lost lakhs of rupees. And then, when you wake up, you realize that you did not suffer any loss. It was just a dream! Nothing happened after all. It is the same in this long dream of life, nothing has ever happened. Mould yourself in this way!

p.strong: 108. Does meditation mean that I have to sit for four hours?

section.p: See, there are no hard and fast rules. If you meditate for four hours, the effect will be greater. If you have sufficient time, without neglecting your duties, you can spend half an hour, one, two, three or four hours, etc, it depends on your circumstances. Don’t neglect your family responsibilities: “Oh sorry father! I have no time. I cannot take care of you because I have to go and meditate”. This is not real. This is not pragmatic spirituality. Whatever time you spend, do it willingly, earnestly, and not with any ill-will.

It is very simple. Everything is within you. Just have some faith, and try to identify yourself. You don’t have to spend any money. You don’t have to, go here and there. There should not be a dual mind: “Oh, I will go to this Master, then that Master”. I am not criticizing anyone, but nowadays, there are thousands of Masters, saying, “do this, do that!”, extracting money from the vulnerable, naive, undiscerning seekers. Be aware! Everything starts from you, and everything ends with you. You are the principle of spirituality! You are the principle of this world. Be loyal, and stay loyal, in this way: “Yes, I have that perfection. I can do it! Ultimate Truth is not separate from me!”

p.strong: 109. Some days I cannot even recite the Mantra for half an hour. What then?

section.p: Forget about how long you can meditate for! Don’t count the length of time. Sitting down to meditate is not compulsory. “You Are to Concentrate on the Invisible Concentrator Within You.” You Are to Concentrate on the Invisible Concentrator, “I Am Brahman”, hammering yourself at all times. After you have reached the Ultimate Truth, the meditation happens spontaneously, 24/7, even in deep sleep.

You are a man named Ravi. You do not have to remind yourself of this while you are asleep, that, “I am Ravi”? That name “Ravi” is one with your Spirit. Likewise, the Master says your name is Brahman. Brahman means Ultimate Truth, Final Truth. So, though you are holding the body, you are still Ultimate Truth. You remain as that “State”. Now the ball is in your court. The way you handle it is up to you. You are there, and there alone, whatever the circumstances that are maybe going on around you.

p.strong: 110. Do I just close my eyes for a few minutes?

section.p: That’s okay. It’s very good. Meditation is most important. Give it your full concentration. Early morning meditation, say at 4am or 5am is an excellent time. Close your eyes, and sit calmly, and quietly. Take a look! Observe! See what’s there within you. You will experience the vibrations, and some feelings of tremendous happiness, and peacefulness. That happiness and peacefulness are not separate from you. The happiness that is in you, is your nature. The peacefulness that is in you, is your nature.

Meditation will clear out all the body-based garbage. When everything has been removed, emptiness will remain. And that emptiness will be filled with happiness. Happiness and peace will flow, gushing out, and overflowing. There is no end to this outpouring, as it is coming from Source, from the bottomless bottom of Reality, the eternal fount of Reality.
This pure joy, this expansion that feels like it is not able to contain the ecstasy, cannot be imagined. It is incomparable, unparalleled, priceless! This will happen, spontaneously, with meditation.

p.strong: 111. When I recite the Mantra, should I concentrate on the words, the sound, the breath, the Master, or just Selfless Self?

section.p: Try to concentrate on Selfless Self! As you know, the Master is formless, but in the beginning, the mind will not easily let you concentrate directly on Selfless Self. If this is the case, when you see the Master in the body-form, you can concentrate on the Master. See the Master in the body-form, and then you will feel some vibrations and rays inside. As a matter of fact, there is no difference between the Master and yourself, but in spite of this, the mind stubbornly refuses to accept this Reality. To encourage the mind to accept this Reality, you have to concentrate on the Master, or on Selfless Self. Concentration on the Master as the form, will help you in the beginning. It will be easier for you to concentrate in this way.

There is no difference between the Master and the disciple. There may appear to be different forms, but there is only One Spirit in all these forms. In all the bodies, Spirit is One, just like within all the buildings, space is One. Similarly, there are lakhs of beings. Every being has the same Spirit called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God or Master. But, directly or indirectly, we consider ourselves as the body-form. Concentration is needed to loosen all our attachments.

We have an egoistic “I”. Dissolve that egoistic “I”! “I” is to be a humble “I”! Again, don’t take these words literally! What is needed is really very simple. Try to identify yourself. This “I” does not know that it is an independent identity. It is vast, it is everywhere – omnipresent.

The Master is not separate from you. Stop playing with the words, “Master, God, Ultimate Truth, maya, Brahman”. Through narration and explanations, the Master is trying to convince, the Invisible Listener within you of your Ultimate Truth. There is only one principle: “Selfless Self is the Ultimate Truth That You Are”.

Why do you need the Naam Mantra and the Master? Because you have forgotten your Identity, and consequently, treat this dream as true. Each and every day, there is a different dream. Dreams are not true. This is a long dream! You need to meditate because you are treating this dream as reality. Meditation is the only medium, through which you can discard all the illusory concepts.

p.strong: 112. While we are going through this meditation process, can we read books?

section.p: Yes, you can read books, as long as you don’t analyze the words. You can read books, and approach the Masters, but bear in mind, that it is what they wish to convey that is of paramount importance. Don’t depend on the words! You are not supposed to analyze the Master’s words or sentences: “Why did Ramana Maharshi say this? What did that Master really mean when he said…?” No! This is not the way! What are they trying to convey from their statements and expressions? What is the gist, or the central point of your Reality? It is: “Except your Selfless Self there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman”. Meditation is needed to enable you to accept this Reality.

The strong force of your mind, ego and intellect is upon you. Directly, or indirectly, you have become a victim of your own mind and concepts. The subtle ego is warring with your mind. When you go to a friend’s house, the subtle ego has expectations: “Oh, she has not offered me any tea? She should offer me something”. If your expectations are not fulfilled, you feel depressed. All your expectations must dissolve.

You have expectations from your children. You have expectations, about how to be a good father and parent. You have expectations from the Masters, too. You want a miracle or two: “Oh, if I go to that Master, something miraculous will happen”. Nothing will happen. The Master does not perform miracles. You are the miracle worker, who makes miracles happen! This body is a miracle box, a magic box, that contains everything. But you are unaware of your box of tricks. The Spontaneous Existence in your body is the Ultimate Truth, the Final Truth.

p.strong: 113. Can you say something about devotion?

section.p: Yes, it is a very good question. Devotion means that you are a devotee. [“seeker” is synonymous with “devotee”] If you are a devotee, your devotion has already begun.
You are a Deity, that manifested as a devotee. Through devotion, you will find out that you are, in fact, a Deity. “Devotee, Devotion and Deity are intertwined”. These are just words!

At the initial stage, you are a devotee. At the last stage, you are a Deity. Devotee and Deity, Devotee and Deity, there is no separation. “There is No Separation Between the Devotee and the Deity. The Deity Knows Through the Devotee. The Deity Lies Within the Devotee.”

You are a devotee. You are a Deity. You are everything. Basically, you are not the body. “Devotee” and “Deity”, are body-related concepts. “Master” and “disciple” are also concepts that came along with the body. There were no concepts of “devotee”, “deity” and “devotion” prior to beingness.

You need to go through the preliminary stages until Spontaneous Conviction arises. I am, therefore, insisting on the Naam Mantra. Thereafter, no further practice will be necessary: no meditation or devotion because you are already a Deity. This means that “You Are Ultimate Truth, Final Truth. That You Are! Be With You all the Time”.

p.strong: 114. Should we keep the sadhana going till our last breath, or only until Spontaneous Conviction is reached?

section.p: You can do your sadhana anywhere in the world. Sometimes it is not possible to come to India due to physical weakness, mental or emotional problems, age, financial limitations, or other problems. You don’t have to come to this ashram all the time. Wherever you are, make yourself believe that except for Selfless Self, there is nothing. Convince yourself! Accept all that you are listening to, and reading. Sadhana is needed for the Conviction of your Reality.

You are considering yourself in the human form, and as you know, that is not your identity at all. Sadhana is essential to remind yourself, to hammer yourself, and demolish the ingrained concept of being a man or a woman. You are Ultimate Truth. “I am Brahman, Brahman I am”. These words serve to make you forget about your body-identity.

Even if you understand that the body is not your identity at all, one day you will leave the body. And, when that day dawns, you must be thoroughly prepared. Die to your body, before the body expires. This way, you will ensure that there will not be any more dreams. This is the final terminus.

Again and again, I am placing before you the same thing. Sadhana is necessary because it is difficult to accept the Reality. The Master is required, the Mantra is required, unless of course, you have full confidence, strong confidence, just like in the case of Eklavya. Here, nothing else was needed. But if you have full confidence, strong confidence, nothing is required, just like in the case of Eklavya. In the absence of an external Master, he invited his Inner Master. His skill and achievements were beyond any, that a Master could ever have taught him!

This can happen. Your Inner Master is a very strong Master. Don’t neglect your Inner Master. Try to invite the attention of the Inner Master. Do your sadhana and concentrate on the Invisible Concentrator, that you are Brahman. When you hammer yourself with the Mantra, the body-form consciousness, turns towards Ultimate Truth.

You are formless and changeless. The changes you appear to see are illusory. You have to mould yourself, and put in some effort. The questioner is within you, so, question yourself calmly and quietly. The answers will come from within. Make your Inner Master dialogue with you. This seems like duality, however, it is not. It is only an apparent duality.
The Master is not separate from you. You are wasting your time if you approach more than one Master, while neglecting your own Master. You are underestimating yourself. You are not valuing yourself.
When you ask questions, make them practical questions. Avoid asking theoretical, or confused questions, which have no value, and will be of no help to you.

Sadhana, meditation, etc, are all illusion. Throw out your knowledge as well! After reaching the destination, you no longer need the address, the directions which you followed. Everything can be thrown out when the Knowledge and you, are One.

p.strong: 115. Would you say, that meditation is letting go of our attachment to thoughts?

section.p: Yes, of course! You are letting go of your attachment to everything. Through meditation, you are hammering yourself. These sacred words have tremendous power. The Spirit or Presence is highly sensitive, and therefore, it will absorb and accept the hammering. It accepts the Truth: “Yes! I Am That!”, without your knowledge. That knowledge is already within you, but you are unaware of it.

Reality is already within you, but directly or indirectly you are posing as a male or female, and expecting to have this knowledge, this Reality, with your body-based-knowledge. You are the architect of your own life. What to do, or not to do, is up to you. You need not go anywhere to find a Master, because there is no Master except for your Selfless Self.

p.strong: 116. What do you think of other kinds of meditation, like Buddhist meditation?

section.p: When did you come across Buddhist meditation? All these concepts came after, with the body-form. If your Presence were not there, would you know of any Buddha? And, will you know of any Buddha, Brahma, or Shiva when the body expires? You say, “This is Shiva, this is Brahma, and this is Vishnu”. When did you come across these? All these concepts began when the Spirit clicked with the body. “Brahman, Atman, Dattatreya, this thing, that thing. This is Krishna, and that is...”, and so on, and so forth.
You did not know anything prior to beingness, and you will not know anything after beingness. You are to come out of the illusory circle of concepts. Come out of it completely.

You have read so many books. You have so much bookish knowledge. Is it going to help you, when it is your time to leave the body? You know the Vedas, you may even be a Master of the Vedas, and/or a Master of many philosophical and spiritual books. What use is that kind of Master? How will that knowledge help you when it is your time to exit the body? Question yourself! All this knowledge indicates Truth. It points to the Truth, but it is not the Truth because the Truth can only be found in You!

We have the Vedas, Buddhas and Buddhists texts, etc. We also have many different spiritual traditions, and many, many spiritual concepts. These have all been created as signposts, attempts to: “Identify the Invisible Listener Within You”. I am not criticizing any of it, but, this man-made knowledge is not your Reality. It is not an end in itself. This kind of knowledge, is only a means through which, you can try to identify, The Invisible Reader Within You, That You Are Ultimate Truth. So, recognize that all this knowledge is body-based knowledge. Try and identify yourself!

p.strong: 117. What does concentrate on the Invisible Concentrator mean?

section.p: We always say: “Concentrate on the Invisible Concentrator within you”. The Invisible Concentrator can be felt as “I Am”, but you cannot see how it is, but it is a feeling that has been called glimpses of “I”, which is subtler than the space or sky. However, there is no comparison to the space or sky because the sky is visible to us. But, the Invisible Experiencer within this body is not visible.

You feel it, but at the same time, because you are caught up in body-based-knowledge, you are lacking in Conviction. We know that we are Brahman, but still, the Conviction is not there. To have that Conviction, or, to make that Conviction appear spontaneously, you are to concentrate on the Concentrator within you.

The concentration is a deliberate act. After some intense practice, and reaching a certain level, that concentration and the concentrator, will disappear. Nothing will remain! This is a trance-like state, or samadhi, Afterwards, Reality will open within you. And you will be filled with incredible, spontaneous peacefulness, and spontaneous happiness.

p.strong: 118. When I recite the Naam Mantra, do I need to fight the illusory world?

section.p: Why fight it? Ignore it! Your inner power will fight the external forces. You have inbuilt power and energy, which you are not aware of. If anything tries to attack you, or destabilise you, you will be protected by that inbuilt power. This will happen spontaneously, without your knowledge. You will be given direct guidance: “don’t go there, and don’t do that”. This stage is called the last devotion, Atma Nivedanam Bhakti.

At this ultimate stage, make a habit of having a dialogue with Selfless Self. This is Self-dialogue, Self-talking. It is really miraculous. You will receive knowledge that is beyond your imagination. When that Self-dialogue starts, knowledge will flow within you. There will be total transparency. Knowledge means just to identify oneself in a real sense. So, some deliberate efforts are required in the beginning.

Calamities, different circumstances, and unpleasant atmospheres will come your way. When they do, don’t give them too much importance. These are temporary storms. Yes, storms will come and go, but you are not going anywhere. You are there, and there only. Be firm, “Let the storms come!” Nothing will happen to you, because you are totally firm, solid, and established in Ultimate Reality. Don’t become a victim of your mind, ego, intellect, or of anybody’s concepts.

p.strong: 4 THE PROGNOSIS

p.emphasis: “After Conviction, you will be able to face any challenge that comes your way. There will be exceptional happiness and peace: a subtle, subtle light. You can’t describe it in words, but it is an exceptional experience, beyond your wildest imagination.”

p.strong: 119. What are the results of meditation?

section.p: You will not get immediate results. I received the Naam Mantra on the 31st October 1962. In the beginning, it was not possible for me to understand what Maharaj was saying. Each evening, I used to go to Maharaj’s house, to listen to his lectures. At that time, I had very little understanding. It felt as if it was really beyond my capacity.

But Maharaj used to say, “Listen to me! Listen to me!” What he meant by this, was that the Invisible Listener was listening to his Talk. And, indirectly, everything was being engraved. Intellectually, you may not be able to understand the teaching, but the “Invisible Presence is Engraving That Reality Permanently”.
You will not see quick results, so you need to be patient. You may feel frustrated, “I can’t understand. This is way beyond my capabilities!” But your Presence is absorbing the Master’s words.

My Master used to encourage us, by giving us some very useful tips. He would say: “What exactly is in the body? Nothing but glimpses of ‘I’.” After a number of years, everything opened up, and flowered. How did this happen? Because after strong meditation, deep involvement, and faith in the Master, your Inner Master opens up the Secret of Spirituality.

The Inner Master is not separate from the Outer Master. “Inner Master” and “Outer Master” are only concepts. That Power that is within you, and behind everything, underlying everything, suddenly opens! This happened to me. It can also happen to you. My Master also used to say: “Listen, Your Presence Is Everywhere. Every being has Presence”. And then, it happened, spontaneously inside. What my Master said was happening. I started to see myself in every being. It was such a wonderful surprise!

At that time, I sometimes had very, very bad thoughts. I felt guilty, and I also felt some hatred and jealousy. I had some terrible dreams, too. All this made me feel very confused. Thereafter, after some time passed, there was a change, a turning, an opening. “Why should I feel guilty?” I asked myself. “This is the nature of the mind, the flow of thoughts. I am not going to give these thoughts any attention. I refuse to accept any more thoughts. And now I know that I am not guilty of anything”.

When Reality begins to open, it is like there is an inner voice, that is explaining everything so clearly. You feel that you are drawing closer and closer. Any sense of separation, that might have been there before, dissolves. “The Merging Process is Marching Towards Oneness”. “You Become One With That.”

Everything will start to flow. You will receive spontaneous instructions, as well as answers to all your questions. It is very funny, a miracle really, that there is a dialogue taking place within you. This happened to me. I had some unresolved questions, so I placed them before my Inner Master, and miraculously, and to my great amusement, they were answered immediately.

The physical Master does not exist. It is your Inner Master, your inner Power that is answering the questions. You will be absolutely amazed when this happens, wondering: “How is it all possible?” These answers appear instantly because your Inner Master is pleased with you. That illusory feeling, that you were somehow separate from your Inner Master, is no longer there. Nisargadatta Maharaj often said, “I am not making you a disciple, I am making you a Master”, because that Masterly Essence is already within you.

The Invisible Listener has no shape. When the Invisible Listener absorbs the knowledge, everything will suddenly fall into place and become crystal clear. You will have no further need of any Master. However, until then, you must follow the instructions from your external Master. This will draw you closer and closer to your inner Master. The outer Master guides you to the door of your inner Master, and shows you how to gain entry. If the outer Master says, “You Are Brahman”, accept it.

All these uneducated and illiterate saints and devotees, accepted what the Masters said without question, without having any doubts. You, however, are not accepting the Truth so easily. You, are in the way, with the rational mind’s habit of questioning everything: “Why this? How is that?” There is no “Why?” or “How?” There should not be any kind of analysis of the Master’s statements’ taking place. It is simple: “My Master says, I am Brahman, therefore I am Brahman”. You are to accept your Reality in this way. Keep it simple.

We have a tendency to complicate everything. Spiritual knowledge develops the spiritual intellect, which, in turn, brings the subtle ego into play again. There are various mental pressures around, so that, even though we know Reality, there is still some confusion around.

You will develop a commanding nature, and know how to act, or react, under different circumstances. That Reality will emerge from you. That inner voice will guide you. All your previous doubts will dissolve. You will no longer let the mind dictate to you. The mind will be your slave!

p.strong: 120. What are the changes that will take place after Conviction?

section.p: After a period of continuous meditation, after Conviction, you will be aware of some changes taking place.

Before Conviction, even though you already had spiritual knowledge, when something unexpected happened to a family member, or if an incident took place, if there were any problems or difficulties, you would react, and become irritated at that particular situation. That irritation is no longer there, after Conviction, because you know that “I am not the body”. Nothing can touch you! As Nisargadatta Maharaj used to say: “If someone says that I am a very good saint, or a very bad saint, there is no reaction, because I am neither good nor bad”. That reality will be absorbed within you, so that if anyone praises you or abuses you, it is like, “water off a duck’s back”. You have nothing to do with that, and so, you remain untouched.

This change happens because after Conviction, body-based-knowledge does not, and cannot touch you. Up until now, you maybe accepted that you were Brahman, but this knowledge was only theoretical knowledge. You did not have practical knowledge because of the influence of the five elements, the ego, the intellect and the mind. As your central point of reference was body-based, you absorbed all the concepts. Whereas, after Conviction, after reaching Ultimate Truth, after the Ultimate Stage, all these concepts dissolve because you know that you are not the body-form at all. Now you know that you are the “central point of the universe”.

If you wish to gauge the changes that are taking place, and see whether the knowledge has been absorbed, there are various, obvious signs of Realization: Forgive and forget, about those who have wronged you. Tolerance is important, too. There will be exceptional tolerance and patience, which was not there before, so that, if you heard something unpleasant, or if something happened in your family, you would not be upset or disturbed. You know that there are no enemies. You know that there is nothing.

Supposing that a close friend, or a relative dies, there may be some grief or sadness there for a moment, but then you remind yourself: “Okay this is going to happen to me also. Today this man’s body has expired, and tomorrow my body will expire”. When there is that Conviction, you will not be affected.
Having a deep longing and desire, to know the Reality is another sign. You have the ability and discernment to know Reality, calmly and quietly.

All your attachments, and concerns will be discarded. You will have no desire for sex, money, or fame. Your view of the world will change completely with Conviction. You will no longer be attracted to the material things in life, or to material happiness and peacefulness. Happiness and peacefulness will now appear spontaneously within you.

This spontaneous happiness and peace will deepen your devotion. You will find within you, all the qualities that you were previously seeking from the world. As this happiness and peace arises, and permeates your being, you will realize that you no longer need any material cause to bring you happiness and peace, because happiness and peace are your very nature.

All this will happen spontaneously. Whatever is needed is already within you. When everything the Master said would happen, happens, this will deepen your trust, both in your Master and yourself. “Yes! this is the Ultimate Truth!” These changes will take place. They are the signs of Realization, or Enlightenment. Everything will happen spontaneously. If meditation is needed, it will happen spontaneously. You will not need to go and sit down to meditate. It will simply happen by itself, and continue throughout deep sleep, as well.

So, though you are holding the body, and using the body, you will remain unconcerned and detached from it. After these changes have taken place, your lifestyle will be totally transformed. But until these changes happen, you need to continue with the meditation practice. Because sometimes after some kind of miraculous experience, you may get carried away, thinking, “Oh! I have the knowledge. I am Realized! Why bother going for meditation?” This is a kind of mental sting, that is trying to distract you from Reality. The mind, ego and intellect are always trying to trick you, trap you, or pave the way for your downfall! Be alert! There are always external forces in the form of half-knowledge, that will try to impress their old concepts on you.

After Conviction, there will be no need for you to visit another Master. Why would you? You know, who or what, you are! You are here to identify yourself, not to identify Brahman. You are to identify the Invisible Identifier within you. It is not subject to birth or death. It is shapeless, like the space or sky.
For that Conviction to appear, you need to make the necessary inner changes.

When you are faced with any kind of problem, you will learn to keep quiet, and not to feed the problem with your attention. That is the first stage. The ego will not allow us to stay completely unconcerned and detached. So, with meditation, you can learn to control your mind, ego and intellect, so that they act according to your wishes, and not, the other way round. You used to be their slave. Not any longer! This is the effect of meditation.

The first lesson in spirituality is strict meditation. It will take some time for the changes to take place. In some cases, they will happen within one month or two, and in other cases, it may take six to ten years. It all depends on whether your involvement is serious or not, and on the strength of your faith. Strong faith is most important here, as there should not be a dull mind, or a wavering mind. Don’t go to more than one Master. And better still, approach your own Master. The Master does not show you anything except your Selfless Self.

Changing Masters is meaningless, but because we have some kind of dual mind, we keep on roaming and changing Masters. You will not get anything from another new Master. “Everything Starts From You, and Everything Ends Within You. That is the Ultimate Truth, the Final Truth.”
We are trying to bring about change, to stop you being led by the mind, the ego and the intellect. Meditation is the only way.

p.strong: 121. After tasting the nectar, how is it reflected?

section.p: In yesterday’s lecture, the question was raised, “after tasting the amrut (nectar), how is it reflected in our spiritual body?” Here, nectar means receiving knowledge through the Spiritual Master, and absorbing it. Through the Masters, you gain the knowledge that you are Ultimate Truth. You become one with the Ultimate. There is no separation. Though you are holding the body, you are one with yourself. “Self” is a word. It has no special identity. It is called swarup, which means Selfless Self. It is One and indivisible. There is no separation. It is just like the sky which is everywhere. The sky is not divided into parts like an Indian, or Chinese sky. The sky is everywhere, and Presence is like that, too. It is everywhere. It does not depend upon the body.

Accept what the Master has told you. The Master has told you that, except your Selfless Self there is no God. It is your Reality. So why are you still looking for Brahman? It is not difficult to find Brahman or God. Brahman is not some material thing. Brahman is Formless, Ultimate Truth which you are holding, which is within you. There is no Ultimate Truth, except for Formless You. That Conviction is to appear in you. That is the purpose of spirituality.

Yes? Okay! So, Be With You. This is the purpose, this is direct knowledge: there is nothing to be done because you are not a doer, and there is no deed. How were you prior to beingness? Do you know anything about your past birth, or future birth? Nothing! And, in between? No!

In order to know nothing, you are to do something. Do something, to know nothing. And that something, is meditation. You will realize through meditation, that the source of spirituality is in you. The entire source of this world starts from you. Now the ball is in your court. Hmm! How to live, and how to act, is entirely up to you.

p.strong: 122. How do I know if Brahman is absorbed in me?

section.p: There are six signs, called “sharguna”. Sharguna meaning six – the six signs, or six symptoms. After absorbing the knowledge, you can look out for the following:

1. Shama means forgiveness. Forgive and forget. I often give the example of Jesus Christ when he was nailed to the cross. At that moment, he said, “Oh God, forgive them, for they know not what they do”. You are to forgive and forget!

2. Dama means tolerance. If anything unpleasant happens to you, you don’t get irritated. You try to understand, and remain peaceful. Suppose, for example, someone shouted at you, or bumped into you, you would not get irritated.
3. And then there is Titiksha which means that you are anxious to know the Reality.

4. Uparati means discarding all the concepts, and worldly attractions. Even if lakhs or crores of dollars are given to you, you will not be interested. You will say, “Why? I don’t want it!” And then, even if a beautiful lady appears in front of you, there will not be any attraction. It happens. Your body-based knowledge, will be totally discarded. You will look at the beautiful lady, and say: “What is that? It is a dead body”.
5. Bhakti means total devotion.
6. Sharda means complete faith.

If there is some irritation, and if you do not have a forgiving nature, or, if there is still some attraction for material powers, this means that something is wrong! The knowledge has not been absorbed within you.
These qualities will appear with meditation. And when they do, you will live life in the world, yet remain untouched by it. You will be in the world, but not of the world.

p.strong: 123. You say, that I am the architect of my life?

section.p: Yes, you are the architect of your own life. It is up to you, to accept or refuse, what comes your way. If you don’t want something, don’t take it. If someone wishes to give you something, and you don’t wish to accept it, then, don’t. It is up to you to take what you need, and reject what you don’t need. There are so many thoughts flowing in the mind. Accept the useful thoughts only. That is why I say, that you are the architect of your own life. Everyone tries to impress you with their own concepts. Meditation will teach you, how to act and react. You will be instructed because you are a mastermind!

“You Are the Central Point of the World.” You are not this illusion. The body-form is not your identity. You are nobody, and you are everybody. Your Presence is everywhere, just like the sky is everywhere. The sky that you see here, is the same sky that is everywhere. You are beyond the sky. Have that Conviction! Try to absorb this knowledge. Try to digest this knowledge, which is your Reality, the Listener’s Reality. Reality is not separate from your Identity, therefore, I define it as “Unidentified, Invisible, Anonymous Identity”.

Everything starts from you, and everything ends within you. You are not separate from Ultimate Truth. But because of your conditioning, and the influences you were under, since childhood, you don’t have the courage to dissolve the body-based knowledge. You accept everything, and sign on the dotted line, without even knowing what you are signing: Everywhere, and all the time, you are signing, “Yes! Yes! Yes”. Though you are not guilty, you live with a guilty conscience. You are not guilty. You are not anything at all, because there is no doer, and there is no deed.

p.strong: 124. If we compare a realized person to a non-realized person...?

section.p: There is no such thing. There is no difference between a realized person, and a non-realized person. You are formless, like the sky. Is the Indian sky realized, and the American sky not realized? Again, you are using your intellect: “This person is realized, and that person is not realized”. This question comes from considering yourself to be somebody, and taking the world to be true. All these concepts are based on the mistaken notion that “the body is my identity”.

You don’t need to recite the Mantra after Spontaneous Conviction, because you have reached the destination. It is like when you were looking for “Nashik Road Ashram”. When you set out, you asked for directions: “Where is Nashik Road Ashram?” You were looking, and searching. You were asking everybody. Finally, when you arrived at the address, you knew where the ashram was. Likewise, after Conviction, you don’t have to read one more single book. You don’t have to go anywhere, because you have reached your destination.

When you were learning a language, you needed to learn the alphabet and grammar. But once you had acquired these language skills, you no longer needed, to put in any effort to know the alphabet and the grammar. So, after Conviction, you don’t need the “abcd’s”, i.e. the Mantra. Meditation is illusion. There was no meditation prior to beingness, and there will not be any meditation after beingness. You lost your memory; you went missing. So, you needed a process, through which you could identify yourself. Now that you know who you are, the process has become redundant.

p.strong: 125. What do you mean when you say, “the body touch dissolves”?

section.p: When we wear clothes, we are not aware that we are wearing them. We don’t really feel them on us, as we go about our daily business. You don’t feel their touch. Similarly, when you know that you are not the body, you are not concerned about it. You feel very light and weightless. There won’t be any type of burden. The body is not a burden to you. You will feel completely free. Meditation dissolves the body sense. It detaches you from the body, so that you do not take its touch.

There is nothing for you to do, except to know that the body is not your identity at all. It is simple. If you try to extract spiritual knowledge, with the strength of body-based knowledge, there will still be duality there. Meditation is essential for seekers of any age, who wish to identify themselves. You have a good background, and spiritual foundation, now all that is needed, is for you to hammer yourself. “You Are to Hammer Yourself.” You are to be totally free from the bondage of the body-form.

That body touch will dissolve with meditation, and you will experience exceptional, internal happiness, and peacefulness. Though you are holding the body, you will remain untouched by it. The body is like an external cloth, and, even though you are holding this external, body cloth, it will not concern you. The body will appear as a kind of subtle, subtle, subtle light. It cannot be described in words, but it is an exceptional experience.

When Bhausaheb Maharaj was in the process of planning and organizing the “Inchegeri Sampradaya Lineage”, he had great foresight. He clearly perceived, that the only way, to combat body-based knowledge, to reach the Ultimate Truth, was for devotees to be immersed in a spiritual atmosphere all the time, at every available moment. To keep them absorbed in the spiritual zone, he prescribed early morning Bhajan, meditation, afternoon Bhajan, and evening Bhajan. This daily schedule kept the devotees’ minds’ busy, while, at the same time, giving them practical knowledge.

While you are attending to your responsibilities, and looking after your body, at the same time, you should be fully involved with Selfless Self, to keep in touch with Selfless Self at all times. This was the idea behind Sri Bhausaheb’s busy, daily programme.

There is no doubt, that Bhausaheb Maharaj knew that the Bhajans and meditation, were all illusion, but, at the same time, absolutely necessary for Conviction, for the Spontaneous Conviction of Your Ultimate Truth, your Ultimate Identity.

The process is very simple, but also very difficult, because you must have strong faith in your Master. There are so many layers upon your Presence, that in order to remove, or burn all these layers, you are to undergo strict meditation, and follow the Master’s every instruction.

p.strong: 126. If this is a dream, can we do anything we want?

section.p: This does not mean that there should be total freedom, “Oh, it is all a dream, so I can do whatever I want”. No! Absolutely not! Some limitations are needed, boundaries are essential because anything taken to excess is poisonous. Control yourself! Self-control is most important. After knowing the Reality, you are to exercise self-control. Otherwise, you will say, “My Master has given me total freedom, so that I can do whatever I want”. It should not be like that. This is a reckless attitude that will create further problems.

The human body is very important because through it, you can identify yourself in a real sense. If there is no identification, you will continue seeing dreams – another dream, and another dream, and maybe another dream. How long do you wish to keep seeing dreams for? This is called rebirth, but as you know, “rebirth” is only a concept. This should be the last dream. There should not be any more dreams. You must convince yourself of that. Make this your last dream!
“After Conviction, Be Calm and Quiet, Be Humble, Be Normal, Be Simple.” There is nothing to do. There are no restrictions, no orders, no bondage, or any conditions.

“There is Nothing to Know.” No knowledge is knowledge. Knowledge is also an illusion. Brahman is also illusion, the entire thing is illusion. Try to identify yourself. “You Are Ultimate Truth.” I am saying the same thing, over and over again, to “Impress The Reality on You. You Are Reality. You Are Ultimate Truth.”

p.strong: 127. Why is devotion needed after Realization?

section.p: Because there are always external forces around that are ready to disturb you. Also, the mind, the ego and the intellect, may try to distract you from Reality. In spiritual language, this is called maya. [there is no maya] There are so many concepts around. Publicity or fame, money, sex and other desires. After Realization, you still need to remain alert to these dangers, and that is where devotion comes in. If you meditate, recite the Mantra, and remember the words of the Masters, no one will attack you, or be able to distract you from Reality.

p.strong: 128. Nisargadatta Maharaj used to pray after Realization. Why?

section.p: Of course! We are also praying. Continuity is required because there are so many concepts pressurizing us without our knowledge. We pray every day. Prayer creates certain vibrations, that keep us in a devotional, humble and elevated state. Continuity is essential, as the human body has so many problems. There is no harm.

p.strong: 5 EXHORTATIONS AND WARNINGS

p.emphasis: “I am giving you a hundred percent guarantee! No one is willing to give you a guarantee, but I am – a hundred percent! All you are to do is sacrifice, and devote some time for you. And then you will see the results. I am not performing any miracles. The miracles happen within you. But you are to be very serious regarding meditation.”

p.strong: 129. Be brave, and live like a lion!

section.p: Be brave! Live like a lion! Don’t be a coward, always anxious, “Oh dear! What’s going to happen?” Nothing has happened, at all. There is no deed, and there is no doer. But we are always under the pressure of fear, and therefore, lacking in courage. Be humble! Be normal! There is no great or small, and no good or bad. Don’t make comparisons such as: “oh, this is a good man, and that is a bad man”. Nothing is good, and nothing is bad. These are your own concepts.

It is the purpose of spirituality, to transform you from a beggar into a multi-millionaire! “You Are Not a Beggar.” But you are acting like one, when you say, “Oh, do something, give me what I need. Bless me, bless me”. Why? You are not a beggar! “You Are a Multi-Millionaire!” There is absolutely nothing to fear. “You Are the Father of This World, the Mother of This World” – one and the same! This is not an egoistic statement. “You Are Final Truth. You Are Ultimate Truth. There is Nothing Except for Selfless Self.”

p.strong: 130. You must have the courage to accept the Reality

section.p: Courage is needed to accept Reality, and, that is what we seem to lack. That courage, power and energy will come with meditation. Meditation means hammering yourself at all times: “You Are Brahman, You Are Atman, You Are Paramatman”. This appears as duality, but out of that duality, your individuality disappears. In the beginning, you say, “When I am sitting in meditation”, or, “I am somebody who is meditating”. This is duality. But we need to take the help of this illusion, to remove another illusion.

All your concepts and fears need to dissolve. The fire is there, but it is covered with ash. Through the meditation, you are removing the ash, removing all the body-based concepts. Then you will be strong and courageous.

It is not difficult or impossible! Don’t stress yourself, or struggle with anything. Go deep, and deep and deep, within your Selfless Self. Then forget all about this world. There should not be any concepts left: No “I am”, no “you are”, “There is Nothing There.” There is no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman. All concepts! Discard them all!

“God” is also a concept. It is a fact because we have given birth to God. This is not an intellectual statement. It is the truth. But to accept this truth with Conviction, we need to meditate. It may feel like a monotonous practice in the beginning: “Oh not meditation again!” But there is no other way. You have to devote some time for yourself, because you are travelling around this unbalanced, illusory world.

We don’t expect there to be another illusory world after this one. This is the Final, Final Dance! This is the Ultimate Truth. Ultimate Truth is not separate from you. Playing with spiritual words, and literal knowledge, is meaningless. You are to go deep within. Go deep, and deep, within you. And then, your so-called existence will disappear. There won’t be any experience, experiencer, or witness. You will be completely aloof from the world, and then your attachment to the body-form will loosen, and gradually lessen, more and more.

Though you will continue to use the body for your work, and for performing some duties, you will not be touched by body-based knowledge. The mind, the ego, and the intellect may be there, but you will use them sparingly, as and when, required. Then, you will forget about them.

In this way, there will be no confusion or temptation. After knowing the Reality, you will not feel drawn to go anywhere. If an attraction arises, this means that you do not know your own importance. You still do not know yourself. You have tremendous power, and great importance. Don’t underestimate yourself. Don’t measure yourself in the body-form. You are beyond that! “You Are The Terminus.”

p.strong: 131. Spirituality gives you courage

section.p: The importance of spirituality, is that it gives you the courage to face life’s many challenges. They may only be spiritual words, but nevertheless, they will give you the courage to live a happy life.

Nisargadatta Maharaj became a disciple of Sidharameshwar Maharaj. Some time later, his problems began! He felt the need to absorb himself in spirituality full-time, to experience and absorb the teachings, and to gain practical knowledge. So, spontaneously, he abandoned everything, left home, and headed for the Himalayas.

When he returned, his business had all but collapsed. One shop out of ten remained! Then his mother died, followed by his daughter, his father, and his wife. He had to face so many calamities! But, in spite of all these devastating blows, he did not abandon his spirituality. He found the courage, to confront these extremely difficult circumstances.

Spiritual knowledge teaches you how to live successfully, and peacefully, in spite of so many challenges, difficulties and depression. That is the purpose behind it. You have the power to overcome all the difficulties that come your way. Use that power! Understanding and Conviction are most important. They will come from meditation and knowledge.

It is a fact that this world is an illusory world. There is no “I”, “you”, “he”, “she”, “it” – there’s nothing. There is no Master, there is no disciple, there is no God, there is no seeker or devotee. This is fact! But, there will be concepts around, for as long as we are holding the body. When the body disappears, there will not be any concepts. Nothing will remain. Who is God? What is God? What is Brahman? What is Atman, Paramatman, Master? Everything came out of nothing, and everything will dissolve within nothing. It is a fact!

Accept your Reality! Spirituality teaches you how to stand on your own feet. It gives you the strength to be independent. Don’t depend on anyone, including the Masters. Encourage your Own Master! Approach your Own Master. Your Master is a Powerful Master. Outgrow your old habits, “Please help me, and give me this, and give me that! Bless me! Give me your blessing”, etc. Why are you asking someone to bless you? “Put your hand on your head, and bless yourself.”

p.strong: 132. You say we need courage, but I don’t feel that I am a very courageous person…

section.p: You need courage because you consider yourself to be the body-form. It is spontaneous courage. Even when difficulties appear, you will not be disturbed or depressed. If some unpleasant incidents happen in your life, you will find the courage to deal with them. You will find the strength, to stay aloof from any problems, that come your way. In the past, if there was an upset, we responded with fear and depression. We were disturbed. But after Conviction, there will not be any fear or disturbance when unpleasant incidents occur because you will be as solid as a rock, established in Ultimate Reality, and therefore, invincible.

If something happens, let it happen! Whatever calamities may come your way, spontaneous courage will arise from within you, so that you float through these particular challenges. Your attitude should be like this: “Bring it on! Let whatever is going to happen, happen. It is not my concern”. If you face the difficulties head-on, the black clouds will disperse quickly. If you are fearful, they will gather momentum.

The clouds are coming, and the clouds are going. But you are not coming and going. “You Are There, And There Only.” After concentrated meditation, you will feel some spontaneous changes happening within you. Slowly, silently, and permanently, all your body-based concepts are dissolving.
Forget about all that is happening in the world! Just forget it! You are not the body, and you have nothing to do with the world. You are formless!

Yesterday, I told this lady who was visiting the ashram, about a young boy who used to come here. He was very innocent. He did not know the “a,b,c’s” of philosophy or spirituality, but then a miracle happened.

He had taken the Mantra. When he sat down for meditation, he immediately fell into a trance, forgetting everything. Then, one day, while he was putting some towels away in a cupboard, he fell from the third floor. Luckily, his fall was cushioned to some extent, by a small bush on which he landed. He attributed this to the miracle of the Naam Mantra!

The specialist doctor, gave him a life expectancy of no more than twenty-odd years. Wrong! He is now married, has a child, and is working as a ward porter, at the hospital where he was treated. He was supposed to receive blood transfusions every three or four months. That stopped, along with all his medication. And so, though he fell from the third floor, nothing really happened! It is a fact! When he was admitted to the hospital, he thought he was going to die! “Oh! A miracle happened”, he said. “My Master saved me!”
This kind of thing happens. Miracles are not Ultimate Truth, but, nevertheless, you have tremendous power. When you become One With You, anything can happen.

I will tell you about an American named “Whittaker” He was a mountaineer. He was not involved in spirituality. Here, I am talking about the power, the spiritual power, that inbuilt power in us all. After a car accident, they had to cut his right leg off, from here, [Maharaj points to his lower legs] In spite of this huge handicap, he wanted to climb Mount Everest. He failed on his first two attempts. But in spite of this, he did not give up. He attempted the climb again, wearing an artificial limb. He almost reached the peak, but then, the screws loosened, and his artificial limb fell off.

In spite of that, with inbuilt courage, he reached the mountain peak. This is a fact! How did it happen? It was a miracle. This story of unimaginable determination, and strength and courage, demonstrates that we all have immense power, miraculous power. I am encouraging that power, through which you can identify yourself. You are still doubtful, unbelieving, saying, “No! How can this be possible?” It is possible, and it can happen. It can happen to you. This kind of remarkable phenomenon cannot be grasped by the intellect. Something happened that is beyond the intellect, and beyond our imagination.

You will have the strength, courage and determination to face anything. Courage is inbuilt in you. It is spontaneous courage. This knowledge is priceless. But you must give of your time, that is all. Sit down for meditation, and recite the Mantra. Here there are no spiritual rules and restrictions, regarding your diet or daily actions. Yesterday, one boy asked a question about food: “I eat fish and mutton. Is that okay?” There is no problem. The body is a food-body, so give it what it needs, in moderation.

Another time, a middle-age man came to me, saying, “Every day, I drink beer, can I still take the Naam Mantra?” It is not a problem. You can drink beer, you can consume what your body requires, but never take anything to excess. What I want to say, is that diet and food etc, is not the concern of spirituality. Concentrate on remaining detached from body-based knowledge, and staying aloof, and untouched by body-based concepts. That is the concern of spirituality, and should be your one and only concern. Don’t mix food-body issues with spirituality. They are unrelated! Okay?

p.strong: 133. “Bless me! Bless me! I need a blessing!” Why are you begging?

section.p: Don’t expect anything from anyone: “Oh! Please do something for me”. “You Can Stand On Your Own Feet.” Don’t depend on anyone. Bow down to you, mmh! That is Reality. How can you bow to yourself? By taking what is given to you. By accepting that you are the Source, the only Source. By accepting that you are All! Value your invaluable Presence. Respect yourself, and respect everyone! I am not saying that you are not to respect the saintly people, the Masters, mothers, etc. You can respect them all, but, at the same time, you must try to identify yourself.

The Holder of the body is Ultimate Truth. Don’t allow the growth of any sense of ego, because human beings have a subtle, subtle ego. If something happens, that goes against your mind, you get annoyed. There should not be any annoyance in you.

A well-known example of how we ignore our power, and underestimate ourselves, is portrayed in the popular story of the “Beggar Boy”. A boy is living on the street as a beggar. Then, one day a man approaches him, saying: “Why are you begging? Your parents left you a lot of money”. The boy responds: “You are making fun of me”. “No I am not”, replies the man. To prove to the boy that he is in fact rich, he takes him to the bank, where he shows him all the documents to verify his status. Seeing the evidence in front of him, with his own eyes gives him the conviction: “Wow! I am not a beggar after all. I am a multimillionaire”. He was under the illusion that he was a beggar, but when he was given the knowledge, his ignorance dissolved.

He was already a multimillionaire when he was begging, but he did not know the facts, and, therefore, he was begging. It is the very same thing with us. We are begging, “Oh God, bless me”. Don’t beg! You are not a beggar, but a multimillionaire.

Why are you begging for blessings from somebody? Give yourself your own blessings. Bless yourself! I am trying to convince the devotees in this way. Devotees also ask how they can realize – when they are already realized – but, put simply, they do not know that they are. “How will I realize?” You are realized! That Realization is unknown to you. Here, you are not learning anything new, we are just refreshing your memory, and removing some unwanted things that don’t belong to you. It is a process of subtraction, not addition. You must have strong faith during this process. The bottom line is that you are a Master. You need a physical Master to assist you with identifying yourself. “Identify the Hidden Master Within You.”

You receive Diksha (initiation), to convince the Invisible Listener That, “Except Your Selfless Self There is Nothing”. You have to be sincere, and always recite the Mantra. Remember the Master’s words, and meditate. One way, or another. you will Self-Realize: “Oh! I am finding so much. What am I finding? I am finding the Finder! Aha! The Finder is itself Ultimate Truth! I am Ultimate Truth”. That Spontaneous Realization will appear within you. This is not an egoistic statement.

Exceptional happiness, without any material cause, will come with that Realization. You were looking for happiness from material sources. You were looking for fame, money and sex. These are not needed to make you happy. Nothing is needed because the happiness that will arise is spontaneous happiness, causeless happiness.

p.strong: 134. Why do we search for a Master?

section.p: Because you do not know your direction Home. You do not know the address, or how to get there. The Master knows all the details, because he was once a disciple, too.

When you are in the Presence of the Master, don’t analyze the words. Instead, try and identify what they are saying, what they wish to convey. Be selective! Be careful which Master you decide to settle with, as the Master who has uncovered his Perfect and Exceptional Identity, is a very rare Master indeed. Many Masters just pile heaps of knowledge onto the seekers. They are not able to take seekers beyond knowledge, because they have not gone beyond knowledge themselves. The Master who shows you God in you, is as rare as a flower in the desert.

There are many so-called Masters, who talk about the kundalini and the chakras. It is all meaningless. There is no kundalini. There are no chakras. This illusory circle of kundalinis and chakras is a vicious circle. Don’t enter it! [Maharaj chuckles]

All the secret assets, invisible assets, the invisible property, the entire assets, everything is within you. So, why go elsewhere, to find your innate and rightful property? This is the principle of spirituality.

With the help of the Master, you can identify yourself, and finally know who you are. After identifying that Master within you, you can attend to your duties, work or career smoothly, and without any difficulty. By this time, you will be able to do anything, because your foundations will be solid. You will have exceptional peacefulness, and exceptional happiness within you. Happiness and peacefulness are not separate from you. You Are That!

p.strong: 135. Why do we need to listen to a Master?

section.p: Why do the Masters talk? Because you are not able to hear your own, Inner Master talking to you. The Master is trying to invite the attention of the Invisible Listener, that, “You Are Brahman”. What is here? [Maharaj points to his body] It is the same thing. The Presence within this body, and the Presence within your body is one and the same. This Reality is to be established.

Have respect for your Master. Why? It is only because of your Master, that you know your true identity. If it were not for your Master, you would still be lost in the world, identifying with the body-form. The Master and the disciple do not have separate identities. After Conviction, there will be eternal happiness. It cannot be described in words. There will be exceptional peacefulness. Even if, say, violence erupted all around you, inside of you, there will still be total and complete silence.
Though we may have theoretical knowledge, we have still not met ourselves in a real sense. For that, you need regular meditation, sincere meditation. Beyond that, there is nothing.

This knowledge is the rarest of knowledge. It is priceless knowledge. But because it is free, it is seldom valued. At first, when visitors come to the ashram, they are very interested. However, their enthusiasm does not last. They get distracted, lose interest, and then decide: “No, no, this is not it”. They listen to the mind, and reject Reality. Concentrate sincerely! This is an opportunity. To avoid the next concept of birth and death, you have to try and identify yourself today, not tomorrow. “Every Moment is Valuable For You.” Give prime importance to your Selfless Self.

Some visitors who may have developed a physical problem and are feeling somewhat helpless, go to see a Master. They will say: “bless me, bless me, bless me, do something, please, do something to help me”. This will not help you. The Master is not here to solve your physical problems.

After Conviction, if something goes wrong with your body, you will have the courage to face that problem. There will be a complete change of perspective. The human body is very unstable. Anything can happen to it, at any time. There may be sickness, depression, impediments, or all sorts of difficult circumstances there, but, you will always be firm, because you are established in Ultimate Truth.

We all feel handicapped to some extent, as if we are incomplete, not whole, lacking. So, we look to others for help: “Do something, do something”. We are desperately running, here and there. With strong devotion, you will never have to go running to anyone else again, because the cure is within you. You can cure anything. It happens.

No one is performing any miracles. When a Master says: “Oh, I will do a miracle for you. When I put my hand on your head, I will transfer some power”. This is a story. It is meaningless. Don’t believe it! No one can give you any power.

p.strong: 136. How can anyone transfer power to you?

section.p: You already have power, exceptional power. Don’t neglect your own power. No one can transfer power to you because all the Power is with you, and in you. “You Are the Principle of This World. The Entire World is Projected Out of Your Invisible Presence.” That Invisible Master, that Invisible Power or Energy, is already within you. You are the backbone, the foundation of Reality.

p.strong: 137. Why go here and there?

section.p: I am not restricting your movements, however, after reaching the destination, don’t go looking elsewhere in search of something. Don’t go with the intention of finding something, like: “If I go to that Master, or that place, I will get some kind of realization”. You will not get anything. You will be misguided, misguided, misguided, because there is no Brahman that is separate from you. There is no Brahman apart from you. So, why go after unnecessary distractions, when you are Reality. “Be With You All The Time.” Don’t mix with the wrong company, and get entangled in body-based, and commercial knowledge. There are so many spiritual shops these days, selling commercial spirituality. Spirituality has become a business, unfortunately. I am not criticizing it, just pointing out a fact. Don’t be attracted to that spiritual business.

Here, the Masters are not withholding any secrets. We are placing before you, your Ultimate Truth, the whole truth! There is no more. So that, if you go elsewhere seeking something else, all you will find is illusory, body-based knowledge. And, you will be back on the hamster wheel once more!

We are pointing out, to the Invisible Listener within you, that you are Ultimate Truth. There are no miracles. There is nothing else. No miracles, nothing is there. Be strong! Tap into your power. “Encourage Your Power. Entice Your Power. Use Your Power.” Avoid going to other teachers, or Masters who say: “Oh, yes, Brahman! I have Brahman knowledge. I will show it to you”. Nobody can show you Brahman. It cannot be shown.

How do you show the sky to anyone in India? How do you show anyone the American sky? No one can! This kind of talk is nonsense, misleading. It is a misrepresentation. The sky is everywhere. Brahman is the name given to that principle that is everywhere, in every being -not only in humans, but in every being. This world is full of Presence, full of Brahman, but we do not know that Reality. Therefore, we meditate to know the Reality.

Some visitors tell me that they have been at a certain ashram for years, sitting crossed-legged in meditation, and fasting for long periods of time. Don’t waste your life unnecessarily. Do your job, do your duties, but don’t sit with your legs crossed 24/7!

I always tell those who come to me for guidance about their marriage and family life, the same thing: “Marry, and have a family, if you wish. There is no problem”. Some of them say, “Oh, I am a spiritual person, how can I get married?” Of course you can marry! There is no problem! The body is a food-body with bodily needs. It has bodily requirements. These kind of thoughts are arising and flowing, but you are trying to restrict them. And because you are trying to suppress them, they blow up again, and explode with double the force. Don’t accept these impressions and concepts, but at the same time, be your own judge. Be normal, and be humble!

In brief, “Except For Your Selfless Self There is No God, No Brahman, No Atman, No Paramatman, No Master”. It is very simple. Reality is placed before you. You may accept it, or you may reject it. It is up to you. Listen to me! “This Is the Final Truth, the Final Ultimate Truth.” There is nothing beyond, and there is nowhere else to go.

You may visit any place you wish, but not for spiritual knowledge. Spiritual knowledge is always within you. Don’t underestimate yourself. You do not know your own greatness. Greatness is always with you. We have created language. We use it to convince you of your True Identity. Ignore the language because it is of secondary importance. Try to identify, and understand what the Master is conveying, the meaning behind the words. Instead of analyzing the words, try to understand what the Master is conveying.

p.strong: 138. Why are we running after a mirage of spirituality?

section.p: Because we are deceiving ourselves that it can give us something. Spirituality is a mirage, an illusion. There is nothing there. Everything is within you, but you are not identifying yourself. You don’t know yourself. This is the basic idea. To identify yourself, we are presenting you with Reality, your Reality, not the Reality of Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master. These words indicate, that except your Selfless Self, there is nothing. Everyone is struggling to reach that Conviction. Every being is struggling. Even though we know that we are Ultimate Truth in the literal sense, happiness is still not there, and peace is not to be found.

p.strong: 139. What does spirituality mean? Touching the Master’s feet, clapping, garlands?

section.p: This is not spirituality. When I was a boy, my Master, Nisargadatta Maharaj, said, “Earn lakhs of rupees, and then throw them away! Stand on your own feet. Educate yourself. Don’t come to me. Lead a practical life”. He asked me: “Why aren’t you in college? Enrol at the college! Don’t come to me! If you have any spare time, then you can come! You are welcome”.

Don’t leave your job, and don’t abandon your duties. You are a doctor, so you must pay attention to your patients. And after that, come to me during your free time. What is spirituality? It just means, “To Identify Yourself In A Real Sense”.

p.strong: 140. We are not doing any miracles

section.p: All sorts of spiritual books are out there. The principle behind them is to identify oneself in a real sense. See, we are not doing any miracles. There are some concepts around, whereby people say, “after going to see Maharaj, after going to the Master, a miracle will happen. My family life and my personal life will improve”. I tell them that I am not performing any miracles. I am placing before them their Identity. We are asking you, to discard all the concepts, that have been engraved in you, from childhood till today. These concepts, like birth and death, future and past and present, last birth, future birth, all of them are to be discarded because you are unborn. There is no birth and death. You are Ultimate Truth.

p.strong: 141. Why does Nisargadatta, say this, and Ramana Maharshi, say that?

section.p: You must have read so many books by Ramana Maharshi, Nisargadatta Maharaj, Krishnamurti and others. Don’t analyze their words. It is the nature of the mind, to analyze and dissect everything, and make comparisons. “Why did Nisargadatta, say this, and Ramana Maharshi, say that?” We are analyzing their words, and their statements, all the time, and missing the most important thing, which is grasping [apperceiving] what they are trying to convey.
