This means that everything comes out of nothing, and everything dissolves within nothing. Out of nothing, something is seen. Whatever is seen is illusion. Avoid playing with the words, and analyzing the words. Instead, it is most important that you try to identify the Invisible Listener within you. And keep in mind that, “Except for your Selfless Self, there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman, no Master”.

p.strong: 188. Once, when I was on holiday in Tokyo, I went to a Buddhist temple. It was very peaceful. Is that okay Maharaj?

section.p: Maharaj: You can go anywhere you wish. I am not imposing any restrictions on you. Visit temples if you wish. There are no rules. Visit any country in the world, any temple, church, ashram or place of worship of your choosing, with self-confidence. At the same time, remember that everything is within you. “Visit the Visitor!” “Wherever You Are, There is Your Ashram.”

p.strong: 189. Maharaj, the body feels like it is vibrating. Is this good?

section.p: Maharaj: Good! It is a good sign. It means that the body-based concepts are vanishing. The process you are going through is a cleaning process, a purification process. The unwanted tenants are leaving. While leaving your body, they are abusing you. Since childhood till today, so many tenants have resided within you, so many concepts are there. Your experience shows that the cleaning process is effective.

p.strong: 190. When do I get liberation, mukti?

section.p: Maharaj: It is already there! Who wants mukti, liberation? You are already free. These concepts keep appearing because you still consider yourself as the body-form, these concepts keep appearing: “I want liberation, freedom, salvation, mukti or something else”. There is already liberation. You are free, but you do not know that you are, because you take yourself to be the body-form.

Yesterday, someone said, “some of the statements made by Maharaj are contradictory”. Don’t analyze the Master’s words! What is the point of analyzing the words? What are you hoping for? Nothing! Reality is not up for debate. This is not a spiritual discussion that is open for argument, and counter argument. There are many saints like Ramana Maharshi, Nisargadatta Maharaj, Siddharameshwar Maharaj, Ranjit Maharaj and so on. What is of supreme importance, is what they tried to convey.

Don’t try to analyze, or define the words. How many times have I told you, that language was created by us to communicate, and exchange thoughts with one another! Language is not ultimate, because we have given the meanings to our vocabulary. And, therefore, the meaning is not ultimate. It is a conventional meaning, solely to convey a message. Don’t struggle with the words, it is an unnecessary waste of time. Try to identify yourself!

You are roaming within a vicious circle, “I am a man”, or, “I am a woman”. Because of this vicious circle, we cannot find happiness or peace. We are always stressed. And, in the background, there is always that fear of death. Why all this unpleasant atmosphere? Simply because our identification is a wrong identification.

Though the Invisible Listener within you is Ultimate Truth, Final Truth, you are measuring, counting yourself in the body-form, as the body-form. You are trying to know yourself through the body-form.

Be clear! The body is an external cover. It is not Ultimate Truth. The Holder of the body, the Invisible Presence within the body, is the Ultimate Truth, the Final Truth, for whom there is no death or birth. Prior to the body-form, prior to this body-knowledge, we had no knowledge of ourselves because there was no physical existence.

Just now, there is consciousness, but there is consciousness with confusion, consciousness wrapped up in various concepts. So, spiritual knowledge teaches you how to act and react, in this illusory world. This is an illusory world, just like a dream world. We are all watching the dreams. However, you cannot watch any dream, without your spontaneous existence. Likewise, you cannot see the world without your spontaneous existence. Therefore, the entire world is projected out of your Spontaneous Invisible Presence.

Know, that you are already free from all these concepts, but directly or indirectly, you have become a victim of your own concepts, which were engraved, in you since childhood days. It is essential to discard all the concepts, otherwise, Ultimate Truth will not emerge.
The Ultimate Truth is there, the Invisible Presence is there, energy is there, power is there. And, just like electricity, this power is an unknown power, an unknown energy.

Existence is there, but it is not experienced or witnessed. That You Are: Ultimate Truth, Final Truth, Brahman, Atman, God, Master. Except your Selfless Self, there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman. What will the results be, after knowing the Reality? You will have complete freedom from all the concepts. Because you were bound to your own concepts, you felt unfree. Now that these concepts have been unwrapped, there is no longer any bondage. You are free because you are formless. You must have a strong Conviction, and for this strong Conviction, for a strong foundation, you must meditate!

p.strong: 191. What will happen after my death?

section.p: We are under the pressure of “I am going to die. What will happen after my death?” You are not dying at all. Death and birth are mere, false allegations, that have been made against the Invisible Presence. There is no death or birth for you. When you know this, really know this deep in your core, all your illusory fears will collapse.

Death means deep sleep. Do you worry about deep sleep? No! When you are about to drift off into sleep, you do not want anyone to disturb you. On the contrary, you will say, “Oh! just leave me alone. I’m going to sleep!”

There is always peacefulness, but you are disturbing it, colouring it with body-based-knowledge which, in turn, brings illusory fear. Identify yourself, and then, you will have the courage, to meet any challenge. “Be With You Always, And You Will See That Nothing is Impossible!”

Follow everything that the Master says. Strictly follow every instruction. Continue with your routine life. Spirituality is not a hindrance or a hurdle. Enjoy! Be Realized! After Realization, make others realized.

Death is a concept, but a concept that has the power to frighten everyone, every single being. Nobody welcomes death. The Spirit is unaware of its perfection, its Perfect Identification. It only knows its identity through the body, as “I am somebody”. And so, it wishes to prolong its existence, and survive at all costs.

There is so much bodily attachment because of this mistaken identity. Your existence, your Spontaneous Existence is everywhere, just like the sky or space. You are subtler, subtler than the sky and space, but you are not accepting this. That is the problem. To accept the Reality, you have to hammer yourself, you have to teach yourself. Be your own teacher. You are your own Master. Discard all your body-based concepts.

It is incredible, unbelievable, that we continue to self-impose all these concepts. We have become victims of our own concepts. Why? Happiness and peacefulness are not separate from you. Spontaneous happiness is there. When all the concepts dissolve, there you are. See, there is only nothingness because everything came out of nothing, and everything dissolves within nothing. That illusory something, that you take for real, must dissolve.

Again, I am not asking you to neglect your body. Take good care of it. And observe your duties and responsibilities as well. However, at the same time, there should be the Conviction that, “I am nothing to do with body-knowledge, because I am totally formless. I am unborn, with no past and no future”. Past and future are for a “something”. This something is illusory. You are nothing, and, at the same time, you are everything.
You are Almighty God. Almighty God is the supernatural power that you are. You have tremendous power. Therefore a firm Conviction is required, “Yes! I Am That!”
You have forgotten your address. The Master is showing it to you: “This is the address. This is your Perfect Address”.

p.strong: 192. After that continuous hammering, what happens to the personality, does it dissolve with the body?

section.p: Maharaj: There is no individuality, no personality at all. I have told you that you are just like the sky or space. Does the sky have any individuality? Is the sky in India different from the sky in China? These questions will keep coming, for as long as you are counting yourself as the body-form. Look at you! See within you! Just be calm and peaceful. What is there? Just glimpses of “I” are there, without any form – pure consciousness.

This is a big computer. You are holding a lot of memories, every single memory since childhood has been recorded within you. Say someone knocks on the door, and you hear a voice, immediately, you will say, “hey, my brother has come”, or, “my father has arrived”. You are recording each and every item of data, like a computer. All the voices are recorded, so that, even if a lengthy period of time has passed, say some fifteen years, you will instantly recognize the voice and exclaim, “Oh! this is my Master’s voice”. Everything is recorded. Dreams are recorded. Who is recording all this? Who is video-shooting this dream? That Invisible Presence Is! That You Are!

Identify yourself! “Who am I? Where do I stand?” How can spirituality help you? Because it shatters the illusion of who you think you are. When this illusion is pierced, you will know your true Identity. The implications of knowing yourself in a real sense are huge. All your fears will simply vanish, along with your suffering. There will be abundant peace, and happiness that is causeless and permanent. The Master promises you 100% guarantee. This promise will be fulfilled, if you follow the Master’s instructions.

p.strong: 193. Is there a God?

section.p: God does not have any independent identity. Listen carefully: “Except for Selfless Self, there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman, no Master. This is the principle of spirituality. What is God? Have you seen God? Be frank, have you seen God? To say God, some Presence is required. If there is no Presence within the body, who is going to identify God? This means that you are the father of God, the mother of God!

The whole world is a dream-world. It is like when you are dreaming. According to science, a dream lasts for a maximum of ten to fifteen seconds, yet when you tell someone about your dream, it is as if it lasted for hours. Apply your intellect! This is not theoretical knowledge, it is practical knowledge. Understand the theory, and then, through that theoretical knowledge, try and identify yourself.

That Masterly essence is already within you, so why are you underestimating yourself? Be bold! Have some courage to accept the Reality. You are lacking in courage, questioning: “how can it happen to me?”, or, “God will punish me if I do something bad”. These are the concepts that need to be erased completely.

I am not asking you to have blind faith. Open your eyes, your spiritual eyes. Bow down to your Selfless Self. “Oh God, bless me! Oh God, bless me!” No! Give yourself a blessing because God is not separate from you. You may not believe it today, but that Conviction will come. It will come. Just be patient, and keep up the meditation.

When you are reading books, read them thoroughly, and very carefully. Don’t just have a superficial glance. Read Selfless Self, again and again. Then the Invisible Reader will awaken, “I Am That, this is my story!” There will be Self-recognition. If someone writes your biography, you will easily recognize yourself in the story and say: “Yes! This is my story”. When you are reading a spiritual book, it should be like this, “This is my story!”. All that is needed is Conviction and courage. You are afraid of everything because you are lacking courage. Be bold! Accept the Reality.

p.strong: 194. Sometimes in meditation, I am aware of the colour blue. I wonder if that is why Shiva, Rama and Krishna are depicted in blue?

section.p: These are the names, these are the games! Imagination! What is the colour blue? Did you know of any colour, whether blue or white or green or purple, prior to beingness? These are imaginary colours given to all the gods. A black Rama is there, a white Rama is there in Nashik. Gora Rama, Kala Rama. These colours have been dedicated to these gods by human beings.

There is no colour. Colour is a concept. Everything is colourless, (this is also a concept). Prior to beingness, did you know anything about colour? No! Therefore, instead of posing such questions, try to identify yourself. God does not have any specific colour. Does the sky have any colour? Black sky, white sky, yellow sky? They are imaginary colours given by people. There is no meaning to all that! It is meaningless. How will this knowledge help you? Where is the sense? You are wasting your energy!

Some people are worshipping the black Rama, the blue Rama, or the white Rama. There are all kinds of colourful Ramas in Nashik. There are many Hanumans here, too. They have no meaning. It is a meaningless question. When you know the Reality, why ask such a meaningless question? It is just like a question that has come from a baby! You are not a baby at all.

Read some books. Read I Am That, read Selfless Self, read Master of Self-Realization, read Ramana Maharshi. God does not have any colour. Rama, Krishna, Shankara, these are the gods and the goddesses, the supernatural powers. Where were those gods and goddesses prior to beingness? If there is no consciousness, who will be there to talk about Rama, Krishna, and all the rest?

This is a meaningless question. If you are still asking these silly questions, then you need to read more and digest more. When it is your time to leave the body, which coloured Rama are you going to accept? The blue Rama, white Rama or black Rama? What do you mean by Rama or Krishna? These are gods, supernatural powers. They are just names that have been given to the supernatural power, Rama, Krishna, and others.

Forget about all this, and concentrate on the matter at hand – how you are going to identify yourself. Ask questions on how you are going to emerge from all these difficulties! You must identify yourself!

p.strong: 195. I wonder if, when I die, when I leave this body, I will realize that Selfless Self?

section.p: Who says you are not realized? Realization means Spontaneous Conviction: “I am not the body. I was not the body. I am not going to remain the body”. These are the concepts that have been impressed upon your Presence. To have strong Conviction, at the initial stage, you have to undergo strict meditation, and recite the Mantra at all times.

Then reciting the Mantra will be Spontaneous, even in deep sleep you will recite the Mantra. You will find that the Mantra is going on inside. It is an invaluable Mantra, but you have to follow it strictly, and you have to follow it seriously. Taking the Mantra is not enough: you have to follow it to the letter.

When the doctor gives you some medicine for an illness, you must take it, from time to time. If you don’t follow the prescription, the medicine will not work. Similarly, the Master has prescribed medicine for you: Mantra meditation. You have to take this medicine regularly, so that it cleanses your body. It is a spiritual body, an illusory body.

You have to take the medicine if you want to discard all the illusory concepts. It is like you are installing an antivirus software into your computer, so that no bugs or viruses will be able to enter inside.

p.strong: FINAL PLEA FROM SRI RAMAKANT MAHARAJ
“Please, Just Accept the Reality!”

section.p: My Dearest Devotees,

Yes, my dear friends, we will have a short discussion. There is one basic thing: as long as you are counting yourself as the body-form, it will be highly impossible for you to accept the Reality. It is an open fact, that this body is not your identity at all. I am shouting this out all the time, in different ways. Listen to me! I am talking about Presence. Keep your attention on Presence, and not on all the illusory layers, that have covered over your Presence, your Reality.

Now that you have the knowledge, listen to your own Master, that Invisible Presence within you. Self-Realization will only arise, if you discard all the dust with a spiritual broom. Are you ready to remove all the dust, that you have accumulated? All I am requesting from you is that you undergo the meditation. It is the only way, the only medium, through which you can remove all this dust, all the garbage of illusory concepts.

You are happy to accept quotations from the great teachers and Masters, and indulge in re-quoting their words, parrot-fashion: “So and so, says it like this, and so and so, says it like that!” The fact that you are happy with all these second-hand quotations is a dangerous thing. You are hooked on the words! As you know, words are not Ultimate Truth, they are only indicators or pointers. You say, “I read I Am That every day. I have read Ramana Maharshi a thousand times”. You are inflating the ego by making these claims. That ego is dangerous for your spiritual career. Please don’t do this! Because even if you have read so many books, and taken the Mantra from me, it is totally meaningless. You either accept the Reality, or you do not. There are no half-way measures. There should not be any duality. That is the drawback of the human being. Read the Reader!

Read books, but don’t become addicted to them. If you have read all these spiritual books, why are you coming here? It means that whatever knowledge you have, is only theoretical knowledge. It has not touched you. You have not yet identified yourself. Don’t become a Master of spirituality, become a Master of Selfless Self!

The body is not your identity at all. When it is your time to leave the body? There should not be any concept of “I am dying” around. Prepare now! The purpose behind spirituality is that you should be fearless. Be strong, and stand on your own feet! You are Ultimate Truth, but you are not willing to accept it.

You are to dissolve the concept of “body-attachment”. Start from you! Everything starts from you, and everything ends within you. You are the central point of this world. If that Invisible Presence within the body were not there, what value does the body have, and what is the value of all your relations? There is no value. There are no relations! Please just accept the Reality!

You have to teach your mind, teach your ego, and intellect. Become their Master! It is very simple. Always remain with your Selfless Self. Ignore the mind, and the thoughts. Don’t waste your energy, by giving these things attention. If you follow my instructions, and meditate sincerely, and recite the Naam Mantra, all the dust will be removed. And, you will be delightfully surprised at the transformation.

After knowing the Reality, forget about all the illusory concepts! Because they are all decorated notes, fake currency, that has no value in the market. Use this approach to throw everything out. You are not fake notes. You are Reality! You are Reality!

When I am sitting here, talking with you, there is a flow coming from my Master which I am sharing with you. This can happen to you also. It is not impossible, if you become one with your Master, and totally accept the Reality that the Master is conveying.

After Spontaneous Conviction, there will be permanent happiness and peace, fearlessness and a stress-free life. You will not need any material cause, to make you happy, or peaceful. Happiness is your nature. Peace is your nature.

Don’t underestimate yourself! You are not a little, insignificant thing. You are Great! That Greatness is already within you. Stop ignoring it! The human body is a golden opportunity for you to finally know who you really are. So, please don’t waste any more time!
Just please accept the Reality!

title.p: PART TWO Excerpts of Talks from U.S.A. October to November 2016

title.strong: Introduction From Sri Ramakant Maharaj SEPTEMBER 14, 2016
title.p: Jai Guru,

As you know, I don’t wish to deliver discussions on spirituality. I strongly expect that all seekers should have practical Spiritual Conviction. As you are aware, meditation is a strong base which leads to Spontaneous Realization. Without any expectations, I would like to share Ultimate Truth amongst all the seekers. I strongly feel that everyone should have complete spiritual happiness, peacefulness, a tension-free life, and a fearless life.

To have the Spontaneous Spiritual Conviction, the seeker’s sincere and deep involvement is necessary. Approaching different Masters, reading endless spiritual books, or merely dry spiritual discussions, will not help you to have Spontaneous Conviction. In the light of the above, I would like to implant Ultimate Truth amidst the Spiritual Heart of all. For this, your devotional participation is strongly expected.
With regards and blessings to all. My affectionate spiritual feelings towards all seekers.

p.emphasis: Jai Sadguru!

p.strong: SEPTEMBER 12, 2016 SILVER SPRING, U.S.A.

p.emphasis: “You are the worshipped. You are the worshipper. You are the worship.”

p.strong: 1. The Quest to Find the Jewel of “Who Am I?”

section.p: Q: I’ve recently started using the “I am Brahman, Brahman I am” Mantra, and it is interesting that it seems to dissolve the questions.

Maharaj: See, at the initial stage, it is necessary to recite, “I am Brahman, Brahman I am”, because through that Mantra, you are hammering your Selfless Self. To forget your body-identity, to dissolve all the concepts, you need to recite the Mantra, Aham Brahmasmi, or, “I am Brahman, Brahman I am”, like that. These are just words. We use language for the purpose of understanding. We have given words their meanings. Through language, we are trying to identify ourselves in a real sense.

Meditation is the anti-virus software. It cleans all the illusion so that you can uncover your knowledge. You have supernatural power. That power is unknown to you. Your Inner Master is very strong. The Invisible Listener within you is the internal Master, Ultimate Truth, God, Brahman, Paramatman.

Casual spirituality will not help you. Your total involvement is most important. “Oh, I have listened to a good lecture”, “that Master gives great Talks”. No! Who is listening to these lectures? Find out! Try to concentrate on the Invisible Concentrator within you. It is called Brahman, Atman. Therefore I have told you, apart from Selfless Self, there is no Brahman, no Atman. You have so many concepts: “last birth”, “future birth”, “last destiny”, “karma”, “dharma”. What is “karma”? Whose karma? Whose birth? There is no birth. You are unborn. Forget about your last birth. Can you even remember anything about your present birth? No! Dissolve all your concepts!

Be free! So many illusory concepts are wrapped around us. Be free! This human body is an opportunity for you to find out “Who am I?” Stop depending on theoretical, literal knowledge. Approach your own Master because you are Ultimate Truth. There is nothing else that is Ultimate, except for your Selfless Self.

Be serious! The quest to find the jewel of “Who am I?” is a full-time pursuit. It is not a casual, part-time endeavour, but demands a serious approach. You are embarking on the greatest adventure of your life. Your strong involvement is essential. Spirituality helps you to stand on your own feet.

We all share an intense fear of death. We live with tension and anxiety, in a fast-paced world. There is no peace and happiness because we are neglecting our own identity. Directly or indirectly, consciously or unconsciously, we are protecting our illusory concepts. Forget them all! Let them go!

The body-form is the medium through which you can identify yourself. Everything came out of nothing, and everything dissolves back into nothing. The main purpose of spirituality is so, that you can identify yourself in a real sense.

This identification leads to the Spontaneous Conviction that “I was not the body, I am not the body, and I am not going to remain the body”. Bow down to your Selfless Self. Everything is within you. We have been depending on an imaginary God who will help us, or a Master who will solve all our problems. “Oh God, do something,” or, “Oh Master, help me.” Why? A child depends on his parents, but you are no longer a child. You are a grown-up! You are a spiritual grown-up.

I am placing before you the facts of your existence, the facts of the Invisible Listener within you. It is free of charge. Presence is free of charge. You are unaware of your Presence. Presence does not know that, “I am Presence”, just like the sky does not know, “I am sky”. Your Presence is beyond that, but we are neglecting all this. The Master is inviting the attention of the Invisible Listener. The Master re-ignites the spark of your Inner Master. Be with You always!

Q: Over the past eight years, I have read so much, I have done so many practices that people have shown me, but I just feel more confused, and even more lost, than ever before. I am tired of it all, and just need everything to stop. I understand the teachings intellectually, a lot of it, but there is no conviction at all. It still seems like a fantasy, and basically, just a good idea. I just don’t know what to do anymore.

Maharaj: It is a fact, an open fact, that the body is not your identity. Forget about spirituality for a moment. You know that intellectually this body is not your identity at all. You were once a small child, and now you have become a young man. After that, you will become an old man, and then, willingly or unwillingly, you will have to leave this body. That means the body is not your identity. Fact! Forget about spirituality. Intellectually, you know that the body is transient.
The entire world is projected out of your Presence. If there were no Presence in this body, what value does this body have? The body is only important because of your Presence.
And therefore, I am inviting the attention of the Invisible Listener within you, that you are Ultimate Truth.

How can this knowledge be absorbed? It can only be absorbed with meditation. Just talking about spiritual knowledge is meaningless. Meditation is the only medium, because through it, you are inviting the attention of the Invisible Meditator within you, that you are Ultimate Truth. All the time, you are hammering: Aham Brahmasmi, “I am Brahman, I am Brahman”! You have accepted the body as your identity, either as a man, or a woman. With constant hammering, there will be acceptance of your true identity as Ultimate Reality, Ultimate Truth, as the Holder of the body.

p.strong: 2. You Have Forgotten Your Identity

section.p: Maharaj: Have you read Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj’s books? I Am That? Selfless Self? Ramana Maharshi and all these spiritual books?
Q: Yes, all the books.

Maharaj: So, you have a good background. Everyone has spiritual knowledge. Everyone knows about the Atman, Paramatman, etc, but only in a theoretical way. Practical knowledge is most important, because what has happened is that we have accepted the body-form as our identity. Prior to beingness, there was no body-form. There was no shape at all. But because of our long association with the body-form, we have accepted it as our identity. We have forgotten our identity, our Spontaneous, Invisible Presence which is our Reality.

We have accepted the body-form as our identity. The body-form has many needs. Everybody wants happiness, peacefulness, a tension-free life, a fearless life. These are the four things that are required in one’s life, so the purpose of spirituality is to identify oneself in a real sense. We are identifying ourselves in the body-form. As a matter of fact, you are shapeless, there is no birth, there is no body at all. Prior to beingness there was no body, and after leaving the body, nothing will remain.

So the purpose behind all of this, is just to identify yourself in a real sense. We are identifying ourselves in the body-form, and that is illusion. To dissolve these illusions, spirituality plays a very important part. What is spirituality? Just to identify oneself.
We are identifying ourselves in this form, and that is an illusion. The question is: “How were you prior to beingness, and how will you be, after leaving the body?” Let us find out!

p.strong: 3. Investigate Yourself

section.p: Q: Can you give some instruction by which meditation can be made incessant? Meaning, you can meditate for a few hours, and after that...

Maharaj: See, meditation doesn’t mean sitting like this [Maharaj poses with eyes closed]. Meditation means involvement, total involvement. I will give you a small example. If someone abuses you, using very bad language, you will usually want to get revenge. You will be thinking about that person 24/7. If someone says, “Forget about it”, you will not be able to. You are focussed, and one-pointed. This kind of concentration, is the quality that is needed for meditation. You are obsessed with trying to find Reality.

Q: Sometimes I get this feeling...and the voice becomes so strong. It says, “There is no value in anything you have done”. It feels like what I am doing is just a waste of time. And that feeling is very strong.

Maharaj: The thoughts will appear upon your Presence, until there is Conviction. Various thoughts are appearing upon your Presence. Good thoughts, bad thoughts, depressive thoughts are appearing. You are prior to all of that. And therefore, I am inviting the attention of the Invisible Presence. Concentrate! You are watching this, and you are witnessing, all that is happening inside. I am talking about the Invisible Witness in you.
Q: Yes, I can feel that.
Maharaj: You are to dissolve that “I”. To say “I” your Presence is needed. If there is no Presence, who will talk about the “I”?
Q: Again, the identity with the body comes back into play.

Maharaj: It does not come and go. Is the sky coming and going anywhere? These difficulties are not your fault. I am not blaming you. It happens because you consider yourself to be the body-form, and have accepted it as your identity, and that is why you have these difficulties. Meditation is needed to remedy the mistaken notion you have about yourself. Meditation is the foundation. It is the anti-virus software. You have to install this software, so that you are always hammering yourself, with “I am Brahman, Brahman I am”.

Take, for example, the way a criminal is interrogated by the police. They say, “You are a criminal. We have this evidence against you”. But the criminal at first, will deny it, and reply, “No, I am not a criminal.” But still they persist, “Yes, you are a criminal! We know you are guilty, we have evidence. You are lying! Admit it!” Under pressure, the police succeed in breaking down the criminal’s resistance. He finally admits to his crime.
You are to hammer yourself in this way: “You are Brahman! You are Brahman!”, breaking down the mind, crushing the ego, until there is acceptance. “Okay, then yes I am Brahman!”
Q: Now, can I have your darshan?
Maharaj: Darshan?
Q: Darshan, a personal blessing.

Maharaj: You already have blessings. You are not separate from me. It is only the bodies that are different. Put your own hand on your head, and bless yourself. Darshan is okay as a formality, or, as a sign of respect. However, blessing yourself is most important for you to recognize, that you are the only one, who can give, or receive blessings. Take your own darshan!

p.strong: 4. Who is Watching the Dream?

section.p: Maharaj: We have so many concepts. We have a lot of literal knowledge: “I am somebody. I am an individual. I am a separate, independent entity. I am a man. I am a woman. I am a personality, etc”.

We have some knowledge of karma, dharma, and many different concepts, various traditions, rituals and religions. We have wrapped all these concepts around us, and then we try to live our lives within the illusory circle of these concepts. Your Spontaneous, Invisible Presence is Ultimate Truth. However, you do not know that. Your Invisible Listener is a Master, but because we are measuring ourselves in the body-form, we have forgotten all about our Master.

Nothing is there except your Selfless Self. Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, these are the names that have been given to your Reality, your formless Reality. Therefore, I am inviting the attention of the Invisible Listener within you, that you are Ultimate Truth. We are not accepting this Truth so easily because of the impact and impressions of the body. It is a fact, even if you think about this intellectually, that prior to beingness there were no bodies, no concepts, and no “I am”, also. When Presence clicked with the body, you became aware of your existence, and announced, “I am somebody!”. You are nobody, and, at the same time, you are everybody. Your Presence is just like the space or the sky. You are everywhere.
Therefore, do not measure yourself in the body-form. Meditation will dissolve all your illusory concepts. This life of yours is illusory. It is nothing but a long dream! You are watching the dream.

What is the purpose of meditation? Through the meditation, you can identify yourself in a real sense. Through meditation you are inviting the attention of the Invisible Meditator within you that you are Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God. Through meditation you are hammering yourself all the time. It is an open fact: We start as a small child, we grow up to become a young man, and finally we become an old man. These are the stages of the body. Who is acting within the body? Who is watching the dream? Identify yourself!

It is a fact that the entire world is projected out of your Presence. Your Presence is Invisible Presence, Anonymous Presence, Unidentified Presence. And therefore, the first thing I wish to say, is to stop considering yourself as the body-form. When you are measuring yourself in the body-form, you are seriously underestimating your Selfless Self.

p.strong: 5. Know That You Are the Central Point of the Universe

section.p: Maharaj: Do not underestimate yourself. Carry out your responsibilities, as if you are acting in a drama, or movie. In the illusory world, we have many relations. We are mothers, fathers, aunts, brothers, sisters, grandparents, etc. We play so many different roles. Act them well! All our relatives came with the body-form, and they will disappear with the body-form. Take care of your family, but at the same time, know that all your family members are a big illusion!

All our needs, and all our requirements came along with the body. We only need spiritual knowledge because we have forgotten our identity. There was no spiritual knowledge prior to beingness? We are not beggars, yet we behave like beggars, pleading: “Oh God, bless me. Do something for me. Help me!” Wherever you go, you are bowing down to someone else, something other. The Master says, “Why are you bowing to everyone else, but yourself? Bow down to yourself! Bow to Selfless Self!” You are the only Power.

After Conviction, you will not bow to anyone. This is not to say that you will disrespect anyone, but, you will know that you are the central point of the universe. Everything is within you. There is nothing else.
Q: You say it is not difficult to achieve this Ultimate Truth?
Maharaj: Of course it is not difficult!
Q: And yet, it takes time for most people.

Maharaj: Suppose it has been dark in a cave for five hundred years. If you enter with a light, will the darkness say, “I have been here for so many years, I cannot leave immediately. It will take some time?” Of course not. Enlightenment is instant. When the light of knowledge shines on the darkness of ignorance, the change is instantaneous.

p.strong: 6. You Are Your Own Master!

section.p: Maharaj: Literal knowledge will not help you. Bookish knowledge will not help you. Be practical. You have a lot of knowledge. The test is whether that knowledge will help you, when your time comes to leave the body. The answer is No! Theoretical or book knowledge cannot help you because it is material, second-hand knowledge. Will it help you at the time of leaving the body? The answer is “No!”
We may understand that we are Brahman, Atman, Paramatman or God, intellectually, but we have not accepted this as our Reality, our practical Reality. We do not have Spontaneous Conviction.

All the concepts that are related to the body-base are supposed to dissolve. It is a very simple process, that only requires your strong involvement. Self-involvement is most important. “I was not the body, I am not the body, and I am not going to remain the body.” The body was not my identity, and the body is not going to remain my identity at all. This is an open fact.

The Invisible Presence within you is Final Truth. It has strong Power, strong Energy. Therefore, stop viewing yourself as the body-form. Though you are holding the body, there should be the Spontaneous Conviction that “I am not the body, and I am not going to remain the body!” The mind, the ego, and the intellect came along with the body, and will dissolve along with the body. All your concepts, needs, and requirements came along with the body. Concepts like “God” and “Master”, are just that, concepts! For you, or anyone else, to say “God”, your Presence is required. To say “Master”, your Presence is required. You are your own Master! But because of your long association with the body, you have forgotten your identity. And so, to identify yourself in a real sense, meditation is needed.

There are always many questions regarding the Naam Mantra. The Naam Mantra is words! There are so many words – Aham Brahmasmi is a word, “I am Brahman”, Soham is a word. Mantra meditation is a medium. Through the meditation, you are inviting the attention of the Invisible Meditator, and reminding yourself that you are Brahman. You will forget your body-identity, and then, the Spontaneous Conviction will arise.

Everyone wants to know about death. What is death? There is no birth, and there is no death, because you are unborn. It is a fact! You may not accept it just now, but with meditation, Spontaneous Conviction will come, and then you will know!

We have a lot of attachment to the body-form, which has obscured our true identity. That is why, at the initial stage, you are to undergo the discipline of meditation. Why is meditation required? To develop Spontaneous Conviction.

p.strong: SEPTEMBER 16, 2016, SILVER SPRING, U.S.A.

p.emphasis: “Why the Naam Mantra? To keep the mind, ego, and the intellect busy. To remain thoughtless, and to erase all body-based memories.”

p.strong: 7. You Are Nobody, and You Are Everybody

section.p: Maharaj: Has everyone read the books, I Am That and Selfless Self? We are here to identity ourselves in a real sense. It can happen through this human body.

Body-based-knowledge is not tolerable. “I am somebody”, is not tolerable. So, once you have identified yourself perfectly, you will know, “I am not the body, I was not the body, and I am not going to remain the body”.

This is a golden opportunity. Your Presence is invaluable Presence. Presence does not know “I am Presence”. This Reality, your Reality has been given many different names, such as “Presence”, “Spirit”, God, or “Energy”, but you can call it anything you wish. It is just like the space. The space does not know that, “I am space”, and the sky does not know that, “I am sky”. Try to identify yourself in a real sense. This misidentification of “I am somebody”, is to be erased.
You are nobody, and you are everybody. You are beyond the sky. The sky is everywhere. If you go anywhere in the world, the sky will be there. But the sky is not conscious of it being the sky.

p.strong: 8. Do Not Underestimate Yourself

section.p: Q: What is devotion? How do you practice devotion?
Maharaj: Yes, good question.

Self-involvement is most important. We have been trying to identify ourselves using words. We have been using language, to try and identify ourselves. You cannot identify yourself using language. Through devotion, you can identify the Deity within you. Devote your time to Selfless Self. Get to know your Inner Master. Your Spontaneous Presence is a Deity, it is God, Ultimate Truth, Brahman, Atman, Paramatman. You are a Deity. The Deity is not separate from you. It happens because we are measuring ourselves in the body-form, thinking, “I am a man or woman”, etc. You are Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master. It is a fact, but you are not accepting the facts. There are so many questions appearing upon your Presence. All these body-based questions will be solved when you identify yourself in a real sense.

There is so much confusion around because we have read so many books, and countless concepts have been impressed upon us. We must break the vicious circle, of body-related concepts. Try to absorb the knowledge which you already have.
Q: Sri Ramakant, after reading Selfless Self, the spaces in my meditation where the “I” disappears have become longer.

Maharaj: “I” appeared upon your Presence. If there is no Presence, who says “I”? You are prior to “I”. “I” is a word. “I”, “you”, “he”, “she”. They are indications, identifications, physical identifications.
Q: But I have begun experiencing so much peace in that. Before my trip, I experienced so much resistance to get here, and it’s like I had to make myself come.

Maharaj: Your experience is okay. It is a good experience. But at the last stage, the Ultimate Stage, there is no experience and no experiencer, and no witness. Experience appeared upon your Presence. When you come, closer and closer, to your Selfless Self, some good experiences will appear, like exceptional peacefulness, and happiness. There will be spiritual intoxication, without any material cause.

You are already whole, self-sufficient, but you are always underestimating yourself, thinking there is something else that is greater than you, some supernatural power that is regulating this world. As I said, do not underestimate yourself. Your Selfless Self is very strong. There is no birth, and no death – these are the concepts.

No one knows that he was born, and no one knows that he has died. You are unborn. Does the sky have any birth or death? Does the sky know hell or heaven? Good deed, bad deed, bad energy, good energy. What is good or bad? We made a circle, and within that circle, we have been trying to live our lives. Break the circle with meditation. It is the only way.

p.strong: 9. All Questions Are Body-Based

section.p: Q: How do you practice Self-enquiry? What is the process?

Maharaj: Self-enquiry just means that we are unaware of our identity. Self-enquiry begins when we ask the question, “Who am I?” There are no questions at the advanced stage. Though our identity has been called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, “God”, we do not know what Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, or “God” is. The Questioner Itself, who asks “Who am I?” is Ultimate Truth. You have just forgotten your identity.

Q: I have gradually been feeling more bliss, than at other times, for some reason. It was stronger than ever, when I was sitting here waiting. I was wondering if I could share this with my mother? She is very old. Is there any way I could help her? I mean, I have told her about this stuff, and she has a little bit of interest.
Maharaj: All your relations, including your mother, came along with the body. You are not the body at all. If you wish to share this knowledge with your mother, then share it.

p.strong: 10. I Am Nothing to Do With the World

section.p: Q: What type of meditation should I do? Should I ask the Self, “Who am I?” or meditate on the Mantra?

Maharaj: You are to recite the Mantra. Aham Brahmasmi, “I am Brahman”, Soham, there are so many. Every lineage uses different words. The principle behind it is, that through the Mantra, you can identify yourself. These are key-words, through which you are hammering your Selfless Self. As long as I am living as a man or a woman, there will be bodily limitations,
Q: Why is the Realization of Ultimate Truth so scarce and rare?

Maharaj: See, “Realization” means Conviction. “Realization” means Spontaneous Conviction. At present, we are saying, “I am somebody”, “I am a man or woman”, and that is illusion. So I am talking about that Realization, that Spontaneous Conviction: “I am Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master. I am nothing to do with the world”.

This is an illusory world. The moment Presence clicks with the body, you can see the world. Where is the world prior to beingness? That means the entire world is projected out of your Presence. Therefore for teaching purposes, I define, That which cannot be defined, as “Unidentified” Identity, “Invisible” Identity, “Anonymous” Identity. It cannot be imagined. You cannot guess what it is like. This Reality is supposed to be totally absorbed.

p.strong: 11. Why Did Spirit Forget Itself?

section.p: Q: What is the benefit of the Naam Mantra and initiation into the lineage?

Maharaj: The Naam Mantra invites the attention of the Meditator, and reminds you that you are Brahman, Atman, Paramatman. This is the beginning stage, the foundation. You have forgotten your identity, always measuring yourself in the body-form. Basically, you are not the body-form, you are shapeless. You could say that we have lost our memory, and accepted the body as our identity, which it is not. To dissolve the body-identity, there is initiation: “I am Brahman, Brahman I am”.
Q: Why did Spirit forget itself?

Maharaj: There is no reason at all. Spirit does not know that, “I am Spirit”. Spirit is the name given to the Presence, the Invisible Presence, Power, Energy. Does electricity know, “I am lighting up all these things”? There is no reason “Why Spirit?”. We are trying to reason and guess, using the intellect. Your Presence in the world is Spontaneous Presence.
Q: Do you teach that mula-maya, all is a dream?
Maharaj: What “maya”? There is no maya. Maya is a concept.
Q: Correct. But mula-maya?
Maharaj: Maya is the name given to the illusory concepts.
Q: Okay, great. And you teach that?
Maharaj: What is that maya? What was maya prior to beingness? Was there any maya there? Maya is illusion, an illusory concept.
Q: Of course. So, you teach that existence is like a dream?

Maharaj: Of course, of course. It is not that “I think” it is a dream. It is a fact! Not “I think”. It is a fact! It is Reality! The entire world is projected out of your Presence. All things appear upon your Presence. It is not my thinking, it is universal, a universal fact.

p.strong: 12. Is Love a Concept?

section.p: Q: Is love a concept as well?

Maharaj: Of course! Everything is a concept, without exception. When did you come across love? “Love” and “affection” are concepts which appeared upon your Presence. When the Spirit clicked with the body, love, and affection, appeared before you. What is love? Prior to beingness there was no love. After leaving the body, will there be any love? As long as we are measuring ourselves in the body-form, love, affection, and many other concepts, will be there. My mother, my father, my husband, my wife, my sister, my brother, my Master, my God – thousands of concepts rule our lives.

When the body dissolves, there is nothing there, no concepts, no relations. All relations are body-based relations, and you are not the body at all, you were not the body at all, you are not going to remain the body at all, because you are Ultimate Truth, Final Truth, Final Reality. Absorb this Reality with meditation. Reading, talking and listening are not enough.
Q: I think of bliss, and impersonal love, as sort of the same thing. And to me, when I experience that, it is a fact for me. But then at other times I forget it, and then it is no longer a fact.
Maharaj: Do not underestimate yourself. There is nothing to forget, and nothing to remember. Dissolve that so-called “I”!

p.strong: 13. Mind Means the Flow of Thoughts

section.p: Q: When I do the meditation, I can’t help but have thoughts coming through. What do I have to do to stop the thoughts?

Maharaj: It is the nature of the mind. The mind means the flow of thoughts. You are witnessing the thoughts. You are different from the thoughts. You are witnessing, both good and bad, thoughts. Do not try to control the thoughts, just let them be. let them be. Suppose you are sitting here indoors, and then something happens outside. You would just ignore it. Thoughts are flowing all the time. It is up to you, to choose which ones to accept, and which ones to ignore.

p.strong: 14. Body-Based Knowledge

section.p: Q: Maharaj, why are we so scared to find this Ultimate Truth?

Maharaj: It is not like that. Because we have had so many concepts engraved upon us, we have accepted that “I am somebody”, “I am a man” or “I am a woman”. After Conviction, you will know that you are not the body at all.
Q: So, we are all Brahman, the Brahman itself. Is that correct? That is the premise?

Maharaj: It is more than a premise. It is a fact! Stop measuring yourself as the body-form. The bodies have different appearances. Q: No problem, so that is the premise. So the question that goes begging is: Why is one Brahman willing to harm another Brahman, if we are one and the same?

Maharaj: Because this is body-based knowledge. Brahman is not harming anything. Does the sky harm any other sky? Does the American sky harm the Indian sky? Sky doesn’t know, “I am sky”, Presence doesn’t know, “I am Presence”, Brahman doesn’t know, “I am Brahman”. Brahman is a word given to that Ultimate Truth that you are. The Invisible Listener within you is Ultimate Truth. It is called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master. It is not an individual. There is no duality at all.
Q: So, can one say that it is okay for this Brahman, to harm that Brahman?
Maharaj: No, no. There is no, “I am Brahman, I will harm another Brahman”. There aren’t two different Brahmans.
Q: Okay, so it is one Brahman. But there are two separate bodies. If I harm this body in some way, do I just turn around and say it’s okay, because we are all one and the same?
Maharaj: Egoistic concepts are there: “I am someone”. Erase that “someone”. Brahman does not harm, cannot harm, anyone else.
Q: But if harm should happen – and I think you said earlier, that nothing is really being done – then no deeds are being done, ultimately.

Maharaj: There is no doer, and there is no deed. We are measuring ourselves in the body-form, and therefore, all these concepts appear. I am talking about that Invisible Presence within you, where there is nothing. Nothing is there: no thoughts are there, and no concepts are there. The concept Brahman is also not there. And as I told you, Brahman is just the name given to that Ultimate Truth. Except your Selfless Self, there is no Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master. So, Brahman is not harming some other Brahman – like, “this Brahman is different from that Brahman”.
Q: Okay, so in other words, what you do to others, you are doing to yourself, right?

Maharaj: As long as you are considering yourself in the body-form, these concepts will keep appearing. There is no other. No other exists! It is only because we are measuring ourselves in the body-form, that the concepts of “he” is there, “she” is there, “it” is there, and, therefore, they appear. Prior to beingness were any of these concepts there?
Q: But, if somebody harms someone?
Maharaj: When did you come across another, a someone? Prior to beingness, was there “someone” there? And, after leaving the body, is there going to be someone there?
Q: Okay, what if this body harms another body...?

Maharaj: I am shouting. I have to keep shouting at you! Because you are measuring yourself in the body-form, therefore, you are under the impression that there are different bodies. There are different houses, but the sky is one. This is called a bungalow, and this is a cottage. They are called by different names. If all the houses collapse, what would happen to the sky? Nothing! Stop considering yourself to be a separate entity or individual. It is because of this misidentification, that all these concepts keep on appearing, and causing you problems.

Spirit or Presence is One. It is only our bodies that are different. It is the basic thing. Since we are measuring ourselves in the body-form, all these concepts appear. It is only the bodies that are different.

The human body is an opportunity for you to identify yourself. Every moment is very important, very valuable. Complete concentration, complete involvement is most important. We are not accepting this. Everybody knows, “I am not the body at all”. Everybody knows that. Some day or other, willingly or unwillingly, we are to leave this body. What remains?

p.strong: 15. Egoistic Meditation

section.p: Q: Can you please say something about a practice that is silent, without words, that supports the aware Presence in recognizing itself?

Maharaj: After Conviction, there will be spontaneous silence. At present, there is no silence, only violence, because we have too many body-based concepts, clambering, and chattering continuously. Directly, or indirectly, we have become victims of our mind, ego, and intellect. To identify yourself in a real sense, intense meditation is needed.
Q: Should the Naam Mantra be recited continuously, after initiation?

Maharaj: Recite the Naam Mantra at all times, continuously, at every available moment. You need not sit in one position to do it. Attend to your duties, your work, and while you are doing this, keep reciting the Mantra at every opportunity.
Q: And will it still the mind, so that the mind is not all over the place?

Maharaj: The mind is always stinging you, pinching you from behind. Concentrating on the Naam is a kind of cleaning process. The mind will not let you concentrate on yourself. So, perseverance is needed at the beginning.
Q: Vipassana Meditation does not use a Mantra. It is basically about observing the breath, and observing the bodily sensations. What are your thoughts on that?

Maharaj: It provides a temporary relief. You can practice Vipassana for a time – one week, two weeks, or one month, but after leaving the Vipassana Centre, you will be back where you started. Again, you will find yourself at the same place.
Q: But what if you practice vipassana every day? I mean, what are your thoughts on vipassana versus...

Maharaj: The principle behind vipassana, or any practice, is to identify yourself in a real sense. Whatever practice you are doing, is based on the principle of identifying yourself, in the true sense. These are processes. Vipassana is a process, and meditation is also a process. But meditation is a strong process, through which, you can identify yourself, within a short time. It enables you to discard all the illusory concepts, and uncover your identity in a simple, quick and direct way.
Q: But in vipassana meditation there is no mantra.

Maharaj: Go ahead, do vipassana meditation if you wish, but you are posing yourself in the body-form, and then, doing some vipassana meditation, therefore, it is meaningless. “I am somebody who is doing vipassana meditation”. This is egoistic meditation. You are to forget about your bodily identity. “I am doing some sadhana”, “I am doing some meditation”, That “I” must dissolve. The process of meditation will dissolve that “I”.
Through the meditation, you are inviting the attention of the Invisible Meditator within you, that you are Ultimate Truth, you are Final Truth. You are hammering yourself, non-stop.
Q: In I Am That, Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj says, “A quiet mind is all you need. When the mind is quiet, all else happens, as it should”. Does it matter, therefore, how one quiets one’s mind?

Maharaj: After knowing your Selfless Self, the mind will not remain. The existence of the mind appeared upon your Presence. The existence of the mind, ego, and intellect appeared upon your Presence. Presence does not have any mind, ego, or intellect. And therefore, try to identify yourself in a real sense. These are the words. I am conveying this to you, through words. After Conviction, the mind, ego, and intellect will not remain. Where was the mind prior to beingness? Where will the mind be after leaving the body? The mind does not have an independent identity. You have given birth to the mind, the ego, and the intellect. Without your Presence, how can you identify these?
Through the meditation, you will be able to control your mind. The mind will be controlled spontaneously. Eventually, you will be thoughtless, as you were prior to beingness.

The mind means the flow of thoughts. There are continuous thoughts flowing which you define as the “mind”. The thoughts enter the mind, and travel to the intellect. The intellect makes a decision, and then the ego implements that decision. This is the process, the functioning of the mind, ego and intellect. It is of no concern to you because you are separate from all that.

p.strong: 16. One Master

section.p: Maharaj: All questions will be solved within you. You do not need to go anywhere to see any Master, I tell you this because that Masterly essence is within you. My Master, Nisargadatta Maharaj, says, “I am not making you a disciple, I am making you a Master”. You are already a Master, but you have forgotten your identity.
Q: I am confused about the best time to activate the Inner Master, and do Self-enquiry.

Maharaj: I’ve told you that “Inner Master” and “Outer Master” are concepts. There is no difference between the internal Master and the external Master. We say internal Master and external Master, just for understanding purposes. There is no difference between any “internal Master” and “external
Master”. The Master is the Master! One Master! There is no inner, and no outer, no external or internal.

p.strong: 17. The Naam Mantra

section.p: Q: Who is eligible to receive the Naam Mantra?
Maharaj: Everybody!
Q: Are there any requirements for that?

Maharaj: If you wish to receive the Naam Mantra, then you should be sincere. I am not expecting anything from you, but you should be sincere, in your acceptance of this sacred Mantra. It happens so often that things which are given free of charge, are not considered to be of any value. I am not expecting anything from you except your involvement, which is most important, nothing else. It is not a problem for me to give the Naam Mantra, but there should not be any ill-faith, or anything like that. Be serious about the Naam Mantra.
Q: How do you practice it?
Maharaj: I will guide you. You are to recite the Mantra according to your breathing. Synchronise the Mantra with your breath.
Q: Because sometimes it feels like we are torturing ourselves.

Maharaj: It is not necessary to torture yourself. It is a very simple thing. Why torture the body? It is a simple thing. It is your knowledge, not the knowledge of Brahman, Atman, Paramatman. The Invisible Listener within you is called Ultimate Truth, but you are neglecting it, you are underestimating yourself. You say, “God is great,” “Oh God bless me” – okay, no problem. But if your Presence is not there, how can you identify God? To say “God” your Presence is essential. It is a prerequisite. If there is no Presence in the body, who will talk about the God? Who will talk about this world?
At the initial stage, I am insisting on meditation because we have forgotten our identity.

You may have read a good number of books, and you may have listened to a number of Masters, but you are not reading, and not listening, as if they were the Listener’s story, the Reader’s story. Your story! The Master is narrating your story. Therefore, listen to it as your biography. Read Selfless Self as your story. It is your biography!
Q: Can you also recite Om Nama Shivaya, or some other mantra as well?

Maharaj: Recite one mantra, one mantra only. But do not take one mantra from one Master, and then go to another Master for some other mantra. Have some loyalty or faith in your mantra. Any mantra will do. Do not have a wavering mind, “Today I will get one mantra from this Master, and tomorrow, I will get another one from that Master”. Collecting mantras is meaningless!
Q: In the morning, if you have a habit of ten minutes...
Maharaj: Okay, no problem. With full faith, full involvement. Any mantra will do. Aham Brahmasmi is okay, Shivoham – any mantra. But, be faithful, and recite it with full trust.

p.strong: 18. Playing with Words

section.p: Q: I am kind of confused. You say that nothing comes from something, and then something becomes nothing?
Maharaj: I said that everything came out of nothing, and everything dissolves back into nothing. Everything came out of nothing, and everything dissolves within nothing.
Q: But how can something become nothing? And nothing become something?

Maharaj: We are playing with the words. Try to identify yourself, so that this question will not arise in your mind. These are the words. I repeat, this is language, through which you are conveying yourself. We have created language. “Something”, “nothing”, “everything” -these are only words. The basic principle behind these words is that you try to identify yourself. Try to identify yourself, the Invisible Listener within you. Instead of analyzing the words, all these spiritual words, try to identify yourself: Who is the analyst? Who wants this knowledge? What is the purpose of this knowledge? To what extent is this knowledge helpful?

Suppose someone has a lot of knowledge. Will that knowledge be helpful at the time of leaving the body? You have lots of knowledge, you have read so many books – fifty years of sadhana, seventy years of sadhana, of whatever kind. Just question yourself, “After reading all these books, after approaching all these Masters, after gathering all this knowledge, will all this really help me, when it is my time to leave the body?” You should be fearless at that time. That moment should be a happy moment: “I am not dying, there is no death. I am unborn”.

Spirituality will give you the spontaneous courage to approach any problem. Thoughts are coming and going, negative thoughts come and go, depression, anxiety, sadness, etc, come and go. But after Conviction, you will ignore all these negative thoughts. They are momentary, like passing clouds. The clouds come, and the clouds go, but you are there, and there only. You are not going anywhere. The sun is always shining!

p.strong: 19. The Six Signs of Realization

section.p: Maharaj: Yesterday we were talking about the principle of spirituality, and the purpose of spirituality. You know that the purpose of spirituality is to identify oneself in a real sense. What is the reason behind identifying ourselves in a real sense? We identify ourselves as the food-body, and that is not Reality. The body-form is not our Reality.

I am not saying that you are to neglect your food-body. This is the medium through which you can identify yourself. There are so many concepts from childhood till today: “I am born”, “I am a man or woman”, social concepts, religious concepts – and within this circle we are living our life.

p.strong: SEPTEMBER 17, 2016 SILVER SPRING, U.S.A.

p.emphasis: “Bow down to That through which you know yourself.”

section.p: After identifying yourself perfectly, dramatic changes will take place within you. These are called the signs of Realization. Spiritual Science teaches that there are six signs, or symptoms, of Realization. Seekers often ask: “How can I identify whether I am Realized or not? What is the sign of Realization? What is the sign of Enlightenment?” After Conviction, after absorbing the knowledge of Reality, some changes will take place.

Shama means to forgive and forget. If anything happens against your mind, you will not get irritated, or become angry. Dama means tolerance or patience. Tolerance will arise spontaneously from within. Where petty matters used to irritate us, where there was even some violence inside us, now, patience will take its place. You will feel it. If an incident happens against your mind, you won’t be irritated. There will be tolerance and patience.

Titiksha means just to know the Reality. You are eager, anxious to know the Reality – “Who am I?” Suppose someone is talking about Reality, Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master. You have the ability to discern, the capacity to discern what you are listening to, calmly and quietly.

Uparati means that there is no longer any attraction for the world. There are so many worldly attractions: publicity, or power and fame, money and sex, etc. After Realization, there will not be any greed or attraction to the world. You are living in the world, but there is complete calm and quiet.

And then there is bhakti which means devotion, total devotion. After knowing the Reality there is total devotion. And finally, there is shraddha which means full trust and faith. You can use these as a barometer to gauge your progress.

After Spontaneous Conviction, these changes will occur. Prior to, and after beingness, at that time, there was no anger, nothing was there. These signs will appear spontaneously. You will be very calm and very quiet.

I sometimes tell this story, the example given by Gautama Buddha, which you may know. The story goes that there was a lady, who took on the challenge of irritating the Buddha, and so, she began abusing him with foul language. Gautama stayed calm and quiet. Eventually, the lady grew irritated, and said, “Why are you not getting angry? Why are you not getting irritated?” And he replied, “Oh mother, you have offered me something, and I have not accepted it. With whom does it remain?” This means that the abuse stays with the abuser. It is up to you whether you accept, or reject these illusory concepts.

There is a similar story, here in Maharashtra. There was a saint called Saint Eknath. He was a very calm, and quiet saint. So, one person took on the same challenge, “I will succeed in making him irritated”.

Every day Saint Eknath went to the nearby river to bathe. On his way back, this person would spit on him. Calmly and quietly, Saint Eknath went back to the river. This happened forty times. Eventually, having failed to irritate Saint Eknath, that abuser became irritated, and eventually bowed down to Saint Eknath. What did Saint Eknath say? “I bathed forty times because of you.” This is patience and tolerance. Saint Eknath then invited him for lunch!

What am I trying to convey with these stories? There will be a big change! Any violence that is there just now, will disappear spontaneously. It can happen. There will always be silence, and no violence. You will feel it, after Conviction. You will feel it. That is the principle of spirituality. Listening, talking, and approaching different Masters will not help you. It will confuse you. Some changes are needed. Only then, will real silence and peacefulness permeate. There will not be any tension. You will be free of the illusory circle in which you were living your life.

Why is there tension? We have expectations, and if they are not fulfilled, tension and irritation appear. Everybody has so many expectations, from family, from friends, from society. It is the nature of the mind.
All expectations and needs came along with the body. Body-based-knowledge is not tolerable. It is a food-body that will survive, for as long as you supply it with food and water.

It is an open fact that this body is not going to remain constant. Spirit or Presence, wants to continue living. That is its nature. Your Presence only knows itself through the body. Meditate! It is only through meditation that you can identify yourself, and have Conviction. No meditation = No Conviction! Knowledge will be absorbed totally. And, therefore, I am repeating: dry knowledge, dry discussion on spirituality are all meaningless. Even if we spent months or years talking together, it would all be meaningless. It is your identity that you need to uncover, not the identity of Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God or Master.
No one but you can identify yourself. And this can only be done through meditation! The Master reminds you of your identity, but the rest is up to you!

p.strong: 20. Meditation is the Anti-Virus Software

section.p: Q: In your book, Selfless Self, you said that meditation is like a spiritual broom. It is a nice image.

Maharaj: Yes, correct, correct. It is the anti-virus software. Our files are corrupted files. There are so many concepts in them. Though we know our identity, and though we know Reality, there are still some concepts around. Because of some internal weakness, these concepts continue to flow and exploit us.

What exactly is it that we want from spiritual knowledge? What is the principle? You want peace. You cannot find peace. Negative thoughts are always infiltrating. There is violence within us. We say, “Oh, I’m okay, I’m really okay”, but there is something inside that is burning. That burning should stop.

For a long, long, time we have been posing as a man, or as a woman, in the body-form. You have totally accepted that “I am the body”. But now you know that you are not the body at all, and never were! The purpose of spiritual knowledge is to identify yourself. When you identify yourself, there will be an abundance of peace and happiness.

Knowledge is meaningless. There was no knowledge prior to beingness, and there will be no use of it, after leaving the body. Knowledge means just to identify oneself, in a real sense. We are identifying ourselves in the body-form, and that is illusion. And again, I reiterate that identification is not possible without meditation. To remove one illusion, we need the help of another illusion. To remove one thorn, we need to use another thorn. Then, we will throw both thorns away.

p.strong: 21. Selfless Self Has No Concerns

section.p: Q: So you do the bhajans, you do the meditation, you do the practice, and eventually it occurs… the penny drops – that you know who you are. You know you are not the body, and you know that the world does not even exist. And then you don’t care, is that right? You don’t even care what happens? So, if the body hurts, or if the body has great pleasures, or if the body is angry, or the mind is angry, it has nothing to do with who I am. And that is the end, that is the finality?

Maharaj: All these actions and reactions are there because we are not identifying ourselves. The moment you identify yourself, nothing will be there. The body does not get angry. If there were no Presence, then how can a dead body get angry? Prior to beingess, did you know about anger? And, after beingness, will there be any anger there?
Q: For me, sometimes, if there is anger in the mind, that anger has nothing to do with me, because I am the ocean, not the individual. And so I see that certain things I take the touch of, like...
Maharaj: Thoughts are flowing inside, and you are taking on ego. You are witnessing the thoughts – good thoughts and bad thoughts. Who is the witness?
Q: Be the witness of the best and the worst.

Maharaj: Your Spontaneous Presence is the Witness, the Invisible Witness. Your Spontaneous Invisible Presence is the Witness that is witnessing all these things. Everything appears upon your Presence. Presence does not have any anger.
Q: I guess what I am trying to say, is that pain and anger, do not seem to be of any concern.

Maharaj: Of course! Selfless Self has no concerns. You are totally different. These feelings came with the body. Prior to body-based-knowledge, there was nothing. We did not know what anger or anxiety was! Nothing was known to us. Who wants peacefulness? Who wants happiness? Who wants a fearless life? Does the body want a fearless life?
Q: The “I am” wants peacefulness. “Paul” wants peacefulness.

Maharaj: That “I am” concept appeared upon your Presence. If your Presence were not there, who will have anything to say about “I am”? That Invisible Presence, Power, Energy, is needed for you to say “I am”. A dead body cannot say “I am”!
Q: Correct, yeah.

Maharaj: If the Seer is not there to see the world, the world cannot be seen. Everything that is seen is reflected out of your Spontaneous Presence. To say, or do, or think, or feel, for any action to take place, your Presence is needed. I am calling it by the name “Presence”. To identify something you have to use some name – Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, or Master – these are the names given to the Supernatural Power within you.

I am inviting the attention of the Invisible Listener within you, that has been called by many different names. But remember! You are that Supernatural Power. You are not separate from That. You Are That!

Do not underestimate yourself. You have great power, tremendous power. But all the time, we are measuring ourselves in the body-form, and that is illusion. It is not your fault, but because of your long association with the body, you have become one with the body.

You are Ultimate Truth. It is a fact. You are unborn. Birth and death are connected to the body-knowledge only, and not to you. But the problem is that you are not accepting the facts. To aid acceptance, meditation is needed, and your deep involvement.

Do not torture your body. Why torture the body, in order to know your identity? Take some time out for you. Be involved! Look at you! “Yes, I want to know myself! Who am I? If I am not the body, who am I?” Ask the question. Find the answer within! Find the courage to find the answer.

p.strong: 22. There is No Difference Between You and I

section.p: Q: When you feel pain, pleasure or confusion, or when you feel great pleasures, or confusion, it is of little consequence, is that correct? Or, how would you say it? When there is pain in the body for you, or pleasures, or temptations, or...?

Maharaj: There is no difference between you and I. We are not individuals. It is only these bodies that are different. Presence is One. You are measuring yourself in the body-form. I am not measuring myself in the body-form, so there is no difference between you and me. “You”, “I”, “he”, “she” – these are only words.
Q: Yeah. I have seen it where you can meditate beyond pain.

Maharaj: Meditation will dissolve all your illusory concepts. All your illusory concepts are supposed to dissolve. That is the most important thing! Dry talk or dry discussion, will not help you. Even if you go to thousands of Masters, it will still not help you. You must approach your own Master. Give importance to your own Master. We are trying to find so many Masters, and you are neglecting your own Master. Recognize, respect, and bow to your Inner Master.

It is a fact that everything is within you. God, Brahman, Atman are not separate from you. If you were to see thousands of Masters, if you were to deny yourself, following strict ascetic practices, and torture your body, nothing will happen. These are only temporary pastimes, spiritual entertainment. You have got to solve your own questions within yourself.

You are to devote some time, have strong faith within you. All the time, we are dependent upon somebody else, thinking, “God will help me,” or “some Master will help me.” Help yourself, by pleasing your own Master within you.

Have some courage! Spirituality gives you the courage to identify yourself. We lack courage. Everybody wants to use some artificial limbs. Stand on your own feet. There is nothing wrong with you. You can fly on your own, without wings. In the beginning it is okay, just like a child depends upon his parents. But, now you have grown up.

p.strong: 23. Knowledge is Free of Charge

section.p: Q: In your book you said, “Be yourself and everything is okay”. How do you “be yourself”?

Maharaj: Yes, it is a fact! You have accepted body-knowledge. “Yourself” means Selfless Self, through which you are talking just now, and identifying some concepts. Self is not separate from you. “Yourself”, “myself”, these names have been given. It is Selfless Self. These are only words.

There are so many Masters saying things in many different ways. Instead of analyzing the words of these Masters, pay attention to what they wanted to convey. Understand the message they were trying to give.

The message is “Except your Selfless Self, there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman, and no Master”. That is the gist of all spirituality. Remember this! It is a fact! Be bold! Have the courage to accept the Reality! You will find dramatic changes taking place. As I have told you, the six qualities of Realization will appear within you. Where you feel some irritation and violence, there will be complete silence, complete silence and peacefulness. Your tolerance will increase. There will no longer be an attraction for the world. Why be attracted to the world, when it is illusion? This can happen. The only thing you have to do is devote some time for yourself, for Selfless Self, not for any Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, or Master. Knowledge is free of charge. Value it! If someone charges five thousand dollars or something, you will say, “Oh, he is a great Master.”

One seeker came to me. His Master was a Hatha Yoga Master. His Master charged him five lakh rupees in foreign currency. Why? He had pierced his ear, and done this, and that. Why is this needed? Wearing different clothes, garlands, and so on, decorating the body? Or, a pierced ear! Why are they decorating the body? Prior to this world, did you come with a decorated body? Or, a pierced ear!

I am not criticizing anyone. This happens because we depend on others, to be able to know ourselves. We are not trying to know ourselves. We are not trying to know, “Who am I?” This is the main question. That question can only be solved within you because you are the source of this identity. Therefore, I define it as “Unidentified, Invisible, Anonymous Identity”. There is no name; you can’t guess what it is. If I am talking about something, that Invisible Listener is listening. There is a flow of thoughts. You are witnessing those thoughts. Who is that? And therefore, I have told you, “Be with you always” because your Presence is Ultimate Truth.
Do not accept illusory thoughts. Thoughts are flowing – why struggle with the thoughts? We are struggling with the thoughts, “Why this? Why that?” It is the nature of the body.
Q: But that’s difficult...

Maharaj: Why is that difficult? You can identify thoughts. Those things which you do not accept, forget about them! If a number of dishes are given to you, you say, “I don’t want this, I don’t want that, I want this food only,” correct? Do not flow along with the thoughts. Don’t follow the mind, let the mind follow you. This will happen through the meditation. Give yourself some time for You! Reading books, and listening to everybody else, will not work. Listen to your own voice, your inner voice. You must listen to your own voice. It will happen, if you are calm and quiet.
Q: But how do you know that the inner voice that you hear is not from your body?

Maharaj: The body is only a medium. You are witnessing thoughts. You are witnessing the dream, you are watching the dream. Who is watching that dream? You are asleep, the body can’t watch. Who has created the dream-world? Have you decided, “Today I’ll see this dream, and tomorrow I’ll see that dream”? You see the sky, the oceans, the gods, and so many things.
