Who has created that dream-world? Similarly, when the Presence clicked with the body, you were able to see this dream-world. The body does not create any dreams. It is the combination of Presence and the body, just like the combination of the matchbox and the stick. When the matchstick strikes the matchbox, you see the fire. Fire is everywhere, just like the five elements are everywhere. American water is not separate from Indian water. Is the American sky different from the Indian sky? Because there are different bodies, we say, “This is one, and that is another one.” Presence is One.

p.strong: 24. Come Forward!

section.p: Maharaj: After Conviction, all your concepts will dissolve. There are so many concepts engraved upon us! “There is a God”, “you are going to die”, “last birth”, “future birth”, “hell”, “heaven” – what are all these things? Who knows hell and heaven? We can’t remember anything about this present birth, so why are we talking about our previous birth? We talk about sin, virtue, and all these things. We are blindly signing, “Yes sir, yes sir”. Why? You are not guilty at all, so why build a guilty conscience? There is no deed, there is no doer. I am placing the facts before you. And again, I am repeating that the only source is meditation, through which you can identify yourself in a real sense. You will build courage from this strong foundation. You will get spiritual courage to embrace the Reality. We are afraid of everything because of a lack of courage.
Q: In the book Selfless Self, you say, “Be how you were prior to beingness”. In the practical world, how do you do that?

Maharaj: Just think about this intellectually. How were you prior to beingness, and how will you be after leaving the body? Is this body Reality? Eventually, we must leave this body, so what is the use of knowledge? Knowledge means to identify oneself in a real sense. We are not doing that. We consider ourselves as body-forms.

You are formless, you are shapeless, it is a fact. When Presence clicked with the body, you said “I”. So many illusory concepts are wrapped around us. We have accepted these concepts blindly. To remove them, we need meditation. Everything will become clear within you through the meditation. When I say that meditation is the anti-virus software, it means that all the viruses will be cleared. Again, I am repeating: Nothing is impossible. You need not depend upon anyone else, even myself. Therefore, Nisargadatta Maharaj says, “I am not making you a disciple, I am making you a Master”, because that Masterly essence is within you. The Master is not separate from you. We are confused because of our body-based knowledge. This is a food-body.

In the beginning, strong devotion is required, and confidence is needed – “I can do it! I know the Reality. I am the source of this world. The entire world is projected out of me.” This is not an egoistic thought, it is a fact. If there is no Presence, who will talk about the world? Who will talk about God? And with this solid base, all your illusory concepts will be dissolved, and there will emerge complete silence and peace. Whatever happens, whatever you do, “It’s okay”. Since you are holding the body, some things will happen against you. There may be unpleasant atmospheres, just like the clouds that are coming and going. But You are there, and there only. You will not be touched by anything.
Q: Sometimes during meditation, I have this fear. I don’t know what the fear is about, but I have some kind of fear. Why is that?

Maharaj: Because of the pressure of concepts, illusory concepts. Directly, or indirectly, we have accepted the body as our identity, and, therefore, there is fear. Where was that fear prior to beingness? And, after beingness, will there be any fear?
Does a dead body have any fear? There is no fear. Because of the influence of the body, the Spirit wants to survive for a long, long time. Spirit does not know that, “I Am Spirit”.

Q: Dear Maharaj, first of all I would like to show my respect for you. I came here a little bit late, so I apologize. But I want to say, that I no longer have any more questions. I want to thank you for helping me develop the Conviction with the identification with Presence.

Maharaj: Nothing is impossible. I repeat, nothing is impossible for you. You just have a lack of courage, “How can it happen?” Siddharameshwar Maharaj says, “Take a step forward, and I will take the next step for you. “Take the first step, and I will take the second one”. This means come forward. Whatever has happened until now, just forget it. Now that you know the Reality, why not accept the Reality? Reality is not separate from you.

Q: Sri Ramakant, there is a seeing that is very peaceful, and there is a seeing, where things lose their substance, but it is so peaceful, so peaceful. But sometimes underlying that, there is a fear of losing the attachments, the emotional attachments that we have, when people around you are saying, “You don’t care about me, if you are not worried about me”. That is the way love is being taught, and somehow there is a little part there, that is afraid of losing that attachment. But like you said, Selfless Self feels peace, and its seeing is peace. Does it take time to move out of that fear, where you lose that old way of seeing things?

Maharaj: What is happening is that concepts are taking possession of your body. When there is no alertness, no awareness, then concepts take possession of your body, and there will be fear. Therefore, meditation and reciting the Mantra etc. are needed. Because through that, you are reminding yourself all the time that: “I am Brahman”, so that there will be no fear.

p.strong: SEPTEMBER 18, 2016 SILVER SPRING, U.S.A.

p.emphasis: “STOP! Put the books away. Go Within. Read your own book. Your Inner Guru is Almighty.”

p.strong: 25. Nothing is Happening

section.p: Maharaj: We have been discussing spirituality for the past two days now. You have the basic knowledge. I do not want to repeat the same thing again and again. You just have to absorb the knowledge, try to know Reality. Just try to identify yourself. It is very easy to discuss spirituality. It is more difficult to absorb it.

What exactly do we want? If you do not know what you want, you will carry on drifting, roaming, here and there. And therefore, today’s message is: try to identify the Invisible Listener within you which is Ultimate Truth. The Master-Key is meditation. All the illusory concepts are supposed to be totally dissolved. Until you have the Conviction that, “I am not the body, I was not the body, and I am not going to remain the body”, Reality will not be clear.

Q: According to the Vedic understanding of the evolution of consciousness, we first have to understand who we are, that is, the unbounded awareness. Then, perception refines over time to perceive finer and finer levels of creation, until one perceives the infinite, and sees that there is no difference between That and that which I am. Is this a sequence that happens over time, or can it happen all at once?
Maharaj: Nothing is happening. This is the Spontaneous Projection of your Ultimate Truth. Nothing has happened, and nothing is going to happen. You are not the doer, and there is no deed.

Up until now, we have been measuring ourselves in the body-form, and that is illusion. So many questions, like this one, appear upon your Presence. Try to identify that Invisible Presence within you. You are not the body, there is no shape, there is no nothing.

p.strong: 26. Meditation, Prayers and Bhajans

section.p: Maharaj: To have Conviction, to absorb this knowledge, you have to undergo the basics of meditation. There isn’t any other way. Questions, counter-questions, and analysis, all this is just temporary spiritual entertainment.
Q: Is there any specific way to do meditation?

Maharaj: Meditation means concentration and self-involvement. It does not mean sitting, and chanting, say, “Ram, Ram, Ram, Ram”. Identify yourself! Sit quietly and calmly, and see what is happening. Who is witnessing the thoughts? What is that? It is not the body. It is your Nameless, Supernatural Power.
Q: So, meditating means...
Maharaj: Meditation means just to identify yourself. Invite the attention of the Invisible Meditator within you, that you are Brahman. Nothing is there except your Self.
Q: And the same question about the bhajans...

Maharaj: Yes, yes, prayers and bhajans are required, because this is the weak, or sensitive point of the Spirit. These bhajans create an atmosphere. As we play the various instruments, we create an atmosphere. Spiritual Science says it is the sattva guna. There are three gunas: sattva, raja, and tama.
Q: Should we sing a specific kind of bhajan, or will any general bhajan do?

Maharaj: Sing any bhajans you wish, that bring you happiness and peace. Like everything else, the bhajans is only another medium, through which you are awakening your Inner Master, and drawing closer to Selfless Self. There will be spontaneous peacefulness, not artificial peacefulness, not peacefulness through any material cause. There will be abundant peace, and overflowing happiness.

Do not underestimate your Selfless Self. That grand secret is within you. You have been ignoring yourself because of your concepts, and expectations, that have kept you in the world. You have been living within the circle of concepts. Break out of the circle. Meditation will give you the courage to break free.
Every single being wants a peaceful life, a happy life, a fearless life, a tension-free life. It is a fact. You can have it! It is yours for the taking.

p.strong: 27. Be Simple, Be Normal

section.p: Q: My mind keeps asking, “Why am I here? To be of service? Or, “Why am I here in the physical body, if I am already Brahman?”

Maharaj: That which says “my mind”, is different from the mind. You are not the mind at all. You are watching your mind, identifying your mind, experiencing your mind. The mind is separate from you. You are supplying energy to your mind. You can control your mind. It happens, it is the nature of the mind. The thoughts are flowing. You are separate from the thoughts. You are thoughtless.

Q: Maharaj, over the last couple of years, my sadhana has progressed quite a bit. More so just sitting in dnyana, seeing the seer, knowing the knower. Basically being able to pivot the mind backwards, in the state of awareness. There is a statement by Nisargadatta Maharaj: “Patience and perseverance are most important at that stage”. So, I have been continuing like this for the last couple of years, and now I can do it for about two hours a day. What next?

Maharaj: There is no next. No next is there, nothing is there. Does the sky have any “next” or “first”, or “earlier”? Think about it. Your Presence is just like the sky or the space. It is everywhere like the five elements. Mind, ego, intellect – these concepts appeared upon your Presence. I am inviting the attention of that Presence through which you say, “my mind, my ego, my intellect.” All relations, all requirements, all needs – who wants all these things? Where was that mind prior to beingness? There was no ego, and no intellect, prior to beingness.

I am placing before you various words in different ways. Try to identify the principle behind these words. This is not dry, spiritual knowledge. It is the Listener’s Truth. The Listener is Ultimate. You are Ultimate Truth. You are the destination. The Absolute is within you.

p.strong: 28. The Body is a Magic Box

section.p: Maharaj: Try and absorb what you have heard. We have so many body-based questions because of the mistaken conviction that you are somebody. You are not somebody, you are everybody. Your Presence is everywhere, just like the space or the sky. You are not limited to the body. The body is an external cover. You can use your body, just like these clothes. We clean our clothes, and take care of our clothes.

Your sadhana is necessary for the beginning stage only. After Conviction, sadhana is not needed. When you begin your practice, you are like the toddler, who needs a walker to help him walk because he is just learning, and still not strong enough to stand on his own feet. So, similarly, we need aids, props and support in the beginning, until all our concepts are wiped out, and we are established in Ultimate Truth. Have a dialogue with Selfless Self. Convince yourself of your true identity, your Reality.

This body is a magic box. This is a magic box that contains unimaginable power within it. But we are neglecting that because we are always looking out there. We have some concepts of an external Power: “There is a God out there, and He is administering the whole world. It is with His grace and with His power, that I am living”. All this is okay, however, it is not true! There is no external power. There is no other power but you! Have some faith within your Selfless Self! Be calm and quiet. Just see what is there! It is just glimpses of “I”, without any shape or form.
At the beginning stage, sadhana is required, meditation is required, to rid yourself of all your body-based concepts, and absorb the knowledge. Sadhana is necessary until Spontaneous Conviction.

p.strong: 29. If you are Realized, Make Others Realized

section.p: Q: Maharaj, will anyone carry on the lineage after you?

Maharaj: It is not like that. The lineage is a spontaneous lineage. We call it a “lineage”, but this lineage does not have any individuals, and no specific personalities. And, it is also an illusion. This Master, that Master, that other Master, this is okay. We have this concept, when we are living in this body-form.

Siddharameshwar Maharaj, my spiritual Grand-Master, says, “If you are Realized, make others Realized. If you are peaceful, make others peaceful.” There are no hard and fast rules. If you are Realized, unroll your rug or mat, and start talking. Share the knowledge, but, do not turn spirituality into a business. Do not make a business out of spirituality. This knowledge is free. It is free knowledge.

We do not expect anything from anyone. We have an ashram also. Visitors who wish to give a spontaneous contribution, may do so. There are no demands. This is the discipline of our lineage which states in no uncertain terms, “not to demand a single rupee, from any person, or any devotee, for personal use, or any other purpose”. So then, the question is often asked, “How are you maintaining the ashram?” From spontaneous contributions given by local devotees. There is no donation box in our ashram. Why is money required? What is the use of that money? Okay, you need it for maintenance, but not in the name of spirituality.
So if you are Realized, make others Realized. But do not do so with any commercial motives.

p.strong: 30. Spirituality Gives You Courage

section.p: Q: If one is no longer identified with the body, and knows what he is, will he no longer feel any pain, and the fear associated with the world?
Maharaj: Undergo the meditation! There is fear because we have developed body-based-concepts. What is the fear?
Q: I mean, responsibility for acting in the world.

Maharaj: Okay. Yes, yes, of course. Spirituality will give you courage. After Identification, you will have courage, and know how to sail through your family life, and your daily, routine life. Be alert, particularly during the meditation, when some negative concepts and atmospheres will, no doubt, appear. Be firm and resolute. Do not give up. Ignore whatever arises, because you know that it is the nature of the mind and, therefore, it is not worthy of your attention. Concentrate fully! Give your complete attention to Reality alone! Have the Conviction that your body-form is not your identity at all. It is a dead body!

Without your Spontaneous Presence, the body has no value...“take it away, take it away”! The Seer must be there to enable any seeing. The Seer must be there, for you to see the world. This means that you are the central point. You are the source of this whole world. Presence is needed to identify everything, and to say, “this world”, “God”, etc.

Q: Pretty soon I am going to have an operation, and I am going to get a pain shot. But this time I am not going to try to meditate. I mean, I will meditate, too, and recognize that I am not the one feeling the pain because I am spaceless, timeless, not an object that can be touched. However, I would like you to comment on that. I will get the pain shot! I used to try to meditate and not use the pain shot. Now I say to heck with that, what’s the difference? I’ll just take it. If there is a headache, I’ll take an aspirin. What’s the difference? It is just the way the knowledge is happening, so it is no big deal. But I would like to hear your comments on that.

Maharaj: These are physical problems. If there are physical problems, accept the help of the doctors. But, at the same time, meditation will give you a lot of courage, to bear all these negative experiences, disease, and all these intolerable things. Where previously there was intolerance, now, unbearable things will become bearable. This happens after Conviction. Since we are holding the body, headaches are bound to be there, depression is bound to be there, illness of various sorts are bound to be there, therefore, accept the doctors’ help. “Oh, I am a spiritual man, how can I ask the doctor for help!”

The body is the medium through which you can identify yourself, so, do not neglect your body. I request everyone not to neglect their bodies. Go and see a doctor, if you need to. This is a food-body. You must supply food, water, and nourishment to it, while at the same time, don’t be attached to it. “I am not the body, I was not the body, and I am not going to remain the body”. It is an open fact. But, we are not accepting it, and therefore, there is painfulness. Directly, or indirectly, we have given importance to the body. It’s okay, you can use the body, but remember that you are the Holder of the body!

p.strong: Guidance From Sri Ramakant Maharaj OCTOBER 11, 2016

section.p: Jai Guru,
There will be miraculous or dramatic changes appearing. Exceptional blissfulness, peacefulness, fearlessness, no tension, and all these, without any material causes.

This is a sign of absorbing the Reality within you, instead of playing with the spiritual words – Atman, Paramatman, God, Master, mind, ego, intellect, maya, Brahman, destiny, last birth, future birth, karma, or religion, past life, future life, consciousness or subconsciousness, subtle bodies, and so on. It is a fact, that prior to beingness, nothing was there. After leaving the body nothing will remain. Spirituality is required to identify the Unidentified Identity, or to have the Spontaneous Conviction that the food-body is not your identity at all. Why play with the so-called spiritual words? What we see is the Seer’s projection. If the Seer’s Spontaneous Presence is not there in the food-body, who will identify the world?

In brief, whatever we see and experience is totally illusion, but the Spontaneous Seer through the food-body is Reality, where there is no experience, no experiencer, and no witness. It is beyond imagination, and beyond logical or intellectual guessing. Everything came out of nothing, and everything will dissolve within nothing. And you know better. Language was created within the circle of the human body. When the Spontaneous Presence touched with the body, the

experience of “I” appeared upon your Presence. A name was given to us, either male or female, and instantly, we started measuring ourselves as, “I am somebody”, along with the existence of the mind, ego, and intellect, which appeared through the food-body and started functioning. Since the food-body experience is not tolerable, one started to seek happiness, peacefulness, a tension-free life, and a fearless-life through material causes. When it is found that these material causes only give temporary relief, then human beings turn to spirituality, through which one can identify oneself in a real sense. The various illusory concepts dissolve spontaneously.

As you know, in the beginning, an external, Realized Master is necessary. As a matter of fact, external and internal Masters are spiritual concepts. However, at the initial stage, it is necessary, until one has Spontaneous Conviction. Furthermore, meditation and sincere involvement in spirituality, as well as complete trust and strong faith in the Master, are all necessary. As I told you earlier, after Spontaneous Conviction, all the activities remain untouched by, or unconcerned with, the illusory world.

So, all spiritual discussion is just like talking about the unborn child. Nothing has been happening and nothing is going to happen, and hence there is no need to refer to any spiritual book. There is no need to approach so many Masters, after the Conviction of Unidentified, Anonymous, Invisible Identity. My Sadguru, Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj, used to say, “How you were prior to beingness, and how you will be after leaving the food-body, remain like that.” Furthermore, avoid playing with the spiritual words. Avoid analyzing or debating the words, as language has been created only to enable conversation, and to share our thoughts.
I think I have tried my level best to convince the devotees. With regards and blessings to all.

p.emphasis: Jai Sadguru!

p.strong: OCTOBER 03, 2016, COLUMBIA, U.S.A.

p.emphasis: “When happiness is no longer required, you have reached the destination.”

p.strong: 31. All Miraculous Experiences Appear Upon Your Presence

section.p: Maharaj: Meditation is the base. Though it is not Ultimate Truth, it is the base, the foundation, which is absolutely necessary until you have the Conviction of Selfless Self.

Literary knowledge and oral knowledge, will not help you, because you are holding this human body. Even though we are formless and shapeless, we started considering ourselves as body-forms. Your Spontaneous Invisible Presence is like the space or the sky. But that Conviction is missing. And, in order to have this Conviction, one should undergo intense meditation. Meditation is your base, your foundation. Don’t make excuses. Just do it!

The body is not tolerable. Body-based-knowledge is not tolerable. To make it tolerable, we resort to material things like power, money, and sex, and all sorts of other things. In them, we find temporary relief! To come out from these body-based concepts, meditation, and reciting the Mantra, are most important. Although the Mantra is a concept, these Sanskrit words have a lot of power. The efficacy of the Naam Mantra has been proven, time and time again, by our great lineage Masters, as well as by the immediate effects, often visible in the majority of seekers.

In the process of your meditation, involvement, and your strong devotion, sometimes miraculous experiences happen. They may be positive, or negative, miraculous experiences. I will give you an example from Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj. It will help your focus.

Early on, Nisargadatta Maharaj was very rich. He owned about ten shops. His finances were in very good shape, when in 1933, or so, he came across Siddharameshwar Maharaj. One of his friends took him to see Siddharameshwar Maharaj. At that time, he said, “I will not believe in any body-based Sage or Saint. I am not going to bow down to him”. But he went there, listened, and then experienced a miraculous inner change. His base was already solid. He was so deeply impressed, that he bowed down to Sri Siddharameshwar spontaneously. His association with Siddharameshwar, lasted for about three years.

I am describing Nisargadatta Maharaj’s experiences to help you focus on your devotional life. After 1939, or thereabouts, he left his routine life, and set out to experience the spiritual world. He faced so many unbearable problems. He left his family and everything else behind. He had numerous shops and apartments which were rented out, etc. But, he was driven to experience this spiritual knowledge. In the course of his spiritual adventure, he faced many negative experiences. He had no money, and he was wandering here and there, physically exhausted.

At one point, he found himself in some barren land where there was no water. There was nothing! Completely exhausted, he said he thought it was going to be the last moment of his life. But then, in that desolate, barren land, he could see a cottage, in the far distance.
Here, I am talking about devotional experiences, that appear when you are deeply involved in spirituality. Though it is not knowledge, and not Reality, this kind of miraculous incident does happen.

He had seen this cottage, and so, with great difficulty, went there. There was an old man there, who gave him some food, bread, and water. Soon after that, he left. After taking a few steps, he looked back. The cottage was nowhere to be seen. It had disappeared.

Nisargadatta Maharaj rarely gave us examples of his miraculous experiences. But I learned from him. After this experience, he wrote a poem: “My Master became a cottage for me. My Master became food for me. My Master became water for me”.

This can happen to anyone. Your Master can manifest in any shape or form, when you are in difficulty. But you must have strong faith, within yourself, and in your Master. When you have strong devotion and deep involvement, then this kind of miraculous experience can happen.

They can be positive or negative. “Negative experiences” mean that you will have some negative or depressive thoughts. There may be difficulties, just like in Nisargadatta Maharaj’s case, where he faced so many problems in his life, so many problems, beyond one’s imagination. But, he never ever lost his faith in his Master. His knowledge was very strong, exceptional, firm. He had total Conviction.

He had other positive, miraculous experiences, as well. He rarely talked about them, but one day, while he was sitting at his shop, he started pouring water from his teapot to passers-by. People started forming queues. It turned out that they were

being healed and cured by that water. But Siddharameshwar Maharaj warned him, “What are you doing? This is not knowledge. It is not Reality. It’s distracting you from Reality”. So he stopped doing this!

In this dialogue, what I am telling you, is that after a time spent in meditation, reciting the Mantra, and absorbing the knowledge, with strong devotion, there may be some miraculous experiences. It is not knowledge. It is not Reality. It is not Ultimate Truth. The point is that you could be distracted from Reality. If there is any ego still lurking, you may think, “Yes, she has had this miracle because of me”. Or, maybe when you are with someone, a miracle takes place. You may then think to yourself: “Oh, I have so much power!” Beware! This will inflate the ego even more. Be careful! Take precautionary measures in the process of your devotional spirituality.

If during your devotion, such experiences occur, do not give them too much importance. All miraculous experiences appear upon your Presence. That Presence, that Invisible Presence, Anonymous Presence, is Ultimate Truth. Miracles have no value. It is the nature of our human body, of the human being. If you see miracles performed in front of you, you may bow down and say, “Oh, you are a very great man.” But no one is performing those miracles. They are within you. You have tremendous power, but do not take on any ego around them. Nisargadatta Maharaj did not take any credit. He did not accept any prestige, “If it happens, it is because of my Master. I am not doing any miracles.” He never took on any ego.

The reason I am drawing your attention to this, is that because of your strong devotional life, these things may happen. Recognize them as progressive steps, and keep going. Don’t be caught by them. Do not indulge in them for long. Do not stop there, keep moving forward, climbing until you reach the mountain peak. Involve yourself in devotion and spirituality till Conviction. I am alerting you, making you cautious, in the process of your devotional life, your spirituality. Ignore these experiences – they are not Reality.

p.strong: 32. Power is Inbuilt in You

section.p: You will go through so many experiences, miraculous experiences, magical experiences. You may also get some power. It happens. But do not use, or misuse, that power. You have miraculous power, but that is not Ultimate Truth.

Power is also a by-product of your deep, spiritual involvement. You will feel it. And again, this may give the ego, the subtle ego an opportunity to enter again: “I have the power. I can do something”. That ego must be smashed to pieces, so that it does not have the opportunity to rear its head again.

Does anyone have any questions? Feel free to ask! Don’t worry! This is friendly chatting. I am not delivering spiritual lectures, and considering myself to be some “great spiritual Master”. Our bodies may differ, but the same Spirit is in us all.

Q: Maharaj, I have been doing different practices for many years, and different mantras come up in different contexts. That has been my practice, if you will. So, would you advise me now to just stick with one mantra, your Mantra?

Maharaj: Recite the Mantra that I have given you. It is said that this Mantra comes from Dattatreya. There is tremendous value, importance, power, energy with this Mantra. It creates some vibrations inside. When you are reciting the Naam Mantra, you will forget your body-identity. You will become one with the Mantra. What is the meaning of the Naam Mantra? “I am Brahman, Brahman I am”. It is not referring to something else, something other. It is practical, it is your Reality. You have forgotten your Reality. You are reminding yourself of your Reality, so whatever mantra you have – it may be any mantra – some say Aham Brahmasmi, Shivoham, Soham, stay loyal to it.

p.strong: 33. The Master Appears

section.p: Maharaj: Until you have Conviction, until you remain untouched by the world, untouched by the body-based knowledge, you need to meditate! As I have already mentioned, during your meditation and involvement, you may encounter a few miraculous experiences.

Spiritual knowledge talks about three types of positive miraculous experiences that can occur: darshan, sparshan, and sambhashan. Darshan means that after having strong involvement in spirituality, you see your gods, goddesses, and Masters near you, and around you. You will get that sight, that transference of sight. Sparshan means that you will feel the touch of your Masters. Suppose you find yourself in some difficulty, you will feel them placing their hand on your head. Sambhashan means that sometimes, your Master, your God, is talking with you.

You will have some experiences. It is a fact, it happens. It is a sign of your strong devotion. Don’t be sidetracked by these experiences. The ego is bound to distract you, at any and every available opportunity. Nisargadatta once said, “Suppose you have some miraculous power. You may use it once or twice, but then, maybe the third time you use it, nothing will happen, and you will then despair.

You are a Master. You are already a Master. I am not telling you this for the sake of pleasing you. It is a fact that you have forgotten your identity, and therefore, the only thing I am doing is reminding you of That Identity, by inviting your attention to your Reality, and keeping you focussed on it all the time. That Invisible Listener within you is the Master of Masters. It is everywhere like the space or the sky. It is like the five elements – fire is everywhere, water is everywhere, sky is everywhere. You are the projector of the five elements, but you do not know it.

Take a dream-experience, for example. In a dream you see all the five elements. You see water, you see the sun, the moon, everything. Not only that, but you see yourself also. You are in deep sleep, but you are watching that dream, you are acting in that dream. Who has created that dream-world? Who is witnessing that dream-world? Who is experiencing that dream-world? Just think about it. It is the same thing here. Your Spontaneous Invisible Presence projected this entire world. If there is no awakening in the morning, who will talk about the world? Who will talk about all these Masters? Who will talk about God?

So, directly or indirectly, you are the base of this world. You are Ultimate Truth, you are Final Truth. You need not go anywhere to find the gods and the goddesses. Devote some time for meditation. This is the solution, the only solution. If we go on for hours together with dry knowledge, if you read so many books, visit so many Masters, you will only get temporary relief. Learn to concentrate on the Concentrator, the Invisible Concentrator.
The Listener and Speaker are one. Do not discriminate. Be with you all the time. Do not depend upon anyone else.

p.strong: 34. The Ball is in Your Court

section.p: Q: You talk about the spontaneous release of really strong emotions, and I have been experiencing that. I am wondering if that can also apply to physical symptoms?
Maharaj: Yes, yes, it happens. As you move closer and closer, to your Selfless Self, it happens. It is good, because you are experiencing those emotions.

Q: The emotions have been pretty intense sometimes, like rage coming out of nowhere, and lasting for half an hour, and then just subsiding. But it is the physical stuff that I was a little more perplexed about.

Maharaj: When one has strong involvement in spirituality, so many emotional feelings of love and affection appear spontaneously. This is part and parcel of your Spirit, of your spiritual knowledge, because you are full of love and affection. Exceptional peacefulness is there. With your strong devotion and meditation, there will be total peacefulness and total silence. No confusion, no confusion – all confusion will dissolve. There will be exceptional silence. You will remain untouched by the world.

Q: I have a broken body, Maharaj, I have a disease. I have an addiction to the medication for this disease, a broken back. I wake up in the morning, and I am in so much pain. The medication affects my meditation, but if I do not take the medication, then I am in so much pain. Will I succeed with the meditation? Can I do it?

Maharaj: Yes, you have to do it. Your tolerance and patience will increase. Since you are holding the body, the body is bound to have problems. There are physical, and mental problems, appearing. You will find some courage, power and energy to tolerate all these things. Not even that, I am telling you, you will overcome all the physical problems you have as well.

I will tell you a true story about Guru Ranade, a disciple of Bhausaheb Maharaj. About a hundred years back, Guru Ranade was suffering from tuberculosis, and in those days, TB was an incurable disease. His mother took him to see Bhausaheb Maharaj. After meditating, as he was instructed to do, Guru Ranade was surprised to find himself completely cured. Really, it was a miracle. This can happen to you because you have immense power. What I want to tell you, the principle behind this, is that you have tremendous power. Anything can happen, and you will find the courage to tolerate, all these body-based concepts, all these body-based problems.

And therefore, I am insisting, at the beginning stage, that you devote some time for meditation. It is very basic. Discussion alone is not sufficient. Now the ball is in your court. Meditate! Go deep within. Be self-involved, nothing more! There are no conditions or restrictions as to what you should eat or do. Just meditate!
Through the meditation, you will identify yourself in a real sense. All these concepts have appeared upon your Presence, bringing confusion. They have obscured your identity.

p.strong: 35. Devotion is Acceptance

section.p: Q: How do you increase your devotion? I mean, how does one become more devoted? If you find that you get distracted by the world, and then forget to meditate?
Maharaj: Accept the Reality. Reality is placed before you. It is the Reality of the Invisible Listener within you. When you accept this totally, that is devotion.

Your body is called a “woman”. Are you saying, “I am a woman, I am a woman, I am a woman”, all the time? Of course not. You accepted it. I say you are Brahman, but you are not accepting it. You say, “Oh, you must be making fun of me!”. I am placing before you the Listener’s Reality, but you are not accepting it. But when someone says something you do not like, you immediately accept it, and react. So it is now up to you, to accept the Reality that is placed before you.

Why go here, and there, in search of Masters? “Oh! God bless me, Oh! God bless me”. Why? To say “God” your Presence is required. If there is no Presence who will talk about “God”? Are you not the father of God, the mother of God? “God” is a concept. Okay, it is not bad. I am not criticizing that “God” concept. But if your Presence is not there, who will talk about the God? How can you talk about God and the Masters? The moment that Spirit clicked with the body, you started identifying the entire world. The entire world is projected out of your Presence. It is a fact.

And therefore, I keep insisting: To have this Spontaneous Conviction regarding your Selfless Self, you are to undergo the meditation. Again, I repeat, meditation is not only sitting. Be devoted to your meditation. Devotion means total involvement. If you recite the Mantra all the time, you will experience something miraculous. You will feel some exceptional peacefulness. Worldly things will be of no concern to you whatsoever.

p.strong: 36. No Hard and Fast Rules

section.p: Q: I have a technical question about the mantra. A person can recite the mantra, while breathing in and breathing out?

Maharaj: See, when you are sitting for meditation, at that time this principle [reciting the first half of the mantra along with the in-breath, and the second half along with the out-breath] is there. The rest of the time you can recite the mantra as it is. Be simple.
Q: So if I can do five...

Maharaj: Yes, no problem. Sit as per the instructions that have been given to you. As for the rest of the time, you can recite the Mantra as it is. Be normal. And even then, there is no hard and fast rule. This is the beginning stage, like when you are a student, learning a foreign language. You are told to write “a” like this, “b” like this, “c” like this. Then, after progressing through the various stages, you are no longer a student, you are a master of that particular language.

So, when you are sitting for meditation, sit in this fashion for half an hour, one hour, two hours or more, it depends on your daily routine. And if you recite the mantra along with your breathing it will have a greater effect. There will be more absorption. But throughout the rest of the day, you can recite the mantra as it is, not concentrating on the breathing, or sitting crossed-legged. Your meditation will, after some time, continue spontaneously within you. Unbeknown to you, even in deep sleep, that meditation will continue. You are at the initial stage, but, you still have to do it. No one can do it for you. Be practical!

p.strong: 37. Physical Needs Have Nothing to do With Reality

section.p: Q: Does a certain type of diet help calm your mind? Like a vegetarian or sattvic diet? Not too many spices?

Maharaj: No, there is no question of diet. It is not a concern. Your physical needs have nothing to do with Reality. Whatever you eat, whatever you do, whatever you like to make the body feel good, whatever your requirements, is of no concern to your Presence, or Spirit. Whether you eat vegetarian or non-vegetarian food, is of no concern. It is nobody’s concern! You are not the body. Give the body what it needs, and what it wants, in moderation. Whether you drink pure water or tap water, is of no concern. Identifying yourself is the only concern you need have. That is of paramount importance!
The questions keep coming because you have so many concepts. Meditate and bring an end to all this illusion. Clear out the clutter!
Q: What about Yoga asanas? Is it just another body-based concept then?
Maharaj: Yoga is good for health. It will help you. Pranayama, for example, has some good effects. There are various types of yoga: padmasana can also bring some good effects. Yoga can help you.

p.strong: 38. The Mantra is a Concept

section.p: Q: There is a lot of chaos and drama in my family during the course of the day, and I am trying to repeat the Mantra during all this drama, because in Reality there is really nobody there.
Maharaj: Correct, correct.

Q: I just want to be clear: I don’t have to focus on the in-breath or out-breath, while I am repeating the Mantra during the day? As long as I am aware and repeating the Mantra, that is all I have to do?

Maharaj: Correct! And remember that the Mantra is to be used only at the initial stage, until you have Conviction. At the Ultimate stage, it is not necessary to recite the Mantra, or do any practice. It will happen spontaneously.

What is a mantra? It is a reminder of your true identity, and helps you to identify yourself. Where was that mantra prior to beingness? Was there a mantra when you came into this world? Will there be a mantra after you leave the body? The mantra is also a concept. It is illusion. Having said that, you have to do it in the beginning. There is no way round this.

Q: Maharaj, I do have one question. I know you said choose one mantra, and stick with that. What I am finding, is that I am equally drawn to two. The Naam Mantra, because I received it from you, and also, “I am Brahman, Brahman I am”. I am doing one quite intensely for a week, and then I switch to the other one. I can’t seem to decide.
Maharaj: You have taken the Naam Mantra?
Q: Yes.
Maharaj: Recite the Naam Mantra only. It has the same meaning. Don’t confuse yourself!
Q: Okay, thank you. So I will stick with that one.

p.strong: OCTOBER 17, 2016, COLUMBIA, U.S.A.

p.emphasis: “Meditation will bring silence, and end the mind’s violence.”

p.strong: 39. Slowly, Silently, Permanently

section.p: Maharaj: After Realization, after Spontaneous Conviction, you will be completely calm and quiet. Whatever happens, you will remain calm and quiet throughout. There will be exceptional tolerance and bliss. All your desires will vanish. There will be no desires, no mind, no ego, and no intellect. It will happen spontaneously.

You will become completely free. Even though you are using this body, either as a male or female, you will remain detached. You will live in alertness, knowing that the body is temporary. And you will use it, as you might use an instrument.

For you to say: “I understand that I am not the body”, is intellectual understanding. And that is not enough. Meditation is the basic thing. You have to devote some time to meditation. “Some time” means, at least one hour! Allot at least one hour out of 24 for meditation, during which time, you are trying to identify the Unidentified Identity within you. It is only through the meditation process, that the Reality will be totally absorbed within you.

Through the Naam Mantra you are hammering yourself. Slowly, silently, and permanently, all your desires, expectations and needs, will miraculously dissolve. You will be completely free, completely free, completely silent, silent, silent.

Intellectual understanding is insufficient, because everything is within you. You cannot identify yourself in a real sense without using the tool, or medium of meditation. Spirituality, Reality, is not separate from you.

Some people come here, and say, “I have been practising spirituality for thirty or forty years”. There is some subtle ego there: “I have been doing this or that”. You are not the doer! There is no deed! Nothing is there. So, at the first stage, this bodily identity must dissolve, totally and spontaneously. It will happen. Nothing is impossible. You will find the courage to let go of the known, and dive into the unknown. There is nothing to fear, the Master is protecting you.

You have been playing with the spiritual words: Atman, Paramatman, thousands of words. You have been playing at being “spiritual beings”. This is a waste of time. Maybe you have a lot of knowledge. Maybe you are a master of knowledge. That’s okay. But, you still do not know who you are. You have not identified yourself with this knowledge. It has not enabled you to identify yourself. It has only given you intellectual stimulation, and a spiritual massage. You can become an Einstein in the spiritual field, but that will not stop your fear and trembling on your deathbed.

You are formless Presence. You were formless Presence. You will remain as formless Presence. Dissolve the concepts of “death” and “birth”. Dissolve all the desires. It will happen. We have many subtle, subtle desires. Therefore, instead of studying spirituality, try to digest that spirituality within you. It is not separate from you. As long as we continue to count ourselves as the body-forms, spirituality will appear to be different from us, separate from us. When spirituality is absorbed, at that stage, there will be no “I”, “you”, “he”, or “she” -there will be nothing there!

p.strong: 40. Read Your Story

section.p: Q: Should I stop reading Selfless Self, or Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj’s books? Is it best to stop all reading at some point?

Maharaj: Do not stop reading! You can read these spiritual books, but read them, as if you were reading your own biography. Read it, as your own story! When you are reading books by different Masters: Nisargadatta Maharaj, Siddharameshwar, etc, read them in this way. They all use their own unique style of language, but ultimately, they convey the same message: “Except for Selfless Self, there is no other God”.

There are many spiritual books out there, describing the different bodies, subtle, causal, this and that body... Where were these bodies prior to beingness? Why concern yourself with all these “bodies”? The principle behind spiritual knowledge, is that this body belongs to the five elements. You are the projector of these five elements. Where were these five elements prior to beingness? The sky is there, the earth, the wind, sun and light. So, these five elements came along with the body. Where were the five elements prior to beingness? Will you know about these elements, after beingness? There will not be anything there. Nothing will be there.

All the knowledge, whatever knowledge you have, will dissolve along with the body. So, why play with the words? All spiritual words are only indicators, that invite the attention of the Invisible Listener, that you are Ultimate Truth.

p.strong: 41. What You See is Illusion, That Through Which You See is Reality

section.p: Maharaj: There is no form. Was there a form prior to beingness? We do not know the answer to the question “Who am I?” To say “I am”, some Presence is needed. Where was that “I” prior to beingness? That Conviction – and I am not just talking understanding – that Conviction is supposed to appear spontaneously. And, as I keep telling you, this can only happen with strict meditation. It is the base. There is no need for any kind of asceticism. There is no need to visit temples or Masters. Approach your own, Inner Master. You are neglecting your own Master because you are measuring that Master in the body-form.

Knowledge is everywhere. That is called Realization. It is Unidentified, Invisible, Anonymous Identity. There is no name. You can’t see it, but you will feel it. What you see is illusion. That through which you see is Reality. The entire world is projected out of the Invisible Seer within you. You need a big dose of spiritual courage to accept the Reality.

p.strong: 42. There is Fire Everywhere

section.p: Maharaj: There are thousands of questions. The answer is within you. There is only one answer: Except your Selfless Self, there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman. They are all concepts which appear upon your Presence. Even “I am Brahman” is a concept, which appears upon your Presence. Presence does not know “I am Presence”. So, instead of studying spirituality, try to absorb what you have listened to, so far. It is the Listener’s story, the Listener’s identity. This body is an opportunity for you. Do not waste time. It is invaluable, your Presence is invaluable. Through the body you can identify that “I”.

A combination of Presence, and the body is needed. When Presence touched with the body, you said “I”. This combination is like that of a matchbox and stick. When the two make contact, you can see the fire.

Be practical! You have a lot of theory. You are not required to study any spiritual books. “The five elements, the four bodies” – why concern yourself with all of this theory – the “four bodies”, and all the rest of it? “Consciousness”, “unconsciousness”, “awareness” – this is only word-play. It will not give you happiness. It is momentary, spiritual entertainment. You are separate from the five elements. When your Presence appears, the whole world is projected, including the five elements. You are the father and mother of the five elements.

p.strong: 43. Why Fill Your Pockets?

section.p: Maharaj: We have so many desires. “I want this, that, and the other thing”. Human beings don’t come to the world with any pockets. They do not leave the body with pockets either. Therefore, why fill your pockets in between?

This means that prior to beingness, there was nothing. Now there is so much anxiety, greed and hedonism, for want of a better word: “I want this. I want that”. Who wants all these things, and what do you want them for? How long do you imagine you are going to use all these things? You are after money, sex, power and many other material wants. These are all temporary solutions to alleviate your unease. They offer only a brief respite from body-knowledge discomfort.
Q: The Conviction, the Spontaneous Conviction, can happen at any time. It does not necessarily happen during meditation. Is that correct?

Maharaj: It happens spontaneously. After Conviction, all the body-based-knowledge dissolves. It is very, very interesting. Go deep and deep and deep within Selfless Self, and you will find peacefulness, “Oh! I Am That!”
Q: Deeper and deeper into meditation?

Maharaj: After meditating, using these sacred words, all the concepts will melt. Your body-identity will be dissolved. And you will see within yourself, “My beingness is everywhere, and in every being. My Presence is everywhere”. That Spontaneous Conviction will appear within you.

p.strong: 44. Whatever Experience Appears is Illusion

section.p: Q: So during the course of the day, when all these family members are around...
Maharaj: Yes, yes, it’s okay. There are many family members around in a dream also.
Q: They are not really there, right?

Maharaj: [Maharaj smiles] I am not asking you to neglect your family members. But at the same time, there should be the Conviction that, “I am nothing to do with all these things”, as if you are acting in some drama. I will give you a little example: You are acting in a drama, playing a role. You are playing a very nice role. Everyone is very impressed. But at the same time, you know that “I am nothing to do with this role. I only have to act for two or three hours”. Convince yourself in this way! Knowledge is there, a lot of knowledge is there. In the light of that knowledge of Reality, you have to convince yourself.

Q: I have noticed that since reciting the Naam Mantra, I have become more inwardly focused, and much quieter. I am still able to take care of my duties, but I am very detached, and much more efficient. It seems like my energy is more inside, much more at peace. So it has been a good experience. I think I understand a little bit of what you are talking about. I don’t know about Spontaneous Conviction, but there has been a big switch, a big change since being given the Naam Mantra.

Maharaj: As long as we are measuring ourselves in the body-form, whatever experiences appear are illusion. If you feel “I am Brahman”, it is also illusion. Whatever feelings you have, appear upon your Presence, and are, therefore, illusory.

p.strong: 45. Presence is a Prerequisite

section.p: Q: I came across the teachings of Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj about ten years ago. It had an impact on my beingness, but I wasn’t really able to handle it. So over the last ten years, I have mainly practised on the Sufi path. The teacher was familiar with the lineage of Sri Ramakrishna also. Last year I came back to reading Sri Nisargadatta, as well as your wonderful book, Maharaj.
Maharaj: After reading so many books, we need to absorb that knowledge. Knowledge is just Reality, your Reality, the Listener’s Reality, the Reader’s Reality. That “I” must dissolve.

That “I” appeared upon your Spontaneous Presence. If Presence were not there, who will talk about the “I”? And as I told you, except your Selfless Self, there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman, no Master. The Invisible Listener within you is the Master. It does not have any special identity. To say “I”, your Presence is required. If Presence were not there, who will talk about that “I” and who will talk about “God”? To say “God”, your Presence is required. The body can’t say, “this is God”. If there is no Presence, the body can’t act. This is a dead body. The Invisible Presence, the Invisible Listener within you, is that through which you can identify the world. That means the entire world is projected out of your Invisible Presence. If Presence were not in the body, who will talk about the world, and God, and everything?

Who knows Krishna? Prior to beingness, did you know Krishna? Did you know of any God? Did you know any Master? Did you have any spiritual knowledge? Nothing. And after leaving the body, of what use will Krishna be to you? All gods and goddesses are concepts. I am not criticizing those concepts. However, I am placing before you the Invisible Identity within you, which is called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Krishna, or whatever name is given to That nameless identity.

Presence is a prerequisite. One cannot say “Lord Krishna” without the power of your Presence. If your Presence were not there, who will talk about Lord Krishna? We know the picture, “Oh, this is Lord Krishna”. Somebody told us, or we saw it in some book, that “this is God, this is Rama, this is Krishna, this is something else” But prior to beingness, did you know anything about all this? Be clear! After leaving the body will you know anything about these divinities? Will you know anything about any Master, or God?

We are using the body to identify these gods and goddesses. Instead, try to identify the Invisible Reader within you. We are collecting information through the books. This information is not going to help you to know yourself. Don’t keep reading, reading. Don’t keep on visiting the Masters, without absorbing what they are trying to convey. What is their message? It is that there is nothing except your Selfless Self. There is no other God, but you.
We are trying to identify God through the body-form. What is the use of this God? In order for us to have some peace, we visit temples and churches. We pray to the gods!
Body-based-knowledge is not tolerable, and that is why we need these gods and goddesses.

Try and see the Master within you. Why don’t you see that Krishna within you? Because your body-identity is in the way. Why is meditation required? Again, I will say, because through meditation, you are hammering yourself, you are inviting the attention of the Invisible Meditator within you, that you are Ultimate Truth. You are Final Truth. That is the base. This is your foundation.

p.strong: 46. We Love Ourselves the Most

section.p: Maharaj: You are to undergo some meditation. That is most important for you. Do you have a Master? Have you had a Master previously?

Q: When I was sixteen years old I met a man, and he taught me how to play the tabla. He told me about Sri Ramakrishna. I lived with his family for a little while, and then, after that, he directed me to the Vivekananda Vedanta Society. And after that, I met some other teachers, too.

Probably one of the most important teachers was from the Sufi lineage. He was also very familiar with other lineages. And then, of course, reading the works of Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj. But I feel that all of this has led me to you, Maharaj. When you say that this is the final destination, I believe it emphatically, with all my heart. What you teach, and what you are saying about who we are is the final destination. I feel I do not need to go anywhere, anymore. Even if I had not had the opportunity to meet you, I would still feel that way.
Q2: I think you have just expressed a lot of our feelings in here. After decades of searching, to finally know, “Here is my Master”. It is just such a blessing.

Maharaj: Constant meditation is most important. Any words will do: Aham Brahmasmi, Shivoham - through which you are inviting the attention of the Invisible Concentrator within you. This is the beginning stage. You already have all the basic knowledge. You have read I Am That, you have read Selfless Self, and through them you have spiritual understanding. After reading the books, absorb that knowledge. It will happen spontaneously within you, because you are the source of this world. Your Spontaneous Invisible Presence is the projector of this world. If there is no Seer, who will talk about the seen? It is very, very simple and clear.

Q: I have been thinking about what questions I would ask you when I saw you, but not many questions came to mind. As I said before, your words are so clear in the book Selfless Self. My only question would be to ask you – and for this I would be eternally grateful – if I might receive the Naam Mantra from you, to help dissolve the body-knowledge that you talk about, and to strengthen the Conviction even more so.
Maharaj: I will give the Naam Mantra to you. But you must follow it strictly. There should not be a wavering mind.

Whatever you have listened to, and read so far, absorb it, digest it – that is number one. Secondly, you must have the Conviction, that “I was not the body, I am not the body, and I am not going to remain the body”. That Conviction will appear spontaneously. How will it appear? It will appear after continuous meditation.

After Conviction, you will not be affected by things so much. You will be more detached. At the moment, feelings are there, sometimes very strong feelings are there, because of all our relations and relationships, which we have given so much importance to, and taken for reality.

“My father”, “my mother”, “my husband”, “my child”, it is okay, it happens. It is natural because we have had a long association with the body, so that if something bad happens to our loved ones, we feel sorry, very sorry. But because we have so much love and affection for the body, we seem to love ourselves more than our relatives.

Do you know the story of the monkey and her infant? There is a flood, so a monkey and her infant are forced to climb up a tree. As the water continues to rise, the monkey tries to climb further up the tree. Eventually, she can’t climb any further. She tries to lift the infant above the water, but the water reaches up, up, and up. Finally, she becomes desperate, and stands on the infant. It is a terrible story! The point is that you have that instinct of self-preservation, for your body, that trumps everything else.

p.strong: 47. I am Sharing This Knowledge With the Grace of My Master

section.p: Q: Maharaj, you said in the book, Selfless Self that Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj taught you, or showed you, how to read the Dasbodh book. But you never said what he taught you, or how he taught you how to read.

Maharaj: Ah, yes, I used to go every day to Nisargadatta Maharaj’s house. The regular reader was not there, so, my Master, asked me to read the Dasbodh. I was reading it with a very quiet voice, so he said, “Your voice should be able to reach the ears of ten thousand people!”

He taught me many things. What I am today, I am, because of him alone. My whole education and everything was due to him. He always gave practical advice. One evening, when I was at Maharaj’s place, he asked me what I was doing, and whether I had a job, or if I intended to pursue further education? I did not know how to reply to him.
He asked me, “What do you mean by spirituality? Touching the Master’s feet? Placing garlands on the Master?
Clapping? This is not spirituality. Do something in life. Why don’t you join the college?”

I was not in a position to join the college because of some financial problems, but he insisted, “Enrol at the college! Do not come to me! Do not bring any garlands! Do not bring any prasad!” Coincidentally, one of Sri Nisargadatta’s senior devotees was also the Registrar of a nearby college. So, Maharaj asked him, “Take this boy into your college!” It was with great difficulty that I joined college, as I had previously received very low marks in my exams. He used to say, “Do something! You should continue learning until the age of forty”. And so, I continued my education until I was forty years old.
My Master treated me just like his child. My college graduation, my law graduation, all these degrees belong to Maharaj.

And with the grace of my Master, I am sharing this knowledge with everybody. And, I am saying the same thing to you that Siddharameshwar used to say: If you are Realized, make others Realized. Spread your rug or mat, and start sharing the knowledge. But on no account, commercialize it. Why should you ask for money? It is free knowledge. This knowledge is free knowledge. It is your innate knowledge.

p.strong: OCTOBER 26, 2016, COLUMBIA, U.S.A.

p.emphasis: “God only exists because your Presence is there first. If there is no Presence, who can talk about God? God is your baby.”

p.strong: 48. In the Beginning, There is Bound to be a Struggle

section.p: Q: I struggle with so many distractions when I meditate. I sit there, and I do not want to meditate.

Maharaj: Concentrate on the meditation. Meditation is the only medium, through which all your concepts will dissolve. In the beginning, there is bound to be a struggle. As I have told you, the mind, the ego, and the intellect, and a lot of concepts, will rebel against you.
There is a nice example given by Nisargadatta Maharaj. Suppose you own a property, and you have several tenants living there. You want to regain possession of the rooms, so

you ask them to leave. While leaving the premises, the tenants will start to abuse you, because they have been staying there for so many years. If suddenly you say, “I want possession of the premises”, they will abuse you.

It is the same thing here. There have been many, many concepts since your childhood days. After some prolonged meditation, the bad elements, or the bad concepts, will rebel against you. This story is just for understanding purposes. Don’t take anything that I say literally!

Some unsettling things may happen, but understand, that this is a cleaning process. It does not have any spiritual importance, but it will happen in the beginning. When the seeker/devotee takes the Naam Mantra for meditation, there are some forces at work, trying to distract you from the Reality. It may be your mind, ego, or the intellect. All the while, they have been using your energy, and consequently, have made us their slaves. You have given so much importance to the mind. “Don’t go against my mind…!” The subtle ego is strong, and the subtle intellect. Be the Master of the mind, ego, and intellect.
Do not struggle with your thoughts. Do not struggle with your mind!

p.strong: 49. Stop All This Thinking. Be Totally Blank!

section.p: Q: When I get to this place of feeling presence, the concepts drop and I just feel this pure experiencing, where there is this confusion and a lost feeling. And there is some resistance that comes, and I can be like that for maybe five minutes, ten minutes, some length of time where I feel like I can’t function in the world. And then there is some part of me that is scared, that doesn’t want to lose its individuality or identity, because it can’t even fathom that. And then suddenly I go, “Wow”, and I come back to “I’ve just got to be this person and do my thing”.
I feel like I have gotten to that place so many times, but then this resistance comes. It is almost like there wants to be some bargaining, “Can I just hold onto a little piece?”

Maharaj: You are experiencing all these things. This is the struggle of your thoughts, of your concepts. And you know the struggles that are going on inside. All these struggles appear upon your Presence. Prior to beingness, there was no struggle. This struggle appears upon your Presence because of that concept, “I am somebody”. So, do not fight with the concepts, do not fight with the mind, do not struggle with the mind. Thoughts are coming, and going. You are scared of letting go of the concept, “I am somebody” because you are attached to this illusion. It is all you know. That is why meditation is needed, to reduce the power these concepts have over you.

Therefore, meditation is most important. Meditation plays a very good part. In the beginning, you have to undergo the meditation, and then all your concepts will be dissolved. You will remain unconcerned with all the thoughts, untouched by the whole world. Because, except your Selfless Self, nothing is there. All these concepts, body-based concepts, appear upon your Presence. These are layers. You are experiencing all these concepts. “Why am I experiencing so many things here?” – these are layers upon your Presence, and they will be cleared away with meditation. Meditation gives you courage to control all these things. It will happen spontaneously. Nothing is impossible for you. You have become a victim of your own concepts.
Q: Yeah, like even right now...

Maharaj: Be strong! Have some courage – You can do it! Do not underestimate yourself! When the Master says, “You are Brahman”, you are Brahman, so live like Brahman, not like a man or woman. It is open fact, it is Reality. Forget everything, and be with you. The Master is not separate from you. God is not separate from you.
Q: Even right now, I can feel the resistance. I mean, I feel presence and I feel resistance, and I just have this little awareness that this resistance is a game, just like it is a game.
Maharaj: Stop all this thinking. Be totally blank!
Q: Yes, that is the part that scares me.

Maharaj: Be as you were prior to beingness. Were there any thoughts there? Are you a woman or a man? After leaving the body, do all these thoughts remain? Nothing remains! Why struggle with the thoughts? Why struggle with the concepts?

Try to absorb this Reality, that is already within you. Be bold, have some courage. I am placing before you the facts of your existence, your Reality, the Listener’s Reality, not the Reality of Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, or Master. Live like a Master, live like a God. It is your identity.

You have supernatural power, tremendous power, miraculous power, but you are not using it. With courage, you can overcome any problem whether it is physical, mental or spiritual. All your problems will be resolved.

p.strong: 50. There is No Waking, and There is No Sleeping

section.p: Q: For me, I find that the Mantra itself is very easy, and it is something I can slip into and stay focused with. I mean, I could do it all day, but what I have discovered is that very quickly the body becomes energetically unsettled, and I do not sleep well. My sleep becomes very restless, and eventually I just get too tired to function and have to stop, take a break for a few days, and let everything equalize.

So what I am trying to find – you call the Mantra the prescription – and what I am trying to find, is the right dosage for me. So, is it half an hour a day, and then I take a break, or is it an hour? But then I find myself getting sucked in, and end up doing three hours, and then I am not sleeping well.
Maharaj: Don’t count the hours!
Q: Well I don’t specifically, but I kind of generally.
Maharaj: The Mantra will dissolve the egoistic “I”. That “I” is to dissolve. That is its purpose.
Q: Yeah, but right now the “I” doesn’t like not sleeping.

Maharaj: Because you are measuring yourself in the body-form. There is no waking, and there is no sleeping. There is no consciousness, and there is no unconsciousness. All concepts are related to the body only. Prior to beingness, was there any sleep? Was there any waking? Was there any consciousness? There were no concepts, and no “I”.
Q: You talk about spiritual courage.
Maharaj: You must have courage to accept the Reality. Have strong faith in yourself, and in your Master.
Q: When I take a break from the Mantra, I just kind of be with the “I am”…

Maharaj: Why take a break from the Mantra? These words are always alerting you to the illusory concepts. They are reminding your Selfless Self. So, recite the Mantra until you have Spontaneous Conviction, “I was not the body, I am not the body, I am not going to remain the body”. After Spontaneous Conviction, there will not be any concepts that will have any power over you. Nothing will impress you, or depress you!

p.strong: 51. The Great Saint is Within You!

section.p: Maharaj: I am not asking you to continuously say “Ram, Ram, Ram”, or spend hours reciting the mantra. Try to have Conviction. Accept the fact, the Reality within you. This body was once a child, then it became a young man, and eventually, it will be an old man. Is this your Reality?
Who is going, and who is coming? Who is taking birth, and who is dying? All these concepts are related to the body only. And as you know, you were not the body at all, you are not the body at all.

We are not here to criticize or to get involved in criticism. This is not a debate, we are not trying to prove anything. But practical spirituality is most important. Practical spirituality means what? That knowledge, that Reality is supposed to be absorbed totally within you. At that moment, you will forget the entire world. You will be untouched by the world. Though you are living in this world, you are untouched by the world. You are unconcerned with the world. All relations, everything came along with the body only.

Everything will dissolve along with the body. This means that everything came out of nothing, and everything will dissolve within nothing. In between, you feel that “I am something”. That “something” is supposed to dissolve.

After Conviction, you will be totally fearless. Problems will be there, but your problems will be reduced, dissolved, and you will take them in a light fashion. So, be strong, and have the courage to accept the Reality. Satsang alone is not sufficient. If you listen to so many Masters, and attend numerous different satsangs, it is pointless! [Maharaj shakes his head]. Have satsang with yourself! You have to please your Self. Please your Self! The Saint is not separate from you. The Master is not separate from you. The Great Saint is within you. There is no Saint except your Selfless Self. There is no Master except your Selfless Self. So, stop measuring yourself in the body-form. Beyond that, there is nothing. This is the Reality. It is Ultimate Truth, Final Truth. Try to absorb, what you have listened to so far.
Do not ignore your responsibilities. This is a human body, so you need to live as a human. Don’t be inhuman! Show love and affection to your family members, and take care of them.

We know ourselves through the body, “I am somebody”. The body is the medium, not Ultimate Truth. It is a food-body. As long as you are supplying food and water, it will grow. Be loyal to yourself, and be faithful to yourself, and your Master. But the Master is not in any form. He is a formless Master.

Strong involvement is necessary. It is often said that when we come into this world, we do not bring anything, and that when we leave this world, we do not take anything with us. It is a common statement. Why, therefore, become a victim of all these concepts? “Mind”, “ego”, “intellect”, so many chakras are there, kundalini, etc, so many words are available. What chakra? What kundalini? What deed? What karma or destiny?

We are under the pressure of so many concepts. Where were all these concepts prior to beingness? Who knows what “karma” is? Who knows what “religion” is? Prior to beingness, was there any religion? Prior to beingness, what religion did you belong to? Did you know anything about the gods or the goddesses? We do not know ourselves. What will happen after leaving the body? All the same questions are asked, again and again. “What is the next birth after death going to be?” What birth is there? Who knows? So, do not become a slave of your own concepts.
Meditation and spiritual knowledge give you the courage to live happily. Whatever pain and misery may be there in this life, can be overcome through the meditation.

p.strong: 52. Where Everything Ends, There You Are

section.p: Q: When I sit for meditation, I sit deliberately to concentrate and focus. And for the rest of the day, I just try to repeat the Naam in the background. I feel like I am making some progress with that, but I want that courage.

You tell me I am Brahman, and I believe you. What does my body need to do, to convince itself to get the Conviction? Because I’m there, I am ready, but letting go… It just seems that whenever I am about ready to let go, somebody else comes at me with another problem, and I bounce back into the dream, instead of focussing on Reality.

Maharaj: Be normal! There are so many things around you. Whether to take the touch, or not, is up to you. Accept the sting, or don’t accept the sting! You are your own Master. There are a lot of thoughts flowing inside, and you are watching all these thoughts. Do not respond, to each, and every thought. It is the nature of the mind, the nature of the body. The thoughts are flowing.

Where all thoughts end, there you are. Where everything ends, there you are. Where were all these thoughts prior to beingness? Will there be any thoughts after leaving the body? Will there be any fighting, struggle or chaos? No, there will not be anything!

Directly or indirectly, we have accepted “I am the body”, “I am somebody”, “I am doing this thing, I am doing that thing”, “I am a devotee”, “I am a disciple”, “I am a Master”, “I am Brahman”, and all these things. That “I am” must go! That concept “I am” appeared upon your Spontaneous, Invisible Presence, and Presence does not know that, “I am Presence”. It is just like the space or the sky, everywhere. The five elements do not know “I am the five elements”. Water does not know “I am water”. The earth does not know “I am earth”. The sky does not know, “I am sky”. You are the Projector of the five elements. When the Spirit clicks with the body, the five elements are projected. The entire world is projected. You are the father of this world, you are the mother of this world.

All these concepts have appeared upon your Invisible Presence, where there is no pain and no pleasure. Body-knowledge is not tolerable, therefore pain is there. This is a food-body. Prior to beingness, and after leaving the body, there is no pain, and no pleasure. Does a dead body have any pleasure or pain? No! That means: You were not the body, you are not the body, you are not going to remain the body. So you ask, “Then who am I?” It is called Brahman, Atman, Paramatman, God, Master, that you are, the Supernatural Power. These are only words. You are beyond words.

Q: So, I can do my duties, my family duties, be the mom, the wife, and the glue, actually, that holds the family together, and makes them all stand upright, and at the same time, simultaneously, I can detach and watch this as a dream. In other words, do my duty, but pull back, and watch it more as a dream. I guess it is about giving myself acceptance not to be the pillar for everyone around me, and that is where the lack of courage is. It is just frustrating getting pulled back into that dream when you really don’t want to be there. And just when you think, “Oh!” somebody has got another problem and something else for you to solve. And that is where I find the difficulty for me comes. I didn’t sleep for a while and was exhausted. But now I go to sleep reciting the Naam, and I do not have any more problems.

Maharaj: In the beginning it happens. It is a cleaning process. All the concepts are being eliminated. Your so-called mind, ego, and intellect are stinging you, pricking you from the backside. That internal force is trying to distract you from the Reality. It happens in the beginning. Because, directly or indirectly, we have a lot of body-concepts. It will take some time.

But do not ignore your family responsibilities. We are human beings. Spirituality is not a restriction, a bondage, or a blockage. Don’t just say “I am Brahman, I am Brahman, Brahman, Brahman”. Who will give you food while you say, “I am Brahman”? You need to attend to your duties, and responsibilities, but at the same time, there should be Conviction.

p.strong: 53. You Are Ultimate Truth

section.p: Maharaj: Now you can ask any question that you want. Am I the only one talking? This is chatting, spiritual chatting. I am not here to give dry lectures on spirituality.
Q: I still experience pleasure and pain.

Maharaj: Pleasure and pain are connected with the body. Where was that pleasure and pain prior to beingness? After leaving the body, who will know anything about pleasure and pain? So long as we are holding this human body, we will be connected with pleasure and pain. But, there is no pleasure and no painfulness.

Q: Maharaj, for the last couple of years with my sadhana, nothing has given me more pleasure, than just abiding as awareness or presence. And the positive benefits of the sadhana have been that it has become a lot easier. There are the synchronicities and coincidences, and there are no problems really, more so, just challenges. Life has become a lot simpler. But on the flip side, my aim has been to try and extend the duration of my sadhana. So it used to be about an hour and a half, now it is two hours, and I am trying to step it up. But it takes my whole day to get that two hours, and I enjoy this more than anything else.

Maharaj: It is not necessary to meditate for one or two hours. One minute is sufficient for you! I am advising meditation. What is the purpose of meditation? You are inviting the attention of the Invisible Meditator within you, that you are Ultimate Truth. Except your Selfless Self, nothing is there. That Conviction is supposed to appear spontaneously. No special sadhana is required. Sitting for one hour, two hours, ten hours, has no meaning. One moment is sufficient. That “I am” is to dissolve! Behind what you are doing, behind that deed, there is some base there, a trace of “I am”: “I am somebody”, “I am doing sadhana”, “I am a devotee”. Dissolve that “I am”.
Q: Maharaj, I call it sadhana, but it is really...

Maharaj: Okay, I can understand. I am not blaming you. It happens in every case. But I say sadhana is not necessary. What you have learned, what you have listened to through various sources, try to absorb it totally. It is an open fact, that except your Selfless Self, there is no God.
