What is the purpose of sadhana? What is the purpose of devotion? What is the purpose of meditation? What do you want to achieve? What is the goal? What is Ultimate Truth? What is Final Truth? You are Final Truth. You are Ultimate Truth.

p.strong: Encouragement From Sri Ramakant Maharaj NOVEMBER 2, 2016

section.p: Jai Guru,

After strong involvement in spirituality through meditation, a flow of illusory concepts, will appear upon your Spontaneous Invisible Presence, and instantly disappear. Continue with your normal spiritual practice, which will result in all the concepts vanishing, including Brahman.

How were you prior to beingness? There was no experiencer, no experience, and no witness. Though you are living with the body, you will remain unconcerned with the illusory world. There will be complete peacefulness, happiness, and fearlessness; no feelings of existence, and hence no confusion. All identities will disappear, including mind, ego, and intellect, which is miraculous, and cannot be expressed in words. In brief, you are very close to the top of the spiritual mountain, so do not look back, and do not share whatever is happening in your spiritual life.

Your Spontaneous Presence is everywhere, just like the sky or the space, where there is no individuality, where experience and the experiencer dissolve or disappear. No body-essence remains. I think that you no longer require excessive practice or sadhana, because Ultimate Truth or Reality has been absorbed within you. Do not pay attention to whatever is happening, or not happening, in this illusory world. Whatever I have conveyed to you for your spirituality is enough.

When you have strong devotion, your crazy mind rebels against you, to distract you from your Reality. Do not struggle with the mind. That good or bad thoughts are flowing, is just the nature of the mind. Do not pay so much attention to your mind, ego, and intellect. As you know, so many unpleasant incidents happen in your life, but with your strong spiritual willpower you can overcome all these problems. Furthermore, with the blessings of Sadguru, your “so many unpleasant problems” are solved miraculously. What more do you want? Sadguru krupa is always with you.

Why worry? Be normal, be simple, be humble, be always with you. Concentrate deeply and fully in meditation. Continue reciting the Naam Mantra. Be peaceful, be tension-free, be happy, be fearless all the time. In this human life, there is more pain and less pleasure. Almost all the spiritual saints face a lot of problems, but with their strong devotional power, they overcome all their unpleasant situations. Do not be a slave of your mind!
With your strong devotion and faith in Sadguru, you can easily control your mind, ego, and intellect. Sadguru bhakti has strong shakti power.
With blessings to all,

p.emphasis: Jai Sadguru!

title.p: PART THREE How The Master Changed My Life

section.p: Hopefully, my testimony will inspire sincere seekers around the world, as so many of us have lost faith because of the hardships of life. My greatest encounter in this existence is Sri Ramakant Maharaj. Finally, I came face to face with the knower of all hearts manifesting Himself in a physical body. Having received the Naam Mantra felt like the detonation of the atomic consciousness inside of me.

My seeking came to an end. The supposedly heavy cloud hanging around my head, started to dissolve slowly, but surely. My power and faith was regenerated. It is a matter of great grace and merit to come across such a rare one.

There are many mystical experiences from the meditation – colourful and bright light! The circumstances around me suddenly became very favourable – from having no job, I am now doing the job of my dreams, and my physical health is improving a lot.

After seeking for about twenty years, I came across Maharaj’s teachings, and the book, Selfless Self. It truly had such an impact on me, but for some time, there was also quite a resistance to it, especially the Mantra and the Bhajans! I was so “stuffed” with concepts from so much reading, that it took a while for the “dissolving” process to allow the humbleness that was needed, to truly embrace Maharaj’s words. The teachings have impacted my “illusory” life in so many ways, especially that there is no longer any drama or conflicts at all. Having total trust and faith in Maharaj, and the teachings is just essential. I can honestly say I feel this sense of “me” dissolving more each day. Bhajans, Mantra meditation and reading Selfless Self is truly a way of life for me now. There is nothing more important! And, because of this, all my “duties” get done effortlessly, in such a natural way. My gratitude to Maharaj is beyond words.

What is unique about Sri Ramakant Maharaj? He is not just another teacher or Master, offering his own brand of knowledge. He has no intention of filling our heads with knowledge! Quite the opposite, in fact. He is here to remove all the illusory knowledge that we have accumulated over a lifetime, so that the light of Self can shine once more. The process is one of subtraction, not addition. When I met Sri Ramakant Maharaj, I knew that “I” had reached the end of the road, and entered into Reality, that has no beginning, and no end: Ultimate Reality, Ultimate Truth – I Am That, without saying it.

When you hear the Master, Sri Ramakant speaking, you know it is Truth. He found me at last, after searching for over thirty years. I finally found a living Realized Master, and life has not been the same since. I am happy and contented all the time, full of laughter and joy.

The seemingly, endless agony of yearning for a true Sadguru like Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj finally blossomed in discovering Sri Ramakant Maharaj’s existence. So, I eagerly rushed to Him and was truly satsified. So, to all the yearning hearts, I implore you to dive deep into this rarest of Knowledge.

I missed Nisargadatta Maharaj. I was not around for Ramana Maharshi. I wasn’t going to miss Sri Ramakant Maharaj. In his presence, I was left speechless. I took the Naam Mantra and it literally, blew my mind! I am empty, thoughtless and beyond happy! I cannot say any more, except that I am eternally grateful to my Master.

My search started when I was about twenty years of age. Two years ago, I visited Sri Ramakant Maharaj in Nashik Ashram. I knew he was the one to bring my search to an end. He did! Every day, I feel so fortunate and so blessed because I know who I am.

When I came across Sadguru Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj’s famous book, I AM THAT, I could not go past the first two chapters. I could somewhat feel the value of what was conveyed, but could not relate enough to it. So I left the book altogether and went in other directions.

A decade after that, I met his disciple, Sadguru Sri Ramakant Maharaj, and after a few months listening to him, meditating, remembering, practicing the Mantra, I opened I AM THAT again and was surprised: I could easily relate to it and started benefitting from it. Maharaj’s simple and relentless hammering and the wisdom of Sadguru Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj, what a combination!

Maharaj is teaching with absolute straightforwardness, utter simplicity, infinite patience and care. It is evident that Sadguru Sri Ramakant Maharaj is totally given over to his Master Sadguru Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj. He is moving his

Master’s teaching forward to reflect the needs of the devotees of our time and help them transform the easily available intellectual knowledge into actual, living knowledge. He is a lawyer that gives the courage to fight one’s case for freedom. He is a doctor that helps dispel the disease and recover the original health. He is, as he once said, the garbage man, calling for your trash!

The Master is completely dedicated, selflessly giving away his energy, time and resources without counting, keeping the oath of the Lineage not to accept any material advantage from devotees. I feel truly blessed that he took my hand. If not, I would probably still be roaming around, in search of myself!

Since meeting Maharaj in late 2015, I have been following His advice diligently with both, daily sitting meditation and recitation of the Guru Mantra, as often as possible. This has been an intense, spiritual journey with many adversities – physical (including surgery), professional, emotional and family-related difficulties, that I faced along the way. The intensity of the meditation is sometimes a bit too much for the physical body to bear.

At times, it has been a struggle to balance my professional, hectic work schedules, and meditation, alongside family members clinging to conventional religion. This has provided much distraction and resistance, since they are not spiritually mature enough to understand, or accept the truth of Advaita. As Maharaj states so often, this is a “cleaning process” that must be undergone in order to get closer and closer to Selfless Self.

Along the way, despite all of the external problems, there has been so much spiritual bliss, and some profound experiences have resulted from the meditation, including expansive and empty states of Samadhi, feelings of body lightness, and growing devotion to our Master. Although Spontaneous Conviction has not resulted yet, full intellectual conviction has occurred. The Master keeps encouraging me, by confirming that I am getting closer and closer to the Ultimate Goal. Thank you.

My professional life is incredibly challenging – daily work with dying patients, and assisting in their immense suffering. At times the stress and anxiety I’ve experienced with this, has been such a weight to carry that I’ve searched for so much meaning in all this.
But since meeting Maharaj, and in reading Selfless Self – everything is lighter. Even though I share in these worldly experiences, I am less and less affected by them.

It is a privilege to care for the dying, but I am constantly reminded with ever deepening clarity, of the meaning behind Maharaj’s words “Who is dying?” and “There is no doer and no deed.” Though I continue to encounter loss and grief, recitation of the Naam Mantra keeps me centred, and away from the mental turbulence that can appear with my work.
I’ve stopped searching. Jai Guru.

Maharaj has had a tremendous impact on my life. My spiritual progress had plateaued, and coming across Him has been like the catalyst between my “B.C.” and “A.D.” I would like to share the most notable experience I have had since coming to know Him.

It began the day Maharaj returned to India from his trip to the U.S. in 2016. Shortly before going to bed, and a few hours after meditating that evening, spontaneously it occurred to me that my mind was very quiet. I tried forcing myself to have a thought and it was quite difficult, like trying to lift a

heavy object. I could just barely have some thoughts, but generally my mind was still. There were no feelings of anxiety, even when I did manage to think about something that usually gave me anxiety. This felt very uplifting, and had put me on the verge of tears. I went to sleep, wondering if I would still be in this state in the morning. I was, but then I wondered if I could still do my job properly, because I’m a translator and, of course, I need to be able to translate and type.

When I got started with my work, to my amazement, even though I wasn’t thinking about how to translate the words, the correct translations just came out of me, as my eyes looked at the computer screen, and my fingers typed. So, not only could I still do my job, I actually had the most productive day I had ever had, as I was able to work with no thought required. All other activities flowed effortlessly as well.

Later on, my mind became totally quiet. It felt like sitting alone in a cavernous auditorium. There was just silence, to a degree I wouldn’t have believed before. No fears, no anxieties! In this silence, I realized how many subtle thoughts and impressions we have that contribute to the typical human state of fear and anxiety.

In the weeks before, I could somewhat reduce the mind’s activity by exerting effort, like flexing a muscle, but now it was like no such effort was needed, because no thoughts were arising. Furthermore, even when there was a flurry of activity around me, such as people talking, and moving about, everything felt dreamlike and inherently empty, like a void. I felt indescribable spaciousness, like the entire universe was emanating from me – not my body, but my true identity as the screen of consciousness that is Selfless Self. There was no “I” thought, just everything happening, which felt like in essence a nothingness. I can’t really capture it all in words.

This experience came to an end four days after it began, but I haven’t been the same since. My recollection of that state continues to act as a beacon. This is all thanks to Maharaj, and for this, and everything else I have boundless gratitude toward Him.

The instruction is very simple: you were not the body, you are not the body, and you will not continue to be the body. Real Self-enquiry only began at that moment Everything that happened in this body I put under the gaze of Self-enquiry: Where was this emotion (or thought), before the birth of this body? Where will you go, when you have to leave the body? Immediately, everything is absorbed in the Absolute Presence. No more roads, no more Gurus, no more teachings, I placed my head at the feet of Maharaj.

I believed in Maharaj. I believed and practised with great determination: meditation, self-inquiry, chanting the Bhajans. A strong Conviction suddenly appeared: if you say “Jai Guru”. with deep respect and love, towards the Masters of this Sampradaya, you do not need anything else. With Love...
RAJA-DHI RAJ SADGURUNATH SRI RAMAKANT MAHARAJ KI JAY!

After having read so many books and scriptures, after having met prominent gurus and teachers, and after having numerous divine experiences, there came a moment, amidst personal crisis and desperation, that I first felt inspired to travel to India, not as a tourist, but for the express purpose of sitting at the feet of a real Jnani. I was not seeking more knowledge, or another experience, I was searching for the end of both. I only wanted true peace, real happiness, and Absolute Truth. Sri Ramakant Maharaj provided the key that was necessary to unlock those inner treasures.
Although I was only with Maharaj for a few short weeks,

his Presence and teachings made a lasting impression. His message is concise and direct, so much so, that the mind has difficulty accepting that Reality can be so simple. For this reason, Maharaj prescribes meditation for preparing the mind to absorb the Ultimate Truth. Maharaj stressed the need for Conviction, and he repeatedly admonished us to actively convince ourselves, by continually remembering that truth.

He never asked for money or promoted himself; he only asked that we did not waste time with endless wandering from books to teachers, to holy sites. He had revealed the essence of the scriptures, and the preaching of the saints. This is the final stop. This is where we exit the train and make our home, the home we never truly left. It is our very self.

Sri Ramakant Maharaj’s words are now a constant inner voice. “The speaker in me, and the listener in you, are one and the same.” Maharaj is not a guru external to me; he is the Master within me. As doubts arise, I hear him say, “I am not making you a disciple; I am making you a Master. The Masterly Essence is already within you.” When meditation feels difficult, Maharaj encourages me: “Be strong! Have confidence! You are the Master of the mind!” When I feel confused, Maharaj reminds me, “You are absolute truth. There is no ‘I’ or ‘you’. There is nothing except for your Selfless Self.” All sense of separation and limitation, are slowly dissolving, as I hear his voice. “You are like the sky. There are many houses, but only one sky. If the house falls, is the sky hurt? There are many bodies, but only one Self. You are not in the world; the world is in you!” Maharaj’s speech is always giving, and never taking. “Become enlightened, and help others become enlightened, but never make it a business.” I am forever grateful for Maharaj’s constant hammering. Jai Sadguru!

Some months, ago I stumbled upon the discourses of Sri Ramakant Maharaji, and I was captivated by his message which was very simple, bold, direct, and potent. I am no stranger, to a wide variety of Indian philosophies, and yogic teachings, by many masters and gurus, but still Maharaji’s message stood out, as an unusually no-nonsense insight into the true nature of our identities. Next, I read his book Selfless Self, and was even more convinced that he was one, who spoke not about a philosophy, but from his own experience. Soon I decided I must see for myself, if he was for real.

I came to his ashram in the hope of seeing him, and hearing his discourses first hand, but upon my arrival, I discovered that the Master had recently become ill, and had just returned from the hospital with strict orders to have complete rest. However, he did attend the evening satsang on my first day there, and though his body was weak, and he did not speak, his Presence was powerful. I continued to attend the ashram satsangs each day, though Maharaji was unable to attend.

Though I had requested the Naam Mantra earlier in the week, it was uncertain if that was going to happen due to his bodily weakness. On my last night at the ashram, I was informed the Maharaji had graciously agreed to meet with me in his home, to give the Naam Mantra. Very soon, I found myself there, as he prepared me for initiation. He placed his hand on my head, and spoke the Naam Mantra, and though his voice was weak, the strength of his Conviction resounded with a Bang! The Naam Mantra said everything that there was to say. Any disappointment I may have harboured from not being able to hear him give discourses, immediately vanished, as I understood that this is what I came to receive.

As truth is beyond words, there’s not much to say about Sri Ramakant Maharaj, but if I were to try to put it into words, I’d say that it was a beautiful thing seeing silence speak. That meeting in Oneness is imprinted in my Heart forever.

Sri Ramakant Maharaj returned attention back to the Reality, and away from ‘body-knowledge’. Slowly, silently and permanently, courage was provided, and with each step there was a growing peace, exceptional silence, and a deepening conviction of the Ultimate Truth: “Except Your Selfless Self, there is no God, no Brahman, no Atman, no Paramatman, No Master, no Disciple, nothing is there”. I bow to Selfless Self through Sri Ramakant Maharaj, and all the lineage Masters. Jai Sadguru!

I really don’t know why I have had the great fortune to meet Sri Ramakant Maharaj! But having been accepted, and initiated, has made such tremendous difference. The greatness of a Sadguru cannot be overstated, and the power of the Naam Mantra, cannot be overvalued. For those who long for Self-Realization, I implore you, drink in these words of Divine Wisdom, submerge yourself in them. As keen and earnest as you are, in equal measure will the unfolding process within you take shape. Better still, read this book thoroughly, and through it, you will be in the Presence of Sri Ramakant Maharaj

I am so, so grateful to my Master! My life transformed completely, after being in contact with Maharaj’s teachings. It is totally changing my whole frame of reference, of what I thought life was about. Yes, I was most certainly full of ego (an egomaniac), and for me (although I wouldn’t have admitted it at the time), my goals were to be rich and famous!

It was shortly after my marriage break-up, that I was introduced to spirituality, through Sri Nisargadatta Maharaj’s I Am That. This was interesting to me, as it offered some relief – a different way of looking at things, than I had ever thought of before. Gradually, my interest grew, and eventually, I had the good fortune of being able to meet Sri Ramakant Maharaj. Taking initiation, and receiving, (and attempting to put into practice), his teachings, has totally changed my life in ways that I could never have imagined.

The Master’s constant hammering that I am not the body, is working. The Mantra meditation is acting just like Maharaj said it would, and dissolving the concepts. It is so freeing to let go of so much baggage. The stronghold that the illusory ego had over me, seems to be subsiding, slowly. But it is not all plain sailing. Sometimes, I slip back a bit, and some old behavior, doubts, thoughts and feelings creep in. But then I realize with a jolt, the sense of bondage and heaviness that these concepts bring.

When something in life feels a bit clunky, sticky, heavy, difficult or charged etc., I find that Maharaj’s voice pops into my head, saying something like: “You are not the body”, or “who want’s this?”. Sometimes it might be a line from one of the devotional songs like Chidananda – “there is no father, no mother, no brother, no sister” -and this reminds me that everything is illusion.

In practical terms, I am getting so much strength, stability and calmness thanks to Maharaj’s teachings. What comes with this, too, is an immense sense of freedom and fun. I am finding more and more of my time, to be perfectly happy and amused for no reason – it’s a kind of spontaneous thing. Thank you Maharaj!

Having known of, and knowing Ramakant Maharaj for two and half years, I can only say that this is it! As the personal effort decreases, the Reality increases. There is no need of anything else. So simple and direct and actually effortless, (if you let it), the “practice” of keeping Selfless Self in mind proceeds and the illusion recedes. My search ended with Ramakant Maharaj, and the blessings are endless. Jai Guru!!!
Ramakant Maharaj is the first Master I have ever bowed to. That says it all!

The waves are dancing all around. I am the wave too, up to this point. My daily prayer is: “Let me fall into the Ocean where I will not dance any more as a wave, but be one with the Ocean.” The Master says you are not the body, mind or intellect. You are the Oneness! Just believe and trust the Master completely. Total trust in the Master’s words is the key to Final Reality. I bow down, and touch my Master’s feet, for this divine teaching.

Outside of your Selfless Self there is no Master! That teaching was made a living experience, when I met Ramakant Maharaj. It was not primarily transferred at one of the satsangs we had, or when Maharaj shared the Nama Mantra. One evening, he invited the visiting devotees for chai at his house, and we spent an hour there in quiet chitchat. Walking to the hotel afterwards in silence, I recognised that what I had encountered within myself in deepest meditation, and Maharaj, who had been sipping chai across the room, were one and the same.
That I’m sitting here, typing these words, can be conceptual; the unity between Maharaj and me is beyond doubt.

Since I was very young, I’ve been trying to understand what I am. I went through various masters and teachings, until I came across Advaita Vedanta and the book, I Am That of Nisargadatta Maharaj. This book was a great revelation, with the lucidity and clarity of the teachings. Nisargadatta stole my heart, and from then on, I would always talk to him, and tell him that, if I had to find a living teacher, he would lead me to him.

So, by chance, the Ramakant Maharaj page appeared on my Facebook. I went to look, and immediately went to Nashik, in February 2018. I felt immense joy and peace in the Ashram, with the bhajans, the teachings, and the simplicity. I noticed in the presence of Maharaj, that all questions, without exception, come from identification with the mind-body. And for the first time, I understood with my heart, what Nisargadatta meant when he said: “I only trusted my Master and followed what he told me”. Maharaj always emphasizes: nothing exists except the Self. But we do not really trust what he says. Having the honour of knowing Ramakant Maharaj was the greatest gift. He is always with me, in my heart. Trusting the Guru, and his instructions is everything. Om Shanti. Jai Guru Sri Ramakant Maharaj!

Since coming across the teachings of Ramakant Maharaj in his book Selfless Self, I knew I had to go and meet him, as I had never heard or read anything like that before. Since then, I have been following the practice with great determination, of meditation, Self-enquiry and chanting the bhajans. And, just like Maharaj said, a strong Conviction is emerging. Now my searching is over, and there is no longer anything missing in my life. No more roads and no more gurus!

I have been searching for decades, but now my search has ended with Ramakant Maharaj. I have met so many Masters but He is the only one who has shown me that the answers are all within me. His teaching showed me, that all the knowledge and concepts that I had accumulated, were in fact blocking the Truth. Thank you Maharaj for helping to discard this illusory baggage. Now I know what Maharaj means when he says “You are a free bird!”

There are three experiences that I would like to speak about. My first experience with Sri Ramakant Maharaj was through a photograph that a friend sent to me. I didn’t know who the man was in the photo, but I felt an immediate attraction. Some time later, I found out that he was Sri Ramakant Maharaj. That photo opened the door.

Naturally, I searched the internet for Sri Ramakant Maharaj, and found a website dedicated to the Master. I contacted the administrators, and they got back to me right away. We immediately became heart friends – perhaps Maharaji’s blessings! I owe a lot to these two wonderful beings One of them has now joined the Lineage’s Masters, in the open space, formless heart. He left this world. His story is a wondrous one.

The second experience of Maharaj and the teaching occurred when I had a dream of Sri Ranjit Maharaj (I know that sometimes it is said, that we shouldn’t publicly reveal certain dreams, but in this case I feel it is appropriate.) Ranjit Maharaj came to me in a dream and bestowed upon me the Naam Mantra of the Lineage that He and Ramakant Maharaj belong to. I didn’t know that it was the Lineage Mantra. A year later, when Ramakant Maharaj initiated me into the Naam Mantra, it was in the exact same way and mantra that Ranjit Maharaj had done previously, in the dream. I told this to Ramakant Maharaj as I wetted tears of joy, amazement and heartfelt gratitude.

The third experience relates again to a photo. I have a photo of Ramakant Maharaj that I use for Puja when I travel. One afternoon, I was reciting the “Afternoon Bhajans”. I lit a candle in front of the photo, after reciting the bhajans (the photo was not in a frame. I had it propped against Maharaji’s book Selfless Self). I left the room for a short period. When I returned, I noticed the photo had fallen over. When I lifted it up it had been burned by the candle. Low and behold an upside-down heart had been burned into the photo right beside the head of Maharaj, and his beautiful smiling face.

All this revealed to me, beyond the shadow of a doubt, the truth of Maharaji’s teachings, “that Guru resides in the space of the heart always”. The true Guru is eternally the heart – beyond form, formless, beyond space and time. But also right here in this space at this time, here and now always. Thank you, Maharaj, for the gift of your Presence and teachings. I bow to Thee! Jai Sadguru.
When I met Maharaj, I knew he was true straight away, and thus fell for him spontaneously.

I had the good fortune to meet various sages in my life and knew that the sense of presence and bliss is vital in determining whether a sage is true, or not. A real sage’s words never repeats learned knowledge from books. What he says is his own and flows spontaneously and fully original from his lips. And so it was with Maharaj!

During my visit, tears flowed from my eyes. “Melting” is what Maharaj called this phenomenon when I asked him about it. It had to do with the power behind his words. There was something crisp and electric about them, and they were spoken in a most direct sense. It is incredibly rare that one is spoken to as Brahman, as who I really am. I surrendered this body to His feet all the time.

I came to understand that all the practices have to do with vibrations. Whether they were Maharaj’s words, the Mantra or the Bhajans, their vibration made the Awareness more subtle, not only in the waking state, but also in the dream state, and in deep sleep. Maharaj says that the repetition of the Mantra becomes spontaneous after a while.

Over time, the Seer became aware of a tiny dot in Nothingness. That dot expanded extremely rapidly from Nothingness and when my room came into focus, thoughts came in, and with thought, my room expanded even further! This left me with the direct insight, that the word is nothing but a thought rising from Nothingness, a process that happens every morning in an instance. Most extraordinary!

Every afternoon we sing about this phenomena in the Bhajans: “In a sesame seed the whole universe remains, and God also remains there. The owner of the whole universe stays inside a small sesame seed, ‘house’. This smallest seed contains all three worlds.
Saint Tukaram says, “in one tiny drop (or one small seed), are the whole three worlds”.

If you read this in a book, it is impossible to comprehend, and it will remain a concept. But If you have seen for your Self it will remain with you for ever. Maharaj says, it will reveal itSelf spontaneously from within you! Jai! Guru Maharaj, my gratitude is too big for a word, but contained in a tiny seed!

I began my search in earnest for a “living”, True Master. Shri Nisargadatta had passed. I felt I had sadly “missed the boat” with Shri Nisargadatta Maharaj, by only a few years. But I was too late. He never mentioned, “Who?” would follow in His footsteps, or follow Him upon His death. I silently pined over His death for many years, but also was so happy to have found the Lineage books. I kept the Masters’ books by my side, highlighted and pencilled arrows on every page. These books would be my Master. They were tattered and torn, but worth their weight in gold. I still prayed one day, to meet my Master before leaving this world.

Years passed, and I came across a book entitled Selfless Self by Sri Ramakant Maharaj, a direct disciple of Sri Nisargadatta. I began to read it, and could not believe my eyes! This book spoke to me, in the same way “I AM THAT” spoke to me! I felt so blessed to find it! “Selfless Self” was FILLED to the brim with Truth and Wisdom, complete with instructions on the way to become Realized! The Naam Mantra! I was in heaven!

I finally met my Master, and received the Naam Mantra. I was beyond elated, overwhelmed, and could not speak much, if at all – as being in Maharaj’s Presence was overwhelming! I had wished for this Truth my entire life, and knew I was now “face to face” with Truth and Reality! My life has changed since that wondrous and glorious day, and all for the better. Nothing in life can match Maharaj. No matter what is happening in my family life, or in this illusory world, I have the powerful tool of the Naam Mantra, to keep me calm and unaffected. The Naam Mantra is extremely powerful, and not to be taken lightly. Maharaj teaches that meditation with Mantra is key; Master reveals ALL the secrets and leaves nothing to the imagination.

So, I heard Truth after Truth that resonated with this form I hold, and slowly the process of Realization began. Watching Him is a meditation, in and of, itself…such a generous and kind spirit is Maharaj.

In the form known as Maharaj, he teaches, and he hammers the Truth into His devotees. The Spontaneous Knowledge we have all searched for, happens – everything and all we need to know is within. Be With You! He confirms there is no one, or nothing there; nothing at all. Master exudes a special kind of ethereal kindness, and is unaffected by whatever is happening around Him, or problems of a personal nature. There is no body; no mind. We are One…no need to be concerned about death and dying because we were never born. Deeper understanding comes with practising the Naam Mantra.

In the years that followed, I have seen progress in my practice. There are many more days of joy and happiness now – and for no particular reason! No more deep concerns that used to seem so real. For this, I owe thanks and deep devotion to Maharaj. Shri Ramakant Maharaj.

Maharaj is a True Master of Truth, sharing freely everything one needs, in order to be free. We are not the body and we are not the mind; we are free…It is a simple Teaching, but it is up to each one of us to fully absorb the knowledge. And as I continue on in this world, there is peace within. I can now be a witness to what is happening, without being affected by it. The simple Truth is that after following the teaching of Dear Ramakant Maharaj, and appreciating and respecting both the Naam Mantra, as well as our Dear Maharaj, the one who is ready, can never look back to the old ways of living. You become transformed to Reality.

Words are inadequate, to describe the process of the beautiful change that just “happens” after meeting a Self-Realized Master. One cannot argue with Truth, and Maharaj is pure Knowledge and Truth. I aspire to reach His level. There is simply nothing left to want, or to desire after receiving the Naam Mantra.. I recite the sacred Naam Mantra every day, and nothing could be more beautiful. Your own inner Master will start talking… until there are no more questions. “I” am still in awe. Thank You, Shri Ramakant Maharaj, for a lifetime dream come true!!! My sincere devotion will never leave Your Feet; Your Footprints are forever engraved in my heart. Thank You, Master. You are the beginning, and the end. We are One. Jai Sadguru!

Meeting Maharaj, and reciting the Guru Mantra has changed my life. Before I used to feel so much anxiety, rage, anger and frustration, but these have now subsided to a huge extent, and now I really know what living in the present is! Even when strong emotions may start to rise, my attention goes to the Mantra.

My anxiety has reduced to the maximum extent, and I now know the meaning of spontaneous living in the present. Though I did not have any dramatic experiences during meditation, all my concepts are getting peeled away, like the peeling of an onion. Whatever is happening, there is an understanding that everything is happening spontaneously.

Words cannot express the transformation that has taken place, since discovering Sri Ramakant Maharaj. There is no longer a sense of time. The days and nights, weeks and years, just flow spontaneously. Everything flows, and unfolds, with a heart that is bursting with joy, and peace, that surpasses all understanding. There are no thoughts, only a vast emptiness that is filled with pure happiness, ecstasy and laughter. I did not know that it was possible, to be empty and full at the same time. I used to think I knew so much. Then, I discovered that I did not know anything at all. Knowing that there is nothing to know, is the greatest liberation! I bow to my great, and humble Master, with immense respect and gratitude!

title.p: GLOSSARY
Aham Brahmaasmi Mantra meaning "I am Brahman", "I am Ultimate Reality".
Amrut Nectar.
Atman The Supreme Self.
Atma Nivedanam Bhakti Highest Devotion, Self-Surrender.
Bhajans Devotional songs, Worship.
Bhakti Devotion.
Brahman The Absolute, Ultimate Reality.
Darshan Vision(s) of the Divine.
Dattatreya Adi-Guru (First-Guru) of the Navnath Sampradaya, considered as the incarnation of The Divine trinity of Brahma, Visnu, and Shiva.
Dharma Cosmic order, Divine Law.
Diksha Initiation.
Gunas Attributes, qualities. The three gunas: Rajas, Sattva, Tamas.
Hatha Yoga A system of yoga for gaining control over the physical body and life-force.
Karma Action, cause and effect.
Jnani Knower.
Krupa Blessing, grace.
Mahasamadhi The Great Merging, the final samadhi. The Ultimate goal of the spiritual seeker.
Maya Illusion.
Mukti Liberation, freedom.
Mula-Maya The primary illusion. Origin, root, base illusion.
Naam Mantra Mantra used by the Navnath Sampradaya Lineage, meaning "I am Brahman, Brahman I am".
Neti-neti "Not this, not this" method of enquiry.
Para, Pashyanti, Madhyama and Vaikhari The four levels of speech.
Prasad Food used as a religuous offering.
Rajas Excitable, activity, restlessness, egoism. One of the three gunas.
Samadhi Oneness with Self. Rapturous absorption.
Sadhana Spiritual practice.
Sadguru Highest and transcendent spiritual Guru, True Guru.
Sannyasin Renunciate.
Satsang Association with the wise, meeting in Truth.
Sattva Illuminating, pure. One of three gunas.
Shivoham Mantra meaning "I am Shiva", "I am Ultimate Reality".
Shakti Spiritual power, spiritual energy.
Shama, Dama, Titiksha, Uparati, Bhakti, Shradda Six sign of realization.
Soham Mantra meaning "I am That", "I am Ultimate Reality".
Swarup Reality, One's own form, nature.
Tamas Restraining - darkness, inertia, passivity.
Vipassana A method of meditation used in some forms of Buddhism, involving concentration on the body and it's sensations.

p.emphasis: Our Beloved Sadguru Sri Ramakant Maharaj Mahasamadhi, 31 August 2018, 11.23am, Mumbai.
